5oxfYP-.description: The longest highline in Poland so far. Established on 26.10.2020 by Adam Kaminski, Kamil Dudek, Kuba Baldych and Kuba Morawski. 70m high on the highest point
5oxfYP-.accessInfo: You have to climb to the anchorpoint in "Lyse Skaly"

LE4cON6.name: Chainsaw Massacre
LE4cON6.description: A beautiful line over Marshall's gulch
LE4cON6.anchorsInfo: Large pine trees on either side of gulley
LE4cON6.accessInfo: Walk 0.25 miles down Marshall's gulch trailhead to obvious gap on your right

5M351Qi.name: Northern Swingers
5M351Qi.description: Big line over high over the pool.
5M351Qi.anchorsInfo: Big trees on each side. They are a both around 6-7m away from the edge.
5M351Qi.accessInfo: Walk around the quarry top from the car park.

AJs0_hb.name: 150 soles
AJs0_hb.description: Linea perfecta para caminar, muy probable que te toque con viento. Una vista preciosa y exposicion única.
AJs0_hb.anchorsInfo: BFK de ambos lados, en lado norte esta un poco chueco al montar. Se usa una piedra como frame.
AJs0_hb.accessInfo: La subida es pasando por las lineas mas cortas y de ahi todo por la arista.

UdBsD0T.name: Bajulaz N°5
UdBsD0T.description: Ligne à l'intérieur du cirque de la Bajulaz, au-dessus d'un vide fort accueillant. Magnifique vue sur le Lac d'Annecy.
UdBsD0T.anchorsInfo:
Ancrage nord : 
3 goujons de 12 à côté d'une tâche de lichen formant le chiffre 5. 2 goujons de 12 pour poser un rappel de 5-6m, légèrement désaxé vers l'intérieur du cirque. 
Coord : 45.822423 , 6.285196

Ancrage sud : 
3 goujons de 12 sur une dalle inclinée, lunule en amont pour poser un petit rappel (facultatif).
Coord : 45.821322 , 6.286136
UdBsD0T.accessInfo: Se garer à Montmin, prendre la route qui monte droit dans le centre du village. Ensuite, il faut suivre le chemin qui monte au chalet du Lars, puis à la Pointe des Frêtes et enfin à la Bajulaz. La ligne est dans le grand cirque évident ouvert sur le Lac d'Annecy.

tFoClmD.name: Opran možgan
tFoClmD.description: Great line above watefall. Cannot be rigged simultaneously with 113m.
tFoClmD.restriction: partial: Ask SlackAlien before rigging for air traffic info.
tFoClmD.anchorsInfo: Tension side A: From backside tree 5m sling across rock and tied to small tree for stabilization. Static side B: from backside tree 10,5m sling and tree for height for stabilization.

QVsN_99.name: Regretamin
QVsN_99.description: A line in memory of the person that drowned here..
QVsN_99.accessInfo:
Walk through the hedge on the roadside for anchor 1..
Walk all the way round to the far side for anchor 2..

dKpYLgq.restriction: full: Slackline it's forbidden it's a natural Park

rlROm6x.name: Marwil III
rlROm6x.description: On either side two concrete poles
rlROm6x.anchorsInfo: On either side two concrete poles.

leaRs6A.name: The Duck’s Nuts
leaRs6A.description: A diagonal line across the top of Waterfall Gorge
leaRs6A.anchorsInfo:
East - Main/Backup - 4 M10 threaded rod with FH. A frame recommended.
   West - Main/ Backup - 5 M10 threaded rod with FH
leaRs6A.accessInfo: At the top of the gorge, cut back around the top on the east side for best access. For west side access, follow the vague path from the top of the gorge westerly for about 200m, and just before you meet the small creek line, go off track easterly to the gorge.

wlAKQ4j.name: Pedra Nayara
wlAKQ4j.description: Local próximo a cidade, com fácil acesso. O fato dos pontos de ancoragem estarem fixados na laje da cachoeira, as vias só podem ser montadas no período de seca, quando o nível do rio está baixo. (Abril a Setembro.)
wlAKQ4j.anchorsInfo: Ancoragem de 3 pontos em cada lado da via. Levar as chapeletas.

y6t7LQP.name: Premiers pas (first steps)
y6t7LQP.description: A short line, not very high but easy to access. A 5-minute walk from the Kuravan Kurathi Statue. The line was first laid on November 29, 2023. Be careful with the leash, as it can hit the rocks if you're too close to the anchors. This line is ideal for learning to stand up and taking your first steps, but not for more experienced highliners.
y6t7LQP.anchorsInfo: The anchors used for the first installation were PETZL Removable Anchor Coeur Pulse 12 MM. The holes are already drilled, but for a new installation you need to bring your own anchors.

Fhtl6Hk.name: North Gaulton Castle
Fhtl6Hk.description: Rigged by the 'Flying Frenchies' for the film Petit Bus Rouge

9oVVBld.name: Lion's Valley Park Gap
9oVVBld.description: Straight shot across the entire LVP from trees next to the bridge.
9oVVBld.accessInfo: It's legal but I wouldn't do it when the parking lot is open.

d0QF1L-.name: la 33
d0QF1L-.description: Need to throw the tag line. Might need to a few branches rappeling from the line. Respect climbers. 4 12mm bolts on each side. bring hangers
d0QF1L-.anchorsInfo: 4 12mm bolts on each side. bring hangers.

D-Q1fiB.name: Avorazadas y Atascadas
D-Q1fiB.description: Estas dos lineas (55s) pueden llegar a tener surf debido a los anclajes
D-Q1fiB.anchorsInfo: Uso de cuerdas, de un lado se usan diferentes arboles del otro es un sliding x a unos bolts.

pmiyjRB.restriction: partial: Police shows up almost everytime since its very popular for regular people to visit

pdAxOGJ.name: KWIaTEK
pdAxOGJ.description: You can't highlining there without a permission.

FeTQTgk.name: Łopata Mamy
FeTQTgk.description: if you want go there contact with me 882-073-846
FeTQTgk.anchorsInfo: One anchor is tree and second anchor is herring in the ground with tree.
FeTQTgk.accessInfo: Really easy access. You dont need to climb there

C7TxY0W.name: Apoteoza Autodestrukcji
C7TxY0W.description: Connection can be done without a drone (webbing on the bottom and taglines thrown from both sides), but the risk of webbing getting stuck in some bushes is relatively high. On the right side of the quarry (looking from the entrance), use an A-fram...
C7TxY0W.anchorsInfo: On the right side of the quarry (looking from the entrance), use an A-frame and set the anchor on the most obvious tree. About 4-5 m of spanset needed to reach the edge. Backup to another tree. On the other side there are several options for trees to use as anchor and backup. In any case use a little birch to set up a hang-frame. 4 m spanset is a minimum for the main anchor, and as much for the backup.
C7TxY0W.accessInfo: The right side accessible by a fairly comfortable path almost till the end. Reaching the other side is a bit trickier, first reach the top of the quarry (partially paths, partially wild), then find a copse of conifers (spruce or fir, I'm not sure) with a steep descend. Or walk on the top from the other anchor, takes longer but is a bit more comfortable. The anchor is a group of trees directly next to an exposed grassy ledge, with another ledge a few meters below.

8SrQQPa.name: Meritum
8SrQQPa.description: One of the shortest lines in UHF

TB9fOpV.name: Kanjonas (aka Karolina)
TB9fOpV.description: There were some landslides during last Summer after strong rain-showers. Spot is much more open compared to current satellite view. Deeper area is widening after the winter. Nice exposure to Neris river and Vingis park beyond that

XMAslTs.name: El pájaro 🐦 congelado ❄️
XMAslTs.description:
El lugar es propiedad privada, “La montaña de don Lauro”, se puede pedir permiso para poder subir a los miradores de la montaña.
Son aproximadamente 2 km de la base de la montaña a los anclajes, es un camino de terracería con muchas rocas sueltas, que te llevan a una intersección, camino a los miradores, a la derecha es el mirador “el zopilote” ahí se encuentra en anclaje del lado dinámico. Y a la izquierda es el mirador “el águila” ahí se encuentra el anclaje lado estático.
XMAslTs.anchorsInfo:
6 bolts químicos, es posible usar cuerda, directamente a los bolts.

-3 lado estático “el Aguila “ 
-3 lado dinámico “zopilote”

NWcNGba.name: Manjada
NWcNGba.anchorsInfo:
Tension side (north): 3x12mm expansion bolts. Use 17m of rope for bfk. Or 15m + 1m spanset.
Static side (south): 2x12mm expansion and a tree. 1,5m spanset on a tree and 4m spanset to equalise all 3. Under the anchor is one 10mm expansion bolt to tie down the anchor and stabilize it.
Both anchors need padding.
NWcNGba.accessInfo:
Tension side: To access you must to pass private land. Best stealthy path could be from the parking area above climbing crag, then up to this abandoned ''road'' and through bushes. We asked the owners and could access through their grass field. They were very friendly.
Static side: Go down in the climbing area, pass the water and follow the ridge to the anchor.

_7CSipN.name: impiegati statali
_7CSipN.description: ask to torino sul filo form more info small rope is already there
_7CSipN.anchorsInfo: in one site big tree in the other site there is a tree and also a bolt of 12
_7CSipN.accessInfo:
the pat is signed with small rock from Campo Pietra is about 15min walk. In one side you have to go on the left side of the climbing wall (La Parte delle gare), on the left site of the river (coming from the waterfall). Overcame the climbing wall on the left side and continuo up on the river then follow the small rock on the right side until you reach the waterfall. There you have to go back and go down (stay on the rock) until the borra, there you take the left side. you will find to reach the bolt you have to go down for the last tree. 
the other site you have to follow the river from the waterfall on he right site go up until you find a rock-wall there you take the left and you will find spit just down of the place where you can walk.

yC3iWXE.name: Hajek na Fpince
yC3iWXE.description: Hajek znajduję się w budynku ścianki wspinaczkowej Fpinka

H985w6u.name: Skor Gasilska
H985w6u.description: Best line on the spot with strange rigging.
H985w6u.anchorsInfo: Far side 3x 12mm expansion bolts. Tensioning side small tree for height and big for anchor
H985w6u.accessInfo: Far side bolts are hidden on an outside rock. Rappel of 10m needed.

UNASWcZ.name: And so it begins
UNASWcZ.description:
For details contact Andrei or Matei from Slacksylvania
 slacksylvania@gmail.com or on fb https://www.facebook.com/slacksylvania/

VN3yEm_.name: Mørkgonga
VN3yEm_.description: Trees. 20 min walk from the parking. Follow signs to Mørkgonga
VN3yEm_.accessInfo: 20 min walk from the parking. Follow signs to Mørkgonga.

RaW1Job.description: Parking at the service station of la Cabrera. Going up the hill is easier on the other side via the white/yellow marked path. When you arrive at the path on the top you turn right Sector 2

Ei0tjv5.name: Lady and the tramp
Ei0tjv5.anchorsInfo:
3x bolts & hangers at each end. 
Bolts = 12mm (x 160mm to 200mm) 316 glued in threaded rod. Glue = Either Hilti re500 or Ramset epcon c6

cBLgm_3.name: O Borze!
cBLgm_3.description: Access is really easy, car leave under the highline, road to the top is also easy. You should find a road (motocross road) to up, dont need to climb there.
cBLgm_3.anchorsInfo: One anchor is a tree, big tree, back-up also tree 5 meters behind the first tree. Second anchor is also tree, back-up again tree 2-3 meters behind main anchor
cBLgm_3.accessInfo: Really easy access. You dont need to climb there

flR0qVP.name: Cröt 1
flR0qVP.description: Highline at the Lezibrücke above Cröt
flR0qVP.restriction: partial: Those lines are higher then 25m, so they need to be announced at BAZL by SwissHighline. Your contact there for the region: ramun@swiss-highline.ch

uAEJPqA.name: The Tail
uAEJPqA.description:
Beautifully located line at a friendly length for begineers.
Bolted 13/7/25
uAEJPqA.anchorsInfo: Two bolts on either side, both need padding.
uAEJPqA.accessInfo: Cheapest parking/shortest walk is the Portland Bill Campsite (£5/car/day). Walk to the bottom end of the campsite and jump the barbed wire fence where it is pinned low. Walk through the quarry and head south until you aproach the line.

1PDvXP-.name: ReBarbora
1PDvXP-.description: Beautiful line linking two obvious points above playground in the valley. Something is making this line quite harder than expected.
1PDvXP-.anchorsInfo:
Eastern side: 3x 12mm bolts with washers and nuts so bring hangers and delta mailons or shackles. 3m spanset + 1/4m to move anchor out of the rock, shackle to connect both slings.
Western side: 3+1x 12mm bolts with washers and nuts so bring hangers a delta mailons or shackles. Fourth bolt is backup of the third. 2x 3m spansets, One for two right bolts and one for third left bolt, connect with shackle together nad put webbing anchors to the left one by sliding X.
1PDvXP-.accessInfo: Both anchors are of easy access. Best way is to get to them from the hill, from the valley its longer way.

nQmCS6M.name: Abelhas azuis
nQmCS6M.description:
O acesso a pedreira é pela rua das pedreiras na subida de uma estrada de chão de uns 100m chega-se no lugar de deixar o carro e começar a trilha. a Trilha tem 2 minutos até a base da Pedreira. A pedreira possui muitas pedras soltas, por esta razão é necessário atenção tanto na montagem quanto para a desmontagem.
A passagem da linha é feita por baixo onde existe um plato grande que é possível caminhar e conectar um nylon.
Atenção a chegada de escaladores no local.
Cuidado pode haver marimbondos ( vespas) nas ancoragens.
nQmCS6M.anchorsInfo: A via tem 3 bolts inox de 3/8 ou 10mm de cada lado. É necessário levar as chapeletas de casa.

otYLRmd.name: La Convencional
otYLRmd.description: Only rig and use for day. Derigg in the evening
otYLRmd.anchorsInfo: Arboles de los dos lados. comparte LADO B con "Las Pencas"

9t1N2Vy.name: Kirby Cove
9t1N2Vy.description:
Highline over a beach with a spectacular view of the Golden Gate Bridge and San Francisco.
FA by Daniel Tulberg in April 2016. Much thanks to Daniel for fixing the access ropes and releasing the beta.
Be careful here! It is steep and very loose. There is a small flat spot just above the tree where you can lay your things. Tape and water bottles love rolling away.
If you're planning to rig Kirby Cove, please say hi and let us know on SF Slackers (https://www.facebook.com/groups/112437018916868/) :)
9t1N2Vy.anchorsInfo:
1  10'-12' spanset + (backup)
1  10'-12' spanset + (backup)
60m+ tagline
Personal anchors + some cord

**Tension side**
The tension side tree is perched on the slope of the cliff and has lots of low thick branches. Wrap around the trunk of the tree just above the second big split (~3’ off the ground) with the two weblocks toward the ocean. (see photo)

**Static Side**
You'll want to be anchored on this side. An extra 6' spanset is handy to girth hitch onto the tree. There is also a fixed rope to a separate tree, which you can use for backup. Attach the weblocks as close to the ground as possible to minimize leverage on the tree. (see photo)

**Tagging**
For tagging on the tension side, anchor into the next closest tree along the cliff. Drop about 40m of either the webbing or your tag line PAST the bushes on the tension side. See beta photo for drop point. From the static side, drop the second line down into the water. Depending on the tide and your throw, you may have to go swimming to grab it! Bring this line back to the beach and tie the two lines together.
9t1N2Vy.accessInfo:
Best parking is at the top of Kirby Cove road: https://goo.gl/maps/5V8ofqnbpmM2
It is sometimes quite busy, so you may need to park up by the roundabout almost a mile up the road. Alternatives are the 30 and 76x buses, which stop at the top of Kirby Cove road. Ride sharing services will also drop off / pick up here.

Walk all the way down the dirt road. You'll go through some Eucalyptus trees, continuing through a white gate. Stay right of the old military bunker, through the day use picnic area and head up the steep slope to your right.

To access the static side, hike up the hill and west to one of the many little trails wrapping around to the other side. Find the small path down the steep hill heading out to the ocean. Descend the fixed ropes and you'll see the static side tree perched on a "fairly sketchy cliff".

WaKaVnz.name: Tros de cep
WaKaVnz.description: Trees on both sides. On the slope side better to use some sling to create a hang frame and get some more altitude. On the cliff side two trees can be equalized. From Avoriaz you get into the golf pitch, from there you go straight to the cliff. Is ...
WaKaVnz.anchorsInfo: Trees on both sides. On the slope side better to use some sling to create a hang frame and get some more altitude. On the cliff side two trees can be equalized.
WaKaVnz.accessInfo: From Avoriaz you get into the golf pitch, from there you go straight to the cliff. Is the only place around the cliff where the connection can be done without a drone.

YceBRzo.name: full combo
YceBRzo.description: for highline-freestyle, but not actually very high. Very easy to access, and may be easy to conrtroll?
YceBRzo.accessInfo: east side is very easy to access, you can apporach it on feet. but the other side is not easy. It's on a cliff,and you have to rope solo or be belayed. BTW, Stick Clip is necessaty

1aoL-sM.name: Oh my balls
1aoL-sM.description: Parking at the service station of la Cabrera. Going up the hill is easier on the other side via the white/yellow marked path. When you arrive at the path on the top you just cross it and already arrive at the Sector 1 of La Cabrera

S2l6Yr_.anchorsInfo:
Close side
2 point anchor with glue-in bolts, 4m+3m sling or rope.
Redirect over hangframe (if used as A-frame it can slip out of position which is a bit annoying) on the left. 
Bring enough padding and a rope to back up slings and tie anchor back to the big tree behind it, 2 quicklinks and 4 shakels. 
Far Side:
2 Glue-in bolts and the root behind the bolts for a 3-point anchor. Possibility to rig on rope or a 3m sling with quicklninks. Back up sliding-X with Rope. If needed, redirect with a big hangfram to avoid dead tree.

Zd4XRCo.name: Panther Creek Falls
Zd4XRCo.description: Easy approach and rig over a beautiful waterfal.
Zd4XRCo.anchorsInfo: Trees on each side.  Tension side is the north east anchor, near the main parking lot.  That tree needs an 8 foot spanset or larger.  Static side tree is south west, and takes an 8 foot spanset perfectly.
Zd4XRCo.accessInfo:
Tension side is an easy 2 minute walk from the trailhead.  Hop the fence and scramble down to the river.  The Tension tree is perched on the rock just above the falls.  TIE IN!  the rock is very slick!  

For the static side, drive around and park at the bend in the road (you can tell it has been used for camping).  Hike out the back of the campsite and follow the trail to the tree with obvious erosion from highliners.

NJAE6uh.name: Learn to drive trailer Dave
NJAE6uh.description: My webbing got stolen here, the tail got cut off, so better to derig after sessionning. Road barrier on both sides. Leave the car on the inside bend, walk the road
NJAE6uh.accessInfo: leave the car on the inside bend, walk the road

-6zGmrO.name: Arena-95
-6zGmrO.description: Tree anchors at both ends, 2,5 and 3 m slings required
-6zGmrO.anchorsInfo: tree anchors at both ends, 2,5 and 3 m slings required

gE7fW0C.name: Keep ya head down
gE7fW0C.description: Over a flooded quarry, if you find it you won't be sorry..
gE7fW0C.anchorsInfo: Tree's on both sides, one side has 5m extension..
gE7fW0C.accessInfo: Park up by the gate and head into the woods, if you don't know where you are going you might get lost.. Use google maps if you have the location, if not you should head west..

yI7SgGD.name: DL Bliss
yI7SgGD.description: Beautiful line right next to the lake, relatively easy rig. No fall zone on static side. Expect to be cheered on by hikers, boaters, and paddleboarders on nice summer days. Go for a swim when you get hot. This is also the site of possibly the largest ever rope swing into water, rigged on different bolts by Mike Wilson in 2010.
yI7SgGD.anchorsInfo: 3 bolts with hangers each side. Dynamic side south, static north. Slight scrambles for each anchor. Nothing too crazy for padding, couple towels and a backpack should do ya.
yI7SgGD.accessInfo: Park at the top and hike/hitchhike down to the trails, or pay the $10 to park in either of the lower lots. Once you're down there it's a short hike from either end of the lighthouse trail.

FSEdb03.name: 8 ore e 3 mesi
FSEdb03.description: Natural anchor + 3 bolts on one side, 4 bolts on the other side
FSEdb03.anchorsInfo: Natural anchor + 3 bolts on one side, 4 bolts on the other side.

DhOdMdZ.name: Žrtveno jagnje
DhOdMdZ.description: Shortest line on the spot, but high in the middle. Nice tie in point.
DhOdMdZ.anchorsInfo:
Tension side(south): 2x 12mm and 1x 10mm expansion bolts; 10m bfk and 2,5m spanset, 1m padding
Static side: 2x 12mm and 1x 10mm expansion bolts; 4m spanset equalised on 3 bolts, extended with 1m spanset, bolt on the edge to tie down the master point, 3x 1m padding, 15m rappel (1 bolt on top of the rock)
DhOdMdZ.accessInfo: Find path up to the top of static side and rappel of the bolt.

9QwzjKB.name: Dornröschen
9QwzjKB.description:
Very complicated anchors
Southern side with 25m slings, A-Frame
Northwrn side on trees 15m far away.

QL3tU3p.name: Coliseum
QL3tU3p.description: Hard to rig. Get some slings, lot of rope. And a lot of free time. And 6-meters spansets.

LWe1Vcv.name: Lolotte
LWe1Vcv.description:
Really chilly spot for highlining during few days : hut, campsite, toilet, swimming hole, watefall at 5min from the lines.

Opened by the team "The Ekiwibrists" in February 2018.
LWe1Vcv.anchorsInfo:
*Connection : use 100m tagline, start walking from the swing bridge with one guy on each side and walk together on both side of the canyon until the anchors.

*Anchor 1 : north side. 3 bolts diameter 12mm for the tension, 1 bolt diameter 10mm for the extra back-up. Bring yours hangers. 1 spanset 3m. 

*Anchor 2 : south side. 3 bolts diameter 12mm for the tension, one tree behind for the extra back-up. Bring yours hangers. 1 spanset 4m.
LWe1Vcv.accessInfo: Park at the end of Jig road at Woolshed creek car park. Walk on the miners track until the Woolshed Creek hut,  2 hours, 5km. When reaching the hut,  cross the small stream and continue your way on Mt somers walkway, just before the swing bridge turn left and follow the arete for 5min until you find the anchors of the 68m highline.

nhEt5ia.name: Split Rock
nhEt5ia.description:
This quarry is located at the top of Milton Lane, park either side of the road before the farm turning..
To approach either follow the tarmac/gravel path through the gate and keep following around to the right,eventually you will see it..
The quicker route is through the turnstile and field, keep the hedge to your left and you will find a gate.. your there
nhEt5ia.anchorsInfo: Tree's on both sides, use a lot of static..

hCYGPJx.name: Ensayo sobre la ceguera
hCYGPJx.description: Both anchors were done with removable bolts, to repeat this line I recommend to contact with local slackline group (Slackriders La Palma). With 4x4 car you can get close, so the hike is no longer than 30 min. With normal car count with 1-1,5h hike...
hCYGPJx.anchorsInfo: Both anchors were done with removable bolts, to repeat this line I recommend to contact with local slackline group (Slackriders La Palma)
hCYGPJx.accessInfo:
With 4x4 car you can get close, so the hike is no longer than 30 min. With normal car count with 1-1,5h hike
One anchor easy to access. The one on the tower needs strong climber, one pitch climbing route is rated by locals as 6b+ but some holds are gone already and the others are near to that. We rate it more like 7a

CTRNIhg.anchorsInfo:
Tension side(west): 3x 12mm expansion bolts and 3x 10mm expansion bolts.
Static side: 3x 12mm expansion bolts.

cm5CstM.name: Charlotte
cm5CstM.description: First line established over the dry quarry
cm5CstM.anchorsInfo: Good tree on one side. On the other side there is a good tree behind a small wall.

Lx3r4f_.description:
You can change the lenght of the line by just going one hole closer or further.
You feel better the exposure when you walk face to the bridge. 
It's an easy rig, just be careful when you go to the other ancho and normally the police comes to say h...
Lx3r4f_.anchorsInfo:
On the bridge side, you can just put a sling (with a lot of padding) in the hole to make the Anchor.
For the opposite side, you need to follow a little "path" until you reach a little tree. Put a rope to abseil 2 meters to reach the 4 bolts (m12 probably but the hangers are there), two for the main line and 2 for the backup.
Lx3r4f_.accessInfo: From Martigny, follow the road to Les Marécottes. Cross the bridge of Gueuroz (you can't miss it !) and park your car juste after, on the small place (3-4 cars). You can easily see where the line is.

jw9POy_.name: The One
jw9POy_.description:
This might be the most iconic line of the California Coastline, with a beautiful arched bridge framing the raw, rocky shore. The powerful waves of the Pacific are often smashing into the breakers, launching waves well above the height of the line, though at a safe distance.

The One was first rigged in 2010, and first sent in 2011. It has a short no-fall zone, as the arches of the bridge are close enough to hit during a whipper.
jw9POy_.anchorsInfo:
Trees and vertical columns normally allow your webbing to lay flat when you use your slings in basket configuration, so these horizontal beams will cause your line to lay 90° on its side using the same technique. To fix this, and add redundancy to your anchor, use two slings (approximately 2.5 meters in length) in basket configuration around the beams. Then, you'll connect all four ends of the slings symmetrically to the main line and backup shackles. Both shackles go through the sling ends in exactly the same way, and will be resting one on top of the other.

The concrete texture of the beam is very abrasive, so be sure to bring adequate padding for your slings.
jw9POy_.accessInfo:
Access to the anchors is slightly tricky. The anchors are cross beams which are about 8 meters above the ground, and cannot be accessed by free climbing. To get to the anchors, you must throw a rope over the beam and tie one end off to the column. Once the it's secured, you climb the other side of the rope (using whatever rope ascension technique you prefer), to get to the beam to build your anchor.

Bring a rope for either side, as rigging with only one shared access rope becomes very time-consuming.

QpPCoYp.name: mochilla rodeante
QpPCoYp.description: ask to local TORINO SUL FILO for more info
QpPCoYp.anchorsInfo: 3 bolt in one side and 4 bolt in the other site, you need 1 sling of 3 + 1 of 2 in one site, in the easy spot you need 1 sling of 3 and 10 m of rope to back up at the tree
QpPCoYp.accessInfo: from the village walk 7 min in the easy path trough Pessinea, when the pat start to go down follow the rock on the left

6Yzq8Rr.name: Klepcomanka
6Yzq8Rr.anchorsInfo:
Tension side (west): 3x 12mm expansion bolts (same as 33m): 17m rope bfk, bring hangers.
Static side: 3x 12mm expansion bolts (same as 55m): 10m rope bfk and 2.5m spanset.

VuBDjkf.name: Yo también me voy
VuBDjkf.description: You're in a natural parc, slackline is not allowed. Don't rig the line for long periods of time as you could get in trouble. Tree on one side, big boulder on the other (around 10m of sling / 20m of rope). From Benasque go up to Refugio de Coronas ...
VuBDjkf.anchorsInfo: Tree on one side, big boulder on the other (around 10m of sling / 20m of rope).
VuBDjkf.accessInfo: From Benasque go up to Refugio de Coronas and follow the GR11 up to Ibón Bajo de Vallibierna.

AR9hfps.name: Mamma Mia!
AR9hfps.description: Astonishing spot! Right after village Charakas you will find canyon. There are two climbing sectors. Plenty options to rig a line from 20m up to 600 with a great see view.
AR9hfps.anchorsInfo: All natural. Road anchor 6m spanset. The other side 3m.

PtaW7ar.description:
La ligne est dans un léger cirque, elle est proche du bord, mais il ne faut pas se laisser avoir par cela. Elle est full gaz et possède une ouverture terrifiante. 

La ligne offre une vue imprenable sur le Mont-Blanc et les Aravis.
PtaW7ar.anchorsInfo:
Ancrage Nord : 3 spits de 12mm accessibles en se couchant sur le bord de la falaise.

Ancrage sud : un rappel de quelques mètres permet d'accéder à 3 spits de 12mm.
PtaW7ar.accessInfo:
Du hameau du Coudray, prendre le chemin des chalets de Varan (1620 m). Des chalets de Varan, remonter la grande combe (Le Vellard), d'abord plein centre, puis rive gauche sur la fin, jusqu'au col de Barmerousse (2325 m). Chemin tracé de points rouges à partir des chalets.
Du col, monter plein W, pour atteindre les contreforts du sommet de l'aiguille de Varan (points orange). Traverser à droite, sous l'aiguille

rlxKJXL.name: Happy Birthday
rlxKJXL.description: The standout line across Mt Greville's 'Great Gully', with Moogarah dam photobombing every shot in such a great way. This line was sent for the first time by Mia Noblet on her birthday.
rlxKJXL.anchorsInfo:
South - Balcony - Main/Backup - 3 RB.
North - Outside Pinnacle - Main/Backup - 3 RB and 1 FH
rlxKJXL.accessInfo: This line crosses the Great Gully to the middle of the outside pinnacle. To get there follow the ridge up the pinnacle to an exposed scramble where you will eventually find a double bolt belay. Lead through a tricky start past a FH and veer slightly left and up a further 8m to the anchors. A shallow crack for a #0.5 camalot can be found on the runout.

cHVG7Wf.name: Gigante do Vale
cHVG7Wf.anchorsInfo: 4 bolts em cada lado e um back up natural no lado B.

9XkoER5.restriction: partial: It's a very popular spot for regular visitors. Police show up every time. Be aware. There is no 'official' way to get permission.
9XkoER5.anchorsInfo: One side 3 glue-in bolts other side 3 metal stakes. Make sure you check the condition of the stakes.

mZga5mN.name: River project
mZga5mN.description: one of the biggest and pretty lines of the Cirat highline festival.  you need a dron

dmvWGUT.anchorsInfo: 3 stakes west side, prominent tree east side

9PueSV9.name: Yati Ochi
9PueSV9.description:
A very nice line with some exposure and quite different than the others of Meteora, wilder. Very easy access and rigging, perfect for raining days or non-climbers highliners as there is no need to climb ! 
Open by :  Vassili Theodorou, Maurice Ammann, Laetitia Gonnon, January 2016
9PueSV9.anchorsInfo:
The anchor on the right side (when facing the canyon) is on a huge round rock just before the one with a little tree. You will find a belay to rappel down 5m (not vertical at all) to the anchors. You can also walk to the anchor if it’s not slippery. The anchor is 3 bolts of 10 (12 ?) mm plates, which you need to bring. You can do a master point on the 4 bolts and then a general back up on the belay above. You will need 4m of slings.
On the left side (when facing the canyon), the anchor is also on a round huge rock. On this side there are 4 bolts for a master point, 10mm, and then a 5th both a bit further from the cliff for a general back up. You will need 5m of slings.
9PueSV9.accessInfo:
From the village Kastraki continue the road towars the Megalo Meteoro monastery. Park here. Then walk  in north direction to the « fielias » rocks. 
Once you reach the canyon, the line is on the second gap.

lNz5Tun.name: Slouse Juice
lNz5Tun.description: short highline/midline perfect for first highline
lNz5Tun.anchorsInfo: 3 bolt anchors with chains on both sides. Easy access to both anchors.

TpZ1rsz.accessInfo: Easy access from cemetery parking lot. Above Il Mito climbing area

q47yE2Z.name: Dolça quarentena
q47yE2Z.anchorsInfo: 4 bolts each side (10mm). On the static side a 3m spanset, 2m of dynema sling to extend one of the bolts and a 10m rope for the backup. On the dynamic anchor 10m of rope to equalize the three points and 4m of spanset to extend it to the border. Some extra rope for the backup.
q47yE2Z.accessInfo: From Saint Pierre dels Forcats or from Eyne up to Pic de Cambre d'Aze.

ZkqyxuR.name: Over Easy (the 1490)
ZkqyxuR.description: A big, beautiful line and a short approach. What's not to like?
ZkqyxuR.anchorsInfo: 3 bolts with chain-link hangers on each side.
ZkqyxuR.accessInfo: To get to the Fruit Bowl, take UT-313 W and turn right onto Mineral Bottom Road.  Follow that road for 8 miles until it crosses over the only cattle grate.  Turn right soon after that onto a well established off-road trail, and take that the rest of the way to the parking area.

bl_SOJ-.name: So ill
bl_SOJ-.description: 50ish meters bounce line directly above the shelter one waterfall.
bl_SOJ-.restriction: none: Located at state park above waterfall. Has potential to be permarigged.
bl_SOJ-.accessInfo: 5-10 minute walk on the shelter one bluff waterfall trail

CNxGWtv.anchorsInfo:
Tension (east) side: tower: 6m spanset wrap around or 8m scorpion style from top.
Static side: 2x 12mm expansion bolts on the edge. Use glue-in and 10m of rope to get to the bolts.  Climbing anchor for backup. Bring hangers.

nWHchZe.name: Rattlesnake Falls Highline
nWHchZe.description: Boulder wrap next to falls, Tree wrap across the way
nWHchZe.anchorsInfo: Boulder wrap next to falls, Tree wrap across the way

EX4hY2s.description: From tree to tree next to Hanmer Bridge. First Send by Cheyne Pearson. Set up/ found spot Petr Polak, Jerome Alxiant, Fer Kpok.
EX4hY2s.anchorsInfo: Big Tree on one side, smaller tree in other side.
EX4hY2s.accessInfo: Go to the Jet Boat company and walk to the Bungee Jumping Platform.

EIg1Xh_.name: Sleklajn na zmaju sm
EIg1Xh_.description: Line from the pool to Stičić junction tower.
EIg1Xh_.anchorsInfo:
Tension side: concrete terrace pillar, 7,5m spanset to the edge of pool. 2,5m higher that Static side.
Static side: 3x 12mm expansion bolts, 15m bfk and 6m spanset to tree for height, 1m padding, tree pro

iY3XgAg.name: Telefonmann
iY3XgAg.description: Midline oder Highline, depends on the water level of the lake
iY3XgAg.restriction:
partial: Due to sensitive relationship with the Verbund (owner of the lake)  highliners need a membership at Sportverein slackline.at for an insurance. 
Please contact slackline.at, before highlining there!
iY3XgAg.anchorsInfo:
nearside: tree on highest top of the peninsula.
farside tree on hill.

kCJ4IJk.name: Leśnik
kCJ4IJk.description: Probably longest line here. Beautifful place easy rig, super close from car.
kCJ4IJk.anchorsInfo: Trees, one side 2,5-3m sling single tree on the edge. On the other side 2 tress equalized to place masterpoint in good place.

G8i8c8V.name: Unnamed
G8i8c8V.description: Nice lil 50m line. More of a no fall zone than on Split Pea Soup but still a great line to cruise
G8i8c8V.anchorsInfo: Same far anchor as Split Pea Soup, redirect off oak to cedar tree and go directly across the gap to a smaller oak tree.
G8i8c8V.accessInfo: Contact "forest@louisvilleky.gov" with the date you plan on visiting along with your vehicle info to get the access code to the gate.

Nms_vaR.name: Narava zajebava
Nms_vaR.description: Epic line, both side edge of rock.
Nms_vaR.anchorsInfo:
Tension side(south): 3x 12mm expansion bolts: 10m rope bfk and 2m spanset
Static side: 3x 12mm expansion bolts: 10m rope bfk
Nms_vaR.accessInfo: Both sides are the most outer part of rock you can reach without rope. Some sketchy rocks to get over on static side.

e6wBbn-.name: 60m Highline at Bärenschlucht
e6wBbn-.description: Highline over the road at Bärenschlucht. hight approx. 40m.

rT-cKFp.name: Mama Africa
rT-cKFp.description: As we have been here in feb 2017, not all of the marokkan people reakted positive. There are some "responsibles" that maybe coul´d want yout to put the highline down. So my reccomandytion is to plan the well visible Highlines in the tour...

u8X_TUr.name: Louis-philippe - Jeunesse
u8X_TUr.description: 30m highline from the top of Jeunesse to Louis-philippe
u8X_TUr.restriction: partial: KBF or CAB member for Belgians. UIAA membership for others is obligatory.
u8X_TUr.anchorsInfo: Each anchor on 4 climbing bolts, You need 12mm delta's to connect your slings
u8X_TUr.accessInfo: You need to rappel +- 15m to get to the LP anchor, you need to bring a 50m rope as fixed rope for easy acces of jeunesse.

Fv2XaKl.name: Cascade des Thermes ???
Fv2XaKl.description:
Une des premières highlines de France. Il ne faut pas se fier à sa longueur, les falaises abrupte la rendent très gazeuse.

Magnifique ligne avec la cascade en arrière-plan. Elle est bruyante, mais elle devient féérique en hiver si elle gèle !
Fv2XaKl.anchorsInfo:
Rive droite : 3 goujons en 12 (rééquipée en 2024), juste à côté de la via-ferrata, on peut faire un rappel de 5 mètres depuis un relais au-dessus pour être plus confortable.

Rive gauche : un arbre en bord de falaise et un deuxième 2-3 mètres derrière pour le back-up.

Attention le terrain est vite glissant de ce côté et surplombe la falaise, une main-courante est fort appréciable.
Fv2XaKl.accessInfo: Depuis le parking des thermes de Saint-Gervais-Les-Bains, suivre le chemin en direction de la "Passerelle des Thermes". On arrive sur la passerelle qui enjambe les gorges. La highline est juste en amont de la passerelle.

8jRwLmk.name: Abandoned boiler station
8jRwLmk.description: To rig highline you should climb on the second floor of construction. Also there is intense wind here. But roof and walls of this building are not destroyed finally...
8jRwLmk.anchorsInfo: Engeneers who build this boiler station left steel loops in the floor which can be useful to tie ropes and connect shackles. You can use concrete columns to put the second tail of the webbing / rope.
8jRwLmk.accessInfo: Its simple to get to the spot by stairs :) If you are not a nerd...

ZqL0ylu.name: “Wild, man”
ZqL0ylu.description: Once you’re at the top of the trail you’ll follow rock Karen’s to the cliff edge where the bolts are. These are 5 inch p bolts set up in an equilateral triangle. Fairly steep hill with trail up the side, it’s steep but short
ZqL0ylu.anchorsInfo: These are 5 inch p bolts set up in an equilateral triangle
ZqL0ylu.accessInfo: Fairly steep hill with trail up the side, it’s steep but short.

I_PTqhK.name: Ackermannbrücke Augsburg Midline
I_PTqhK.description: Midline, not a waterline, since the Wertach is typically too shallow. West anchor is the street light on the bridge. East anchor is a tree
I_PTqhK.restriction:
partial: We have been sent away by the police a couple of times (assertive but friendly).
The reason given is that drivers on the big road nearby would be distracted.
So when you want to rig here keep in mind it might become a very short session ;)

AGJCCGR.name: Ya pareces
AGJCCGR.description: 53m (L) x 70m (H),  One of Ecuador´s top lines. Just below the Chimborazo volcano, the country´s Highest location and the planet's closest point to the Sun : ) Below an incredible climbing park. Sport climb and trad.
AGJCCGR.anchorsInfo: 3 bolts on each side, need padding
AGJCCGR.accessInfo: is located in "La chorrera", its a five minute walk from the parking lot, where you can camp. Its 3500MASL altitude so its cold.

UBtp3ki.name: Rain as a gift
UBtp3ki.description:
The Moai - natural anchor
The wall - 4 10mm bolts. No hangers
Crew: Kamil Sustiak, Stepan Novikov, Alexandra Bogdanova

Bax2WYC.name: Skorajšnji povratek
Bax2WYC.description: Interesting closed line in the canyon.
Bax2WYC.anchorsInfo:
Tension side A: tree, 2m spanset
Static side B: trees, A frame

T0zXShB.description: La ligne historique des Dents de Lanfon, dans un gap visible depuis le bas. Vue plongeante sur le lac d'Annecy.
T0zXShB.anchorsInfo:
Côté Annecy : 
3 goujons dans une dalle.

Côté Doussard : 
Un arbre + un goujons et un arbre en arrière sur une plateforme.
T0zXShB.accessInfo: Rejoindre le col des Frêtes depuis Planfait ou Villards dessus, puis redescendre légèrement pour rejoindre un câble qui rejoint l'arête et l'itinéraire classique de la traversée des dents. Arrivé au col continuer sur la droite en suivant le chemin, passer deux petites dents pour trouver un gap évident.

VFv6_UF.name: Highway to Megakante
VFv6_UF.anchorsInfo: You have to rappel down 25m on the west side. Both sides are bolted 10mm but without plates.
VFv6_UF.accessInfo: You can drive up on the top and than it's a 10min walk to the line

8YKPNbc.name: Ensayo sobre la Ceguera
8YKPNbc.description: It's necessary to climb the tower
8YKPNbc.anchorsInfo: 3 holds each side, It would needs to set up MMS

x27H6Tw.name: Krvavica
x27H6Tw.description: Nothing special highline sometimes used as a winter perma line. Above a small local climbing crag.
x27H6Tw.anchorsInfo:
Tension side A: tree: tree for height, 6,5m spanset + 1m for height
Static side B: 3x 12mm bolts

iuA9YYZ.name: Coral e Borboleta
iuA9YYZ.description: Use tree protector, and stay cliped all the time. Hard line with a diference between the anchors of 7 meter or less of high. Tree anchors. Hard trail, the entrance is on the right side of the way

1RbNIwY.name: Harwoods Hole – Hard day for lazy people
1RbNIwY.description:
Opened in december 2017 by the team Ekiwibristes.
Harwoods Hole, Golden Bay, New Zealand.
1RbNIwY.anchorsInfo:
* Connection :
Use a drone is the easiest way and maybe the unique way ! If you set up the 50m you can definitely try to make the connection with a tagline. From the rappel of the right anchor of the 50m, walk 20m on the right in the lapiaz to the edge in the direction of the anchor of the 100m. Throw a tagline to the guy rappelling down to the anchor. 
* Anchor 1 :
South side (left anchor facing the hole): same place as the 50m. 1 big tree, the highline start between 2 verticals trees as a ski race start ! 1x3m 1T spanset.
* Anchor 2 :
North side (right anchor facing the hole): 5 expansion bolts 10mm for the rappels, 5 expansion bolts 10mm for the highlining anchor, 10 hangers 10mm, 1x3m 1T spanset, 100m static rope for the rappel, lot of protection for the rope and the spanset.
1RbNIwY.accessInfo: Check the description of the highline of 50m (Pits, nits and 10mn too late). The left anchor (facing the hole) is located as the same as the 50m. For the right anchor (really tricky one, that explain the name of the line), first go to the right anchor of the 50m, instead of rappel down, continue your way in the sharp lapiaz following the hole during 20-30m, then it is a mix of easy bush and lapiaz that brings you to some trees. Rappel down from the lowest one, 1m before the lapiaz (take lot of protection for you rope !!!).  First rappel (5m) brings you to a slab rock where there is a bolt to make a rebelay that allows you to reach another bolt 2m on your right. Continue to rappel down (10m), you will find another bolt to make a rebelay. Another rappel again (10m) brings you to a slab rock with a horizontal crack you can catch with your hands, 3m further on your right, a last bolt to make a last rebelay for a last rappel of 5m to the anchor of the highline, phew !!!!

j20Yo9b.name: Augen Zu und Henrik Weg
j20Yo9b.description: Line overlooking the sea at Morro Falqui
j20Yo9b.accessInfo:
2 minute walk from road parking for Morro Falqui.
Main side: below some big bushes close to the ledge
Seabound side: rappel 5 meters to 3 bolts. build your rappel anchor using big holes in the limestone (abalakov)

EijvTLx.name: Slap
EijvTLx.description: Shortest line on the spot, positioned directly above the waterfall.
EijvTLx.anchorsInfo: 3 glueins 10mm and 1 for backup both sides.
EijvTLx.accessInfo: One anchor is on the ledge between two bushes(it could be hidden under the gravel) the other one is on the corner of the ledge where u need to climb down a little.

J9DWsFh.anchorsInfo: Três pinos no lado do estacionamento, árvore do outro lado.

ul5ycsy.name: BABEL TOWER
ul5ycsy.description: To climb the tower you need trad equipment. It's about 100m high, we did it in 3 pitches with a single 60m rope. It's not difficult climbing, but there are lots of loose stones. It would be very helpful to make connection with a drone, but we mana...
ul5ycsy.anchorsInfo: Tower side: two pine trees, back as a main anchor, front as a hang frame, long rope for a backup to the relativ big tree some meters(maybe 10?) behind. Other side 3 holes 12mm for removable bolts plus random rope backup to some trees.
ul5ycsy.accessInfo:
about 40 minutes-1h walking from the car, at the beginning there will be a path, but not till the end, if possible try to walk later more in the forest than between the bushes. Use GPS coordinates: 44°01'06.4"N 21°55'01.7"E

Park here: 44°01'06.4"N 21°55'01.7"E

xiT5laI.name: Inespérée - L : 42m
xiT5laI.description:
Linking :
Really hard, if you don't have a bow and arrow. We dropped a 160m rope from the Vase de Chine side to the ground, and then climbed the 120m west angle to link the two anchors.

Rigging :
The two anchors are suspended (no platform), so we used a removable pulley technic to get the line without any pulley in an anchor. The pulleys were installed on the Vase de Chine anchors. It is possible to use the weight of somebody to rig the line, because the cliff is overhanging. Rigging very engaged.
xiT5laI.anchorsInfo:
Anchors :
- Vase de Chine side : From the main footpath, step down to go behind the vase de chine. You will find spits to install a belay that will allow you to belay down from 5m to access the anchors on the overhanging cliff under. There are 5 12mm spits (3 for force and 2 for backup).
- Roche décollée side : To go on the top of the Roche décollée.
2 options :
Climb the 120m west angle (3 pitches - 6a+ max)
OR
From the main footpath, walk up the main cliff, to find a small path on the right that lead to a 2 pitches belay. You will arrive on the north face of the Roche Décollée. From there climb up a 25m 5b pitch. Walk a bit, jump the characteristic gap of the Roche Décollée and head for the north west angle. There are 5 12mm spits + 2 bolts (climbing belay) + a good tree for backup
xiT5laI.accessInfo: Drive to Le Rozier, then go to the Gorges de la Jonte (1km), park on the first good parking on the right of the road. From there take the footpath on the left side of the road. You will pass left to a climbing sector called "les femmes et les grimpeurs" and go up to a small gorge (fixed ropes), then arrive on the top of the cliff. 45mn.

D2dKjA-.name: Baby steps
D2dKjA-.description: On the side of the gate it is easy to anchor directly to the drains on the wall, for the other side a strong tree is used not so on the shore
D2dKjA-.anchorsInfo: On the side of the gate it is easy to anchor directly to the drains on the wall, for the other side a strong tree is used not so on the shore

3k8sQhB.name: Left one (Левая)
3k8sQhB.description: Top of the crown of mount Induk.
3k8sQhB.anchorsInfo: 4 glue-in stainless steel bolts both side. You need only 12mm hangers.
3k8sQhB.accessInfo: The left side is easy to get by foot. The right one you need to climb from the back of the cliff. It's not quite hard, maybe 5c or less.

_b55WlG.name: Emosson
_b55WlG.description: Line over the lake, first installed July 2023, separate signalisation line is required
_b55WlG.anchorsInfo: East side, massive metal bar (WW2), small A-frame, West side 4x12mm short slings no A-frame

VwrmZpp.name: Illusion of flying

1wSDw0_.restriction: full: Permission is required for use. For inquiries, please contact us on our Instagram account @bodyproducts.ibaraki.

Bolts = 12mm (x 160mm to 200mm) 316 glued in threaded rod. Glue = Either Hilti re500 or Ramset epcon c6

DjcF7-a.name: C'est du buis
DjcF7-a.description: Ligne mythique du var, visible depuis le bas des gorges d'ollioules

4qOAvJL.name: Villa Rica
4qOAvJL.description:
Una linea en una quebrada al mar
se puede acampar en la playa del pueblo y es un lugar seguro
4qOAvJL.anchorsInfo: Anclajes de dos puntos epoxicos de titanio

_RIuPs4.name: Prohibido el paso
_RIuPs4.description: Highline at Cap de Formentor. It takes a while to go there because its a super touristic place. I wouldn't go during the summer because its super crowded with cars and i heard that sometimes they close the road, only letting in tour buses

MZOMpi1.name: Getting hair on my inch worm
MZOMpi1.anchorsInfo: X2 glue in 150mm carrots on lhs and rhs is a bomber tree which can also be backed up

KmYfF3k.description: Magnifique ligne en parallèle de la première, un peu plus ouverte. Elle surplombe le lac d'Annecy, les montagnes en toile de fond, accès facile, installation facile, le combo parfait.
KmYfF3k.restriction:
partial: Pas besoin de NOTAM pour cette ligne, mais merci d'envoyer un message à la liste de mail, au moins 2 jours avant l'installation de la ligne.

Il faudra aussi appeler les deux numéros le matin de l'installation: 

Ce mail devra contenir :
- La date d'installation et de désinstallation de la ligne 
- La commune d'installation : La Thuile
- La longueur de la ligne : 300m
- L'Altitude de la ligne : 750m
- Les coordonnées des ancrages :
Ancrage 1 : 45° 48' 29" N, 6° 11' 51" E
Ancrage 2 : 45° 48' 38" N, 6° 11' 56" E
KmYfF3k.anchorsInfo:
Côté arbre : Prendre un gros fayard à sa base et se servir de l'arbre avec les branches coupées comme "Frame". il est vivement conseillé de prendre un deuxième arbres pour renforcer le "Frame", le premier est relativement penché.

Côté cailloux : 2 points au sol au niveau de la petit sente et 2 points en arrières.
KmYfF3k.accessInfo:
En 20 min depuis le hameau "les Maisons", se garer en bord de route et suivre la piste. Accès facile, mais parking en bord de route et en pente.

En 15 min depuis le parking de la carrière.
Prendre la route qui longe le camping de l'Idéal, elle se transforme en piste, puis se garer au parking en bout de piste.
Prendre le chemin qui monte 20m avant le parking. Le chemin est raide, l'idéal est de RIG depuis l'autre côté et se poser à ce parking pour dormir.

ELlPJC9.name: Andy's line
ELlPJC9.description: About an hour hike from parking, 24 direct but 240m added exposure with beautiful desert view. Easy rigg easily hand taggable.
ELlPJC9.anchorsInfo: Three titanium glue in bolts per side, only need some static rope for a bfk.

VLPeKaZ.name: Little Wind
VLPeKaZ.description: First highline in Hejszowina. Really cool line for the first highline. Rigging from one side out of the steel structure of the viewpoint and on the other side sling a huge sandstone boulder. Has been freesoloed lately by Jan Gałek.

eXT0vcZ.name: Volveré y seré millones
eXT0vcZ.accessInfo: by foot, 15/20 minutes from the road

3vpo8sB.name: Txai Line - linha do txai
3vpo8sB.description:
This is one of the three lines that have been conquered in this location.  It goes right across the opening of the cave. This is a beautiful place in Chapada Diamantina Bahia anda its open for visitors. Two of the lines at this spot were conquered by me and my partner while traveling all over Brasil in search for spots to put up the most incredible Highlines. The trail is quiet easy but its also easy to get lost. If you want to get to know this spot or any of the others in the Chapada Diamantina, get in touch with us for more information.
Follow on instagram at @_desinfluente and @juliabluu to check out some of our other adventures conquering Highlines all over Brasil.
3vpo8sB.restriction: partial: get in touch at equilibrionomade@gmail.com
3vpo8sB.accessInfo: You will need a guide. If you want to come to Bahia and walk on many insane Highlines, get in touch, you will be very welcome with us on this mission.

AwbXnSx.name: Medo de Chuva
AwbXnSx.description:
Linha de Highline aberta sobre o Canyon do Rio São Jorge. 
Devido ao desnível na altura do terreno, é necessário utilizar um Hang-Frame de pelo menos 1,5m no lado (A) da cachoeira para compensar a altura.
AwbXnSx.anchorsInfo: Ancoragem com 4 parabolts 10mm no lado da cachoeira e 3 parabolts 10mm na outro lado do canyon. (levar chapeletas)
AwbXnSx.accessInfo: Para acessar o lado B do Canyon é necessário fazer um rapel até o local das chapas.

qR2vlbF.name: Beck Hole Viaduct
qR2vlbF.description: A nice low level line over the top of the higher waterfall in Beck Hole
qR2vlbF.anchorsInfo: Tree to tree anchor allows easy rigging
qR2vlbF.accessInfo: A narrow track behind the main waterfall at beck hole takes you under the viaduct and through the other side. Here you can set a line up just in front of the waterfall

NQe6xIs.name: Lots to learn
NQe6xIs.description: Beautiful spot with a sweet line over the water hole, watch out for the crowds though!
NQe6xIs.anchorsInfo: Large tree on the north side, Boulder wrap backed up by a tree on south side
NQe6xIs.accessInfo: Arborist required for north side anchor and scramble for south side.

MA_-iPT.name: Once Upon a Time
MA_-iPT.anchorsInfo: Uno de los anclajes se encuentra en un gran bloque, tener cuidado.

q8C_2Gi.name: Cats Crack
q8C_2Gi.description: A line from the outside to the middle pinnacles. This is one of the stand out lines on the mountain with the king position. This line is a bit of an adventure to get too so make sure you get an early start.
q8C_2Gi.anchorsInfo:
East - Outside pinnacle - Main - Trad, two cracks that take medium to large nuts/hexes/cams
    - Backup - Boulder 3 m back and slightly off to the side
   West - Middle pinnacle (FV) - Main - 3 RB
		    - Backup - 3 RB from Cat to Main line
q8C_2Gi.accessInfo: To access the outside pinnacle climb the ramp for a short exposed scramble to the top. The main middle pinnacle can be accessed via the under protected trad climb (30 m grade 8) starting at the saddle between the two middle pinnacles, or the 20 m grade 18 sport climb (9 FH), which is the second sport climb heading down the gully between the outside and main pinnacles. From the top of this climb scramble to the slight right to find a final double bolt belay (exact 30m rap to the ground), then a short scramble to the top.

B2RUkKE.description: All lines bolted in the area
B2RUkKE.anchorsInfo: All lines bolted in the area

bbxWfqQ.name: Primeiro as Damas
bbxWfqQ.description: Praia do secret setor big line
bbxWfqQ.anchorsInfo: 4 pinos de ancoragem química de cada lado, dispensa conectores
bbxWfqQ.accessInfo: Trilha rápido com algumas subidas no começo, o lado B na metade da trilha para esquerda, segue para praia trilha em baixo da ancoragem do presente

a5K-s68.name: La Jaiuno
a5K-s68.description: 4 bolts in each side. Car nearby the spot

w1b6kyR.name: The 47m Line - T-shirt Gully
w1b6kyR.description:
NW anchor four by threaded rod with rounded hangers
SE anchor three by threaded rod with rounded hangers, and one by bolt with hanger
w1b6kyR.anchorsInfo:
NW anchor four by threaded rod with rounded hangers
SE anchor three by threaded rod with rounded hangers, and one by bolt with hanger

78OcK9r.name: Douceur d’automne
78OcK9r.description: Magnifique ligne en pleine montagne. Un pur bonheur
78OcK9r.anchorsInfo:
Ancrages sur deux arbres bien solide de part et d’autres de la rivières. Le premier est en contrebas de la colline, il est un peu isoler et se divise en plusieurs troncs ( quelque branches sciés à la base ). 
L’arbre en face se trouve au dessus des petites dalles rocheuses que l’on aperçois sur les photos ! Mettre les élingues bien haut dans l’arbre pour être à niveau ! ( quelques branches scies aussi )
78OcK9r.accessInfo: Se garer à La Chapelle et prendre le sentier direction les bouillouses. Au bout de 10 minutes, le chemin se dégage et on aperçois une colline sur la droite. Le premier arbre se situe sur l’autre versant ( côté rivière ).

0u8TABZ.name: Espírito Livre
0u8TABZ.description: highline conquistado durante o festival Espírito livre
0u8TABZ.anchorsInfo:
Ancoragem A - estrutura de uma antena com 3 pontos
Ancoragem B - Natural em árvores
0u8TABZ.accessInfo: highline realizado apenas com autorização prévia

vGGhSui.name: HS 110
vGGhSui.description:
OG king line straight across the back of the quarry.
Tagline can be thrown in from both anchors and connected. Alternatively, it is possible to walk around. This must be done with caution near the cliff edge.
vGGhSui.restriction: none: Be respectful of climbers, this is a BMC owned quarry so if the climbers complain about slackliners then it will create access issues.
vGGhSui.anchorsInfo:
East anchor - 3 ground stakes

West anchor - 4 glue-in bolts (ca. 2013)
vGGhSui.accessInfo: Park at the main Horseshoe Quarry carpark or lay-bys, approach the quarry then follow the track up the hill to the right. Once level with the top of the quarry follow the path to the left, up a few steps then carefully make you way the large tree.

QcKyTMr.description: Between Old Easy and Friction Tower
QcKyTMr.anchorsInfo: Natural anchors, wrapped boulder on dynamic (Friction Tower) side and wrapped flake on static (Old Easy) side.
QcKyTMr.accessInfo: Established by climbing F. T. via Strawberry Jam and O. E. via MRC Direct, and some scrambling and rappelling to and from anchors. O.E. anchor can be accessed without technical climbing.

WPeiFXv.name: Highline Mirante do Leblon , by: Edney Junior

6Z1Ut9U.restriction: partial: Only rig this line in winter or you will get into trouble quickly. No sleeping in vans/cara near the spot. The police will come and fine you. Highlining was forbidden here in the past, take care of the spot!
6Z1Ut9U.anchorsInfo: 2x10mm and one backup Bolts on one side, 4x10mm INOX bolts on the other side. You will need hangers and rope/slings of about 4m on each side.
6Z1Ut9U.accessInfo:
This line can only be rigged of season, meaning only in winter. Really easy access from
 the road, parking right at the spot.
