BKB_oiI.description: Sweet little highline spot at a beautiful crag. 3min approach from the car park! :D

NIxCeZ-.name: Bastardo
NIxCeZ-.description:
1. side (common with 135m) - 2 bolts, backup to the tree.
2. side - 2 trees, backup to third tree. Path on both sides
NIxCeZ-.anchorsInfo:
1. side (common with 135m) - 2 bolts, backup to the tree.
2. side - 2 trees, backup to third tree

uoMX1rc.restriction: full: Don't go in this spot to highline, they really don't want us to

vXzhn_F.name: Right Ball
vXzhn_F.description: From the top of Madonna near the random pole, walk down the mountain through a slightly beaten path. You'll eventually come to a few large boulders just kinda hanging out. There is a tagline set up between two of the larger rocks. Use the tagline to set up the line and you are good to go.

soJq_a1.name: Lac Tseuzier (Rawil)
soJq_a1.description: This line can not be installed anymore, authorisation from the dam expoitation company refused

8sP1mYL.name: Cumbre San Bernardo
8sP1mYL.description: Dangerous aproach to secondary side (West side)

jOibLcm.name: Socka
jOibLcm.description: Nice line in old quarry. Hard to rig, you need lots of sling and ropes.
jOibLcm.anchorsInfo: Anchors are natural, Trees on both sides, but far from the edge, so you need 40m of spansets and about 60m of static rope to make solid anchors :D Bouldering pad is good friend on southern anchor.
jOibLcm.accessInfo: Both anchors are of easy access from the path bellow the line.

xbj62zL.description:
very easy to access. the line stays right above the casa cantoniera restourant (very king and helpfull owner and staff) parking lot. you can camp overnight and there's water, a lot.
for infos find SLACKLINE BRESCIA ASD on facebook and ask us!
xbj62zL.anchorsInfo:
west anchor got 3 bolts, you need to repel from the top, ask at the restourant the easy access route for the top.
east anchor, the hard one, either you "climb" the valley throght the trees on the right of the wall and repel 10m down from a tree, either you come down from the road on the other side of the valley. you'll find some bolt here we used to rig this and the other line, usually 4 system bolt.

no need of padding both sides are hanging, not touching the rock

dH7--99.name: The Sea Gully
dH7--99.description: lines could be setup from 20m to 35m, bolted anchors..

Y-d5gpn.name: Linea costera
Y-d5gpn.description: Esta linea esta dentro de centro turistico, es una linea de anclajes naturales y sencilla de montar
Y-d5gpn.anchorsInfo: Los anclajes son naturales unos arboles muy fuertes que utiliza buenas slingas
Y-d5gpn.accessInfo: Hay camino hacia los dos lados

gJRQ-3S.name: Kalnu 53
gJRQ-3S.description: Cozy highline spot mostly enclosed in trees.
gJRQ-3S.anchorsInfo: Far side anchor requires either ladder or a sling around a tree to set it up high enough

mD6IV4Z.name: Na czyściocha
mD6IV4Z.description: Old quarry lines from 90m to 270m height 50m.
mD6IV4Z.anchorsInfo: Trees, there is option to instal bolts in future
mD6IV4Z.accessInfo: Get to Wojcieszow by car. On the place there is small parking spot then you walk 10-15min

R_9aQyJ.name: Western Rider
R_9aQyJ.description: One anchor has 4 bolts and the other its a natural anchor (6m sling) and then 2 bolts for the backup
R_9aQyJ.anchorsInfo: One anchor has 4 bolts and the other its a natural anchor (6m sling) and then 2 bolts for the backup.

400e_Zw.name: Remembering the future
400e_Zw.description: Established by Zachariah Cowan and Peter Patzt, FA: Peter Patzt
400e_Zw.accessInfo: Park at Indian Staircase Trailhead. The trail goes right past it. On rocky terrain the trail is hard to make out.

DA9Oa2-.name: Soho sendage
DA9Oa2-.description: The smaller extraction point shaped like a 'V' if I can see the house they can see me..
DA9Oa2-.anchorsInfo:
1) 2 tree's that look like twins..
2) Find enough bushes or tree's to wrap until you feel safe..
DA9Oa2-.accessInfo:
1) Park up at the gate and walk along the path on the right, past the smack-head tree house until you find the twin tree's..
2) Walk along the road and then climb through the bushes..

lVPcrQb.name: La Grande elle est longue
lVPcrQb.anchorsInfo: 3x10mm bolts on each side, not much padding needed. Bring your hangers.

nMQQedK.anchorsInfo:
Close Side
Use the 2 bolts and the tree as  3-point anchor. 5m + 1,5m slings or rope (weblock has to be over the cliff edge!!). Redirect extension around the small tree with a 0,5m sling. Bring enough padding for the cliff edge (fire hose, treepro). Bring 4 shackles, 2 quicklinks. 
Far Side: 
1,5m sling is enough for the tree, another for the backup. 
The tree should be backed up to another tree further back with a 15m ratchet strap or beginnerline.

REs9bW_.name: LA de la Cascada
REs9bW_.description: ideal para bungees y freestyle. entorno increible

qbgmHAp.name: Pinitos
qbgmHAp.description: Primer high de la región, se pasa el cordin fácil caminando por abajo del cañón. El riggeo es fácil, de árbol a 2 chapas en roca. Para acceder se deja el auto y caminas 20'
qbgmHAp.restriction: none: Hay que pasar la tranquera, es propiedad privada pero nadie lo ve y nunca hubo inconveniente
qbgmHAp.anchorsInfo: A es un pino, anclaje simple. B son 2 chapas que quedan en el lugar
qbgmHAp.accessInfo: Vehículo queda abajo (hotel resto "mirador") o se llega en colectivo. Caminata empieza pasando la ruta, cruzar una tranquera y caminar hacia la derecha (pino verde de referencia) subir por el lateral del cañadon 20'

qoOgfDF.name: Bolting White Horses
qoOgfDF.description: Goes across an intimidating sea cove
qoOgfDF.anchorsInfo:
One very large boulder on the north side, needs roughly 13-15m of slings to be sling and still have a favourable angle at the anchor point. 

The south side is accessed by a down climb/abseil from the top of the cove. 2  large boulders can be slung with 12m round slings. Then extend out over the cliff edge with 2-2.5m long spansets. 

Both sides require A LOT of padding, especially the south side..

dBT_ZgR.name: Jean Romeo le Cascadeur
dBT_ZgR.description: The line goes in front of the waterfall
dBT_ZgR.anchorsInfo:
3x12mm bolts on the cliff above the path on one side, easily rigged with a sliding X. The 12mm bolts have no hangers on! 
A tree in the slope on the other side. 4m spanset around the tree, plus backup. Padding for the tree and another small tree just in front.
Bolted in summer  2023
dBT_ZgR.accessInfo: Follow the escape path of the canyon

DPecXAe.name: Medic Spot 1
DPecXAe.description: Good highline spot, comfortable to rig. Its windy up there. 12-13 floor, ~40 m height. Use columns, different lengths, 20-40 meters.

qJY03KH.name: La Cavalerie
qJY03KH.description: Opened by Sébastien Bouchet and Clément Fabre. Janvier 2013.
qJY03KH.anchorsInfo:
Bring the bolt hangers) 12 of 12mm and for anchors and belays
- Stairways side: To access the bolts, you have to rappel down from a leaning tree around 40m above. There are 6 bolts of 12mm in the middle of the slab (up the two grey tracks) and one last bolt approximately 2m above. You can use 3 points for force, 2 for backup, and the top one 2m above for general backup. We recommend 4m long spanset for force and 2m (minimum) spanset for backup.
- West side: There are 6 12mm bolts they are the highest bolts on that side. Use 3 bolts for force and 3 for backup. There again 4m long spanset recommended for the force and minimum 2m spanset for backup, to get out of the rocky platform.
qJY03KH.accessInfo:
The parking spot is located here: 43.524056,5.505447
Drive to Le Tholonet from Palette. When you reach “ Le château du Tholonet “ make a left and turn right 300meters further on the “ chemin de la paroisse “. Stop at the end of the slope on the muddy spot.

The parking lot is tiny ! So if you plan on parking with more than a couple of cars it is better to park inside Le Tholonet ( here for instance: 43.521901,5.510436 ) to keep parking space for hikers.

The line is located here in the Gorges more or less parallel to the bridge of “ la petite mer “ :
43.528405,5.509408
15 to 20 minutes hike, start walking from le chemin de la paroisse, take the right path at the end of the slope to reach the cliff. Enjoy the point of view on la sainte-victoire and hike down along the cliff until you reach the bridge. The 49m is the fourth line more or less parallel to the bridge.

870YDx-.name: STUPID MAJA
870YDx-.description: Line is in Albania, not Montenegro, on about 2400 m above see level, so probably the highest in Albania, try to  beat it ;)
870YDx-.anchorsInfo: Left side 2 holes 12mm for removable bolts (handdrilled and not realy deep, good enough for Petzl Coeur Pulse – 6,5 cm) + rope(or sling) backup around the rock. Right side 3 holes 12mm for removable bolts.
870YDx-.accessInfo: Follow the trail to Maja e Rosit and just before the top, turn right. It can take about 6 hours(or more) from Valbone. Follow GPS coordinates: 42°28'41.8"N 19°50'57.8"E

pLQttNe.name: no name
pLQttNe.description: Have 1 fix bolt and 7 other points is a fixie bolt removable
pLQttNe.anchorsInfo: have 1 fix bolt and 7 other points is a fixie bolt removable .

sB0_OP8.description:
Nice Highline, on both sides bolted.
each side has 3x10mm and 2x12mm bolts

1TDwG85.name: Mincheguaddu
1TDwG85.description:
3 bolts + one big tree on both sides.
10mm bolts, plates are not there
1TDwG85.anchorsInfo:
3 bolts + one big tree on both sides.
10mm bolts, plates are not there.

LHM0VAK.name: Emosson lazy
LHM0VAK.description: Lazy version for installation to cross the lake, only registration at OFAC (BAZL) needed. Access must be authorised by comune Salvan and Emossion exploitation
LHM0VAK.anchorsInfo: 4x12mm bolts both sides, East requires A-frame 1.5m, short slings

QkyCLGv.description: This place have a many highlines but this is the biggest and longest
QkyCLGv.anchorsInfo: One side we make natural anchors and the other side we use bolts
QkyCLGv.accessInfo: This place have easy acess for trails

vHSLRLR.name: The 110m
vHSLRLR.description:
/!\ You'll need to ask permission to the mayor, go to the spot page to see the protocol.
An aesthetically pleasing line which is easy to rig. Take care not to use climbing bolts on the left side anchor.
vHSLRLR.restriction: partial: Before the 1st of september might be restricted because of bird nesting. See the spot page for all the infos.
vHSLRLR.anchorsInfo:
Left anchor
There is a lot of bolts on the chill zone. Many should not be used at all, they are meant to hold the feet of a plateforme for a rope jumping activity. Bring your hangers and nuts, and lots of extension 
Right anchor
Two bolts near the big tree for tension, and sling around the big tree for backup, it is very important to use tree padding. Visible damage to this tree will lead to it being banned as a highline anchor.

tl7dwEc.description: Schopfloch Highlines. Taglines are already installed. Be aware that this is a climbing area. Be very careful to not drop any equipment or rocks. Two lines can be rigged, tree to tree. Contact the slackliners from Stuttgart to get more info.

7zy5kDs.name: 60m
7zy5kDs.restriction: partial: Only acces for UIAA insured members. Like in Belgium KBF or CAB
7zy5kDs.anchorsInfo: Tree side and rock side with 4 bolts.
7zy5kDs.accessInfo: rock side access by via ferrata. Propper VF PBM's mandatory

UgDVa5g.name: Hay gente pa to
UgDVa5g.description: Short highline between two peaks in beautiful spot.
UgDVa5g.anchorsInfo: 3 bolts on north peak, 2 bolts and one friend on south peak.
UgDVa5g.accessInfo: North peak has to be trad climbed and rappeld and south peak can be hiked.

7U-1-tN.name: HS 20
7U-1-tN.description: Short line across the top of the quarry, seldom rigged and arguably unsafe without bungees.
7U-1-tN.restriction: none: Be respectful of climbers, this is a BMC owned quarry so if the climbers complain about slackliners then it will create access issues.
7U-1-tN.anchorsInfo:
East anchor - 4 glue-ins (Ca. 2013) shared with 40m

West anchor - 4 glue-ins (Ca. 2013)
7U-1-tN.accessInfo: Park at the main Horseshoe Quarry carpark or lay-bys, approach the quarry then follow the track up the hill to the right. Once level with the top of the quarry follow the path to the left, up a few steps then carefully make you way the large tree.

e_3cHSF.name: La Mutanterie
e_3cHSF.description: The parking spot is located here: 43.524056,5.505447 Drive to Le Tholonet from Palette. When you reach “ Le château du Tholonet “ make a left and turn right 300meters further on the “ chemin de la paroisse “. Stop at the end of the slope on the mu...
e_3cHSF.accessInfo: The parking spot is located here: 43.524056,5.505447 Drive to Le Tholonet from Palette. When you reach “ Le château du Tholonet “ make a left and turn right 300meters further on the “ chemin de la paroisse “. Stop at the end of the slope on the muddy spot. The parking lot is tiny ! So if you plan on parking with more than a couple of cars it is better to park inside Le Tholonet ( here for instance: 43.521901,5.510436 ) to keep parking space for hikers. The line is located here in the Gorges more or less parallel to the bridge of “ la petite mer “ : 43.528405,5.509408 15 to 20 minutes hike, start walking from le chemin de la paroisse, take the right path at the end of the slope to reach the cliff. Enjoy the point of view on la sainte-victoire and hike down along the cliff until you reach the bridge. The 38m is the 3rd line from the bridge.

fofxsO3.name: Saint Antonin - 20m
fofxsO3.description:
La première ligne ouverte a Saint Antonin.
Attention a la hauteur....
fofxsO3.anchorsInfo:
Point de 10 et de 12mm de chaque coté. A ne pas confondre avec les broches de la via feratta.
Ancrages évidents.

TatxUtf.description: Very nice, second line above the Kesselfallklamm.
TatxUtf.anchorsInfo:
Farside: large spruce tree. > 2m spanset.

Tensioning side: 3 glue in bolts plus pine tree for hight. 15m Rope for BFK plus 1m spanset for hight or some longer slings.

Taglines need to be connected down in the Klamm. After derig please deconnect and place as you found them to avoid complications with the helicopter rescue people!
TatxUtf.accessInfo:
Farside: Park at Sandwirt, walk into the Kesselfallklamm, after crossing the 6th bridge (the one above the waterfall) leave the trail and turn right, walk a small path up the hill (ca. 30 Meters) for the 30 Meter line go right, for the 70 m go left.

Tensioning side: Cross the 3rd bridge, turn left and climb up ca. 10 meters of a small slope. Turn right and follow a small path through the forest until reaching the highest point above the Klamm. 
47.20536° N, 15.39881° E (approximateley)

GPH3DAk.name: Primeira do Tocantins - Cachoeira da Roncadeira
GPH3DAk.description: First Highline ever in the state of Tocantins, and still the only one. We were invited to rig this Highline by Freestyle atlhete Gabriel Pereira, he is from the state of Tocantins and dreamt of accomplishing the first Highline ever in his state. This was one of the many lines conquered by me and my partner while travelling all over Brazil in search for spots to set up the most incredible Highlines where no one had ever seen such thing, we went up north and expanded the highline frontier for new eyes to see.

HUNigpc.description:
awesome line for expirienced riggers. beautiful for sunset sessions. all natural! climbing skills, and oceans knowledge and skills necessary!

crossing the ocean to the needle: only when waves are tiny and currents are low... at least take a bodyboard with you. watch the ocean. stay away from the rocks strong current. can be very dangerous!!!
HUNigpc.anchorsInfo: all natural! brings slings and padding
HUNigpc.accessInfo:
park at the lighthouse, follow the dirtroad, pass a few ruins, then the take the right road... bevor 2 obvious serpentins you turn right, leave the road and follow a path to the top of a cliff where you can see the needl.

Or go by boat ;)

1Y98mE4.description: Lemongrass. The next level after the newbie line. Can be riged along with the Thyme with one piece of 60m webbing.
1Y98mE4.anchorsInfo: Three 10mm and two 12mm wedge bolts on both side. You need hangers.
1Y98mE4.accessInfo: The same approach as to Thyme.

yaxR40o.name: Pesqueiro apimentao
yaxR40o.description: Maybe the first highline of madeira ?
yaxR40o.anchorsInfo:
3 holes for 12 mm bolts on tension side
4 holes for 12 mm bolts on far side
yaxR40o.accessInfo: 5 m rappel from the path, there are 2 holes for bolts

QHxiZmW.name: Candy Line
QHxiZmW.description: 50m line with best sunset view in Cyprus. Line needs to be rigged with one hang-frame due to difference in height between sides.
QHxiZmW.anchorsInfo: Marine-grade glue-in bolts (3 equalized for anchor + 1 independent back-up bolt). One hang-frame needed. Can easily use either slings or static rope for anchor.

iO0iRA5.name: Dent de Broc to Dent de Chamois
iO0iRA5.description: Line first time installed in July 2024
iO0iRA5.restriction: partial: Temporary closed for wildlife protection, check the seasonal opening

vCaU5dT.name: "Linea de humo"
vCaU5dT.description: Es una linea junto a uno cascada y sobre un sotano de 80m de altura dentro de la sierra de zongolica donde se condensa el agua constantemente mientras estas en ella.
vCaU5dT.restriction: partial: "Don Lorenzo" es la persona dueña del acceso al lugar. El conoce nuestro deporte y es muy accesible.
vCaU5dT.anchorsInfo: lado estatico con cadenas y lado mecanico con anclajes epoxicos (esta en proceso de reequipar)
vCaU5dT.accessInfo: Lo mas comodo es llegar con la familia que da acceso al lugar. Hay que buscar a "Don Lorenzo"

kkBVA-h.name: Yurtito
kkBVA-h.description: Linea para freestyle, fácil de montar
kkBVA-h.anchorsInfo: 3 chapas en el lado norte. Un cipres muy grande en el lado sur. Abierto por Leono, Gon Catu y GonPi

YHOVDsG.name: SlacklinePorto V2
YHOVDsG.description: Climbing area with fixed highline anchors and waterline

EXCV-iE.restriction: full: Permission is required for use. For inquiries, please contact us on our Instagram account @slackaholix

Sq64hY_.name: Pedrock Style
Sq64hY_.anchorsInfo: Lado B Pinheiro robusto, Lado A 2 pontas de eixo no solo.
Sq64hY_.accessInfo: Acesso pelo Morro do cristo da cidade de Pedreira

XQTjMpX.name: Burica
XQTjMpX.accessInfo: Tensioning side is a rock above the path, bolts for 135 and 170m lines are laying on top of it. Static side bolts are on the other side on the cleanest edge.

iOHm3NU.name: Zeckenzoo
iOHm3NU.description: tree to tree highline (do not use the big) tree on the north western side!

H5pp7s2.name: Paralela 222
H5pp7s2.anchorsInfo: 3 parabolt de cada lado. comparte chapa del lado A

vYPZQNN.name: tooLong
vYPZQNN.description:
never installed, but sure possible
the rock is not the best, we have to test it if we can use regular bolts,
an other option would be natural anchors, please check out the sport first

hNbqCZm.name: Flying mozarella and falling drone
hNbqCZm.description: Surreal view of the bay of kotor
hNbqCZm.anchorsInfo:
Static anchor: scramble down on the left side of the top, you find 3 12mm bolts.
Dynamic anchor: follow the ridge  on the right . Natural anchor. We used the first realy big boulder behind the boulder you see on the anchor point foto. 

Red pointer shows the anchor of the other lower line. The blue pointer shows the abseiling anchor
hNbqCZm.accessInfo: go into the road in front of "etno village ", follow this road till the end. Then follow the hiking trail to Pestingrad viewpoint, its about 45-60 minutes hiking. The lines are at the U shaped vieuwpoint.

VpOxfm0.name: fingers in the nose
VpOxfm0.description: ligne démente de 53m. démente car semi-troglodyte, cad dans un immense porche.
VpOxfm0.anchorsInfo: très difficile d'accès. Grande voie des deux côtés. / Multi-pitch climbs on both sides. Bolts, bring hangers.
VpOxfm0.accessInfo:
Envoyez moi un message facebook. PM me on FB.
https://www.facebook.com/arthur.lefebvre.39

Hhn1AMD.anchorsInfo: beams in window and wrapped tower

yYc2Hcn.name: The Krampus
yYc2Hcn.description: Highline in la Cabrera, the Krampus etween 110 and 115m, one of the most beautifil lines, it goes from "el Purito" to the "K2"
yYc2Hcn.anchorsInfo:
Static side (east): You have to get over the K2 needle, there's a bolt helping for the access, after you are on top, you have to climb over two balancing rocks to get to the anchor. 3 bolts moon shaped and one for the tails. Bolts 10x90. Using the 3 bolts, with a 2.5/3m spanset it's perfect, no abrasion and little protection needed.
The tensioning side (west) it's on the side of the rock, under the 60 and 80 anchors. Same configuration 3 bolts moonshaped and one for the tails. No protection needed (except if you surf it). 3 meter spanset fits perfect.

3YeplL-.accessInfo:
Hike
One bridge crossing to get to the other side

VWzWKBC.name: Carousel
VWzWKBC.description:
One of the most beautiful highlines out there ;-) The point of view can almost be reached by car. From there 5min hiking to the close anchor point where we bolted in stainless M12. 4 bolts between 2m and 4m from the cliff. An anchor point more to the north closer to the greek flag might make sense! This would make the line go longer - about 650m long. But the line we rigged was 60m off-level! An anchor point more to the north could bring this down to about 40m off-level which is totally OK for a 600m+line. But nothing is bolted there yet. 

The far anchor point is a bit more of a hike. Take good shoes. It's 20 min hiking. A tree 15m behind the cliff can be taken as anchor point. No bolts as the rock is shit on this side. The line is 30m off-level! Btw.: The beach can not be reached by foot. 80m repelling down on loose rocks to get to the beach - last 20m overhanging! So be careful if you plan to go down there or take a tourist ship :-p

R-pkQyo.name: "Un camino con las mariposas"
R-pkQyo.description: Es el primer highline en Veracruz y para montar la "Linea de humo" hay que poner esta primero. atraviesa la mitad del sotano y directo hacia la cascada.
R-pkQyo.anchorsInfo: Esta linea ya esta reequipada y cuenta con anclajes epoxicos.

FNQR446.name: Blancaneus i els 7 nans
FNQR446.anchorsInfo: Big tree on one side, 7 smalls trees on the other (3 for main, 2 for back up, 3 frame the line over a natural frame, a boulder)
FNQR446.accessInfo: Path that goes from Fontalba to Núria. In the valley where there is la Cabanya de Gombrèn.

7RGPN-y.name: Golden Dream
7RGPN-y.description: Only 2 bolts for anchoring in not the most safe. 4 is preferred for most highline scenarios. This is also a popular rock climbing area, if you are going to rig here, do so in the middle of the week as not to irritate other patrons who come to use ...
7RGPN-y.accessInfo: Rock scramble path to the top

RzNrQn5.anchorsInfo: Solid branch of a tree on one side (2m spanset) - Metal pole of a turbine statue on the other side (2,5m spanset)

qjrrx6q.name: English Breakfast
qjrrx6q.description: Teabag line beside the highway, a nice spot for a sunny afternoon. Probably 1/3 the line is no fall zone but its good for practicing freestyle
qjrrx6q.anchorsInfo: Wanaka side anchor is a tree wrapped low down, other side is 3 bolts (12mm, rods only byo hangars and nuts). The bolts are up on a rockface which is a little bouldery to get to. Be careful on the climb up as its a little sketchy. If in doubt you can rap in over the top. The tree side runs over a small A-frame made from two waratahas, hopefully these are still there... we have been wrapping the anchor spanset around the waratahas to keep them together.
qjrrx6q.accessInfo: Drive up SH6 towards the neck. Spot is around 15 mins from Hawea and the first corner after the isthmus peak trail head

Xe2C8pN.name: Obriens Favourite
Xe2C8pN.description:
Dylan Bennett Hoel Yugg Miguel Obrien 

Easy access, Can tag by throwing fishing line over the whole gully.
Xe2C8pN.anchorsInfo: Natural trees either side. Optional boulder on left hand side.

AJdvN6t.name: Jebemu mast
AJdvN6t.anchorsInfo:
Tension side(south): 35m rope bfk to 2x 12mm expansion bolts and a tree. Or use ~2+3+4m spansets on bolts + tree to reduce stretch and do 10m rope bfk from these points.
Static side: 3x 12mm expansion bolts; use 3m spanset equalised on 2 bolts. Equalise this point with another bolt, use 2m spanset.  Extend ~1m to A frame masterpoint. Or just equalise with ~4m spanset to A frame masterpoint.

OIq0Hh_.name: Dogfight
OIq0Hh_.description: This is a great highline for beginners.  Shorter, but not too short.  Exposed on one side, but not to exposed on the other.
OIq0Hh_.anchorsInfo: Big, 3 bolt anchors on either side with chain links for running static.  One side is walk-around access with a no-fall zone that can use some extra padding.  The other is in a dish with a steep drop on either side.  Anchor to some bolts above and rappel in, or at least clip into a rope.
OIq0Hh_.accessInfo: Hike up Misery Ridge.  From the summit, look for a herd path near the trail post going downhill.  Keep going down and eventually you will find a large cave that is great for some cool shade.  Dogfight is the first line in the ravine in front of the cave.

0sYw7vw.name: no named yet
0sYw7vw.description: There is no shades in the place, so bring your sunscreen, hats and a lot of water...all stuff to protect you from the sun and the heat. There is 5 anchors each side, 3 main and 2 for backups
0sYw7vw.anchorsInfo: there is 5 anchors each side, 3 main and 2 for backups

uaxoBIh.name: Solid Security
uaxoBIh.description: First (and probably the longest possible) highline from an abandoned hospital on the slope of the mountain Łysa Góra. On the side of the tree it's low, but on the side of the hospital it's 38 m high. The line offers amazing view on neighboring town Bielsko-Biała and villages, while remaining quite well hidden from unwanted eyes.
uaxoBIh.anchorsInfo: Natural anchors. On the side of the tree 2x 1.5-2 m spansets is enough. On the side of the hospital you need 8 m of spanset for mainline and about 4 m for backup to another iron pillar. Some extra padding is needed for the edges of the building, as shown on pictures.
uaxoBIh.accessInfo: The hospital is surrounded by a fence with "Teren prywatny" ("Private property") signs, but nobody guards it and it shouldn't be a trouble. Currently there is a hole in the wall allowing to access the building from ground level. If the hole isn't there you can climb up the wall on the outside to the first floor level, it's a very easy climb. For the tree side there are no branches to climb up to a desired height, but it's possible to climb using three slings (therefore two spansets for rigging and one extra): wrap it around a tree and clip in to it, then wrap another higher up, and so on. To unclip from bottom sling you need to put your weight on something else, hence the third sling.

j9GinHm.name: The Queen Line
j9GinHm.anchorsInfo:
3 Bolts both sides.
Far side (static) requires some scrambling to access.

yf-Ptg4.name: struja
yf-Ptg4.description: both anchors are plenty strong and safe, easy to rig, throwing the tagline over can be tricky if done by hand.
yf-Ptg4.anchorsInfo: tree anchor on the north side, transmission tower leg on the south side.
yf-Ptg4.accessInfo: is accessible from the road, following the dotted line on the map

JRKeImV.name: Land Send 2
JRKeImV.anchorsInfo: Sling natural rock anchors. Throw line required.

Q3EnLSd.name: Double Wedding
Q3EnLSd.anchorsInfo:
Static side (nose):
 4 Bolts in wall where you have to repel down, use near Tree as fix point, and redirect on the bolt on top of the nose.

Tension side (house):
Wrap house with slings and use a frame (not available at the spot)

Attention: climbing routes right beneath the anchors!!!

_ColWHo.name: 90m
_ColWHo.description: Beautiful line in the climbing crag Flône managed by KBF
_ColWHo.restriction: partial: Only acces for UIAA insured members, like in Belgium KBF or CAB
_ColWHo.anchorsInfo:
Boomkant: idem 100m

Rotskant op bolts:
Main = 2 delta's, 2m sling als sliding X, shackle, 1m sling als verlenging tot net over de rand (foto 7) voorzie voldoende padding!
Back-up = 2 delta's, 2m sling als sliding X, shackle, 1m sling als verlenging tot net over de rand

Extra groene bolt om de hoek = Om de hoek zit een groene bolt, met een delta + 0,5m sling + stalen musketon kan je de main en backup tegen de
rand trekken, hierdoor heb je veel minder wrijving (foto 8)
_ColWHo.accessInfo:
Rappel
De bolts staan 2m onder het anker van diazepam (8b), op de hoek van de hoekversijding. (foto 9). Rappel vanaf de bovenkant, anker op boom met ingegroeide staaldraad. 30m touw is voldoende.

XZDv-vG.name: Cabra Muerta
XZDv-vG.description: Tree anchors. Really close to the road

ZOKaH5c.description: The smallest line on the spot, really nice to begin
ZOKaH5c.anchorsInfo:
On the first side (side of the bridge), there are two m10 bolts and one m12 behind the rock that makes the falt space. You need to bring the hangers. I usually use the two m10 bolts for the main line and the backup and the third bolt for a general backup. You need long slings to exdend the weblock over the edge and a lot of padding too.
To reach the other side, you need to follow the little path, it's logical. There are two bolts m10 on a vertical part of rock. You also need to bring hangers, long slings and padding. Use the two bolt as a masterpoint and the tree behind is for the backup G.
Normally there is a little rope for the connection.
ZOKaH5c.accessInfo:
Park the car on the place after the bridge and go down with the stairs. You'll see the place for chilling and the first anchor.
The path to the second one is logical and already explained.

CLTb3qB.name: Mirador de mierda
CLTb3qB.description: 3 holds for MMS each side. Need a rappel in the south anchor for releasing
CLTb3qB.accessInfo: need a rappel in the south anchor for releasing

AEQAWh9.name: El Chuzo Como en tu Chozo
AEQAWh9.description: 3 bolts each side. Park your car 10m from the anchor
AEQAWh9.accessInfo: Park your car 10m from the anchor.

NkD65Ff.name: Buenas Crocancias
NkD65Ff.description: Dificult and Dangerous aproach! Specially Secondary side (north side)

AMh-xj9.name: Schodek
AMh-xj9.description: Projekt zrealizowany na Wyszków Highline Camp
AMh-xj9.accessInfo: Wyszków płyty przy Bugu obok szpitala

PY6vE8Y.name: Cavalo de Cristal
PY6vE8Y.description:
Easy access, quick rigging. Very careful with the mist. It is frequent the appearance of many clouds making null visibility. I recommend visiting the local farms.
https://www.facebook.com/tocadaoncaeco
PY6vE8Y.anchorsInfo:
6 fixed bolt inox with 12mm diameter.
need 6 bolt hanger

7JW0VJl.name: Monkey Boy
7JW0VJl.description: If you look up Epic in the dictionary, you see a picture of this line.  A moderately long line walking towards the very point of a huge tower with hundreds of feet of direct exposure and the Cascade Mountains in front of you.
7JW0VJl.anchorsInfo:
The Static side (on the Monkey) is bolted.  Two large bolts with large, round hangers will take static rope directly.  Two smaller climbing bolts further back are often used as a backup, but would require quicklinks.

The Tensioning side anchor is a wrapped, detached  boulder.  Bring some trad gear to back it up if you'd like.
7JW0VJl.accessInfo:
The static side is on an eastern point of the Monkey.  To get there you will need to top out the Monkey somehow.  The easiest way is to take the Pioneer Route. 

The tensioning side can be accessed easily, just off the trail.

XG4Cu0k.name: Sin Nombre aun
XG4Cu0k.description: Anchors with parabolts 3 per anchors. Easy acces

upV6HLq.name: Wiatrołom
upV6HLq.description:
1. side (common with 70m and 80m) - 2 rings for main, backup to the other rings.
2. side - 2 rings for main, backup to the third ring. Path on both sides
upV6HLq.anchorsInfo:
1. side (common with 70m and 80m) - 2 rings for main, backup to the other rings.
2. side - 2 rings for main, backup to the third ring.

lYVcGC0.name: Vida da fumaça 2
lYVcGC0.description: The place is very isolated, so bring a lot of food and get some guide on the city to go there. Bolts. By foot, easy trail

JBbTyiB.description: Resort. Expedition highline in a Western Caucasus. Altitude is about 3400m. The line is stunning with views of the Main Caucasian ridge and the landmark top of Dalar. On the other hand are Dolomiti lakes.
JBbTyiB.anchorsInfo: Three 12mm wedge bolts both side and big stones for wrapping around for the backup. You need hangers.
JBbTyiB.accessInfo: The approach of base camp Uzunkol takes 3-4 hours. From the lake about 20 minutes walk in the direction to the pass Ohotnichiy.  The line is located on the left side - in a small canyon.

vO7N-p2.name: The 230m
vO7N-p2.description:
/!\ You'll need to ask permission to the mayor, go to the spot page to see the protocol.
A stunning line which encloses the sector with great views in both directions.

The left side anchor was originally rigged using 27m of spansets to equalise nine bushes! The 170m bolts have since been repurposed for this line.

Watch out for the bolts on a loose rock next to the left side anchor bolts, these should absolutely not be used for main or backup.

An eye should be kept on the weather when rigging this line as it is quite exposed to wind.
vO7N-p2.restriction: partial: Before the 1st of september might be restricted because of bird nesting. See the spot page for all the infos.
vO7N-p2.anchorsInfo:
Left anchor
Two expansion bolts painted black which were originally for the 170m. Various possibilities for backup using the holes in the rocks behind the anchor, this is left to the riggers discretion.

There are some nearby bolts on a boulder which are not safe for highlining. The boulder is loose.
Right anchor
Three expansion bolts which were originally placed for the 400m line. Backups are possibly using the holes in the rock which are several meters behind. Take care when choosing which holes to use as there are loose rocks. It is recommended to install some kind of security line for accessing this anchor both for rigging or in the event that the highliner wants to exit from this side.
vO7N-p2.accessInfo:
Connection:
Walk around with a tagline. May require a few people and a few throws to finish the connection between the 130m right side anchor and the 230m right side anchor.

zx1c10m.name: I-Landcruise
zx1c10m.description: First line at Kořenáč/Wurzelstein and a very beautiful Ostrov classic.
zx1c10m.anchorsInfo:
Long spansets wrapped around the rocks. Cleanest rig is to use a butterfly-style on the tower side, so the line leaves out of the crack dividing the tower.
The southern anchor is easy with long spansets around the rock.
zx1c10m.accessInfo: The south ancor is reachable by easy climbing from above, the tower (Kořenáč in czech or Wurzelstein in german) has to be climbed. Rated with czech IV the crux is the climbing from the ledge to the top.

HjsQ00K.name: Long Monkey
HjsQ00K.description: This line gets its name from some of the OGs of of highlners in Smith Rock.  Currently, one of the shorter lines that can be rigged off the Monkey.
HjsQ00K.anchorsInfo:
Bolted anchors on the static side in the cave on the Monkey.  The bolts have climbing hangers and they are a little small, so bring quicklinks.    These bolts are shared with the Long Monkey line.

The tensioning side anchor is climbing-style bolts and hangers so bring quicklinks.  A small boulder is used as an a-frame that should be padded up well.

The Tensioning side anchor is natural.  Bring lots of spansets, static, and padding to wrap a large boulder.
HjsQ00K.accessInfo:
The static side is in the west-side cave of the the Monkey.  To get there you will need to climb up the Monkey somehow.  A strong climber could take the "West Face or Astro Monkey routes directly to the cave.  A more moderate way may be to take the Pioneer Route, and from the summit rappel into the cave. 

The tensioning side can be accessed by just off the trail into a short cave.

Qm31TNY.name: Fox Sol Brother
Qm31TNY.description:
3 bomber trees one side, 1 decent tree and few small dodgy (dead) trees the other side. Highest point in this quarry/ hole. Getting the line across may prove problematic.
I didn't name it.

gcY5jBY.name: Highline "Scoglio l'highusteri"
gcY5jBY.anchorsInfo: Natural anchors on each side. It is forbidden to drill on this area
gcY5jBY.accessInfo:
You can climb on each side easily without any rope
You need a boat to get the spot

TsknglF.name: Original Line
TsknglF.accessInfo: Hike the chief to the 2nd peak, and use the trail to the 3rd peak (from the 2nd peak) to access the other side

vvSPrPq.name: Passarinho da Vovó
vvSPrPq.description:
Parque Estadual Serra dos Martírios/Andorinhas - São Geraldo do Araguaia/PA
Setor Casa de Pedra
Rochas sedimentares e metamorfizadas com predominância de Quartzito

w627ehn.name: Devils Lake Highline
w627ehn.anchorsInfo: Natural anchors. East side has a tree. West side needs a lot of anchor material to wrap boulders. Tagging needs good throughing abilities.

6CWrscd.name: Stomping it down
6CWrscd.description: Another option for a freestyle line to add in for crazy sessions
6CWrscd.anchorsInfo: X2 glue in 150mm carrots for lhs or other trad options avaible if the other 2 line are rigged off the carrots, rhs is a tree

vPBqiuJ.name: Espanta bichos
vPBqiuJ.description: Se puede hacer fácilmente la reunión del árbol con una cuerda estática y del lado de la compuerta con eslingas
vPBqiuJ.anchorsInfo: Se puede hacer fácilmente la reunión del árbol con una cuerda estática y del lado de la compuerta con eslingas

7xMjJJc.name: Lažja Kot Najlažja
7xMjJJc.description: Line to the pier, suitable length for the stretchiest nylon.
7xMjJJc.anchorsInfo:
Static side: 3x12mm expansion bolts and 1x10mm for height. (bolts can be shared with 62m line)
Tensioning side: Tree
7xMjJJc.accessInfo:
Static side: ~20m of rappel.
Tensioning side: No problem :)

-DlL0mn.name: Juriš na Ajdno
-DlL0mn.anchorsInfo: Tension side A: Tree, 2m sling. Static side B: 3x 12mm expansion bolts, 2m sling.

bGA4SWy.description: For this line you have to ask the owner of the area first. He lives near the big farmer building, close to it. He is a nice guy. Some days in summer he makes the field

BAadOhK.name: Wyżej już się nie da
BAadOhK.description: Highline on the highest point of Jura
BAadOhK.anchorsInfo:
Both sides have holes for screw bolts HILTI HUS3-H10. You need 3 pieces each side. 

On Mala Cima we used 2.5m sling for main anchor and 1.5m for backup. 

On Skala 504 we used 1m sling for main anchor and 1m sling for backup. In the future we will bolt it with glue-in rings.
BAadOhK.accessInfo: Easy access from any parking in this area

wU43Fum.name: Viado trem cargueiro - Slack Vibe ABC Team
wU43Fum.description:
This place is forbidden because the highline pass throught train way, so the security prevents mounting.
Este lugar a montagem não é permitido por que o highline passa por cima da linha do trem então a segurança sempre impede a desmontagem.
wU43Fum.anchorsInfo:
The anchors can be made with in trees, use hope only to assembly highline
As ancoragens podem ser feitas nas arvores usando cordas para montar o highline.
wU43Fum.accessInfo:
To acess this place is easy and necessary use short trails with less risks
Para acessar o local é fácil, é necessário tomar pequenas trilhas sem risco.

C6LzGhL.restriction:
partial: Highline spot on the “Forest ofTHEN” property.
Entrance fee required.
For more information, visit @forest_of_then on Instagram.

KXLZMuR.name: La Cadenita
KXLZMuR.accessInfo: 2.5 hours aprouch, not so easy access to far side, not so hard either.

t6XCvuR.description: Really esposured line with big drop down.
t6XCvuR.anchorsInfo: 4 glue-in stainless steel bolts both side. You need six 12mm hangers.
t6XCvuR.accessInfo: Easy to get by foot to the right side. The left one you need climb 10m easy wall to the balcony. The bolts are on the edge of it.

9KJ1THR.name: Deja Tus Miedos Atràs
9KJ1THR.description: Beautiful highline at 30 min car from Torino. Ask to the association Torino Sul Filo for infos in order to rig.
9KJ1THR.accessInfo:
comfortable anchor: pilastro san marco
unconfortable anchor: climb to the top of Oasi dei Castagneti

u8pGweZ.name: Wind Mountain Gap
u8pGweZ.description: What a spot!  Two lines, not too far from Portland with some beautiful perceived exposure, and an approach around 1 mile.  What more can you ask for?
u8pGweZ.anchorsInfo:
The Northern anchor is 3 1/2" bolts.  Bring your own bolt hangers and hardware, plus something to pad the edge of the rock (a haulbag works great!).

Southern anchor is in the trees.  The ground is steep and loose over there, it may be safest to tie in or be on repel.  Pick a big tree, and extend out of the brush over the edge.  Long spansets would be useful here.
u8pGweZ.accessInfo: 1 mile approach from the Wind Mountain Trailhead.  When the trail turn sharply West, look for a spur trail to the East going down hill.

ay5TQ3n.description: Super beautiful spot with many possibilities for highlines between 35m and 65m. Depending if you rig straight or diagonal between the bridges. We passed 2 highline-days there and all people who passed liked what we were doing. No police appeared, but not sure if this will happen sometime... Better go on weekends as there is a road nearby being repaired. Rigging takes about 1 hour per highline.
ay5TQ3n.restriction: full: Sadly its not allowed to Highline in between the brigdes anymore. The village Bonaduz doesn‘t allow it. If the lines will be rigged the police might show up.
ay5TQ3n.anchorsInfo: Steel-beems of 20-25cm diameter on the rusty bridge and railing (take the lowest part!!!) on the new bridge.
ay5TQ3n.accessInfo: Walk 30m on the bridge from the parking slot...

RZQkJmH.name: Rock Canyon High Line
RZQkJmH.description: Three plus bolts on each side. From the parking lot, hike up the north side of the canyon behind the Boneville Shoreline Trail. Leave the trail and hike up the ridge and look for the shortest gap in the cliffs. There will be three or more anchors ...
RZQkJmH.anchorsInfo: Three plus bolts on each side
RZQkJmH.accessInfo: From the parking lot, hike up the north side of the canyon behind the Boneville Shoreline Trail. Leave the trail and hike up the ridge and look for the shortest gap in the cliffs. There will be three or more anchors on each side. You can walk from edge to edge easily.

taZ-D-Z.name: Martillo a Testa
taZ-D-Z.description: Not need for climbing on the aproach, just scrambling. Testa side is a natural anchor, Martillo side 3 bolts on the wall below the summit

e00z7zt.name: Journey in White Rooms
e00z7zt.description: A great french classic, established by the founders of Landcruising, Damian and Stefan.
e00z7zt.restriction: full: Even the smallest one is banned, the full area can't be use to go highline.
e00z7zt.anchorsInfo: Bolted with M12 express anchors. Bring hangers with you. In 2011, the nuts on the fortress side were missing, so it's got to have also some M12 nuts and washers. On the view point side, the anchor is clean riggable by wrapping spansets around the anchor plate - but it is also bolted.
e00z7zt.accessInfo: Drive to La Turbie and then the Route de la Tete de Chien to the end. You can park at the fortress gate, it is a view minutes walk to the viewpoint.

qXXRsOL.description: 3 bolts west side, 3 stakes east side

LgUTtv8.name: Homespot nr 2
LgUTtv8.description: Highline in the Forest, complications with private people, please consider contacting for closer Informations.
LgUTtv8.anchorsInfo:
Near Side: 2 m Spanset & rope Backup.
Farside: 2 m Spanset & rope backup, Backup the tree aswell with the next tree behind it. 
Use Line Sleeve and  treeprotection.
LgUTtv8.accessInfo: 15 minute by feet from bus station

r9e-kpm.name: NEMO
r9e-kpm.anchorsInfo: Southern bolts are badly wiggling and are unusable as of Nov ‘25

9qi-6UY.name: Malak'highline
9qi-6UY.description:
A nice (mid)line, not very high but quite long (probably the longest in Greece so far ?) and with amazing view and surroundings. You can climb and highline on the same day !

Open by : Tanassis Sotir, Laetitia Gonnon, Maurice Ammann in February 2016
9qi-6UY.anchorsInfo:
Right side (when looking at the lake), 3 bolts of 10mmx85mm, inox, 1 for general back up 6m behind on the top of a huge rock. (see picture 2)
Left side : 3 or 4 bolts of 10mmx85mm, inox on the ground (see picture 3)
9qi-6UY.accessInfo: same than the climbing sector of Kalogria called Ourliahto. The line is right above the cliff. Walk on the left side to get above the cliff. There is a way walking up, then on the left you will see a huge pile of rocks, the anchor of the right side (when looking at the lake) is there, behind the big rock, vertically placed. Around 3m bellow the only tree. Then walk at the top of the cliff around to reach the second anchor, at the same height, on the ground.

dy2Ifiz.anchorsInfo: Sling legs of the bridge on both sides. East side will require climbing/abseiling. Bridge can be slippy so place gear on the way up if you choose to climb.

o1CVuEP.description: This line was made possible by the invitation of mago do equilibrio, matheus vidal, who dreamed of making this happen at his hometown. We went on his kombi motorhome from São Paulo to Itamaraju, Bahia during Christmas 2022. We got to Itamaraju and stayed by vidal´s family home, getting to know his parents who are fishermen and his grandparents who live on a little farm in Itamaraju. This line at the time, was a record breaker for the biggest Highline in Bahia and in the whole northeastern of Brasil. A group of crazy and young hippies, some with great experience and some just getting on the road for the fist time. We once called ourselves "chorumes na estrada" and almost ran away with the circus and started a company to put on shows. But none of this happened, except for the Highline, that was a dream that came true. The 500 meter Highline was born on the 31st and walked on the first day of the year 2023.
o1CVuEP.accessInfo: 7km trail full of jaca in decomposition and mud, wear boots

Wpcj_Yt.name: Allium Victorialis
Wpcj_Yt.description:
ATTENTION : DES PLANTES PROTÉGÉES POUSSENT SUR LE SITE. Réduisez votre impact au minimum. Soyez discrets (pas de musique), évitez de dormir sur le site (il y a un chalet CAF, ou alors campez en dehors des zones protégées) Dans le bosquet qui sert d'ancrage à la 45m pousse naturellement un parterre de fleurs blanches qui sentent l'ail. Elles sont très rares et à laisser tranquille impérativement, sous peine d'interdiction de la highline sur le site Si vous montez la 45m, faites les ninjas pour ne pas toucher les fleurs. La police de l'environnement veille (vraiment). La 115 porte le nom de la plante : Allium Victorialis.                                                                                                   
                                                                                                                              
           Super jolie ligne, ouverte en Juin 2018. Ancrages naturels sur arbres, prévoir deux A-Frames de 1m de haut environ, et beaucoup d'élingues (14m côté sud et 9m côté nord). Prendre les arbres tout en bas en tête d'alouette et prolonger jusqu'au A à 1m du bord. pour le passage de ligne, faire le tour à pied est possible mais engagé, un drone serait idéal. Bonne slack !

ryX9xS0.name: Mirna Bosna
ryX9xS0.description: Pure epic line from top of tower to top of other tower. Both anchors easily reachable so you can walk just one direction. Connection can be made by zipline.
ryX9xS0.anchorsInfo:
Tension side(west): 3x 12mm expansion bolts; 15m bfk and 5m spanset to A frame(1m and 2m long legs), two bolt to tie down legs of A frame, 1m padding.
Static side: 3x 12mm expansion bolts; 25m bfk to A frame (1,5m long legs), bolt to tie down A frame.
ryX9xS0.accessInfo: Easy via ferrata to west side and easy hike to east.

K4I3WdF.name: Blue Dream
K4I3WdF.anchorsInfo: Three bolts per side, less than 10' (3m) from each edge.
K4I3WdF.accessInfo: $10 Req'd to park (Nat'l Parks Pass works here) at the Mitchell Lake Trailhead in Brainard Lake Recreation area. Take the Mitchell Lake Trail about 2.5 miles to the anchors.

l7uXLaH.name: Forever Young
l7uXLaH.description: natural anchorages in both sides (one side a huge rock, the other a crack)

Oi4_Xez.name: Dva trakova i jedan pas
Oi4_Xez.description: Just another line from Stičić tower.
Oi4_Xez.anchorsInfo:
Tension side(tower): tree; 6m spanset over rock, 2m padding, tree pro.
Static side: 3x 12mm expansion bolts; 2m spanset equalized, 1m padding.
Oi4_Xez.accessInfo: Reach static side by going down on the start of via ferrata bridge.

2RMVXTe.description: Super fun line with exposure view to Alushta and Ghost valley.
2RMVXTe.anchorsInfo: 3 glue and 1 wedge bolt each side. All are 12mm.
2RMVXTe.accessInfo: Ascend from Luchistoe takes 1.5-2 hours. The road goes along the beautiful valley of ghosts. You can also get on a solid 4x4.

6PL4V4Q.name: la de Ricardo
6PL4V4Q.description: Parking at the service station of la Cabrera. Going up the hill is easier on the other side via the white/yellow marked path. When you arrive at the path on the top you turn right Sector 2

pbjkw_z.name: Todes são bem vindes  "All are welcome"
pbjkw_z.description:
This is the second line at this spot, it was only done to respect the navy´s wish to take the line out of the way of the ships and keep the trip going. 
This is one of the many lines conquered by me and my partner while hitchhiking all over Brasil in searh for Highline spots. If you want to get to know this spot or any of the others in Bahia, get in touch with us for more information.
Follow on instagram at @_desinfluente and @juliabluu to check out some of our other adventures conquering Highlines all over Brasil.
pbjkw_z.accessInfo: Esay get in by the entrance of the restaurant located in Morro do Pernambuco

JwUDBYa.name: Metabief
JwUDBYa.description:
Se garer au parking le plus proche de la falaise. Longer la falaise vers le nord jusqu'à trouver le spot (évident), à mi-chemin entre les téléskis. Accès plus difficile en hiver.


Park on the closest parking to the cliff. Walk north along the cliff, you can't miss the spot. Harder to access in wintertime.
JwUDBYa.anchorsInfo:
Sur les arbres. Le mieux est de prendre un A-frame de 50cm pour le côté du sapin, sinon il faudra mettre l'élingue plus haut, et la ligne sera en descente.
trees. It's ideal to bring a small (50cm) A-frame for the pine tree side, but not compulsory (you'll have to rig this side higher, and the line will be off-level

Dn7Mi0a.name: Alfombra Roja
Dn7Mi0a.description: More info: slacklinecanarias@gmail.com. 3 chemical anchors each side
Dn7Mi0a.anchorsInfo:
3 chemical anchors each side

Bolts are badly rusted and unusable as of Nov ‘25

bJPLUJl.name: Symonds Yat
bJPLUJl.description:
Rigged from trees on observation deck to slings around the pinnacle. People have been known to sling around a small chunk on the pinnacle in the past which resulted in boulders being pulled off the top. Sling around the entire pinnacle to avoid this. 

DO NOT BOLT. The valley is a conservation area and is also used by trad climbers. Access is fairly simple down from the car park at top of hill. 
Abseil down from the observation deck to the bottom where you'll find a cave (which acts as a great camping spot). From here, you can access the base of the pinnacle and climb up. Trad gear is required, but the route is a relatively easy scramble up the south face. 

No known issues with the local authorities. Use appropriate protection, rig wisely and leave the spot as you found it, and you should be fine.

coXk-rl.name: Cheburashka
coXk-rl.description:
Most beautiful highline I have yet walked! 
Bolted and first walked by Matt, Paula, Reto and Laura

Careful: 
1. The marked spot in the map is probably not the exact spot, we don't have GPS data
2. You'll need some climbing/mountaineering skills to get there or at least move safely in alpine grounds..
coXk-rl.anchorsInfo: There are 3 bolts (12mm) on every side (if I remember correctly), no hangers. Bolted in 2015.
coXk-rl.accessInfo: To get to the first anchor, there is some easy climbing/ steep hiking required. For the second anchor, you'll need to cross a little stone field about 20m "over" the line (there's a U shape) and then repell about 20-30m. There is a bolt for repelling, though I don't remember the position exactely..

y_ns3sJ.name: Bunny Hop
y_ns3sJ.anchorsInfo:
There is a big docking bollard about 12m back from the edge on one side. This can be backed up by ground stakes if needed. 

The other side you need ground pegs.
y_ns3sJ.accessInfo: Park at the New Polzeath waterfront and walk along the coast with the beach on your left. Basically roadside.

O2PUBs_.name: No tengo el chocho pa farolillos
O2PUBs_.description: 3 bolts each side, easy access and easy rigging, very beautiful line

BqNUtI-.name: Expose Your Waterballs
BqNUtI-.description: Over the waterfalls just down from the bridge near the Ogwen Cottage.
BqNUtI-.anchorsInfo:
North side: 2 inconspicuous boulders quite far back from the edge on a long ledge. Both need at least 3m long slings to get around the boulders. The bigger of the two has a tree 6m beneath it you need to attach a rope to from the sling to prevent it riding up over the boulder.

South side: sling a huge cannon like boulder with a 6m sling. Back up with a 2m sling on the boulder to its right.
BqNUtI-.accessInfo: Park at the layby next to the bridge. cross over the wall and walk down to the river. It needs to be crossed to get to the north side.

rBDrwnN.name: Si això és guerra
rBDrwnN.description: There's a road underneath. Take it into account when doing the connection. 3 bolts on each side (12mm on the dynamic anchor, North and 10mm on the static side, South). No hangers on place. Easy access from the bridge on the road
rBDrwnN.anchorsInfo: 3 bolts on each side (12mm on the dynamic anchor, North and 10mm on the static side, South). No hangers on place.
rBDrwnN.accessInfo: Easy access from the bridge on the road.

mStzKh7.name: Que no vingui la pau
mStzKh7.description: There's a road underneath. Take it into account when doing the connection. No hangers on place.
mStzKh7.anchorsInfo: 3 bolts on each side (12mm on the dynamic anchor, North and 10mm on the static side, South). No hangers on place.
mStzKh7.accessInfo: Easy access from the bridge on the road.

j67eSmT.name: Stig of the Dump
j67eSmT.description: Bigger line over the quarry, diagonally across the Sludge pit. Slight tilt towards the northern side
j67eSmT.anchorsInfo: A redirect to gain hieght for the dynamic side, all quite simple. Tagline been in place since 2022.

NdVCUHq.name: Wisienka na torcie
NdVCUHq.description:
1. side (common with 135m) - 2 bolts for main, backup to the tree.
2. side - 2 bolts for main, backup to the ring below. Path on both sides
NdVCUHq.anchorsInfo:
1. side (common with 135m) - 2 bolts for main, backup to the tree.
2. side - 2 bolts for main, backup to the ring below

q-xnQ0w.name: Coffeeshop
q-xnQ0w.description:
A line over the canyon towards the hut in the wall. 
Bolted by Ludo, Inis and Garance
q-xnQ0w.anchorsInfo:
You can reach it by following the path to climbing sector sube and when you are almost at the wall turn right towards the 73m anchor. Scramble a bit more to the left of this anchor to find the 4 12mm  bolts in the wall for the 105m line. 
The other anchor is when you face the hut in the wall on the left side of it with 4 12mm bolts in the wall. You can reach the anchor by hiking/scrambling and abseiling you way down to the hut or you can climb the multipitch

fdDxd4u.name: taft 170
fdDxd4u.description:
First and foremost- please be aware that there are no campsites on taft point and that the park would not like any line left unattended.  Following these simple rules helps keep our access open to amazing places such as Taft, and all of Yosemite.  
The Taft 170 is one of those iconic lines with great exposure, and if you exposure turn in the in the middle, you get one of the best views of El Capitan in the valley.  Bomber anchors with a bit of padding and you are good to go.  Huge exposure makes this line a bit more heady than its distance alone suggests.  Taft is a great first stop in the valley.  If you rig this line, please be ready for and expect large crowds of tourists watching, asking questions, and taking pictures.  The group I rigged it with made signs that explain what highlining is and that cut down on a bit of the conversation, but not much.  Nice drops make for 0 no fall zones.  Overall a safe rig, and line if you do it right.
fdDxd4u.accessInfo: Park at the Glacier Point trailhead.  Hike 1.2 miles in to taft point, there are signs the entire way.  Both anchors can be accessed by foot.  Go to the observation point and look left- you will see the bolts, then look across the the large boulder: that is your other anchor.

IoV9Dei.name: "Muszę odpocząć"
IoV9Dei.description: Generalnie miejsce otwarte dla wszystkich i bez przypału. Idzie spotkać dużo ludzi (miejsce turystyczne ) zabezpiecz drogę żeby motory nie wjechały w napięte zawiesia/liny
IoV9Dei.anchorsInfo: Uważaj  na drzewa które będę twoimi stanowiskiem  ( niektóre idzie wyrwać ręką)
IoV9Dei.accessInfo: Parking w okolicach najbliższych domów na trawie

h18NGy8.name: Moose Knuckle
h18NGy8.description: short highline/midline perfect for first highline
h18NGy8.anchorsInfo: 3 bolt anchors with chains on both sides. Easy access to both anchors.

xGIW9Aj.name: Cuerda de vil
xGIW9Aj.description: 101m highline at Peña de la Muela, Hidalgo.
xGIW9Aj.anchorsInfo: 3 Fixe glue-ins on each side. Rope can be threaded directly through the anchors. Watch out for loose rock and holds in this area!

sjNle0_.name: Where Swallows Fly
sjNle0_.description: What a line!  Right on the precipice of Red Wall with a beautiful view of the Marsupials.  If you're lucky, the swallows will come out and join you while you're on the line.
sjNle0_.anchorsInfo: 3 bolts on each side with chain-link Terry Bolt hangers.
sjNle0_.accessInfo: Slog up Misery Ridge.  From the summit, look for the heard path near the trail post.  Follow that heard path downhill towards the river until you see the large hang-out cave.  The tensioning side anchors are easy to walk to:  just keep going down hill towards the river.  The static side anchors requires a short rappel, so bring an extra static rope, and some way to ascend back out when you're done.

mQjyFlq.name: Big Melons (the 190)
mQjyFlq.description: Nice spot!  This used to be a really big line back in the day!
mQjyFlq.anchorsInfo:
Eastern bolts are 7 chain link bolts with a glue in stabilizer bolt.  2 bolts are about 5 feet back from the main anchor, the main anchor is 10 feet to the lip.

5 chain link bolts on a vertical face with a stabilizer bolt.  21 feet from anchor to stabilizer, then another 2.5 feet to the lip.
mQjyFlq.accessInfo:
You can see google map directions from Moab here:  https://goo.gl/maps/WFWL6gKqRw6vknwm6
Take UT-313W and turn right onto Mineral Bottom Road.  Follow that for 8 miles until it crosses the only cattle grate.  Turn right soon after that onto a well established off-road trail, and follow that the rest of the way to the parking area.

RVBJKJp.name: Slide Party
RVBJKJp.description: Highline opened in 2018. Low exposure but very plesant. Great option in the valley, when you don't have a lot of gear.
RVBJKJp.anchorsInfo: One side the same like "Keczobokiem" 70m line. To the second side you need 2,5m sling to open and put on the rock and 2m sling to extend to the edge.
RVBJKJp.accessInfo: Paths or steep hills both sides

x_ZEs8j.description: An easy to rig longer highline in a decent climbing area. 10min from car to either anchor.

kmowxJo.name: Kundl Backofen
kmowxJo.description: Bolts are available on both sides
kmowxJo.anchorsInfo: bolts are available on both sides

t76qSld.name: Never ending fear
t76qSld.description:
Nice highline over a big river.
Both sides tree anchors for main- and backup.
Do not necessarily need a drone for the connection. You can also rappel down on both sides and throw a rope over the river.
You might need a permition as well.

mOBMeb4.name: Črnca
mOBMeb4.description: Definition of highline. Much higher than is long.
mOBMeb4.anchorsInfo:
Tension side(west): Few trees and a tree for height or a frame.
Static side: Use 1,5m spanset on good tree. 10m abseil.
mOBMeb4.accessInfo: Static side: Follow some animal paths and your intuition to reach it. :D

whZqBH1.description:
Area scouted by Dylan Bennett, Potential for great highlines. Hard access 
Worth more of a look!
whZqBH1.anchorsInfo: Large natural anchors, requires many spansets or much rope!
whZqBH1.accessInfo: Wye Creek Car Park, left at the forks towards North Wye and then take a left 15 minutes up track to aim along the true ridge of South end of Remarks, hit the corner and aim low to wrap around the west face. Allow 2-3 hours approach, Steep. HARD approach

DBQpVBp.anchorsInfo: Tree to tree, tagged with a bow and arrow, but perhaps can be walked around. Options for a wide variety of lengths if memory serves

ffCamg3.name: Șarpele
ffCamg3.restriction: partial: It is a national park, so respect the people, and the animals, don't feed the animals, try to not disturb the nature, and most importantly, try to leave no trace, take with you all the trash, and hang the food, so the animals can't get it.

Qpu1n01.name: Kinder Surprise
Qpu1n01.description: Probably the shortest highline in the UK with 5 steps in between the danger zones

NyOEdEA.restriction: full: They really don't wan't to see any highline here.

kyD_EbW.name: Walk on Boy
kyD_EbW.description:
Probably the best line in the area. Properly exposed with stellar mountain views. 

Established August 2025, FA February 2026
kyD_EbW.anchorsInfo:
Bolts on both sides. 

Homeside (south) 
4x bolts shared with 115 and 240 home side at ('). Bolts are 1/2 in glue in threaded rod set up for terry chains. 

Farside (north) 
3x bolts at (35°44.802', -106°6.692') Bolts are 1/2 in glue in threaded rod set up for terry chains. 

To preserve the integrity of the bolts, please do not leave zinc-plated hardware on the glue-ins.

VwEGFPe.name: Engine No.9
VwEGFPe.description: Crossed but still awaiting the first send.
VwEGFPe.anchorsInfo: 4 glue in bolts each side. All requiring abseil access.
VwEGFPe.accessInfo: Walk up either the steps by Australia or Serengeti. Carry along the level at the top of the steps away from Australia to reach the Lost World.

ytB-oRY.name: Gitalian Job
ytB-oRY.description: Parking at the service station of la Cabrera. Going up the hill is easier on the other side via the white/yellow marked path. When you arrive at the path on the top you just cross it and already arrive at the Sector 1 of La Cabrera

xl7rEYT.name: Hometown Hero
xl7rEYT.description: Highlining over or near Hidden Beach. Park on the side of CR 550, follow trail to the lake shore. 5 minute approach.
xl7rEYT.anchorsInfo: All anchors are off of trees. Please use tree pro!

C3DDrxW.name: Pepa Cube
C3DDrxW.description: 1 side Tree anchor and bolts. Close to despiste.. Climb rock crack
C3DDrxW.accessInfo: Close to despiste.. Climb rock crack

E_Mahps.name: La Plaisanterie
E_Mahps.description: Opened by Clément Fabre, Guillaume Rolland, Dorian Dampfhoffer le 8 Juin 2013
E_Mahps.anchorsInfo:
11 12mm bolts
Bring the hangers and nuts
Masterpoint on 4 bolts on each sides works fine:
2 2m spansets on two bolts then another one to link the  first 2 spansets.
You have then one bolt (west anchor) and 2 bolts (east anchor) for general backup.
E_Mahps.accessInfo: The parking spot is located here: 43.524056,5.505447 Drive to Le Tholonet from Palette. When you reach “ Le château du Tholonet “ make a left and turn right 300meters further on the “ chemin de la paroisse “. Stop at the end of the slope on the muddy spot. The parking lot is tiny ! So if you plan on parking with more than a couple of cars it is better to park inside Le Tholonet ( here for instance: 43.521901,5.510436 ) to keep parking space for hikers. The line is located here in the Gorges more or less parallel to the bridge of “ la petite mer “ : 43.528405,5.509408 15 to 20 minutes hike, start walking from le chemin de la paroisse, take the right path at the end of the slope to reach the cliff. Enjoy the point of view on la sainte-victoire and hike down along the cliff until you reach the bridge. The 49m is the 5th line from the bridge.

K90O7WW.name: Hixon Falls
K90O7WW.description: Beautiful waterfall line at hixon falls. Fairly straightforward rig.
K90O7WW.anchorsInfo: South side anchor is a single large spruce tree near the cliff edge. North Side uses a few large douglas firs. Each is big enough but using both puts the anchor in a better spot.
K90O7WW.accessInfo: Scramble up the rough path on either side of the falls to reach the anchors.

ieBJg1W.description: Line goes from the single Thunderbolt to the wall of mount Sokol. Nice view to the old castle, Meganom cape and sea below.
ieBJg1W.anchorsInfo: Three 12mm wedge bolts and old anchor on the single rock and three 12mm wedge bolts plus climbing anchor for backup on the wall. Need a hangers.
ieBJg1W.accessInfo:
Half an hour walk from the road below.
To get to the top of rock you have to climb trad using cams and nuts. It's not hard, maybe 5c/6a max. On the wall there is a sport climbing route so you need only quickdraws.

v7ya9R-.name: Domingueros al Sol
v7ya9R-.description: First midline/highline in this climbing valley. Very easy line without expo.
v7ya9R-.anchorsInfo: 3 holes for MMS in one side; 2 parabolts and 1 hole for MMS in the another side (without plats)
v7ya9R-.accessInfo: walking in one side, climbing easy route in the another side

8s5THMM.name: Galleline
8s5THMM.description: Easy rig highline with view over the walensee!
8s5THMM.anchorsInfo: Two trees, medium size, so 2m slings are enough. A throw rope is nice to have as there are some trees on the side where you have to pass the line through.
8s5THMM.accessInfo: There is a bus stop called Amden Lehni and parkingplace 5min from this spot. At the highline spot there is a small field where you can hangout and make a fire.

9ikaR2e.name: La vida lactea-La chilampi
9ikaR2e.description: lado A natural(10m) lado b en un huco en la pared del frente

-rGEyQ9.name: If Pigs Could Line
-rGEyQ9.description:
Who/When: Luke Sarantos and Shane Yates, 2012
FA: Luke Sarantos, August 2012.

Anchors: South - Main - 3 RBs
                            - Backup - 2 RBs
   North - Main - 2 RBs
	      - Backup - 2 RBs
Linking: Throw a line across or walk down to ...
-rGEyQ9.anchorsInfo:
Who/When: Luke Sarantos and Shane Yates, 2012
FA: Luke Sarantos, August 2012.

Anchors: South - Main - 3 RBs
                            - Backup - 2 RBs
   North - Main - 2 RBs
	      - Backup - 2 RBs
Linking: Throw a line across or walk down to the base of the gully.
Remarks: Rap off small boulder near the point to get to the anchor on the northern side.

DfHfpEC.name: Schluck und Spuck
DfHfpEC.description:
/!\ You'll need to ask permission to the mayor, go to the spot page to see the protocol.
An alternative to the classic 65m line which is simpler to rig and access in the sense that no rappelling is required.
DfHfpEC.anchorsInfo:
Left anchor
Three expansion bolts fairly close to the cliff edge with a backup bolt further back. It seems the bolts have been spray painted black, presumably in an attempt to distinguish them from climbing bolts. At the time the photo was taken two of the hangars were missing.
Right anchor
This was initially a natural anchor but two bolts have since been added. The original natural anchor can still be used as a backup, it consists of two large fairly hidden holes. The anchor view photo was taken before the bolts were installed and only gives a very rough indication of the anchor location. The holes are not indicated as their strength is unknown.

The anchor is significantly higher than the other side, this can be fixed with an A-frame around one to two meters tall.

TyJNyI_.description: First highline on bigger querry in Chwałków
TyJNyI_.anchorsInfo:
anchor #1
3x M12 Bolts, hangers needed + about 3m sling 
anchor #2
natural, 3 small trees (about 5m from the edge) conected with ~25-30m static rope and BFK. A-frame can help or good edge protection (crashpad)
TyJNyI_.accessInfo: Path to both anchors. 500m from parking. easy acces to anchors

sFl-Fxl.name: La Rebelión del Sur
sFl-Fxl.description: A Highline above the biggest open air cenote in Mexico near a town called Miguel Colorado
sFl-Fxl.anchorsInfo: Natural anchors, you will need 2 slings for one side and at least 40 meters of rope and 2 slings to make an extension for the other side in order to be high enough to clear the trees and a lot of connectors, you can cross the tagline with the ziplines you just have to comunicate with the managers and explain the riggin plans
sFl-Fxl.accessInfo: You will approach walking easily just have to go up some stairs near the highest zipline anchor

cSnz9yi.name: Michael’s Line
cSnz9yi.description: First line established at the Balcony. This line can't be rigged simultaneously with the 201m line.
cSnz9yi.anchorsInfo:
East - Main/Backup - 4 FH, M12 threaded rod.
   West - Main/Backup - 4 RB
cSnz9yi.accessInfo: Exposed scramble to get to the west side anchor. East side is located over the edge of the cliff.

oHiOJ6W.name: Rock and Spindle
oHiOJ6W.description: Need static rope and protection to sling around the Rock and the Spindle
oHiOJ6W.anchorsInfo: Need static rope and protection to sling around the Rock and the Spindle.

cFK6VEX.name: Luna Lobo
cFK6VEX.description: Short highline in Los Picachos area in Peña de Bernal.
cFK6VEX.anchorsInfo: 3 SS glue-ins on both sides. An A-frame on the closes side is needed to achieve the proper height and avoid a no-fall zone on that side.
cFK6VEX.accessInfo: Preferred ascent is from Chichidho side. It's an easy 20 minutos walk to the Picachos

wzTf4vw.name: FLYING MOON SOUP
wzTf4vw.description: Natural anchors, 40-50kg of equipment depending, multi point anchors possible left hand side 3 point anchor sliding X Used 2 Hexes and one 2.5m Spanset, 25m static rope for main, left hand side 18m static for main off boulder and one 4m spanset
wzTf4vw.anchorsInfo: Can use Spansets, Can use trad gear to assist static rope useful for mains
wzTf4vw.accessInfo:
Remarks Car park, Hike to Shadow Look Out and look left
Allow 1-1.5 Hours up with gear, allow 3-4hours to rig. Tagging is not too hard.

QV0sXnd.name: Pente'dévalée
QV0sXnd.description:
Very beautiful line, not so high especially at the beginning, but with a very very beautiful view and exposure to the vallée of Vénosc and the Pic of Vénosc.

Easy to rig as the tagline can be crossed by walking, but be aware that it is steep.
QV0sXnd.anchorsInfo:
Base of a birch redirected up to another birch. Anchor backup is possible with the big pine at the back.

The other anchor is a tall pine, you need to climb around 8 meters on this one, but is it basically a ladder so very easy. I decided to secure this pine with 2x15m of static rope attach to the base of another pine at the back.
QV0sXnd.accessInfo: 5-10min walk from the parking at the end of Les 2 Alpes

7yFwKOa.description:
Bolts on top of the citadel for home side. Bring a wrench to re-adjust anchor angles.
For the far side wrap the top of the tower with two 12 foot spansets or similar. Having a climber with you is helpful for accessing anchors
7yFwKOa.anchorsInfo:
Bolts on top of the citadel for home side. Bring a wrench to re-adjust anchor angles.
For the far side wrap the top of the tower with two 12 foot spansets or similar. Having a climber with you is helpful for accessing anchors.

i8Rsi95.description: 4 bolts on every anchor. near the edge
i8Rsi95.anchorsInfo: 4 bolts on every anchor. near the edge

Vd6tHIK.name: Wisha's Luck
Vd6tHIK.description: This is a very fun highline to play on. Located at a local swimming hole you will be among many other people who are swimming, cliff jumping, and just having fun! This is a great summertime highline! Crush the line and then immediately afterward swim in some cold, refreshing water. The tagging / rigging is extremely easy, There is just one dead branch (about 10 feet off the ground) that gets in the way of tagging. The no fall zones on each side stretch for about the first 10 feet of the line.
Vd6tHIK.anchorsInfo: Two solid trees to anchor to on both sides. Be careful not to rig to the dead trees that are around the area. Trees are about 1-2 feet in diameter.
Vd6tHIK.accessInfo: Drive into Sequoia National Park from highway 198. After passing the Potwisha Campground, park in the first turnout on the right hand side of the road. You will see a trail going down from the turnout, take it all the way down until you can hear a waterfall. Go toward the waterfall and the first anchor will be just before the clearing of trees. To cross to the other side, simply hike down and around the other side. You can also jump into the water and swim your way down into the gap.

v1t5gTt.name: Half Bitch
v1t5gTt.description:
Nice beginner line. Only about 15m of walking but a big landing to harness up and get your rollex on the line without standing on a cliff edge. 

The line splits the gap right in front of the waterfall. The anchor on the far side is a standalone tree about 10m from the cliff edge, the other anchor is on the near side. (Stand at the far anchor tree and look across the gap to the tree on the cliff edge beyond the two trees in the gulch.)
v1t5gTt.restriction: partial: Contact "forest@louisvilleky.gov" with the date you plan on visiting along with your vehicle info to get the access code to the gate.
v1t5gTt.accessInfo: Contact "forest@louisvilleky.gov" with the date you plan on visiting along with your vehicle info to get the access code to the gate.

Me9GY38.name: Glej in se čudi, kaj ti Gorenjska nudi
Me9GY38.description: Line with the best view on lake Bled.
Me9GY38.anchorsInfo:
Tension side: tree: 4m spanset + 1m for height
Static side: small tree: 20 abseil, 1m spanset
Me9GY38.accessInfo: Its recommended to do a hike from Kupljenik village.

v4m4DC9.description: For this line you have to ask the owner of the area first. He lives near the big farmer building, close to it. He is a nice guy. Some days in summer he makes the field

ljo2zje.name: Mirroir d'Argentine
ljo2zje.description: Installed 2022 very delicate, climbers below the line
ljo2zje.anchorsInfo: East 4x12mm short slings, West 4x12mm long (10m) slings, natural A-frame on rocks

9YRFxPh.name: Tunnel lowland
9YRFxPh.anchorsInfo: Tree anchoring. 1m spansets are enough. Prepare some equipment to rise 10m on a tree.

Mntyj_y.name: Cat's Mane
Mntyj_y.description: A line from the the middle pinnacles back to the main face. This is one of the stand out lines on the mountain with it being in a superb position. This line is a bit of an adventure to get too so make sure you get an early start.
Mntyj_y.anchorsInfo:
East - Middle pinnacle - Main - 3 RB
    - Backup - 3 RB from the Cat’s Crack
   West - Main cliff - Main - 2 RB
		    - Backup - Tree 3 m back
Mntyj_y.accessInfo: Access to the main middle pinnacle as per Cat’s Crack. Access to the main cliff via 20 m rappel in from a small tree on the ridge above. This rappel location can be reached by hiking over the summit, or climbing up the large gully 20 m south of the pinnacles. Alternatively a 30 m under protected grade 10 trad climb to the ledge.

DkOTHDU.name: Rebojo
DkOTHDU.description: O vento nessa linha forma movimento de rotaçao
DkOTHDU.anchorsInfo: 4 pinos de ancoragem química de cada lado dispensa conectores

9YNpV7n.name: Vipavtocesta
9YNpV7n.description: Low line but witch nice view on the canyon and river. First 25m from east are near trees so a no leash fall zone.
9YNpV7n.anchorsInfo:
Tension side (east): Same 3 expansion bolts as 48m line. We have used 6m spanset equalised on first two bolts and another 2m spanset to extend to A frame. A frame legs used are 3m and 3,5m high.
Static side (west): Tree, 9,5m spanset over the edge of rock.
9YNpV7n.accessInfo: West side anchor is next to a parking of climbing area and anchor is right next to climbing anchors.

o7Haus1.name: Buchenegger Wasserfall
o7Haus1.description: easy to rig and about 40m over the first pool of that waterfall.

qBmdCq3.name: C'est l'Amour
qBmdCq3.description: Rigged to the middle of the waterfall!
qBmdCq3.anchorsInfo:
Bolts on both sides. In the waterfall, the bolts are in the dry patch above where the last abseil starts. The hangers are removed. 3m spanset + backup or BFK.
On the other side, bolts are in the good rock quite far back (3m extension minimum) from a redirection bolt in bad rock in the front.
Bolted in summer 2023
qBmdCq3.accessInfo:
Follow the canyon from the top (3 abseils max 50m) to get to the waterfall anchor,
For the other side start reversing the escape path of the canyon. Just before reaching the waterfall, climb the small cliff on right side and continue up along the edge of the cliff. There's a smaller cliff that you have to climb. It's easily done by going around to the right to find a chimney (pull the bags on the outside of it). Then you're almost at the anchor.

hWHoQgR.name: Kesselfallklammfreikörperkultur (KFKFKK)
hWHoQgR.anchorsInfo:
Near side (west):
3x bolts with hangers.

Far side (east).
1x tree.
hWHoQgR.accessInfo: Simple rope access required for near side.

bVEytJD.name: El SanBerMausy
bVEytJD.anchorsInfo:
Main side (South side) natural anchor, at least 30mts of rope
Secondary side (North Side) 3 bolts, scrambling and then rappel to access

HgDw7Zh.name: Bernardo's line
HgDw7Zh.description:
A perfect line for beginners. Short and not too high but still quite exposed and very nice, with very easy access and rigging. Enjoy the view above the village !

Open by : Michael Schreiber, Moritz Wohlauf, Laetitia Gonnon, Maurice Ammann, in February 2016
HgDw7Zh.anchorsInfo:
When looking at the village, the left anchor is horizontally placed on the edge of the rock, at the anchor of the climbing route called « Megakante ». Use the belay to secure yourself. The anchor is about 2m further than the belay. 3 bolts of 12mm, then general back up of the belay behind the rock, bring enough padding. 3-4 m slings are needed. Bring the plates.
To get to the right anchor, you need to rappel down about 10m from the top ot the multipitch, use the belay of the climbing route « Mignonette ». You will find the anchor on the corner, 3 bolts of 12mm. 2 of them are behing the corner, bring enough padding. Bring the plates. 4-5m of slings.
HgDw7Zh.accessInfo: Park at the top of the big wall cliff, where the flag is. Then walk towards the cliff, follow the cliff on the left until you get to the obvious gap where the overhanging climbing sector is.

r3i8-0L.name: Drone Crusher
r3i8-0L.restriction: partial: Line is located on private property. For now the owner seems to enjoy the presence of the lines, but please be friendly and respectful when approached about it! Also make absolutely sure you leave the spot cleaner than you found it.
r3i8-0L.anchorsInfo:
3 12mm bolts on each anchor
bring hangers
r3i8-0L.accessInfo: hard but fun access to the south anchor

i4ZfbJg.name: An Abomination
i4ZfbJg.description: I named the line "An Abomination" because of the effort it took to rig this short line.
i4ZfbJg.anchorsInfo: Natural anchors. Trad gear is needed for the right side (slings, nuts and hexes). The left side is anchored by slinging a large rock feature.
i4ZfbJg.accessInfo:
