The hike to the line is a difficult 8kms and requires an ascent of 1000m.
The anchor on the left requires either trad climb (not sure of grade) or a traverse over a steep narrow gully. The anchors on the right are accessible by a difficult scramble down.

iR44iTu.name: Nido d'amore
iR44iTu.description: 4 bolts per side, 2 for the line and 2 for the backup. 2m sling needed. Easy to reach in 20 min from Rifugio Melano following the path in red (any doubt ask to Rifugio Melano)
iR44iTu.anchorsInfo: 4 bolts per side, 2 for the line and 2 for the backup. 2m sling needed.
iR44iTu.accessInfo: easy to reach in 20 min from Rifugio Melano following the path in red (any doubt ask to Rifugio Melano).

hdsHXmz.description: Buena linea para hacer freestyle, spot fresco y rico
hdsHXmz.anchorsInfo: Uso de frames en ambos lados, sacar lo mas posible la cuerda de la piedra.

TRZkHh-.description: Radio Induk. An interesting line on the mountain Induk. The place is very popular among climbers, and now a highline is available.
TRZkHh-.anchorsInfo: On a single rock three 12mm wedge bolts and a climbing anchor for backup. On the wall three 12mm wedge bolts and a tree above for backup.
TRZkHh-.accessInfo:
From the main road 1,5-2 hour walk. There are a lot of tent places in half an hour so it's very comfy to stay for a few days.
To get to the single rock you need to climb route: Orchard and garden (5c), there is a good platform for rigging on the top.
To get to the other point you need to climb 1,5 pitches of route: Dance in the Kremlin (F6c). Landmark is a tree on the right site after the first top. Anchor is below.

WrRXrDx.name: Pleasant screams and Maori dreams
WrRXrDx.description: Big line with a lot of work, has an awesome view of the coast and beautiful beach below. Please be respectful as the tension side is the ancient site of a PA and Maori chief dwelling
WrRXrDx.anchorsInfo:
Static side uses the group of trees on the hill that shares Sheila you pick which ones, the higher up you can get the better 

Tension side requires a lot of rope and extending, I think I used 80m of static to connect three pohutukawa trees which are between 12m-20m back from the anchor. 
I used a BFK and it’ll probably help to use an a frame
WrRXrDx.accessInfo:
Follow the track leading up the ridge of the island till the end, track leads through heavy flax bush to tension side.
 The static side can be reached from the small track down through the flax bushes to the South or repelling down the choss to the beach. Then up cliff navigating through the poor choss trail till you reach the pohutukawa Don’t fall and die

Tx6JV4i.name: Stalusa 105
Tx6JV4i.description: Nice Highline with some gaso below in the wild canyon of Val Russein.
Tx6JV4i.restriction: partial: Those lines are higher then 25m, so they need to be announced at BAZL by SwissHighline. Your contact there for the region: ramun@swiss-highline.ch
Tx6JV4i.anchorsInfo:
Riggingside: 
 3 x Raumer Glue-In Bolts 
1x 10m staticrope for BFK  
(2x 3m Slings/whoopies for backup) Shakles, quickllinks 
Quite alot padding required

Farside:
3x 10mm stainless steel Expansion bolts, redirection on the three
1x 1m roundsling for redirection + shakle
1x 2m roundsling 
1x 10m staticrope
Padding for the three
3x quicklimks (majon rapid)
Tx6JV4i.accessInfo: Park down at the bridge and walk from there to the anchors, it‘s forbidden to drive the street without permission of the village Sumvitg. Also Camping is sadly not aloud in that area. We recommend to drive to Lukmanierpass or the Camping Fontanivas in Disentis for sleeping.

t6FqMER.name: Char sad roub kam
t6FqMER.description: one of the longest longhighline have been rigged in Iran by Iranian team (EAL PROJECT)

0fyZHc1.name: Race Condition
0fyZHc1.description: Part way between Where Swallows Fly and Dogfight, rigged off a small tower that overlooks the Marsupials.
0fyZHc1.accessInfo:
Hike up Misery Ridge, and then down the heard trail towards the river.  Keep walking down hill and around until you see the big hangout cave.  The Tension side anchors are on the right side of the gully (facing the river), just on the other side of the anchors for Dogfight.  

Similar to Where Swallows Fly, an extra static rope is recommended to rappel down to where the static anchors are, and don't forget prussic or ascenders for the trip back!  A short, 4th class scramble gets you to the summit of the small spire.  Bring plenty of padding or line sleeves.

4hfVKs4.name: san bernardo
4hfVKs4.description: one of the paralel church lines of the festival.

fa6rZij.name: Slacklinersofbristol
fa6rZij.description: slackliners of bristol is a dream line on top of the iconic Avon Gorge, this has yet to have anchors or even permission but is in the pipeline!

DjXzeNM.name: Wild Sarah
DjXzeNM.description: epic highline project found by Kwjet and Smolda back in 2009.. leads over the valley, first 50m+ highline in Czech Republic
DjXzeNM.anchorsInfo:
natural anchors, about 10 m of spansets per side.. 
anchors are solid rock blocks, 3m diameter
DjXzeNM.accessInfo:
Prague - area of Divoká Šárka
access by tram or bus (from metro Dejvická)

hqd7DFJ.name: Bukvica
hqd7DFJ.description: Well hidden place in Bohinj. There is no bypassers because there is no path directly to the place. Really great line for beginners.
hqd7DFJ.accessInfo: You can ascend by foot, the sloaps are pretty steep and the leaves can be slippery on the other side. But you don't need special climbing gear to set it up.

E9hU9sp.name: Lucy in the Sky
E9hU9sp.description: Línea de 110 metros frente a la cascada con barrancas de fondo
E9hU9sp.anchorsInfo: Se ha realizado utilizando anclajes naturales de arboles del sitio, requiere mucha cuerda si se quiere hacer redundancia hacia varios arboles

kpgxAjN.name: Urzędnik
kpgxAjN.description: The biggest line of Urban Highline Festiwal in Lublin.

yYPN0Tc.name: Twister
yYPN0Tc.description: There's a twisting little river below the line and there happens a lot of twisting on the line as the spot used most often for freestyling sessions.
yYPN0Tc.anchorsInfo: Tensioning side is easily acccessed and convenient. Far side requires ascension to 7-8 meter height on a strudy tree with rope or slings. Best to use a girth hitch going off of the left side of the trunk - to get as much height as possible and for the line to avoid touching nearby branching trunk. Might be sensible to use another slackline behind the anchor tree to somewhat equalize bending forces.

HqIdDN_.name: Jeździec Apokalipsy
HqIdDN_.description:
First side: 2.5m sling for main, do it longer by shackle and 5m sling to the edge. Throw 4m sling on the rock, it perfectly fit for backup. You need a lot of pads.
Second side: About 15m slings for wrap the rock on the top and few meters for backu...
HqIdDN_.anchorsInfo:
First side: 2.5m sling for main, do it longer by shackle and 5m sling to the edge. Throw 4m sling on the rock, it perfectly fit for backup. You need a lot of pads.
Second side: About 15m slings for wrap the rock on the top and few meters for backup.
HqIdDN_.accessInfo: Ascend without climbing, watch out for loose rocks on the paths

JNmBVrK.name: 25m Wind Mountain Gap
JNmBVrK.description: What a spot!  Two lines, not too far from Portland with some beautiful perceived exposure, and an approach around 1 mile.  What more can you ask for?
JNmBVrK.anchorsInfo:
The Northern anchor is a HUGE (but maybe dead) tree not too close to the edge.  
For the southern anchor, equalize a few good trees. The ground is steep and loose over there, it may be safest to tie in or be on repel.  The southern side of the line could use some pruning.
JNmBVrK.accessInfo: 1 mile approach from the Wind Mountain Trailhead.  When the trail turn sharply West, look for a spur trail to the East going down hill.

3_Yw3o9.name: Lolo Libre
3_Yw3o9.description: It's necessary to climb the tower

2YR_g8v.name: Pavouk na káblíku
2YR_g8v.description: Permission required. Concrete pillars. Through balcony

WLtwd14.name: La mordidita
WLtwd14.description: On the side of the gate it is easy to anchor directly to the drains on the wall, for the other side a strong tree is used not so on the shore
WLtwd14.anchorsInfo: On the side of the gate it is easy to anchor directly to the drains on the wall, for the other side a strong tree is used not so on the shore

dgaxlBK.name: (no send)
dgaxlBK.restriction: partial: We didn't manage to send it yet, so please let first riggers to do so or just come with us for a session :D
dgaxlBK.anchorsInfo:
Tension side(south): Tree; 6.5m spanset to a 2m high Aframe.
Static side: 3x 12mm expansion bolts, 15m rope bfk, one more bolt on the edge to tie it down. 20m rope for abseil recommended.

IWRRKG1.name: Highlife
IWRRKG1.description: Mellow highline/midline with views of Golden and Coors.
IWRRKG1.anchorsInfo: 3 bolts on each side. 6 ft spans get masterpoints over the edge. Hangers on each side so steel biners/quicklinks required.
IWRRKG1.accessInfo: Hike up to brown cloud climbing area, follow trail up rock steps to the top of the table. Once you are at the top, the bolts are about 40 feet to your left.

32DKycM.name: Castle Rock – Squid dick
32DKycM.description:
First opened in February 2015 by Zane Rowe, Shane Yates, Jacob Druce, Kaleigh Anne and Oo simo.
Castle Rock, Coromandel, New Zealand.
32DKycM.restriction: full: There is a Rahui (prohibition) on rock climbing until further notice which I would assume applies to Highlining as well
32DKycM.anchorsInfo:
5x12mm glued threaded bolts on each side. Bring your hangers and nuts.
* Connection :
Throw a tagline from each side in the steep gully and connect them.
* Anchor 1 :
West side : 5x12mm glued threaded bolts. Gear : 1x3m 1T sling.
* Anchor 2 :
East side : 5x12mm glued threaded bolts. Gear : 1x4m 1T sling.
32DKycM.accessInfo: Walk to the saddle between the 2 pinnacles (30mn) Go to the left pinnacle, on a rocky step next to the anchor of “Ganesh” you will find 5 glued threaded bolts. By scrambling down the other pinnacle you will find 5 others.

HcO31GQ.name: Nipel
HcO31GQ.description: Short line directly into the wall. Intimidating for begginers.
HcO31GQ.anchorsInfo: Static side: 2x 12mm bolts and 3x 10mm bolts. Rappel for 15m is needed into a rock face to get to anchor. On tensioning side there are strong trees and you need atleast 6m of sling with some padding.

zr9vr_0.name: Saka Suur
zr9vr_0.description:
You camp and make a fireplace right there next to anchor. 
There is another little highline with small waterfall next to it. One side is from the tree. 
Second side(camping area) - anchor is unused electricity post which is pretty much 15m away in...
zr9vr_0.anchorsInfo:
One side is from the tree. 
Second side(camping area) - anchor is unused electricity post which is pretty much 15m away in from the edge. You need to build a-frame here.
zr9vr_0.accessInfo:
You can get by car, but the road is bad, be slow!  
Anchor 1 is right at the end of the road. 
Second anchor across - you need to walk around from left side.

Ln0xKL6.name: Stalusa 90
Ln0xKL6.description: Nice line with some gaso below in the wild canyon of Val Russein.
Ln0xKL6.restriction: partial: Those lines are higher then 25m, so they need to be announced at BAZL by SwissHighline. Your contact there for the region: ramun@swiss-highline.ch
Ln0xKL6.anchorsInfo:
Riggingside: 
3 x Raumer Glue-In Bolts
1x 10m staticrope for BFK 
(2x 3m Slings/whoopies for backup)
Shakles, quickllinks
Quite alot padding required

Farside:
4x 10mm stainless steel Expansion bolts
4x quicklink (Majon rapid)
1x 2m roundsling
1x 1m roundsling (or whoopies)
Ln0xKL6.accessInfo: Park down at the bridge and walk from there to the anchors, it‘s forbidden to drive the street without permission of the village Sumvitg. Also Camping is sadly not aloud in that area. We recommend to drive to Lukmanierpass or the Camping Fontanivas in Disentis for sleeping.

9mvolaY.name: Freestyle Siurana
9mvolaY.description: You can drop the tagline from the farside then walk up on the side where the two trees are close together(close side)
9mvolaY.accessInfo: 25min approach from the Parking (next to the farm in the curve)

d_yqWzl.name: Angry Man
d_yqWzl.description: One anchor has 5 bolts and the other its a big natural anchor (9m sling) for main and backup
d_yqWzl.anchorsInfo: One anchor has 5 bolts and the other its a big natural anchor (9m sling) for main and backup.

So0E4mS.name: Hell's line
So0E4mS.description: amazing place for highline and cimbing
So0E4mS.anchorsInfo: there is 4 bolts 10mm on each side, but one one side there is no hangers

iKAJodQ.name: New Horizons
iKAJodQ.restriction:
partial: Need to be activated. Contact Swiss Highline - Below 40 m
Signalisation Recquierd
iKAJodQ.anchorsInfo:
1x 3m Sling West Side
-- Bring around 10m Staticrope for the Backup at the Top of the Climbing Rout
1x 2.5m Sling Eastside
--Bring about 25 m Static rope for access and Backup

3uMcnAQ.name: We Told You Sol
3uMcnAQ.description: A highline that really soaks in the sun, when there is some! right in the bowl of the Snowdon Horseshoe.
3uMcnAQ.anchorsInfo:
North side needs about 8m of slings plus about various length s of static rope and extra slings to extend a trad back up. Thread a big spanset behind the boulder and make sure it is well paded. Then there are some cracks up to the right hand side that are about 8m back. Equalise some gear placed in these then add them to the masterpoint for added backup.

The South side is one giant boulder that can be easily slung with a 6m long spanset. @ spansets can be used, one for main and the other for backup. Padding is only needed for the edges of the boulder.

Both sides have an increasing slope with no real cliff edge so not edge padding is really needed.
3uMcnAQ.accessInfo: Follow the miners path from the Pen Y Pass car park until you can see the first lake. Walk around the left side of the lake until you get to the pipeline and then you can split off to get to each anchor.

4_9Rgo0.name: Loška biba
4_9Rgo0.description: It's short, it's low, it's biba.
4_9Rgo0.anchorsInfo:
Tension side (south): 3x 12mm expansion bolts: 15m rope bfk
Static side: 3x 12mm expansion bolts: 2m spanset, 10m abseil

qZCnOqn.name: Call of the Wild
qZCnOqn.description: A highline off of the top of the Landing Strip above the Great One ski line.
qZCnOqn.anchorsInfo: 3 Sleeved 1/2" Bolts on both sides. The west side (Static side), has the BFK routed around a tree in order to create a good angle for the bolts.
qZCnOqn.accessInfo: This is accessed by hiking towards the base of the landing strip buttress off of Naya Nuki Peak. Hike around and lookers left of this buttress through scree to get to the middle of the Great One couloir. Do a few sections of 4th-5th class scrambling to get to the landing buttress's ridge for east side anchor. To access the west anchor hike/scramble directly up from midpoint of the couloir. After roughly 200 feet of uphill scrambling scramble lookers right 300 feet to a tree and perform a 30m rap to the anchors below.

c0YzE_D.name: Whakapapa Skifield – Keep cool in the Mordor
c0YzE_D.description:
Opened in November 2017 by the team Ekiwibristes.
Whakapapa Skifield, Tongariro National Park, New Zealand.
Parking : -39.236943, 175.556267
Highline : -39.242158, 175.562157
c0YzE_D.anchorsInfo:
Anchors
12mm bolts and 33cm long 12mm threaded bolts (main) + 10mm bolts (back up).
Connection :
Walk around the canyon with your tagline, easy as bro.
Anchor 1 :
West side : 1x12mm expansion bolt + 2x12mm threaded bolts (33cm long) on a horizontal slab (main), and 1x10mm expansion bolt on a boulder behind it (backup). 
Anchor 2 :
East side : 2x12mm expansion bolts + 1x12mm threaded bolts (33cm long) on a horizontal slab (main), and 1x10mm expansion bolt (backup). 
Gear :
East side : 1x3m 1T spanset, 1x33cm long 12mm threaded bolts, 3x12mm hangers, 1x10mm hanger
West side : 1x3m 1T spanset, 2x33cm long 12mm threaded bolts, 3x12mm hangers, 1x10mm hanger
c0YzE_D.accessInfo: Park at the ski station and walk at the top of the 1st chairlift and to the canyon on your left, where the warterfalls are (20mn)

x4xTcIQ.name: Somewhere over the rainbow
x4xTcIQ.description:
Created by Jocelin Laporte and Alexander Bonov. 1 side natural with 2 nice trees, you will need some spansets.
Other side 3x10mm and 2x12mm bolts with no hangers. 5 minutes from Zasele. Getting the line across the gap can be tricky
x4xTcIQ.anchorsInfo:
1 side natural with 2 nice trees, you will need some spansets.
Other side 3x10mm and 2x12mm bolts with no hangers.
x4xTcIQ.accessInfo: 5 minutes from Zasele. Getting the line across the gap can be tricky.

TsH9Eml.name: The Wave
TsH9Eml.accessInfo: Pasando la ola debes bajar un poco

NwynwO_.name: Unexpected party
NwynwO_.description: First opened in 2017 by Petr Polak. We opened a new one, longer one, with the French team “Les ekiwibristes” in September 2017
NwynwO_.anchorsInfo:
Obvious « natural » anchors (a tree and 2 concrete pillars below the wooden balcony).
Connection : Throw a tagline from each anchor and connect them by walking around the pool, quite easy.
Anchor 1 : 
Carpark side: go to the wooden balcony on the right side of the fall (looking downstream). Below the balcony use 2 of the concrete pillars, back up on the wooden structure or a tree behind (not the best as it crosses the walkway)
Anchor 2 : 
Park side: use the base of a tree approx. 4m behind the cliff edge (use long sling to drift the weblock after the edge) You go around the waterfall to the other point of view and jump over the fence on your right, where the grass patch is. Back up on the wooden fence.
Gear : 2x2m 1T slings and 1x4m 1T sling.
NwynwO_.accessInfo:
Whangarei Falls, Northland, New Zealand. Carpark at 24 seconds walking.
Above the waterfall pool.

rFzYSm-.name: La shunga del lago
rFzYSm-.description: 3 12mm bolts on each side. From Col de Puymorens, following Vall d'En Garcia. Parking close to the spot but difficult road
rFzYSm-.anchorsInfo: Two 12mm bolts on one side. A tree on the other.
rFzYSm-.accessInfo: From Col de Puymorens, following Vall d'En Garcia. Parking close to the spot but difficult road.

3MG9_s6.name: Swing Line
3MG9_s6.description: Originally established as the launch point for a rope swing, this has now been established as our short Waterfall Gorge highline.
3MG9_s6.anchorsInfo:
East - Main/Backup - 4 FH
   West - Main - 3 FH
- Backup - Horizontal crack 10m back: #4 and #3 Camalot, purple and gold hexcentrix
3MG9_s6.accessInfo: At the top of the gorge, cut back around the top on the east side for best access. For west side access, follow the vague path from the top of the gorge westerly for about 200m, and just before you meet the small creek line, go off track easterly to the gorge. The anchor is located on the second tier of the cliff, this is best accessed by an exposed climb down. This can be located approximately 5m north of the anchors position.

ToqRep9.description: Perfect line for newbies, super easy to rig and safe. Name is Thyme.
ToqRep9.anchorsInfo: Three 10mm and two 12mm wedge bolts on both side. You need hangers.
ToqRep9.accessInfo: There are two ways. The first one is through the devil's staircase (about an hour's journey) and the second from Foros Church (1.5 hours). Bring water, in summer water sources can be dry.

imqB-WN.name: Wings of Desire aka Siemenslufthaken
imqB-WN.description: 2024 Slackline Berlin managed to rig this beauty, just before the renovation of the stadium started. The light masts in the east will be taken down and the line will not be repeatable.
imqB-WN.restriction: full: Permission by the stadium management is crucial.
imqB-WN.accessInfo: There a leaders inside the light masts. Doors are locked.

FMDtRnF.name: First Moldavian Highline
FMDtRnF.description: 4 glued 12mm bolts on each side. Bring hangers with you
FMDtRnF.anchorsInfo: 4 glued 12mm bolts on each side. Bring hangers with you.

N4Q8a15.description: Rigg above the pond (it's not on the map), possible option to rigg spaceline.
N4Q8a15.anchorsInfo: Only trees, so easy to rigg, you need 11m of slings

Usp8jmZ.accessInfo: Scramble from large trail north west of anchors.

zluEurk.name: 1st highline
zluEurk.description: safe place, easy setup, good for urban highline.

3vC0OH9.name: Highline to heaven
3vC0OH9.description: 35m highline at Peña de la Muela, Hidalgo.
3vC0OH9.anchorsInfo: 3 Fixe glue-ins on each side. Rope can be threaded directly through the anchors. Watch out for loose rock and holds in this area!

Kcljdw9.name: FOUNTAIN OF YOUTH
Kcljdw9.description:
A beautiful highline running into waterfalls of The South Wye branch, 
Steep ascent but very rewarding. 

Dylan Bennett, Miguel Obrien,
Kcljdw9.anchorsInfo: One Anchor close to climbing top with options for bolts for diversions, natural boulder wrapped behind a ledge. Hard access to enter line from this anchor. Far Anchor requires bush bashing, waterfall hopping and creative rigging into a crack created by two LARGE boulders touching, create a diversion to off centre rig for safer exit. Tagging done with Fishing rod.
Kcljdw9.accessInfo: Wye Creek car Park to North Wye Branch, get to the top of major climbing either far right (safer) or left via a crack with existing climbing rope. Allow 2- 3 hours ascent

GI5BNJZ.name: La Comunidad del Anillo
GI5BNJZ.description: Highline, second of 4 in the same Spot. New Zealand 1st and 2nd Higline Festival Spot.
GI5BNJZ.anchorsInfo: 5/6 bolts all glue in in one side. Rock here is very soft, equalize minimum of 3 bolts for main, never use BFK for main. 3 bolts for backup (2 really usable). Far side 2 bolts as backup and natural anchor around rock. Use 2x 3m spanset or longer, or rope.
GI5BNJZ.accessInfo: Cooper's Knob - Summit Road - Toilets are 700m from Spot on the track direction to Christchurch.

UEgU8xA.name: Muito prazer, eu sou o Rio Tapajós
UEgU8xA.description: Praia Pedra da Moça, Alter do Chão/PA
UEgU8xA.anchorsInfo:
Anc. A: 60m de corda, 2 manilhas, softrelease, linelock
Anc. B: 70m corda, manilha.

jjqy9is.name: Horizon line at Wield Il- Mielah
jjqy9is.description:
With one side anchored to a big rock arch, this extensively developed climbing crag offers great views from top and below.
It's also a nice diving spot
jjqy9is.anchorsInfo: The arch has at least three resin glued bolts that serve as one anchor point, but I think there's two separate points for rigging on that side, the other side has at least two resin bolts, with a bunch of natural anchors for all the backups you'd like.
jjqy9is.accessInfo:
Easy access
Roads close by are accessible by car

Ilgr_ws.name: Blanca Mariposa
Ilgr_ws.description: Short line with easy anchors, one tree on one side and a zip line anchor on the other
Ilgr_ws.accessInfo: Ask for permission to the local community, better go with someone who knows the community

NRJRp5L.name: Loża Szyderców
NRJRp5L.description: Access is really easy, car leave under the highline, road to the top is also easy. You should find a road (motocross road) to up no dont need to climb there.
NRJRp5L.anchorsInfo: One anchor is a tree, big tree, back-up also tree 5 meters behind the first tree. Second anchor we used two trees beacause they are thin, back-up again tree 4-6 meters behind main anchor
NRJRp5L.accessInfo: Really easy access. You dont need to climb there

g5rcFuh.name: The big wave
g5rcFuh.anchorsInfo: Uso de bolts, ya estan puestos

PQd_LPq.name: MiniMet
PQd_LPq.description:
Such a fun and exposed beginner line for your first highline. we ran the line along the ground from the tension side 20 feet to the cliff, wrap and pad your line properly.
FA and established by Pat O'Brien and Steve Malkos Nov 8 2020
PQd_LPq.anchorsInfo: All natural on both sides. Trees on the south tension side was about 20ft from cliff edge, bring padding. North static side had a perfect cliff side tree that was backed up to a tree 3 feet behind it.
PQd_LPq.accessInfo: As easy as it gets. Hike the Metcalfe practice wall, small 2 foot gap to cross then before the point the path opens up cliff side and your at the tension side. Hike back and around to get to the static side.

JYOvCjM.name: No bad days by: Jürgenstein Von Hammershmark
JYOvCjM.anchorsInfo:
Dinámico:Tres anclajes mecánicos con cadena.
Estático: árbol

dHoqp51.name: Mehka žica
dHoqp51.description: Starts high then its above forest. Still great line.
dHoqp51.restriction: partial: Ask SlackAlien before rigging for air traffic info.
dHoqp51.anchorsInfo:
South (tension side): Tree for height, 7,5m spanset around rock pillar, you can use via ferrata anchors for backup with 15m rope, 2x 1m padding, tree pro.
North (static side): 3x12mm expansion bolts, 2m spanset.
dHoqp51.accessInfo:
Tensioning side: Directly across road at parking there is steep path which lead you to the top where you go left and then you reach steel cables. On the end is 15m steel wire bridge. Alternative access is by 2 via ferrata choices.
Static side: You go in the forest towards the gorge and a few meters in you turn left to small path which leads you to the edge of gorge on top. Then follow a ridge and and then left somewhere where you can cross the hill to come to the rock above anchor. Then some scrambly ridge down. Alternative option is also at the road where is high built wall directly up in very steep terrain, but you reach anchor faster and more direct.

zD71B-T.name: HS 80
zD71B-T.description: OG most classic line of the spot. Tagline can be easily walked around the gap, take care not to knock loose rock onto climbers below.
zD71B-T.restriction: none: Be respectful of climbers, this is a BMC owned quarry so if the climbers complain about slackliners then it will create access issues.
zD71B-T.anchorsInfo:
East anchor - 3 ground stakes.
West anchor - 4 glue-in bolts (Ca. 2013)

IMPORTANT - The 80m east anchor should be rigged off the stakes rather than the tree as the line goes over a better edge.

When both the 70m and 80m lines are rigged, a small and a large A/hang frame can be supported against each other. See photos.
zD71B-T.accessInfo: Park at the main Horseshoe Quarry carpark or lay-bys, approach the quarry then follow the track up the hill to the right. Once level with the top of the quarry follow the path to the left, up a few steps then carefully make you way the large tree.

yHO9xC8.name: Static Sunday
yHO9xC8.description: Top of the waltz, next to fish face
yHO9xC8.accessInfo: hike or climb to top of waltz

qyt3sGj.name: Spa deluxe
qyt3sGj.description: Beautifull line across the canyon, both anchors are fairly easy to reach and its pretty exposed
qyt3sGj.anchorsInfo:
Dynamic side: big sliding x on 2 trees with a little extension going trough a redirect coming from the rock underneath to keep the anchor nicely in place on the rocks. Bfk backup to the same trees

Static side:
Bfk on 3 bolts with a long extension going through a redirect at the cliff edge to prevent the anchor from floating. For the redirect the is a nice square piece of rock sticking out that you can sling.
qyt3sGj.accessInfo:
Coördinates for the dynamic anchor: 40.244662,20.438026
You can reach it walking from the parking, cross the ottoman bridge and follow the hiking trail up for around 15 minutes.  Its at the cliff where the river makes its first big bend to the left. You get there by follow a small trail from the dirtroad through the bushes following for a while a dried up waterfall and then to the left of that.
The static anchor is located at the edge of the cliffs corner and is easy to reach walking past the bridge on the bottom of the left side of the canyon and hiking up to the bottom of the second higher wall of the canyon. Follow this wall till you reach the corner of the canyon. Scramle or abseil a bit down on the corner to find 3 12mm bolts with plates.

HiOZnF8.name: Split Pea Soup
HiOZnF8.description: Best line in the gulch, so far. Tree to tree rig with a redirect on the far anchor. Great walking distance, beautiful views of the area.
HiOZnF8.anchorsInfo: Near anchor is the same near anchor as Half Bitch. Far anchor is 20m down the path from the far anchor of Half Bitch. Look for two cedar trees by the cliff edge on the right side of the path with a big oak tree on the left side. Redirect off the left cedar to keep clear of the tree in the gap
HiOZnF8.accessInfo: Contact "forest@louisvilleky.gov" with the date you plan on visiting along with your vehicle info to get the access code to the gate.

kSRhUJR.name: PLUS T'ES FORT PLUS T'ES CON
kSRhUJR.description: C'est la ligne pour débuter autour d'Annecy. Facile d'installation, elle ne fait pas peur mais reste magnifique avec une vue imprenable sur Annecy.
kSRhUJR.accessInfo: 30 mn - Depuis le parking du col des contrebandiers, suivre le chemin qui monte à l'ancien téléphérique. Quand on découvre la vue sur le lac, suivre une sente qui part tout droit et qui descend légèrement.

asZUg_3.name: Por Medio Peso
asZUg_3.anchorsInfo: Natural anchor in one side, 3 bolts in the another side (one of them was MMS)
asZUg_3.accessInfo: One side is able to go walking to the top, the another side need to be climbed. You have different routes to get the anchor point. Ask to the locals or contact with Slackline Canarias.

LpHCwAR.name: Mezmai
LpHCwAR.description: Highline  над водопадом Яворовый. Высота от 60 до 110 метров, длина 92. Первая станция организована над водопадом спомощью 4 распорных анкеров 12мм, также чуть выше есть 2 10мм анкера для страховки. Вторая станция организована на скальном выступе за 2 больших дерева, на котоый можно попасть по перешееку.

i0DsBge.name: Rim Rock
i0DsBge.description: What a line!  Escape the hot summer days in the Gorge by coming up to the mountains.  On a clear day, you can see Mt. Hood, Mr. Adams, Mt. St. Helens, AND Mt. Rainier!
i0DsBge.anchorsInfo: Facing Mount Hood, the left side (static) anchor is a large pine tree, at least 6 feet (2 meters) around.  Extend out past a smaller tree (6ft, 2m extension), using one of the large branches as an A frame.  The Tensioning side has 4 glue in bolts.
i0DsBge.accessInfo: Park on Surveyors Ridge Road, and bushwhack west.

MAV-OZR.name: unkown
MAV-OZR.anchorsInfo: 5 glue-in bolts on each side, you might want to consider to use the back two bolts as mainline anchor

_5umbCJ.name: Ask and its given
_5umbCJ.restriction: partial: In a national park where highlining isnt mentioned in the management plan yet
_5umbCJ.accessInfo: Need to abseil dow  to lhs anchor with a open 3m spanset over a natural horn for rappel, 10m static is ample

xDEV5i0.name: Makina
xDEV5i0.anchorsInfo: Tension side A: Tree, 2m sling, 2m high on tree. Static side B: Tree, 2m sling lowest on tree.

ic30F-P.name: Flying low
ic30F-P.anchorsInfo:
on the east side,  stone only
and on the west side, there are three bolts

ew4vVp6.description: Road to Hyperborea. Extremely hard to rig highline. Not recommended for people don't have a huge alpine experience.
ew4vVp6.anchorsInfo: Four 12mm wedge bolts on a side wall, two 12mm wegde and two 12mm glue bolts on the Touth rock.
ew4vVp6.accessInfo: About an hour walk from the lake in Kasimovka. On the wall you need to climb 5 pitches of route Hyperborea (Aid climbing A3). On a Touth the right one of multipitch F6A. Bring about 500m of static rope to fix it on the wall and to transfer the webbing from one side to another.

2yC-NoN.name: Cambio pegue por pasta al pesto
2yC-NoN.anchorsInfo:
Four 10mm bolts on each side. You have to bring the hangers. On the static side there are two bolts that doesn't work.
About 5m of spanset / 10m of static rope are needed on both sides.
2yC-NoN.accessInfo: Normal ascension to Pic de Cambre d'Aze. The line goes over couloir central. The dynamic side is just before the entrance to the couloir, the static side is on a big boulder just before the summit, on the other side of the couloir.

5xMf3QW.name: Serengeti 1
5xMf3QW.description: Do not rig. Bolts in bad state and are in need of replacement.

ukzcZpD.name: Bertie and Ernie
ukzcZpD.description: Beautiful exposed line with a nice view to the see on one side and an abandoned medieval village on de other side.
ukzcZpD.restriction: partial: We didn’t ask permission to the authorities but we talked to the owner of the Konoba (restaurant) and he was totally oke with it. If you want to repeat the line it’s a good idea to ask him again, especially since the line goes over a road.
ukzcZpD.anchorsInfo: Natural anchors on both sides. We used a lot of slings (26m in total). But I’m sure if you get creative you can do it with less. For the south side anchor we slinged 2 big boulders and used a lot of padding. There are quite some boulders you can use around the wooden cross. For the anchor on the north side we used 2 trees behind the rock. On this side you need a bit less padding.
ukzcZpD.accessInfo: Approach to southern side is a hiking trail and and the anchor is marked by a wooden cross. The northern anchor requires an easy scramble

sT2KCS7.name: Bachelor Party
sT2KCS7.description: Highline, fourth of 4 in the same Spot. New Zealand 1st and 2nd Higline Festival Spot. 1st send by Fer Kpok
sT2KCS7.anchorsInfo: 5 bolts all need hangers 10mm and 12mm. Rock here is very soft, equalize minimum of 3 bolts for main, never use BFK for main. Far side 5 bolts, need hangers . Equalize with 3/4 bolts.
sT2KCS7.accessInfo: Cooper's Knob - Summit Road - Toilets are 700m from Spot on the track direction to Christchurch.

W8B2lUF.name: CBNC
W8B2lUF.description: The line was bolted and rigged on the canceled Vitality festival 2024. CBNC (cancelled but not cancelled) Line is a 115m (lasered) line, going in front of a big cave to the other side.  With an amazing view towards the cave and also towards the city of Travnik
W8B2lUF.anchorsInfo: Each side has 3x12mm bolts (plates probably removed). As we are a small community  feel free to improve if you think it needs improvement
W8B2lUF.accessInfo:
The access side is easy (mountain path). Far side is a scramble through bushes. The camp is just above on a field with an amazing view. The road is usually good -> https://maps.app.goo.gl/vpXFbU2S5hXs8aZg7

To rig the line you need to fly the drone, the easiest way is to fly it from the far side.

hxlixY8.name: 40 skinheadov
hxlixY8.anchorsInfo: Tension side A: Tree, 9m sling to tree for height. Static side B: Tree, 5,5m sling to Y tree for height, some rope to build master point inbetween needed.

GOQfrTn.name: La Quemada
GOQfrTn.description: Highline rigged around 2014 by the Balance GDL community
GOQfrTn.anchorsInfo: Mechanical bolts, bring your own quicklinks/SS quickdraws, 6 in total.
GOQfrTn.accessInfo: Park your car on El Diente parking and then walk towards the mountain called "El Centinela". The highline spot is behind the climbing wall.

tJ8Z6ru.name: King in my Castle
tJ8Z6ru.description: The King-Line. This Line is going above the HADA-Cave. To reach the anchors, you have to climb/hike at the far end of HADA up to the top. There should be a fixed rope, if not you can make your way if you have a little bit of experience.
tJ8Z6ru.anchorsInfo: Bolted with glue both sides in beginning of 2022
tJ8Z6ru.accessInfo: Go to HADA at the far end of the cave. here climb/hike up to the top of HADA. Then turn right and you will find the bolts. To reach the other anchor you can hike above HADA to the other side or climb up some harder routes on the left if you are facing HADA.

nt2ZUEb.description: highline established on drill and chill festival 2016

q15wWsU.name: Mayahuel
q15wWsU.description: El lugar es el Cerro de la Campana, en el Estado de México, que también se conoce como "Salazar" entre escaladores.
q15wWsU.anchorsInfo: Tres tensores Fixe de acero inox. de 3/8 in - 6 1/2 in en ambos lados
q15wWsU.accessInfo: En domingo se puede subir en carro y llegar hasta un par de metros del lugar. El resto de la semana el camino está cerrado y ahí que subir a pie por un sendero por el lado poniente del cerro. La subida es de unos 20 mins. aproximadamente.

7Agaa9I.name: Vojaška
7Agaa9I.description: Military have this beams to build a rope bridge sometimes. We do the same in a much cooler sense :D

oJh-nS7.accessInfo: by foot, 10 minutes from the road

-G7eSZO.name: La pêche aux champignon
-G7eSZO.description: La ligne passe d'un bous à l'autre de l'arête des oreilles du loup
-G7eSZO.anchorsInfo: Ancrages naturels  des deux côtés, cerclages de gros blocs. 12m de périmètre de chaque côté, max padding

PM_w1ok.name: Napoline
PM_w1ok.description:
In a natural Park of Stelvio 
Need to ask to the natural Park before rig

4qoisLu.name: Burny Burny Snap Snap
4qoisLu.description: Absolutely gorgeous line! But please DO NOT RIG!!! Healthy looking tree snapped just up the hill and would not recommend putting life in the hands of the trees there.
4qoisLu.anchorsInfo: No need to describe anchors as this should not be rigged again.

B21_9rg.name: Horseshoe Highline
B21_9rg.description: The perfect 'first time' highline.  Only about 40 feet (12M) and a beautiful view of the Juniata River.
B21_9rg.anchorsInfo: No bolts, bring a bunch of padding and spansets  (or static rope) to wrap a large boulder on one side and a wedge of rock on the other.

UAI6Rw1.name: Голубая бездна -Golubaya Bezdna-Blue Profundity
UAI6Rw1.description: The line s above a precipice with a beautyful sea scenery. The height is from 60m to 100m (the sea level). Линия над обрывом с красивым видом на море. Высота от уровня моря около 100м, вертикальная часть около 60м. Линия проходит над вершиной сосны, которую можно подвязать к соседним деревьям, чтобы не мешалась.
UAI6Rw1.anchorsInfo: Fix your anchors on the trees, one of them is at the edge of a precipice. Станции расположены на деревьях, одно из которых находится на обрыве. На нем станцию следует разместить на высоте порядка 2м, чтобы избежать перепада высот.
UAI6Rw1.accessInfo: Leave your car at Golubaya Bezdna camp. Автомобиль можно оставить в кемпинге Голубая бездна. От него около 10-15 мин пешком.

jXwMhSl.name: Rosagation (Original)
jXwMhSl.description:
Remarks: To get to the anchors on the northern side you will need to do small down climb, then rappel off the boulder, take a 3m sling for the boulder and rope protection for the backup.
This line is very sheltered from winds coming from the north...
jXwMhSl.anchorsInfo:
Rosagation (Original) - 23m Long, 35m High

Who: Lucky Chance
FA: Lucky Chance
Anchors: South - Main - 3 RBs
                            - Backup - 2 carrots (take bolt plates)
   North - Main - 2 RBs
              - Backup - RB above to the right and large boulder above agian
Linking: Throw a line across or walk/rap down the gully.

r99OazB.name: 2 million views / 2 милиона гледания
r99OazB.description: The highline is located on top of Boyana Waterfall
r99OazB.anchorsInfo:
1 side natural, around a big boulder
other side 3x 12mm bolts with no hangers
r99OazB.accessInfo:
Either by foot from Boyana, around 1 - 1:30min walk
or with car to Momina Skala and then 15-20min down by foot

-4XJ7wu.name: Not Known - Adrian Spot
-4XJ7wu.description: Highline, 2 of 2 on this side of the cliff.
-4XJ7wu.anchorsInfo: 5 bolts on each side, all of them use hangers, 12mm.
-4XJ7wu.accessInfo: Cooper's Knob - Summit Road - Toilets are 700m from Spot on the track direction to Christchurch.

ky6U_hd.description: Big line over the highest drop in the quarry.
ky6U_hd.anchorsInfo: Obvious big trees on either side

IVQjoRe.name: Remarkables, V for Vendetta
IVQjoRe.description:
Opened in January 2017 by the team Ekiwibristes.
The Remarkables Ski Area, Queenstown 9300, New Zealand.
GPS point of the line on topo50 : 1269518,84 5001304,92
Thanks to Cassidy knight for the pictures
IVQjoRe.anchorsInfo:
* Connection :
Throw a tagline from both sides to the gap. From the north anchor, rappel down (5m) to the bottom of last pinnacle and climb it, from this pinnacle you can reach the gap throwing the tagline.
* Anchor 1 :
South side : Surround a really big rock (3m long and large) with spansets. 17m 1t spanset, lot of padding used as protection.
* Anchor 2 :
North side : 1x3m 1T spanset to surround a big rock to do the rappel. 1x4m 1T for the anchor, 3 hangers 12mm, 4 protections (1 for the rappel, 3 for the anchor’s spanset).
IVQjoRe.accessInfo: The line is located at the very obvious notch just South of the telecom tower on the Double Cone ridge. From the remarkable ski field carpark, walk up to lake Alta, then scramble up a little path on the right (when you look the lake) bringing you to the edge. Walk on the edge until the bottom of the telecom tower. Scramble down a scree following the bottom of the cliff (east face) to reach the gap when you can see the highline and Queenstown in the back. The south anchor is located at the top of the pinnacle, you can easily climb it. To reach the north anchor, you have 2 options. First one is to walk to the telecom tower and follow the edge with a succession of rappels, the rappel to get to the highlining anchor is located before the last rappel bringing you to bottom of the last pinnacle of the traverse before the gap. Use a 3m 1T spanset around a big rock to rappel down (10m to reach the bolts). Other option, faster but maybe harder to find if there is no more kern. From the gap, walk back 100m to a cairn, scramble across the rocks straight up then follow a first edge on your right, scramble again 2m using your hands, a last long edge on your left (10m) brings you to the summit. From here walk back 30m to the telecom tower to find the big rock used for the rappel.

a1HXzFY.name: 100m
a1HXzFY.description:
Beautiful line in the climbing crag Flône managed by KBF
Up to date access info: https://rockreservations.cmbel.be/nl/massief/highline-spontin
a1HXzFY.restriction: partial: Only acces for UIAA insured members, like in Belgium KBF or CAB
a1HXzFY.anchorsInfo:
Boomkant:
Main = 17,5m sling (gebruik boom (foto 2 en 3) op de rand als re-direct, cluster van boom kan je als main anker punt gebruiken)
Back-up = 12m sling (ook door re-direct op de rand)

Rotskant op bolts:
Main = 2 delta's, 1m sling als sliding X, shackle, 5m sling om tot net over de rand te komen (foto 4) (padding is nodig op rots hoek)
Back-up = 2 delta's, 1m sling als sliding X, shackle, 6m sling om tot net over de rand te komen
a1HXzFY.accessInfo:
Rappel
De bolts aan de rotskant zijn moeilijk te bereiken. Je moet een rappel installeren met minstens 30m touw. Maak een anker punt aan de bovenkant van de carrière in de uiterste hoek (foto 5). Rappel naar beneden. De bolts zitten aan de achterkant. rappel tot aan de eerst boom achter de plaat. De bolts zitten 3m onder de boom om 5m van de rand. (foto 6) Je touw komt over scherpe rotsranden tijdens de installatie. Neem touwbescheming mee of gebruik een 2de touw als back-up.

nOcTsfy.name: Desfiladeiro das Catedrais
nOcTsfy.description: Via conquistada juntamente com escaladores locais (Ronaldo Franzen, Irivan, Bonga) entre a Torre dos Sinos e o cume do abrolhos, dentro do conjunto Marumbi. Faz parte da segunda via conquistada nesta montanha. A primeira foi realizada em 2009 no mesmo vale.
nOcTsfy.restriction: full: Para realizar este Highline deve-se abrir um e-protocolo (https://www.eprotocolo.pr.gov.br/) e apresentar um projeto que esteja de acordo com as normas instituidas pelo instituto Água e Terra. Além de ser o orgão que fiscaliza e autoriza qualquer projeto dentro das unidades de conservação do estado do Paraná é apenas por este meio que o projeto é viável.
nOcTsfy.anchorsInfo: Todas as ancoragens foram feitas com parabolts removíveis de 12mm FIXIE. Lado B conta com 2 furos para acesso de rapel (traçado azul na foto da ancoragem, sendo que o traçado verde são trechos de escalaminhada) até o plato que foi instalado mais 3 parabolts removíveis. O lado A foi feito 3 furos de 12mm também com parabolts removiveis e mais um backup feito com spacets em boulder.
nOcTsfy.accessInfo: As trilhas para acesso tanto ao cume do abrolhos quanto a Torre dos Sinos são de fácil identificação (Trilha NOROESTE VERMELHA) porém são de nível alto de dificuldade com acesso por vias ferratas e cordas/correntes fixas. O ponto da trilha para acessar o cume da torre dos sinos (22S 709586 E 7183566 S) é fechada e durante todo trajeto é mato fechado e deve ser evitada a abertura por se tratar de uma unidade de conservação.  As trilhas tem duração média sem mochilas de 1hrs30min (Lado A abrolhos) e 3hrs (Lado B torre dos sinos). Com peso pode aumentar consideravelmente o tempo da trilha pelo fato da inclinação.

GKTipAV.name: O Jah Jah
GKTipAV.description: Via de fácil acesso, e fácil montagem, bem exposta devido a sua altura, e também por ser ao lado da Cachoeira, que quando está em tempos te chuvas, pode se molhar na linha com a "nuvem" que se forma...
GKTipAV.anchorsInfo: Via com 3 Pontos de ancoragem em cada Lado, onde não costumamos deixar as chapas no local, somente os bolts 3/8' estão por lá!

tZg3b-5.name: Skjer med at franskmenn kan gå på line
tZg3b-5.description: Sweet spot with quite the exposure. Be prepared for some wind and rain, you're in Lofoten.
tZg3b-5.anchorsInfo:
North anchor: Holes for 3 'Petzl coeur puls M12' bolts after scrambling/climbing up from the gully underneath the line.
South anchor: solid rock spike/flake on the backside of the platform. Used a 30m static rope and a small frame to get the line over the edge. Double the rope into two 14m loops and tie a 'bfk' for the anchor. Prepare to pad the spike, 1.5m
tZg3b-5.accessInfo: Hike straight up the gully from the bouldering spot down by the road. Rather steep and almost 400m of elevation.

ZOFCsyl.name: Saami
ZOFCsyl.description: First highline in Khibiny mountans bolted in 2017 and rigged in 2018. Nice 40m line in a canyon of the noname col.
ZOFCsyl.anchorsInfo: Four 12mm bolts both side. Bring hangers and a shovel in winter season.
ZOFCsyl.accessInfo: From the parking near ski resort take a ski lift to the top of mount Kukisvumchorr. Then have a ride on a ridge toward the spot. When get to the large col take off your skis and go ahead by foot for 40 min aprox. The way back you'll have fun freeriding a big powder valley. Don't forget avalanch stuff.

ctIk3e2.description: Instead of a bridge. Line goes from the main massif to a tooth with a cross. Insanely beautiful and unique. In summer bridges are on on this place, so the only possibility to rig is offseason.
ctIk3e2.anchorsInfo: On the tooth there are a lot of huge bolts of the bridge. Just connect some of them with long slings. On the other side the same stuff but also 3 wedge 12mm bolts without hangers.
ctIk3e2.accessInfo: From the parking near the lift 20min walk to the top of Ai-Petri. Rappel down for 50m from the anchor, than go to the touth and climb the biggest crack you see. There are some old bolts on the way for belaying. Climbing isn't hard, maybe 5c or less. If you're not sure bring some cams number 3 or 4. Don't forget quickdraws.

4eY6sFV.name: Harwoods Hole – Pits, nits and 10mn too late
4eY6sFV.description:
Opened in 2012 by Pedro Pimentel.
Topo by a the french highlining team : The Ekiwibrists.
Harwoods Hole, Golden Bay, New Zealand.
4eY6sFV.anchorsInfo:
* Connection :
A drone is the easiest way ! If not, throw a tagline from each anchor to the bottom platform (further point you can reach at the end of the track).
* Anchor 1 :
South side (left anchor facing the hole): 2 trees for the master point (main and back up), you can use an extra tree in the back as general back up. 1x4m + 1x2m 1T spanset.
* Anchor 2 :
North side (right anchor facing the hole): 5 expansion bolts with hangers, bring quick links.
4eY6sFV.accessInfo: Walk to Harwoods Hole from the carpark (45mn). For the left anchor (facing the hole) go back 100ish meters on the main trail and find your way through the slopes to a small terrace right next to some lapiaz (5-10mn) For the right anchor (tricky one), use the trail that goes up to the viewpoint (10mn) and then get lost in the awfully sharpened lapiaz and forest until you reach the hole, slightly higher than the other anchor (the best way is probably to stay on the edge and not go down too fast). Rappel down 20m from a tree. Take protection for you rope to rappel down, the rock is really sharp.

rLHLT52.name: Desculpa R7
rLHLT52.description:
Use treepro and ALWAYS stay clipped. An awsome line with a 55m direct exposure and 100 meter of distance
Conquered By: Vitor Luz, Ronaldinho, Vitor Martins, Gustavo Féo, Gabriel Bruno.
Our instagrams: @vt_luz, @ronaldinho_ras_r7, @mmartinsvitor, @...
rLHLT52.anchorsInfo: Simple natural anchors on both sides

cdORRKJ.name: Babakamp
cdORRKJ.description: Possibly the most scenic line in Turkey. Incredible sunset view. 4-5 lines from 40m-100m are possible
cdORRKJ.restriction: partial: Do not rig this line without contacting Babakamp. It's on the paragliding route and paragliders need to be informed.
cdORRKJ.anchorsInfo: North side has 3 expansion bolts (no hanger) and a big tree. Bolts are visible and close to the edge.  South side is more complicated but has all-natural anchors. See the images. No drone is needed but a tag rope and some 30 mins work.
cdORRKJ.accessInfo: It's inside the land of Babakamp. Contact them.

OZdnh4d.name: Ecos do Horizonte
OZdnh4d.anchorsInfo:
Lado A: 3 bolts.
Lado B: Natural, são dois blocos grandes de rocha, uma para a principal e a de trás para back up. Alongar a ancoragem e colocar bastante anti abrasivo.
OZdnh4d.accessInfo: Tem uma escaladinha para acessar a ancoragem B, mas pode ser feita sem equipamentos de escalada.

TrDCDb3.name: Keczobokiem
TrDCDb3.description: Beautiful line, with nice exposure. Line have a big potential to epic photos.
TrDCDb3.anchorsInfo:
Side #1 "Edge": There are a few options to rigg. I rigged first time to small rock to main and big rock in the back to backup. You can also made a backup to the climbing rings, but if you have a enough slings, please dont do this :). I used there 3 slings: 5m I opened and put on big rock to a backup and i extended this with 2m sling. To main I used only 2,5m sling and this length is perfect.

Side #2: Here are 4 holes for removable bolts, so you have to have them. Please dont leave bolts permanently ;). You can use 2 for main and 2 for backup or do backup to the tree, but then you must have longer slings. For this anchor I used 2x2,5m slings for main and 2,5m and 1m slings for backup. In holes are the plugs, if you remove it to build anchor, please take it back when you derigg all.
TrDCDb3.accessInfo:
To #1 side you can go from back/
To #2 side you can go from upper forest or from down path and little bit climbing in the end, but everyone can do it. To do anchors you need slip down on the rope, anchor to rope can be pine tree above.

OP-fFkB.description:
In this spot we have set up bolts for 1 line, 4 bolts on each side. there is a magnificent exposure view to the Arpi village and the Aghavnadzor valley and also there is opurtunity to rig longer or shorter lines, just need to be bolted. 
both side anchors are easy accessible (easy hiking) only on the west side rope needed to be safe while establishing the anchor.
Keep in mind that this region is very hot during summer time (+40C degree  or more), best seasons are from end of april to start of june or end of september to start of november. 
If there is some more details or help needed you can contact us, we will be happy to help or have a highline session together --> https://www.facebook.com/slackliners.of.armenia
OP-fFkB.anchorsInfo:
12mm stainless steel glue in bolts, Hangers and nuts needed.
4 bolts on each side.
OP-fFkB.accessInfo: The anchor side next to the road is very easy accessible, but for the opposite side you need to walk around the gorge as there is no another safe way to cross it.

-p7cMfS.name: Si tu te vas
-p7cMfS.description: You're in a natural parc, slackline is not allowed. Don't rig the line for long periods of time as you could get in trouble. Tree on one side, two boulders on the other (around 20m of rope needed). From Benasque go up to Refugio de Coronas and fol...
-p7cMfS.anchorsInfo: Tree on one side, two boulders on the other (around 20m of rope needed).
-p7cMfS.accessInfo: From Benasque go up to Refugio de Coronas and follow the GR11 up to Ibón Bajo de Vallibierna.

O5UvpZf.name: Ryujin Suspension Bridge Highline 350m
O5UvpZf.restriction: full: Permission is required for use. For inquiries, please contact us on our Instagram account @bodyproducts.ibaraki.

1r4phy8.name: Dobra kenja
1r4phy8.description: Easy to reach freestyle line. A bit less exposed maybe.
1r4phy8.anchorsInfo:
Tension side(south): From backside tree 11.5m spanset to tree for height. 
Static side: 2m spanset on good tree on the edge. Use some rope to tie yourself.

-p1K8IX.name: Arena
-p1K8IX.description:
the line is located over Arena sector of the Park Etgarim climbing crag.
there is a big boulder below the middle of the line
-p1K8IX.restriction: partial: requires coordination with the park management
-p1K8IX.anchorsInfo: chemical bolts, the rock is sandstone so it needs proper padding
-p1K8IX.accessInfo:
static anchor requires climbing,
dynamic (northest one) can be access by walking around through the canyon

f4u1Mtd.name: Not Known
f4u1Mtd.description: Highline, 1 of 2 on this side of the cliff.
f4u1Mtd.anchorsInfo: 5 bolts on one side, 3 specially for main anchor. 2 extra bolts from a climbing route/toprope to be used as backup. 2 bolts and natural anchor on the other side. Please equalize all 3 (2 bolts + natural anchor) as natural anchor is a small rock.
f4u1Mtd.accessInfo: Cooper's Knob - Summit Road - Toilets are 700m from Spot on the track direction to Christchurch.

BzFSKSG.description: La grande sœur de la 60. Ligne plein gaz en haut de la falaise du Parmelan, vue imprenable sur le lac. Encore plus magique avec une mer de nuage (automne ou hiver).
BzFSKSG.anchorsInfo:
Côté refuge : 3 spits de 12. Accès possible (sketchy)  sans rappel, possibilité de poser un rappel avec un arbuste derrière.

Chill side : des arbres permettent de faire un rappel pour accéder à l'ancrage. 3 spits de 12. Ancrage commun avec la 60m.
BzFSKSG.accessInfo: Monter au refuge du Parmelan depuis le parking de Villaz ou de l’Anglettaz, puis suivre les crêtes vers le col du Pertuis. Le chemin descend dans le lapiaz puis remonte sur la crête, la ligne se trouve 50 m après que le chemin ait retrouvé la crête.

9b2Www3.name: Blackhawk Derribado
9b2Www3.description: Highline in la Cabrera, 162m line. "Blackhawk  Derribado" the highest line in la Cabrera for the moment and most exposed
9b2Www3.anchorsInfo:
Static side (NE): Three points, bolted for MMS 8mm screws. Rig with a 3-4m spanset. Little pretection needed
Tensioning side (SW): 5 points bolted for 8mm MMS screws on the wall 5 meters behind the edge due to bad rock quality. 2 holes in one rock and 3 on the other one, quite low in the wall. You will need a good amount of spansets and rope, to equalize everything. Protection needed for the edge and a part of the spansets, up there there's a little trunk to use as a "frame".
9b2Www3.accessInfo: The access to the anchors it's a bit tricky, you have to trust the location given on the static side, quite difficult to find, and with the last part a bit exposed, going around some bushes. Side SW easier

2xJaeve.restriction: partial: this line has to be registered with the BAZL.
2xJaeve.anchorsInfo:
Near side in the east: one tree as main load anchor two trees for a hang frame / back up.
Far side in the west: on tree with a rope for back up to prevent the tree from falling over to a tree on the other side.
2xJaeve.accessInfo: you get to the far side anchor by folowing the ridge, there is a little paht.

mG5E0AI.restriction: none: Slackline it's forbidden it's a natural Park

_R7r68Z.name: Ready at Dawn
_R7r68Z.description: Relatively easy rig. Beware of the brambles in event of leash fall. No fall zone is further out than expected. Anchor tree to tree. Great highline for beginners: not too exposed.

VavHT0-.name: Perłowa Wojcieszowianka
VavHT0-.description: Oba dwa stanowiska to drzewa, z jednej strony zawiesie 1.5m. z drugiej strony trzeba mieć około 10.5m zawiesi + frame. Auto parkujemy na parkingu przy drodze(nigdy nie było problemu że tam stoimy) przechodzimy mały strumyk i idziemy scieżką aż do ...
VavHT0-.anchorsInfo: oba dwa stanowiska to drzewa, z jednej strony zawiesie 1.5m. z drugiej strony trzeba mieć około 10.5m zawiesi + frame.
VavHT0-.accessInfo: auto parkujemy na parkingu przy drodze(nigdy nie było problemu że tam stoimy) przechodzimy mały strumyk i idziemy scieżką aż do kamieniołomu

4eJSU5P.name: Bloody Frog highline
4eJSU5P.description: Short line in nice climbng area near Krakow
4eJSU5P.anchorsInfo: anchors: Only one anchor is bolting. One side: huge rock tower (10m spansets)

u1UBIGV.name: Marry me to the sky
u1UBIGV.anchorsInfo: Solid bolts on each side, to reach far side you have to repel down 3m (the 2 bolts on top are not 100% trustworthy, better back them up on a tree for repelling)
u1UBIGV.accessInfo: Easy access by foot, 5min from parking space.

1ZlxzrN.name: Double YA
1ZlxzrN.description: Nice view line with easy start.
1ZlxzrN.anchorsInfo: 4 steel 12mm glue anchors without hangers on each side.
1ZlxzrN.accessInfo: Easy access to start point. Access to far anchor point with rope from top.

p1VL-T6.name: Long highline in Niederhorn
p1VL-T6.restriction: partial: Not so good relation with the restaurant. Please take contact with the local association (SlackAttack Bern/Thun)
p1VL-T6.anchorsInfo:
Anchor Start point
Need to rappel

Anchor far side
Under the deltaplane start. 
Need to rappel

xzT4j_k.description: An unsuccessful rig by Hoel Yugg And Dylan Bennett
xzT4j_k.anchorsInfo: Large natural anchors, requires many spansets or much rope!
xzT4j_k.accessInfo: Wye Creek Car Park, left at the forks towards North Wye and then take a left 15 minutes up track to aim along the true ridge of South end of Remarks, hit the corner and aim low to wrap around the west face. Allow 2-3 hours approach, Steep. HARD approach

O41DGJV.name: O Jezu!
O41DGJV.description: Access is really easy, car leave under the highline, road to the top is also easy. You should find a road (motocross road) to up no dont need to climb there.
O41DGJV.anchorsInfo: One anchor is a tree, big tree, back-up also tree 5 meters behind the first tree. Second anchor is also tree, back-up again tree 2-3 meters behind main anchor
O41DGJV.accessInfo: Really easy access. You dont need to climb there

Ysr3drF.name: Vesela kokoška
Ysr3drF.anchorsInfo: East side: 2 trees, use 4m sling. West side: tree, 1m sling (1,5m high on tree), but needs to be backed up with atleast 15m rope.

FSgRLon.name: Ursa Minor
FSgRLon.description: Holcomb Valley Pinnacles is a great sport climbing crag near Big Bear lake. This is the smallest line at Holcomb.  It's not too high, but the view is worth it.
FSgRLon.anchorsInfo: The Eastern pinnacle (Sky Slab) has bolted anchors.  Two glue in, threaded bolts are your main anchor. Bring your own bolt-hangers and nuts.  You can use smaller climbers bolts further back as a backup.  The Western anchor pinnacle (Tombstone Pit) is a scorpion-wrap around the top of the pinnacle with a large spanset.
FSgRLon.accessInfo: The highline is in the Central Pinnacles area of Holcomb valley. The eastern anchor can be accessed with an easy scramble to the top.  The western pinnacle requires climbing one of the sport routes to the top, such as "Shoot At Will" (5.8).

OJ-Xid5.name: Rubbernecker
OJ-Xid5.description: The first line bolted in the area. 55m coming from the tower and going across the bowl to a nice ledge.
OJ-Xid5.anchorsInfo: Three 1/2 inch stainless steel bolts and hangers for each anchor. Bring quick links to feed your rope through.
OJ-Xid5.accessInfo: As stated in the area description, approach the base camp tree and then either jump the gap or lower yourself off the tree in order to get to the obvious tower with the large detached block on top of it. One bolt was strategically placed to the right of the large block in order to allow you to clip into it as you scooch around and get to the other two. The block is stable and will not go anywhere if you sit or stand on it but we  avoided throwing bolts in it in an attempt to conceal the anchor a bit. In order to approach the other anchor, scramble down the hill following the lightly trafficked trail on the north side of the bowl. The best way to tag the line we have found is to rappel off the static side anchor on west side of the bowl and walk the rope up to the bottom of the bowl where a friend can walk it the rest of the way up as you hike back to the static side anchor.

-S4a11w.name: Czarna dziura
-S4a11w.description:
#1 anchor
2x 12mm hangers (2m sling is enough) + backup on tree ~4m higher
; #2 anchor
tree (1,5m sling)
-S4a11w.anchorsInfo:
#1 anchor
2x 12mm hangers (2m sling is enough) + backup on tree ~4m higher
; #2 anchor
tree (1,5m sling)

LyygWih.restriction: full: Contact Slackline Freiburg for information

YkvtiuH.name: 3 ans plus tard
YkvtiuH.description: La grande sœur de "plus t'es fort, plus t'es con", plus gazeuse, plus grande, plus belle et toujours au-dessus du lac d'Annecy.
YkvtiuH.restriction:
partial: Pas besoin de NOTAM pour cette ligne, mais merci d'envoyer un message à la liste de mail, au moins 2 jours avant l'installation de la ligne.

Ce mail devra contenir :
- La date d'installation et de désinstallation de la ligne 
- La commune d'installation : Veyrier-du-Lac
- La longueur de la ligne : 90m
- L'Altitude de la ligne : 1145m
- Les coordonnées des ancrages :
Ancrage Nord : 45°53'26''N 6°11'22''E
Ancrage Sud : 45°53'23''N 6°11'22''E
- Le contact d'une personne du groupe installateur.
YkvtiuH.anchorsInfo:
Côté Annecy : 3 spits dans la falaise (cf photo).
Côté Menthon : 3 Spits de l'autre côté de l'arête par rapport à la ligne.
YkvtiuH.accessInfo: 30 mn - Depuis le parking du col des contrebandiers, suivre le chemin qui monte à l'ancien téléphérique. Quand on découvre la vue sur le lac, suivre une sente qui part tout droit et qui descend légèrement.

UgJBZnQ.description: from the top of Highway to hell to Thunder Blaster

Ol8B7gM.name: Third base
Ol8B7gM.description: Cleaner line than first base. Typically the line I put up for beginners.

R3rSrV8.name: Watch Me Wallaby Slack Jack
R3rSrV8.description: A huge step up from the lines below it in length and exposure.
R3rSrV8.anchorsInfo:
West side: 3 bolts with a bolt and a boulder for backup further back, these need a 40m rope to connect.
East side: 2 boulders and two trees needed a long static rope to connect. You can use a small boulder near the edge as an aframe and abseil point.
R3rSrV8.accessInfo: Climb a short sport route on the east side.

fT_QNk7.name: Outlook Gully
fT_QNk7.description: Over the gully, above the tree's..
fT_QNk7.anchorsInfo: Tree's on one side with 5m extension, wrap the pinnacle on the other side..
fT_QNk7.accessInfo:
1) Head left from the outlook over a fence and walk down the gully edge until level with the pinnacle, rig from the tree's and extend the anchor to the edge..
2) Head down through the gully until you reach the base of the the pinnacle, climb up the right hand side and you can get on top to set the anchor..

bfDys1a.name: 170 x Okol + Klopca
bfDys1a.anchorsInfo:
Static side: 3x 12mm expansion bolts and 1x10mm for height. 
Tensioning side: Trees, 9m spanset, A-frame is recommended
bfDys1a.accessInfo:
Static side: ~55m of rappel
Tensioning side: No problem :)

3Q2S0xF.name: The summit line
3Q2S0xF.description: Between Tour de Mayen and Tour d'Ai, installed 2022 and 2023
3Q2S0xF.anchorsInfo: Tour de Mayen, 4x12mm and A-frame 2m, Tour d'Ai 4x12mm, short slings needed only
3Q2S0xF.accessInfo: Separate signalisation line needed, aks authorisation at comune Leysin and Police

KP4RV9R.name: The Unnatural Bridge
KP4RV9R.anchorsInfo: On tension side, wrap live tree and extend around dead tree as a "redirect" to help keep webbing/anchor material off the rocks. Around 70 meters of static rope and 2 short spannsets to create a redundant anchor. On static side, wrap the thick tree near the edge of cliff.

wSRWB2K.description: Huge abandoned stone mine with a lake in the center. Stunningly beautiful spot with opportunities for lots of lines.
wSRWB2K.anchorsInfo:
Solid single-tree anchor on the west side, it is best to tension from here. The tree can be nicely wrapped with 2m spansets. On the east side anchor, small trees and bushes can be equalized to a masterpoint and the big tree can be used as an A-frame to hold the line up (see photos).
Bring at least 50 m static rope and a few spansets to extend the anchors!
The connection is best made with a throwline/throwbag from the east side anchor.
wSRWB2K.accessInfo: There are several parking spots at the street 2288 (pay attention to the signs, you can`t park everywhere!). From there, walk straight up the hill through the forest. The whole area is pretty difficult to navigate around, dense vegatation and no easy terrain, but once you find the correct routes it will be OK.

yGsQzsn.name: El cristo
yGsQzsn.description: Dificult and dangerous to finde cara del inca anchor, dificult to find the other anchor (Main side) Both anchors 3 bolts

JeE5aYD.name: Carne Quemada
JeE5aYD.anchorsInfo:
3 holds for MMS each side

Eastern bolts are badly wiggling and are unusable as of Nov ‘25

L5dXM-C.name: Camino a Xibalbá
L5dXM-C.description: Highline in the eco-touristic park "El Arcotete", it has also a cave for climbing
L5dXM-C.anchorsInfo: Natural anchors you will need many pieces of rope, slings shackles and carabineers to make the anchors in one side there is a huge tree but in the otrer side you will have to make an extension and conect it to two trees like 6 meter back of the cliff and remember to bring alot of padding
L5dXM-C.accessInfo: Super easy, you can approach walking to both anchors you just have to be secured

loUF0jw.description: One anchor you have to rappel few meters. The are 2 bolts for the rappel that are easy to see
loUF0jw.anchorsInfo: One anchor you have to rappel few meters. The are 2 bolts for the rappel that are easy to see.

IuQ4R8H.name: Prva perma lajna
IuQ4R8H.description: First permanent line in Slovenia. Otherwise also quite nice.
IuQ4R8H.anchorsInfo:
Tension side: 3x 12mm expansion bolts. Use 2m sling. 
Far side: tree, 1m sling, back it up.
IuQ4R8H.accessInfo: Far side: you go past the climbing area  on road for about 50m and then left at small wall. Then up the hill. You come from north side above wall which is not main climbing one.

4Rr67C4.name: Tzatziki
4Rr67C4.description: One of the most beautiful highlines out there ;-) The point of view can almost be reached by car. From there 5min hiking to the close anchor point where we bolted in stainless M12. 4 bolts between 1m and 3m from the cliff. Take a bit of padding for the edge. The far anchor point is a bit more of a hike. Take good shoes. It's 20 min hiking. A tree 15m behind the cliff can be taken as anchor point. No bolts as the rock is shit on this side. The line is 30m off-level! Btw.: The beach can not be reached by foot. 80m repelling down on loose rocks to get to the beach - last 20m overhanging! So be careful if you plan to go down there or take a tourist ship :-p

1qLvPa2.name: Arenalina
1qLvPa2.anchorsInfo: 4 bolts main side, long anchor. 3 bolts far side, short and easy anchor.
1qLvPa2.accessInfo: 2 hours aprouch, way up the crist, passing to the left of it. Main side access is the first clear dry culoir to the left, 200mts passing the crist, then walk uphill, after a bit of scrambling, anchor is below the big boulder at the edge befor the big walls.. to the far side, walk to the end of the crag, before reaching the big stones and then turn right, little srambling and walking to a big and clear white boulder buried in the sand.

JCXSoYl.anchorsInfo: 3 SS glue-ins on each side. The closest anchor is right under the anchor of "2o. molar". Watch out for loose rock and holds in this area!

dltl789.description: Over a disused but still registered as active quarry. May start working again at some point

7JzZ4Km.name: ⴰⵉⵍⵢⴷ ⴰⵀⵔⴷⴰ  Ailyd Ahrda

West side by the dry waterfall before the climbing routes

CGFtaUj.anchorsInfo:
Static side: 2x 12mm and 3x 10mm expansion bolts. Rappel for 10m is needed. 
Tension side: 2x 12mm bolts and small pine tree. Make a master point from both points and line go next to the tree on the edge. Established beta: 2,5m spanset on 2x 12mm bolts + 5m spanset to master point. Then from pine tree 8m spanset to master point. From master point to edge 2m spanset.

Bouldering pads ideal edge protection on both sides.
3aNAn_m.accessInfo: Walk up from the slate museum car park

S34Nye9.accessInfo: Really easy access. You dont need to climb there

xsoolZR.anchorsInfo:
Tension side(tower): 3x 12mm expansion bolts; 10m bfk and 4m spanset, stabilize the master point with 5m rope to tree.
Static side: 2x 12mm expansion bolts; 2m spanset, 3,5m spanset wrapped on trees for backup.
xsoolZR.accessInfo: You can reach static side by either 15m rappel or walking on steep hill from the side.

_NfoUYb.name: SahaK&A
_NfoUYb.description: nice little line outside the touristic part of the gorge.
_NfoUYb.anchorsInfo: 2x 4  10mm Bolts without hangers, 1,5m + ?2m? spanset, some protection
_NfoUYb.accessInfo: more or less visible from the road.

dRLMFQl.description: Takes some time to tag without a drone. Easy access on both sides. One side has 3x 10mm bolts and 2x 12mm, the other 3x 12mm. No plates on both sides, no nuts on the side with 10mm and 12mm bolts
dRLMFQl.anchorsInfo: Easy access on both sides. One side has 3x 10mm bolts and 2x 12mm, the other 3x 12mm. No plates on both sides, no nuts on the side with 10mm and 12mm bolts

mpXDo-i.name: Dreaming of Bear Creek
mpXDo-i.description: Beautiful naturally protected 48m(160') line above Tomboy road. The beautiful gully is easy to spot, but not exactly easy to get to.
mpXDo-i.anchorsInfo: On the approach side you have to rig over a big branch. I recommend using a 100' piece of static rope in a basket formation with a master point. But make your own call, figuring out the rigging is half the fun.
mpXDo-i.accessInfo: Walk up tomboy road and look for a carian on the left. Head up into the field to the nearest big pine tree. Look for more cairns heading up and right on a visible animal trail. Follow the trail/cairns up to the base of the cliff band and head lookers left up through the cliff band to the large gully with pine trees on each side. Use the most prominent trees for DOBC

hHo-nYl.description: Shortest line rigged in the quarry
hHo-nYl.anchorsInfo: Lots of different sized trees. Riggers choice
hHo-nYl.accessInfo: Park at the end of Waterslack Road. Cross the train track and follow a path up the right hand side. Turn left up a vague path when it  presents itself and follow it to the quarry edge. Follow the edge into the north west corner.

W8ls-Se.name: is a possibility, not rigged yet

3QjnSbK.name: We don't give a ship
3QjnSbK.description:
Feel the breeze and set sail. Tree's on both sides with 5-10m extension. 1) Follow the path and climb up through the woods..
2) Beat a path through the woods until you find the outlook area
3QjnSbK.anchorsInfo: Tree's on both sides with 5-10m extension..
3QjnSbK.accessInfo:
1) Follow the path and climb up through the woods..
2) Beat a path through the woods until you find the outlook area..

kGXoS8S.name: Vegan Cheese
kGXoS8S.anchorsInfo: Both anchors has plattforms to work on trees
kGXoS8S.accessInfo: San Juan del Obispo , 5km away from Antigua Guatemala

yikLZmn.name: Smoking Weed With The Bees
yikLZmn.description:
Who/When: Chris Wallace, Chiko Shah, Pyn Chin, French Dude, March 2017

FA: Chris Wallace, March 2017
yikLZmn.anchorsInfo:
Stalactite  – Main – Girth hitch the pillar on top of the stalactite, 3-4m spanset

– Backup – Same bomber pillar.

Cave Side – Main – Sling 2 pillars with rope or spanset, (3m spanset)

– Backup – Sling 1-2 more pillars (2m rope for close, 8m rope for far pillar)

Remarks: The masterpoint on the cave side must be extended far beyond just slinging the pillars, save a 2-3m spanset for this as it must rub on the rock every time someone enters/exits/whippers on the line. Its possible to rig the line more level by moving the cave anchor about 10m higher onto a small stubby stalactite (7a climb) and moving the melting wall anchor up another few meters, in this case, the easiest anchor to access becomes the melting wall (big stalactite) side. If rigged from the cave, the easiest access to the line is via the 6b+ climb. It can be worth fixing a static line to the bolts on this and using it as a via ferrata. It is an unpopular climb and does not see much traffic, however, be mindful of climbers wishing to climb there and don’t leave it too long.

Access: Probably one of the easiest/fastest lines in tonsai to access, perfect for early morning or cruisy afternoon practice. It takes about 3min to walk from the beach to the base of the climbs.

Cave Side: Walk to fire wall (where Burnt Offerings is) and climb the hard 6b+ (hard start, easy middle, hard end) and after clipping the anchors, traverse 5m right on easy but exposed climbing into the cave.

Stalactite Side: Walk further on the track through the jungle to melting wall and climb the slick 6a or 6b (both 2 pitches). From the anchors traverse left into the dirty slippery underneath of the stalactite with possible thread protection and mantle up onto the top. Fix a rope to rap/jug on.

S7IM2km.name: The Oncoming storm
S7IM2km.description: Have fun with the tag line

is860OR.name: Cuatro Picos
is860OR.description:
A beautiful line with nice exposure. Easy access on the line on one side - good for beginners. Established in the beginning of 2015.

For more information ask on "Slackline Jaen" or "Slackline (Granada)" fb groups.
is860OR.anchorsInfo:
- The anchors are the bolted metal plates on both sides.
- !!! You need 4 plates for one of the sides (on the photo - right) and a wrench !!! (17 or 19, not sure). The bolts are permanent, but the plates are taken off after every session.

sAJ1Sdt.name: Libertad
sAJ1Sdt.description: Dificult to find, Main side 3 bolts, secondary side natural

bDyzr4i.name: Kazikli Ali
bDyzr4i.description: Above the climbing spot with a nice view. Contact to aladaglar camping.
bDyzr4i.anchorsInfo: 4x expansion bolts 12mm no hanger each side.

trXJbhT.name: La pasarela
trXJbhT.description: In one hand, surround a  rock and the other a tree
trXJbhT.anchorsInfo: In one hand, surround a  rock and the other a tree

O6ffnUA.description: Trees really far back from both edges. Needs lots of extension
O6ffnUA.anchorsInfo: Trees really far back from both edges. Needs lots of extension.

YL5VAh0.name: Sure Does
YL5VAh0.description: Moderately short highline with views of the river and beautiful granite formations.
YL5VAh0.anchorsInfo: North ancor is 3 bolts on a fairly wll protected ledge. A long extension is recommended to minimize rubbing on the rock in front of the anchor while rigging. The south ancor is a large boulder wrap, on top of the south precipice there are 3 boulders that are slightly disconnected, the furthest south boulder is connected to the actual cliff beneath your feet, wrapping all 3 required 28 feet of spanset.

c_7UeJJ.name: La Croix et la Bannière
c_7UeJJ.description: Mid-waterline, fully bolted on both sides
c_7UeJJ.anchorsInfo: 3x10mm bolts on each side, hangers removed, not much pading needed
c_7UeJJ.accessInfo: Walk the path, it's easy. Bolts are at the bottom of the rock, to be accessible standing on the grass. Far side needs a small downclimb

66s8reC.name: Mowgli
66s8reC.description: Nice line go over a small gap in the quarry. Goes over water, however there is spike directly underneath the line almost in the middle making it a spicy water midline.
66s8reC.anchorsInfo: 2 trees/ trunks either side. One side about a 2m extension, other side is between a 5-8m extension.
66s8reC.accessInfo: you can walk to the top of the anchors either side, however to link the line you need to connect them in the middle, so a boat/ a swimmer is needed.

Sm0uWQD.name: Dwieście pięćdziesiątka
Sm0uWQD.description: This is a private area. If you have questions, ask Urban Highline Festival.

njkZ_OE.name: Joe Flaco y el Sr. Sean Penn
njkZ_OE.description: 55m highline in La Muralla, in the State of Querétaro.
njkZ_OE.anchorsInfo: 3 SS bolts with hangers each side. Maillons or carabiners are needed. At least 30 m static rope for the static side and 15m of rope for the near side. We recommend to attach some static rope from the masterpoint to 1 or 2 of the bolts of "Espejo autoexistente" (65m) to prevent the master point from falling to the side (see pictures)

nrL8mS2.name: Maurro's Shady Side
nrL8mS2.description: Line spanning the gap between climbing cliffs at Shady Side.
nrL8mS2.restriction: partial: Forest Service Will Only Allow Lines to Stay Up for 24 hrs.
nrL8mS2.anchorsInfo: 3 Glue In Monster Crux Bolts Both Sides
nrL8mS2.accessInfo: Follow hiking trail to Shady Side

SJHcETB.name: Sonoran Beauty (A Gift of I'itoi)
SJHcETB.description:
~95' highline over a cascading waterfall in a desert oasis? Yes, please.
For best experiences, walk when there's water flowing (snowmelt near New Years seem to never disappoint)!

FA: James Xu (12/22/14)
FFS: James Xu (1/03/15)
SJHcETB.anchorsInfo:
4 bolts on each side. You will need 12' spansets for each anchor to equalize all of them.
Bring padding (a backpack will do) for both sides.
SJHcETB.accessInfo: To walk webbing over to the south side will involve a short Class 4 scramble over a small butte. Careful about loose rock and cacti.

QOPG9cV.restriction: full: Slackline it's forbidden it's a natural Park
