U0omm3-.name: Billy Line
U0omm3-.description: Pleasant place to spend the day, practice slackline, next to an abandoned quarry that flooded. Very easy to reach, the vehicle is very close.

FqIhx73.description:
Ligne iconique des Dents de Lanfon entre la dent sud et la petite molaire. 

Bien gazeuse comme on les aime. 

Elle surplombe le lac d'Annecy et possède une vue splendide sur le Mont-Blanc et le reste des Alpes .
FqIhx73.anchorsInfo:
Chill-side : au moins 12m d'élingue ou corde de 30 m pour BFK. Prévoir de la protection (+ ou – 4 en fonction de la construction de l'ancrage).

Far-side : au moins 12m d'élingue ou corde de 30 m pour BFK. Prévoir 3 protections.

!--! Amener les plaquettes de 12mm !--!
FqIhx73.accessInfo:
Rejoindre le col des Frêtes depuis Planfait ou Villards dessus, puis redescendre légèrement pour rejoindre un câble qui rejoint l'arête et l'itinéraire classique de la traversée des Dents. Arrivé au col, continuer sur la droite en suivant le chemin et s'arrêter sur la première dent.

Pour le Far-side, il faut grimper 3 longueurs en 4+ max.

Voici le lien du topo :

https://www.jeremyjanody.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/Les_Dents_de_Lanfon-Arete_Ouest_du_Pilier_Sud.pdf

sUUiyS5.name: Sveri - Cavemen
sUUiyS5.description: Nice highline in Sveri village near Chiatura city
sUUiyS5.anchorsInfo:
Both anchors are accessible with Via Ferrata. They are in 2-3 meters away from ferrata main cable.
One anchor is in cave on the wall - 4 bolts.
Second anchor - 4 bolts near really small oak tree

ObK_3JM.name: Fucking Big One
ObK_3JM.description:
never installed, but sure possible
the rock is not the best, we have to test it if we can use regular bolts,
an other option would be natural anchors or a dead man anchor,
please check out the sport first

EdY5HTU.name: Šrekas
EdY5HTU.description: Simple highline with short no fall zones as the slopes are quite steep there.  Goes over a small river and mostly swampy area around it - thus the name...
EdY5HTU.anchorsInfo: Far side is best to be approached around because of steepness and an overhang at the end.  Advisable to use a tagline to get the line up there. Tensioning anchor is ~2m above the ground as the anchors trees are not at the equal height.
EdY5HTU.accessInfo: From south side of the ravine

s-5GKPu.name: Sueños Compartidos
s-5GKPu.description: Bella cinta frente a una cascada con las barrancas de Cuentepec de fondo
s-5GKPu.anchorsInfo: Anclaje naturales desde arboles, requiere usar mucha cuerda si se busca ser redundante hacia varios puntos de árboles.
s-5GKPu.accessInfo: Se debe cuidar el espacio y solicitar acceso a la comunidad del pueblo

WltbgMi.name: To zase jooo...
WltbgMi.description: Perfect line for restday at Pedra Longa with beatiful exposition and fast access.
WltbgMi.anchorsInfo:
Small tower (W side) - spansets (15 m) girth-hitched around the smaller top of the tower and directed by rocks on main top (you can use some more spansets for backup)
Big tower (E side) - 3x 10mm bolts (without hangers)
WltbgMi.accessInfo:
Small tower - short easy climb from W, but unprotected and with lots of loose rock; NO anchor on top, bring some friends and nuts
Big tower - follow descent route from Pedra Longa, at first anchor turn right and follow traverse protected by slings and bolts

3w27s47.name: Viafucked
3w27s47.description: Near the abandoned viaduct, in the extraction zone..
3w27s47.accessInfo: Walk through the field and approach from the top, if you are in the bottom use the stairs to reach the field..

jp5gH8u.name: Joel's Gap
jp5gH8u.description: Highline. Bolts and natural anchor on one side. Rock is very Sharp and sketcky. Highline has to be super tight as there is a 6m cliff you are walking on. Bolted by Ben Gingold, 1st send Ben Gingold. Protect the edge of the CLIFF lots. Use high tension. If low Tension you should extend the anchors 6m and line become shorter than 50.
jp5gH8u.anchorsInfo: Natural on both side. You need 10/15m Spanset/Ropes on one side and at least 2 3m ones in other side. Protect your gear, rock is soft and sharp.
jp5gH8u.accessInfo: Walk up Castle Hill Peak (4 Hours), then go down for another 30minutes to the GAP. http://www.doc.govt.nz/parks-and-recreation/places-to-go/canterbury/places/korowai-torlesse-tussocklands-park/things-to-do/tracks/korowai-torlesse-tussocklands-park-routes/

qrBjgYj.name: hexenkessel
qrBjgYj.description:
easy rig, throw a rope/cord from both sides for the connection.
The line is in kind of a quarry...
qrBjgYj.accessInfo: park at the street, walk 50 meters and you'r below the line. 5 min to each anchor

LAp2kLJ.name: summer's frost
LAp2kLJ.description: Very exposed line one one side with amazing views.
LAp2kLJ.anchorsInfo:
3 glue-in ring bolts (each side) for a main line + 2 fixed hangers 12mm for a backup. You should bring bolt  hangers and nuts.
One small ptrotector is essential on each side.
LAp2kLJ.accessInfo: The cable car from passo Pordoi brings you to the top (~16 euro return, cost from summer 2014). It is also possible to hike. It is about 500 m to the line from the cable station . We stood there in a tent on stones, used water from a small stream (in the cable station water wasn't drinkable). In the winter time all this area covered with snow, some glaciers are exist even in summer.

kpDaXo8.name: Rasta
kpDaXo8.description: It has a sketchy entrance on the west anchor.

UPlm-xx.anchorsInfo: São 3 bolts no pilar da ponte para cada lado.
UPlm-xx.accessInfo:
Para o lado A é necessário um pequeno rapel. 
No lado B da pra acessar os bolts pela ponte e se clipar neles para trabalhar.

CcVVJL-.name: The sum of all fears
CcVVJL-.accessInfo: 500m from the lookout towards Queenstown take the 4wd track down to the river

IByL14X.name: La pace di Alex
IByL14X.description: 3 points for the mainline, 2 points for the backup in the both sides. You need long slings
IByL14X.anchorsInfo: 3 points for the mainline, 2 points for the backup in the both sides. You need long slings.

tC7jNsx.name: A PLACE OF PEACE
tC7jNsx.description: Rigged in winter By Gavin Lewis, Lilou Cheveau, Conor Reiland , Dylan Bennett
tC7jNsx.anchorsInfo: Left hand size long anchors (15m) from back corner wrapped up and around front for clean exit. Right and size LARGE stone pillar wrapped with spansets.  Midline 35m-40m high.
tC7jNsx.accessInfo: Moonlight trail 45 minutes in take a left at the fork and head left up the ridgeline (30mins) You will see the stone pillar on far side. Easy tag.

5j7QV9t.name: Via dos Três Lagos

7dj2D0R.name: Jumanji
7dj2D0R.description: An even more a cozy highline with lots of trees all around

6xlzwVe.name: Full Exposure
6xlzwVe.description:
/!\ You'll need to ask permission to the mayor, go to the spot page to see the protocol.
One of Verdon's feature lines with an incredible view down the gorge.
6xlzwVe.anchorsInfo:
Left anchor
The bolts are around one meter down from the cliff edge (the included picture is just a tagline for making the connection, this is not the anchor) on the side of the cliff that faces out to the gorge. The bolts are a little distance back from the edge of the cliff necessitating anchor extension. There is a bolt to hold the extension in place and to limit friction.This anchor can require a bit more material and protection than usual.

It is a good idea to add an access rope to allow tieing in safely.
Right anchor
The three pictured bolts are for a rope swing and are not the anchor, which must be accessed by rappel. The three bolts can be used for the rappel.

From memory the anchor bolts are in a fairly classic french four bolt pattern.
6xlzwVe.accessInfo: A small rappel is required to rig each anchor although the left side anchor is only a short distance below the cliff edge and can be accessed using just a safety sling.

Le2Db-l.name: Yury Yury
Le2Db-l.anchorsInfo:
Far side (static) - 4 bolts (chain links), safe access requires a fixed line, watchout for loose rock;
Tension side (tension) - 4 bolts with common elevator tree, epic tie in ledge (webbing will likely go over edge, pad the line).

xws4KFy.name: Linha da fantasia
xws4KFy.description: Procurar Diego ou Bruno do açaí em Milho Verde, facilita para chegar ao pico, e podendo explorar outros.
xws4KFy.anchorsInfo: Ancoragens naturais laçando as pedras com cordas de 4 metros de um lado mais back up e 2 cordas de 2 metros para outro.
xws4KFy.accessInfo: trilha de fácil acesso, cuidado com os marimbondos!

fRr9GT2.name: Azghar
fRr9GT2.description: Easy to rig, no need for slingshot, just walk over
fRr9GT2.anchorsInfo:
4 bolts. We use 3 for mainline, 2 for backup. 10 foot (3m) spanset is a perfect length for 3 bolt sliding x
Bring 8 quicklinks (or steel carabiners) to clip to hangers
fRr9GT2.accessInfo: There is a road from Lessa that gets you over the cliff. From there you walk through the Apple tree fields to the spot.

SyTKqEd.name: Titan
SyTKqEd.description: The biggest urban highline in Belarus

VVE-oAH.description:
In this spot we have already set up bolts for 2 lines, 4 bolts on each side of each line. This spot is very popular and beloved by climbers as there are many rock climbing routes. The lines are not to high and not to long but it is very nice to wolk here, and the spot itself is very relaxing, as there is small river and place is very quiet, best place to relax and rest. 
The anchors on west side are easy accessible (easy hiking) but for the east side, you'll need a single climbing rope to attach to the tree and rappel down to the anchors. rope needed to be safe while establishing the anchor and also you will need rope again to havce easy descent/rappel down from the anchors after derigging. 

Keep in mind that this region is very hot during summer time (+40C degree or more), best seasons are from the end of April to start of July or middle of September to start of November usually here is windy (not too hard). 
If there is some more details or help needed you can contact us, we will be happy to help or have a highline session together --> https://www.facebook.com/slackliners.of.armenia
VVE-oAH.anchorsInfo:
12mm stainless steel glue in bolts, Hangers and nuts needed.
4 bolts on each side. 
on the East side anchors you'll need 3 hangers, there is one hanger left for descending.
VVE-oAH.accessInfo:
If you use public transport, so take public transport Yerevan to Vayk, which are strarting from Yerevan, near to Gortsaranayin metro station. You can get out to the water resource on the road and walk from there to the gorge. if you have a car so just use google maps or mapse.me and search for Hells gorge, and navigation will take you right there. 
In the gorge is the best place for camping, 
The anchors on west side is very easy accessible, but for the opposite side you need to walk in to the gorgethe gorge as there is no another safe way to cross it.

LYengbP.name: Fur seal line
LYengbP.description: To rig this line quickly you should have a drone. Trees. By feet

-ZW0V2J.name: Valterline Frajola da Conceição
-ZW0V2J.description:
This is the second line ever conquered at the second largest waterfall in Brazil. The name "Valterline Frajola da Conçeição" is in honour of Val, a guy from Rio de Janeiro that we met in Vale do Capão, He got excited with the idea of climbing up the waterfall and helping us get this line done. He came along and also brought his cat who camped with us at the waterfall for five days. This line was never authorized, we had to stay hidden and work overnight to get it done before the guides could catch us in the morning, it took a couple of days because we didn´t have a driller and made the holes on the rock by hand, using a hammer. This is one of the many lines conquered by me and my partner while travelling all over Brasil in search for Highline spots. If you want to get to know this spot or any of the others in the Chapada Diamantina, get in touch with us for more information.
Follow on instagram at @_desinfluente and @juliabluu to check out some of our other adventures conquering Highlines all over Brasil.
-ZW0V2J.restriction:
full: The Guides of Vale do Capão for some reason hate Highline and Highliners and they are not ok with this but we still do it anyway, so if you´re interested in walking on this line and don´t mind breaking a couple of rules get in touch with us and we can set up a date :)
email: equilibrionomade@gmail.com
-ZW0V2J.accessInfo: if your not familiar with the community I dont recomend goig there on your own.

nHDQNP0.name: Laczek Cygana
nHDQNP0.description: On rock side watch out for loose rock and use only holes that I mark in the photo. Both sides - 3 holes for 10mm Screw Bolts (We used 110mm long)
nHDQNP0.anchorsInfo: Both sides - 3 holes for 10mm Screw Bolts (We used 110mm long)

pu4y7PI.anchorsInfo:
Solid tree ancor (big pine tree) on the tensioning side (south)
On the static side (north) you have to equalize 4-5 smaller trees, the masterpoint shuld be at the small pine tree directly at the edge. Lots of padding necessary!

gN4c7lo.name: Med sanjami in resničnostjo
gN4c7lo.anchorsInfo:
Tension side (north): 2x12mm expansion bolts. Use 8m rope for bfk + 3 m spanset.
Static side (south): 9m spanset to good tree. On the edge is one 10mm expansion to hold point.  Use 15m static rope to a different tree for backup.
Both anchors need padding.
gN4c7lo.accessInfo:
Tension side: To access you must to pass private land. Best stealthy path could be from the parking area above climbing crag, then up to this abandoned ''road'' and through bushes. We asked the owners and could access through their grass field. They were very friendly.
Static side: Go down in the climbing area, pass the water and follow the ridge to the anchor.

ze4Te2e.name: Slack Gods Temple
ze4Te2e.description: Pray before the pyramids of slack..
ze4Te2e.anchorsInfo:
1) Climb the pyramid, don't be scared.. Wrap the top, if you dare..
2) Head up through the tree's but prepare for war, its a difficult climb.. Use as many of the tree's as you can because nobody knows how deep the root goes..
ze4Te2e.accessInfo: Climb the pyramid and climb the hill, it's obvious..

_YKo5uB.anchorsInfo:
View side (tension) is difficult - requires specific gear for trad leg, tree leg starts just behind fence and wraps around;
Far side (static) is difficult - uses 2 trees and wraps around a third, a trad anchor in crack on the slab allows for stabilization.

-exTMy1.name: The Bishop
-exTMy1.description: Right next to The King Line, the Bishop is a little bit longer and a little bit easier to get to.
-exTMy1.accessInfo:
Start by crossing the bridge into the park and follow the river to the south end of the park and around the point. When the trail turns north, start watching for heard paths going uphill towards Phoenix Buttress. The trail doesn't look like much at first, but has well established switch-backs higher up. You will reach a point where you can see the King Line gap at a rocky outcropping. 

To get to the tension side, you will need to climb a 20m, 5.4 section. A proper belay and trad pro is recommended. People often fix a rope once they reach the top so that others can prussic-in while climbing up, or rappel down. The anchors for The Bishop are a little bit further from the edge than the anchors for The King Line.  A hanging frame would be ideal, and extra padding is required.

For the static side, scramble up the tower the same way you would go to get to the King Line, and you will find the bolts for the Bishop.

Ueqj5_I.anchorsInfo: far side: two 12mm inox for the main anchor and one 12mm futher back as backup. above the cave 3x 12mm inox bolts with heavily corroded nuts (probably contact corrosion)

vt5m4u5.name: Overdose
vt5m4u5.description: At one time the highline world record, Overdose is still one of the longest highlines in the park.
vt5m4u5.accessInfo:
The quickest way to the top of the gulley is to hike directly up through Agro or Cocaine gulley.  From the bridge into the park, follow the river before turning uphill following the climbers trail up through either Cocaine or Agro Gulley.  Either are a steep slog uphill, but Cocaine gulley has a 1 move boulder problem mid way.  Alternatively, you can hike up Misery Ridge, and then around towards the Monkey before hiking downhill towards the anchors, though this is much longer.  
The tensioning side anchors can be found about as far downhill as you can go.  The bolts are on a ledge with a large boulder, and the bolts for Blue Dream is on the same ledge.  The static side anchor anchor can be accessed by hiking around the tops of the pulleys and then scrambling down the fin.  The bolts can be found near a small cave.

REUQdsv.name: Please don't right this line.
REUQdsv.description:
These bolts were placed on top of the cliff, and this goes against the local bolting ethic (trip hazard). This line is short, and not as epic as the other lines nearby, and should not be rigged. 

Access is open, but sensitive at this location. No new bolts are to be placed, but most highlines can be rigged. Be sure to evaluate the integrity of the bolts before rigging. Please read the information below. 

Kjerag is a pristine natural gem with 1000m high fjords and glacier lakes. It is a very popular tourist attraction, due to the fjords and the curiosity of Kjeragbolten, a large boulder jammed between two cliff faces. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kjeragbolten)

Highline have been in this area since 2010 when it was opened by a group of French highliners who placed 4 lines (~20m, 22m, 23m, and 55m) in the area around the bolt (http://highlinedatabase.blogspot.com/search?q=norway) between 2010-2012. Since then, a few more lines have been added and this site is one of the most striking and well known highline areas in the world with vertical exposures of 1000m sheer drops. 

Bolting or any other permanent change to the natural landscape is illegal in Norway without express permission of the landowner or land managers and they take this very seriously. 

The first lines were bolted without permission, as the "ask forgiveness not permission" approach was more common at that time. However, in 2016, the local tourism, climbing, and conservation organizations became aware of highline bolts being placed without permission. A line was placed by Czech slackliners across the large gap in front of Kjeragbolton. With 3000 people a day hiking to the site to take a photo, this drew too much attention. 

The local organizations initially considered banning highlining in the area. The International Slackline Association worked with these organizations to maintain access in the area. 

Currently, no new lines are to be bolted at this site. The current bolts can still be used, with the exception of the bolts placed for the line behind Kjeragbolton (marked on this map for clarity but noted as off limits). 

Additionally, the ISA has had reports of rust on the bolts of the 23m line, BASEline, which was made famous in the film, I Believe I Can Fly. Be very cautious using these bolts. If anyone is motivated to replace them, please contact the ISA at info@slacklineinternational.org for more information. 

Please see the ISA post: 
http://www.slacklineinternational.org/2016/11/update-bolting-and-highlining-in-norway/
REUQdsv.anchorsInfo: Don't rig this line. These bolts will be removed if they haven't been already as they go against the local climbing ethic of not placing bolts on the top of the rock.
REUQdsv.accessInfo: It's a fairly long but beautiful uphill hike from the parking area. Look up "hiking Kjeragbolten" for information. You can camp at the top without a permit, and don't need to bring much water since the water up there is pristine and drinkable.

NuuBqeB.name: The Teeth of the Tiger

in very low flow it is possible to climb down and hop across the rock to access the other side otherwise you need a kayak

Far side (tension) uses a large tree set back and another closer to the edge as an elevator.

Xac8n5I.name: Oh merde le marteau
Xac8n5I.description: Ligne plein gaz dans le cirque de la Pointe de la Bajulaz. Vue incroyable sur le lac d'Annecy. Gaz.....Aspirant ! N'oubliez pas la full Expo !
Xac8n5I.anchorsInfo:
Ancrage nord : 
3 Goujons de 12, juste sous une petite terrasse sur l'éperon sud du cirque. Un rappel de 10 mètres permet d'accéder en biais à la ligne.

Coord : 45.822596 , 6.284719

Ancrage sud : 
3 goujons de 12, en plein milieu d'un dalle. 2 goujons de 12 pour le rappel de 20 mètres sur le rocher carré dans l'alpage.

Coord : 45.82074 , 6.285514
Xac8n5I.accessInfo: Se garer à Montmin, prendre la route qui monte droit dans le centre du village. Ensuite, il faut suivre le chemin qui monte au chalet du Lars, puis à la Pointe des Frêtes et enfin à la Bajulaz. La ligne est dans le grand cirque évident ouvert sur le Lac d'Annecy.

HNJWYVg.name: BASEline (Inspect bolts carefully)
HNJWYVg.description:
Access is open, but sensitive at this location. No new bolts are to be placed, but most highlines can be rigged. Be sure to evaluate the integrity of the bolts before rigging. Please read the information below. 

Kjerag is a pristine natural gem with 1000m high fjords and glacier lakes. It is a very popular tourist attraction, due to the fjords and the curiosity of Kjeragbolten, a large boulder jammed between two cliff faces. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kjeragbolten)

Highline have been in this area since 2010 when it was opened by a group of French highliners who placed 4 lines (~20m, 22m, 23m, and 55m) in the area around the bolt (http://highlinedatabase.blogspot.com/search?q=norway) between 2010-2012. Since then, a few more lines have been added and this site is one of the most striking and well known highline areas in the world with vertical exposures of 1000m sheer drops. 

Bolting or any other permanent change to the natural landscape is illegal in Norway without express permission of the landowner or land managers and they take this very seriously. 

The first lines were bolted without permission, as the "ask forgiveness not permission" approach was more common at that time. However, in 2016, the local tourism, climbing, and conservation organizations became aware of highline bolts being placed without permission. A line was placed by Czech slackliners across the large gap in front of Kjeragbolton. With 3000 people a day hiking to the site to take a photo, this drew too much attention. 

The local organizations initially considered banning highlining in the area. The International Slackline Association worked with these organizations to maintain access in the area. 

Currently, no new lines are to be bolted at this site. The current bolts can still be used, with the exception of the bolts placed for the line behind Kjeragbolton (marked on this map for clarity but noted as off limits). 

Additionally, the ISA has had reports of rust on the bolts of the 23m line, BASEline, which was made famous in the film, I Believe I Can Fly. Be very cautious using these bolts. If anyone is motivated to replace them, please contact the ISA at info@slacklineinternational.org for more information. 

Please see the ISA post: 
http://www.slacklineinternational.org/2016/11/update-bolting-and-highlining-in-norway/
HNJWYVg.anchorsInfo: Lines are bolted, but no new lines should be bolted in this area. Bring hangers for 12mm and 10mm bolts (see http://highlinedatabase.blogspot.com/search?q=norway for more info on the original lines).
HNJWYVg.accessInfo: It's a fairly long but beautiful uphill hike from the parking area. Look up "hiking Kjeragbolten" for information. You can camp at the top without a permit, and don't need to bring much water since the water up there is pristine and drinkable.

Rvw8Sgw.name: (no send)
Rvw8Sgw.restriction: partial: We didn't manage to send it yet, so please let first riggers to do so or just come with us for a session :D
Rvw8Sgw.anchorsInfo:
Tension side(south): Tree; 2,5m spanset and 15m static for backup.
Static side: Tree; 8,5m spanset across the rock,  10m static for redirect, 15m static for backup.

ZO7iI3E.name: For Neco
ZO7iI3E.description: A decade-long dream to rig a line between two continents. We have finally managed. But, without Neco, so it's for Neco. Turkish & Estonian crew together on the project of Redbull Turkiye.
ZO7iI3E.restriction: full: Unless you have couple million dollars, or know really powerful people.

yzsCed-.name: Bad Barbie
yzsCed-.anchorsInfo:
3 trees into a big knot on the west side. 20m rope required. 
2 12m round slings needed to sling a huge bolder on the east side. Much padding needed for the boulder. Easiest to tension on this side.
Bouldering pads are ideal edge protection for both edges
yzsCed-.accessInfo: Walk up from the slate museum car park

wLg6t1c.name: So Long, Farewell
wLg6t1c.anchorsInfo:
South side: All tree anchors. A lot of spanset and rope needed. Used the forked tree by the scaffold fence as an aframe.

North Side. about 9m of spanset length is needed to basket the tree and make it over the edge of the cliff through the fence. Back up with ground pegs and static rope.
wLg6t1c.accessInfo: Park on the road opposite the small track that leads up to the quarry.

XdcOuSf.name: Classic 55m
XdcOuSf.restriction: partial: Advisable to contact someone that is a local, contact above, because Cabril is a nesting area for falcons, and is a little sensitive to rigg during certain period of the year, specially during the summer.
XdcOuSf.anchorsInfo: 3 Bolts each side. M10 and M12 mix
XdcOuSf.accessInfo: Climb the cave using the rope

ZjtcbWM.name: Me Gusta Mato
ZjtcbWM.description: Highline em cima de represa hidrelétrica,
ZjtcbWM.anchorsInfo:
Ancoragens naturais
Lado A: Bico de pedra/entalamentos
Lado B: 5 Árvores de ancoragem + 1 Árvore de cavalete

ZbViJR_.name: Namaste
ZbViJR_.accessInfo: Eastern anchor is a bit harder to access, scramble up the rock formation above from the west and belay down for about 10m. Western anchor is a few meters below the highest point of the wall.

4c8rbkX.name: SLACKRATS
4c8rbkX.description:
Moonlight track, First left after Gate. Up ridgeline and work your way right once above 'bushline' Anchor to LARGE Boulder on left, 25m of anchor gear per side. (main)

GAVIN LEWIS CONOR REILAND MIGUEL OBRIEN DYLAN BENNETT LILOU CHEVEAU
4c8rbkX.anchorsInfo: Natural. LARGE boulder left, and use of land and rock on right. tagging not too hard.

lLW3-kY.name: Bowfell
lLW3-kY.anchorsInfo: A large boulder on the north side. Needs lots of extension. The south side you have to go onto the pinnacle and wrap part of the cliff. The line is noticeably off level but not to it's detriment.

kcbkXru.name: Have You Titaniumed Your Fairy
kcbkXru.description: A great gap with a stunning sea view.
kcbkXru.anchorsInfo:
On the side of the car park, sling the two boulders with one 12m round sling and then use another 12m round sling to a boulder behind it and run it through the middle of the boulder.

Three U bolts. Some extension is required to get over the edge.
kcbkXru.accessInfo: Walk down the goat tracks from the road.

HH_Nznv.restriction: full: Permission is required for use. For inquiries, please contact us on our Instagram account @bodyproducts.ibaraki.

fdXYg7G.name: Old Man Gap
fdXYg7G.description:
Arizona's first highline, and one of the first ever highlines! 
Walked (and soloed) by Darrin Carter in 1995.
Short and classic, this highline's perceived exposure is massive and offers a breathtaking view of Tucson and southern Arizona.
Incredibly easy approach from Windy Point Vista (5 min.) so expect onlookers and climbers!
fdXYg7G.anchorsInfo:
Bolted with 3 bolts on each side.
Static rope and padding necessary!
fdXYg7G.accessInfo:
Park at Windy Point Vista, and walk past the bathroom across the open granite for a few minutes.
Once you get towards the lookout, go down the right side and walk down some sandy steps through some trees. You will see the gap right in front of you.
Getting to the left side (West Fin) involves some scrambling and hopping until you get to the end.
You can pretty much walk the entire right side (East Fin) with some minor exposed scrambling. The anchors are on a ledge which you should carefully get to.

MJq26eW.name: Millenium cave
MJq26eW.description:
Highline Millenium, Millenium cave, Cala Gonone, Sardaigne
Opened by Wanderboat

tsCrgOE.name: Plaisir canin
tsCrgOE.description: Une belle ligne de 40m au dessus de la Valée de la Creuse
tsCrgOE.anchorsInfo:
5 points de chaque coté - plaquettes de 12mm en place
Prévoir des élingues courtes 1,5 ou 2m 
Les 2 accès se font à pied : on peu lancer un petit pois pour passer la ligne et contourner la falaise par la haut
tsCrgOE.accessInfo: à pied en contournant le spot

D98uG-k.name: Under The French Moon
D98uG-k.description: Most obvious line of the area. Goes over a stunning sea cove.
D98uG-k.anchorsInfo:
The west side 2 boulders lung and then equalised together and extended over the big boulder. Roughly 5m of extension is needed to bring the anchors over the edge. Two climbing glue in bolts can be used to help stabilise the anchor.

The east side has 3 U bolts that are accessed by scrambling down to a ledge. These can then be backed up by trad gear if you wish.
D98uG-k.accessInfo: Walk down the sheep paths to the levels required.

_iyQBrz.name: Sun Jumper
_iyQBrz.anchorsInfo:
The western anchor has three bolts and can be reached by belaying down from the 110m-neighbor-line’s western anchor. 

The eastern anchor is a rather easily accessible rock pillar that is to be wrapped with slings/static rope.
_iyQBrz.accessInfo:
Recommended parking at the cemetery. Walk further up the road past the olive and orange plantations until you see a sign to namaste and mars climbing sectors and turn left there. When almost having reached the Craig namaste, stay left and keep going up for another ~10 minutes. 

The eastern anchor on the big rock can be reached easily while the western anchor requires going up to the 110m line’s western anchor and belaying down from there.

hH8I1Dy.name: Ruzova Cvernovka
hH8I1Dy.description:
A beautiful place to put a highline - indoor, urban highline in Bratislava.
Few possibilities for length cause there are lot of wals to put the highline. From 15 to 30 metres. On picture it is about 15 metres.
hH8I1Dy.accessInfo: I think officially it is clothes for public, but you have to jump inside throw the window

Hmc4lrW.name: Come l'acqua
Hmc4lrW.description: 3 points for the mainline, 2 points for the backup in the both sides. You need long slings
Hmc4lrW.anchorsInfo: 3 points for the mainline, 2 points for the backup in the both sides. You need long slings.

zE4HfFQ.name: White Beam Dream
zE4HfFQ.description: Ridge anchor you can easily access and it has 4 bolts installed for anchoring + a series of bolts to aid access to the spot.
zE4HfFQ.anchorsInfo: Vertical static anchor requires a 50m rope to abseil down to. Then 4 bolts are there to use for main and backup.

TqjTXGa.anchorsInfo: Bolts on both sides. Far side bolts are the lower set of 4.

_2q9vzI.name: Sandgrube Grunewald
_2q9vzI.description: Not rigged yet, but scouted. More info to follow ;)

spNWSH4.name: A ty Co robiłeś w środę

pl4plrB.name: Điđimelka v gatah
pl4plrB.anchorsInfo: Tension side A: Tree, 2m sling. Static side B: Tree, 1m sling.

rCvIFsd.name: Słoneczna poprzeczka
rCvIFsd.anchorsInfo:
First: huge boulder ( take something about 10m spanset

second: two bolts 12mm (take your hangers)
backup to the climbing anchors

Bz4wrdz.name: Highline soteska Mučevo
Bz4wrdz.description: There are 2 anchors (you need equipment for connecting 4 bolts) on both sides of canyon.

YcjVPGJ.description: easy beginner line, climbing routs and boulder around the line
YcjVPGJ.anchorsInfo: Bolts and a tree on the other side
YcjVPGJ.accessInfo: park the car directly next to the  highline

_aEzunn.name: La virgen
_aEzunn.anchorsInfo: Se debe escalar para lado estatico, lado dinámico tambien requiere estar anclado a la pared. Uso de sliding x

9P9ccwf.name: Bire u Brot
9P9ccwf.description: Rather easy access and rather exposed highline. One anchor is reached by walking up a hiking path and anchoring to a tree at the top. The other anchor in bolted in the wall. For further topo, contact the author
9P9ccwf.restriction: partial: This highline is in a protected area, please contact the locals so you know about the restrictions, details: https://www.geo.apps.be.ch/templates/geoportal14/makepdf.php?url=https://www.geo.apps.be.ch/de/geodaten/suche-nach-geodaten/listing.html?type=layer&code=WILDRZ&mpdf=1
9P9ccwf.accessInfo: One Anchor hiking, the other rappeling

1QF-ulg.name: Krasotica
1QF-ulg.restriction: partial: Forbidden in the birds season. Late February to October.
1QF-ulg.anchorsInfo: Tension side A (north): Wrap around rock, 7m spanset, 10mm bolts  without hangers for backup or wrap around more rocks. Static side B: Wrap around bushes, 6m spanset, backup tree 10m behind (12m rope needed).

6DL0iLq.name: Linha concreta
6DL0iLq.description: Classic easy line from Campinas for begginers at highline. Really easy to rig and you can use 100m line for it. If you have any doubt contact "Kong Slackline " at Facebook for more infos
6DL0iLq.anchorsInfo: There is 4 bolts at Static side and 3 other side. Dont need to rappel and any extra force. Really easy rig. Usually we use 5m of rope 9mm. But ot can be also rigged with slings

f0TkZBL.name: David
f0TkZBL.description: Una linea muy expuesta y bella.
f0TkZBL.anchorsInfo: Por un lado se debe escalar, por el otro se llega caminando. Uso de BFK y sliding x

CcvjD7w.name: Tupananchiskama
CcvjD7w.description: Es una linea con su nombre en nuestro idioma natal que es el Quechua y significa "Hasta que nuestras almas se vuelvan a encontrar"
CcvjD7w.restriction: partial: Se debe pedir un permiso personal al dueño de la entrada de las siete cascadas
CcvjD7w.anchorsInfo: Anclaje a se instala en un árbol anclaje b se instala en la terminación de una cascada con volts
CcvjD7w.accessInfo: Se accede a Baños de agua santo al cantón de Ulba

kqCaeA4.name: Minilodon waterfall
kqCaeA4.description: 3 spit each side. Easy ascend thanks to some ropes we put on both sides
kqCaeA4.accessInfo: Easy ascend thanks to some ropes we put on both sides

kJ2SKdt.description: schöne line, direkt über dem Becken, des großen Wasserfalls
kJ2SKdt.anchorsInfo: Der große Baum, direkt neben dem Geländer + irgendein Baum auf der anderen Seite

EJi2FaU.name: Elbrus outlook
EJi2FaU.description: Gorgeous line on the slope of mount Elbrus with a view to main Caucausus ridge. You can see mt's Ushba, Chatyn, Donguzorun and others. Height is 3100m above sea level.
EJi2FaU.anchorsInfo: Natural anchors. You need at least 20m of static rope and some nuts. There are some solid stones to wrape around.
EJi2FaU.accessInfo:
Approach is by sky/board from the slope. Take a traverse from the 
ropeway support and you get to the crag.

DL5W6ML.name: Hours Of Operation
DL5W6ML.description:
All the info you need is here:
Access, Topos, Pictures..ect
https://docs.google.com/document/d/13jCr8XnQwt4UAovNtPMn-jGK_JxW6GCqdM59J00CedI/edit. South Side (lookout side): Main: 3x 10mm Threaded rod (take hangers and nuts), Backup: 3x Fence posts...
DL5W6ML.anchorsInfo:
South Side (lookout side): Main: 3x 10mm Threaded rod (take hangers and nuts), Backup: 3x Fence posts and rusted 20mm bar.
North Side (tree side): Main and Backup: 4x 10mm Threaded Rod (Take Hangers plus nuts).

xYb_xwK.name: Purple gringos
xYb_xwK.description: Inquire in the mirrador restaurant for access.
xYb_xwK.anchorsInfo: Bring a bunch of static line to equalize anchors off of trees. The mango for the main on the mirrador side. And lots of padding for going over the sidewalk.
xYb_xwK.accessInfo: You will need to scramble up the little hill on the far side. There is only one sketchy move

B4BxoNz.description: ANy line up to this length in the quarry

bf7eL1R.name: The old road
bf7eL1R.description:
Natural anchor on the railings of the street. 
Possible from 60 - 100m, 
Direct vew on the lake!
Parking next to the line
there is also a climbing possible here check out the craig for topo
bf7eL1R.anchorsInfo: Natural anchors on the railings and concrete of the street

GwSA3aw.name: BUCKLE UP
GwSA3aw.description:
Dylan Bennett Hoel Yugg Miguel Obrien 

Easy access, Hard to tag, two stage tag and get a good runner to access lower part of gully. Try to tag to the true right of river to avoid it all together, can be strong. Also hard bush bashing for tagging.
GwSA3aw.anchorsInfo: Natural trees either side. Optional boulder on left hand side.
GwSA3aw.accessInfo: Park at Buckler Burn car park for access to left-hand side anchor head along the track a few minutes and head through the bush to find most exposed tree facing far anchor. Park Glenorchy side of bridge for access to otherside with similar approach, 5 minutes up take a right in the bush and find most exposed tree with round boulder behind. Goat track runs down from this side down to the river for access to tie tagline. Alternatively use drone.

g_z2Bry.name: Showboat
g_z2Bry.description: Semi-difficult to rig. The closer anchor from the approach is pretty easy to get to but the father anchor requires a little bit of bouldering skill, only one or two moves but its enough.
g_z2Bry.anchorsInfo: All natural anchors. Far backup is off of a large boulder and the close backup is off of a large stump. I used static rope to minimize rubbing for the stone anchor.
g_z2Bry.accessInfo: the farthest anchor is definitely the most difficult. Cross the water and follow a large fallen log up the hill. Once at the rock face, start climbing. The anchor tree is on the first tier. The second being a great chilling spot for friends who don't want to deal with the tourists.

ooCBz5b.name: Águila Cóndor
ooCBz5b.description: This highline was 33 meters long, the main and backup is a Lift 2be (tube). It was installed in the Ojos del Salado volcano, the highest volcano in the world (6893m). The line was from the Chilean summit to the Argentinian summit, because this mountain is on the border of both countries. The height was about 20 meters and it was very near from the summit, just at 6880 meters over sea level. For each side we used 3 bolt anchors.
ooCBz5b.anchorsInfo: For the anchors in the rock we used 3 points for each side, with expanding bolts. We used static ropes for both sides. From A side we used "BC Shackle SS" (Balance Community), Soft release with Sea horse (Slacktivity). For the B side we used a steel carabiner to connect the main. The main was a lift 2be (tube), for the backup we used the same and used Soft Shackle for connecting. We used a Big Loop from Balance Community.
ooCBz5b.accessInfo: From the Chilean side we ascended to the Chilean summit, and 13 meters below we installed a handrail (security line) for accessing to the rock where we anchored. For the argentinian side we get by a route not enabled were we climbed and once we get there, we went down 13 meters where we leved with the chilean side.

j7JKY43.restriction: full: Permission is required for use. For inquiries, please contact us on our Instagram account @bodyproducts.ibaraki.

9Xvh7l5.anchorsInfo:
Near Side: 3m girth hitch on bomber tree. Some possibilities for backups on other trees.
Far Side: 3+1m to equalise two trees. Possibility to attach the backup on either. No significant edge danger.
Stay low on the small trees, lower than on this picture.

Ada7glO.name: El Mostro
Ada7glO.description: Highline. Opened by Ben Gingold. 1st send by Jerome Alxiant
Ada7glO.anchorsInfo: 4 bolts on each side. Far side has 3 bolts together pulling out and another far bolt 4m from the anchor on one side. All Glue in Bolts.
Ada7glO.accessInfo: Cooper's Knob - Summit Road - Toilets are 700m from Spot on the track direction to Christchurch.

aRfkCdb.name: Line of crack
aRfkCdb.anchorsInfo: Tree's both sides with lots of extension..
aRfkCdb.accessInfo: Climb the hill either side, its shaped like a horseshoe..

Jh_Pq_m.name: V morju stoji Jurca
Jh_Pq_m.description: Exposed line above waterfall in peaceful environment.
Jh_Pq_m.anchorsInfo: East side: 2 trees, use 4m sling. West side: 3m sling to a tree for height.
Jh_Pq_m.accessInfo: Very steep hill without path on left side of waterfall.

amIWoZh.name: "Highline Spot Mönch"
amIWoZh.description: Its a nice highline for beginners but you have to climb a little. On one side you will find 3 bolts and on the other side is a little nose(you can use a 4m spanset).

bpJjhul.name: Porteille d'Orgeix
bpJjhul.restriction: none: En période de chasse il faut contacter la mairie d'Orgeix pour demander.
bpJjhul.accessInfo: Accès soit par le village d'Ascou soit par le village d'Orgeix.

XxEhNas.name: Mahinepua Bay – The Lone Survivor
XxEhNas.description:
First opened in October 2018 by the team Les Ekiwibristes.
Accès général
Mahinepua Bay, Northland, New Zealand.
GPS coordinates :
Parking : -34.999757, 173.843628
Highline : -34.990573, 173.854522
XxEhNas.anchorsInfo:
Connection
Throw a tagline from the left anchor down the slope. You can carefully make your way down from the right side in the steep grassy slopes, and connect the line.
Anchor 1 :
West side : one tree in the steep slope.
Anchor 2 :
East side : the obvious big Pohutukawa tree.
Gear : 
East side : 1x4m 1T spanset
West side : 1x3m 1T spanset and 1x2m 1T spanset (back up)
XxEhNas.accessInfo: Park on the beach and walk approx. 40mn on the Mahinepua Bay trail. At the junction head left and walk another 10mn until you’re above a nice bay with a big tree on each side. The right side (looking at the sea) is the easier to access with a small trail, you’ll have to bushbash your way to the left tree.

EB-dvgx.name: Estrellita fugaz
EB-dvgx.description: La linea mas alta de esta parte del cañon. Super linda para caminar
EB-dvgx.anchorsInfo: Es necesario escalar a lado estático. Uso de bolts

HDttdR4.name: Gabelhorn Highline
HDttdR4.description: The Gabelhorn highline is an alpine highline with a beatiful view. You have to climb a 5c to get on top of the higher tower.
HDttdR4.anchorsInfo: two 12mm bolt for the line and two 10mm bolts for the backup on each side. You will need around 8 quickdraws for the climbing ascent of the large tower. The climb is not too difficult because it is secured with a lot of bolts. A 60 meter rope is long enough to use it as a backup and as an abseiling rope from the big tower.
HDttdR4.accessInfo: Walk from Grächen to the Gabelhornpass. Follow the ridge to the gabelhorn.

cFZdFva.name: El Acyh
cFZdFva.description: Some nice blocks that work as anchors in one side and 3 bolts and other block to anchor. There's no other way by climbing two routes: a 5.11. and 5.7 in favor angle
cFZdFva.anchorsInfo: some nice blocks that work as anchors in one side and 3 bolts and other block to anchor
cFZdFva.accessInfo: There's no other way by climbing two routes: a 5.11. and 5.7 in favor angle

vLSXauv.name: Combiner
vLSXauv.description: Spansets on the columns, without anchors. Use stairs :)
vLSXauv.anchorsInfo: Spansets on the columns, without anchors

sefnHbf.name: Surfer à ceuillir les fleur
sefnHbf.description:
La ligne a été ouverte au printemps 2021 par mon frère et moi-même. Elle est relativement proche de la falaise, mais elle donne une belle vue exposée sur la vallée de la Courbière. 
Le rig est à éviter par vent de SUD 
Les 5 premiers mètres cotés tension sont en no fall zone et l’arête de la falaise à se niveaux là peut être protégé en cas de backup fall.
sefnHbf.anchorsInfo:
-Ancrage Cotée tension master pointe 3 goujons 12 mm + backup générale 3 goujons 12 mm 
-Ancrage fixe 3 goujons 12 mm + backup générale
 2 point plus cerclage pieds d'un petit arbre
sefnHbf.accessInfo:
Rabat les trois seigneur 
Chemins de la Roche Ronde 
arrivée au col prendre direction roche ronde la ligne est en face du petit abris

1jbHjvg.description: Première Highline au Taillefer. Elle surplombe le lac d'Annecy, les montagnes en toile de fond, accès facile, installation facile, le combo parfait.
1jbHjvg.restriction:
partial: Pas besoin de NOTAM pour cette ligne, mais merci d'envoyer un message à la liste de mail, au moins 2 jours avant l'installation de la ligne.

Il faudra aussi appeler les deux numéros le matin de l'installation: 

Ce mail devra contenir :
- La date d'installation et de désinstallation de la ligne 
- La commune d'installation : La Thuile
- La longueur de la ligne : 300m
- L'Altitude de la ligne : 750m
- Les coordonnées des ancrages :
Ancrage 1 : 45° 48' 30" N, 6° 11' 51" E
Ancrage 2 : 45° 48' 38" N, 6° 11' 56" E
1jbHjvg.anchorsInfo:
Côté arbre : Prendre le gros pin à sa base et se servir d'un deuxième pin comme "Frame".

Côté cailloux : 3 points sur un gros rocher qui dépasse clairement, prévoir un A Frame. Il y a aussi 2 points pour contraindre le Frame vers le bas, car s'il est trop petit la ligne à tendance à le soulever.
1jbHjvg.accessInfo:
En 20 min depuis le hameau "les Maisons", se garer en bord de route et suivre la piste. Accès facile, mais parking en bord de route  et en pente.

En 15 min depuis le parking de la carrière.
Prendre la route qui longe le camping de l'Idéal, elle se transforme en piste, puis se garer au parking en bout de piste.
Prendre le chemin qui monte 20m avant le parking. Le chemin est raide, l'idéal est de RIG depuis l'autre côté et se poser à ce parking pour dormir.

0cg_V9t.name: The Canyon Falls Line
0cg_V9t.description: A stellar highline destination in the UP. 30' cliffs on each side of the sturgeon river accessible by a short hike from a wayside on Highway 41.
0cg_V9t.anchorsInfo: Trees and boulders. A-frames would make rigging way easy.

whp3uVi.name: Monika
whp3uVi.description: The more exposed line on the spot. One of best lines around this area.
whp3uVi.anchorsInfo: Tension side A: Tree, 2m sling. Stastic side B: Tree, 1,5m sling, 15m rope to rappel and backup.
whp3uVi.accessInfo: Far side west side some rappel needed.

7_DzLkA.name: Gardening Party
7_DzLkA.description: The Wanaka crews desparate dreams to get a 100m training line... not the most aesthetic spot, this goes overtop of the old party pit and is fully within the forest. It shoots right down the middle of this small gorge and stays reasonably close to the ground. Both anchors require a significant tree climb and there are lots of hazard trees... saying that its been a great line to practice big walks on.
7_DzLkA.anchorsInfo: Both anchors are pine trees, they require decent climbs especially the north side which is around 15m up the tree.
7_DzLkA.accessInfo: Walk in from the outlet campsite is the quickest. Or from anywhere along the outlet road

svdlT1g.name: Ryujin Dam Highline 145m
svdlT1g.restriction: full: Permission is required for use. For inquiries, please contact us on our Instagram account @bodyproducts.ibaraki.

3ssthcJ.name: Pont du diable (09)
3ssthcJ.description: Highline du pont du diable, ligne évidente, qui sort de la fenêtre pour aller sur le caillou en face. Attention a la hauteur (qui varie avec l'eau en plus...)
3ssthcJ.anchorsInfo:
2 points de 12mm coté fenêtre.
4 Points de 12mm coté rocher.

gc8u-6O.anchorsInfo: Above the cave there are 3x 12mm bolts (one hanger missing), you need about 11m of slings and some ropes for backups and good padding. On the far side there are two options: The original line is rigged on 2-3x 10mm bolts plus one redirection bolt (no hanger), but the nicer option is slinging a rock structure with about 7m of slings and lots of padding (see pictures), in this way the line is not so close to the cliff.

oShZWEK.name: Skitour Highline
oShZWEK.description:
Only rigged in spring with skitour approche
Bolted Line
beautyfull view


https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZhXZNNgCapU&ab_channel=SLACKTIVITY
oShZWEK.anchorsInfo:
On the east side, bolts are on a vertical rock face. two holes are made and removable bolts were used. A deadman anchor in the snow as a backup was made.

On the west side there is a little tower. Here there is one bolt on the back of the tower, from here a sling/rope can be attached, and there is a redirection bolt on the top to the tower.
oShZWEK.accessInfo: Cable car from Oberrickenbach to bannalpsee and then a 700hm skitour

uWIP3mq.name: Early morning fuck
uWIP3mq.description: Very obvious line to a beautiful tree.
uWIP3mq.anchorsInfo:
Tension side (south): Use 2m spanset on a big tree.
Static side: 3x 12mm expansion bolts: 10m rope bfk and 2m spanset, use small rope to tie it down.

S6OMpgv.name: Maršal
S6OMpgv.description: Line in line with the wall.
S6OMpgv.anchorsInfo: Tension side 2 rail beams. Static side a tree.

6uDf7eO.restriction: full: Slackline it's forbidden it's a natural Park

90JUNnD.description: 3 U bolts on south side anchor, 4 U bolts in the wall on the north side. Need to abseil down to north anchor. Tag line (hopefully) still there.
90JUNnD.anchorsInfo: Bolted, 3 U bolts one side and 4 U bolts other side
90JUNnD.accessInfo: To get to the south side follow the path up the left hand side of the quarry. To get to the north side, follow the path up the right hand side of the quarry, hop over the fence at the top and abseil down to the ledge

7yMFnG_.description:
Nice quiet spot in an abandoned stone mine.
Somewhere between a hgighline and a midline, here are no fall zones if rigged loose.
7yMFnG_.anchorsInfo:
Solid tree anchors on both sides.
On the tension side 3m spansets are necessary to wrap a big pine tree.
The staticside has to be extended approx. 11m and redirected over a small tree directly at the edge (see photos)
7yMFnG_.accessInfo: You can park at the road HAS20 and hike up in 10-15 minutes.

Yn04zCJ.name: Mixcoatl
Yn04zCJ.description: 300 m line with amazing view and exposure
Yn04zCJ.anchorsInfo: glue in bolted anchor on both sides

4p30ZTT.description: 4 bolts on every anchor. near the edge
4p30ZTT.anchorsInfo: 4 bolts on every anchor. near the edge

YIiGcYs.name: Cara del inca a la nuez
YIiGcYs.description:
For Nuez side, climbing 6a+ trad.
For Cara de Inca, scrambling aproach, dangerous

ycMHeAh.name: Parental Advice
ycMHeAh.description: Highline, second of 4 in the same Spot. New Zealand 1st and 2nd Higline Festival Spot. 1st send by Fer Kpok
ycMHeAh.anchorsInfo: 4 bolts all glue in in one side. Rock here is very soft, equalize minimum of 3 bolts for main, never use BFK for main. Far side 4 bolts, 1 of them need a 12 mm hanger, others are glue in. Equalize with 4 bolts, rock is shell which is very soft after 5cm into the ground. Use as backup bolts from highline just next to it.
ycMHeAh.accessInfo: Cooper's Knob - Summit Road - Toilets are 700m from Spot on the track direction to Christchurch.

9A1NDkt.name: 130m Line
9A1NDkt.description: Bolted Line. One side the bolts are on a rock in between the bushes, a little bit hidden.  On the other side the anchor is on the top of the cliff.

eby-qj2.name: Ostatnia
eby-qj2.description: Line was established 4th November 2017 by Kamil Dudek and Paweł Jarosiewicz. One of the longest highlines in the Poland at the time.
eby-qj2.anchorsInfo: Both anchors are nautral. On the rock with a cross (Zabi Kon) you need: Main (3m open or 7m closed slings) and backup 2m sling. On the second rock (Turnia z Krokiem) you need a lot of slings, because it will be rigged on a rock on the back, NOT ON FIRST ON EDGE. It's important, because there are a lot of climbing roads and it's only way to not disturb. We want to live with them in harmony. You need about 15m of slings and long tail of webbing.
eby-qj2.accessInfo: To the side with a cross you can walk the path. To the second rock you must climb easy road

o1nU2UH.name: La terapeuta
o1nU2UH.description:
Esta es una cinta de muy fácil montaje.  Se montó por primera vez en el 2020 con Giovanni Ortiz y nos ha dado terapia a casi todos los que actualmente hacemos highline en Ecuador. Se pone o a menudo los martes y jueves en la Escuelita de slackline, que funciona desde el 2021

Hasta Enero del 2025, aproximadamente 70 deportistas, principalmente de Colombia, Ecuador e Italia han podido entrenar aquí.
o1nU2UH.anchorsInfo:
El anclaje A es un árbol que se encuentra en el Parque Natural San francisco de Asís, que es parte del barrio La victoria. Ahí vive la señora Nelly con sus hijos.

El anclaje B es la estructura del puente.  los dos anclajes son de fácil acceso y el tagline se puede pasar manualmente por el puente.
o1nU2UH.accessInfo: El acceso al puente es muy simple ya sea desde la Calle Carlos Barahona en el barrio la Victoria, o desde la calle el Alpargate del barrio homónimo. Se llega a pie al punto exacto, pero se puede parquear vehículos en cualquiera de las calles mencionadas,

IVFRXqe.name: Linija najmanjšega odpora
IVFRXqe.anchorsInfo: Tension side A: Tree, 2m sling, 1,5m high on tree. Static side B: Tree, 2m sling, 1m high on tree.

M4ue0VW.name: Skippers Canyon, For a good time text Danielle
M4ue0VW.description:
Opened in January 2017 by the team Ekiwibristes.
Skippers Canyon, Queenstown 9300, New Zealand.
M4ue0VW.anchorsInfo:
* Connection :
Easy as, walk the bridge with your tagline.
* Anchor 1 :
South side : rappel 10m to the anchor slab (rap from a small bush, you’ll need a 25m rope). 5 expansion bolts 12mm, bring your hangers. 1x3m 1T spanset + 1x2m 1T spanset for back up.
* Anchor 2 :
North side : next to the small hut (where you’ll find Danielle’s number) around a big pine tree (dead), back up on a bigger one behind. 2x3m 1T spanset.
M4ue0VW.accessInfo: Drive all the way up Skippers Canyon, the line is located next to the last bridge, 1km before the DOC campground, on the right side of the bridge (on your way up).

Gbr_LLE.name: 17m  Saussois
Gbr_LLE.description:
5 broches par coté, 3 points pour la force (devant), et deux points pour le backup (derrière).
Prévoir élingues de 3m et 2m par cotés. Se garer au sommet des falaises, sur le petit parking prévu pour les randonneurs et grimpeurs.
La ligne se situe...
Gbr_LLE.anchorsInfo:
5 broches par coté, 3 points pour la force (devant), et deux points pour le backup (derrière).
Prévoir élingues de 3m et 2m par cotés.

h9Vg4lm.name: Boyabat
h9Vg4lm.description: Nice exposed line at the city. It cannot be rigged without official permissions
h9Vg4lm.restriction: full: Super exposed to public. You will be stopped in 10 mins by police. Do not rig without permissions. Impossbile

K2-upOr.name: The Duck (Short)
K2-upOr.description: This is the version of The Duck rigged between the two bolted anchors instead of threading the old crane base. It is shorter and has sketchy fallzones. Not recomended.

mMlvjCc.restriction: partial: Only acces for UIAA insured members, like in Belgium KBF or CAB
mMlvjCc.anchorsInfo:
Tree side:
Main = 2m sling around tree
Back-up = 3m sling around tree

Rock side on bolts:
Main = 2 deltas, 2m sling as sliding X, shackle, 5m sling 
Back-up = 2 deltas, 2m sling as sliding X, shackle, 6m sling 
Redirect = 1 delta, 0,5m sling, 1 delta

Access rock side:
via ferrata

XWBmPTZ.name: LVýK
XWBmPTZ.description:
Nice tree to tree line in a protected natural area. Easy to rig. Too hot during the summer. 
// Hezká, relativně vysoká a lehce natažitelná lajna. Jde tahat v pohodě bez drona i bez repky (dokud bude dole paseka). U bodu 2 je hezký felící plácek na ostrohu. Nejlepší přístup je od SV přes louku s krávama. Nedoporučuju v létě, kdy se vyprahlé údolí změní v horkovzdušnou troubu.
XWBmPTZ.accessInfo:
Access through a private meadow with cows from Tubož. Or from Střezivojice. 
//Dá se sem dojet, ale skrz zákaz vjezdu a lepší je zaparkovat v Tuboži u zastávky.

RG_lJKv.name: 2o. molar
RG_lJKv.description: Una segunda línea en la magnífica peña de La Muela en el parque nacional Mineral del Chico.
RG_lJKv.anchorsInfo:
4 tensores Fixe de inox. de 3/8" por 3.5" de cada lado. Se recomiendan deltas de 10mm (los de 12mm entran con dificultad) para usar eslingas o usar cuerda estática directamente en el tensor.

Si se piensa instalar los dos highlines, se aconseja instalar primero la línea de 30 metros y luego pasar la línea más larga por la de 30, con ayuda de un hangover. De esta forma es más simple la instalación
RG_lJKv.accessInfo: Hay que llegar al centro de visitantes del parque nacional Mineral del Chico, ahí es posible estacionarse y se debe pagar una cuota de $30 pesos por persona (julio de 2017). Ahí mismo nace un sendero que lleva directamente a la peña de La Muela. El sendero está muy bien marcado y señalizado por lo que no hay forma de perderse. Es una caminata muy sencilla de 20 minutos desde el estacionamiento. El lugar se reconoce porque hay un pico de roca con una cruz en la parte más alta. La línea está en una pared de roca con forma de herradura atrás de este pico. Tras haber llegado a la cima de la peña por el sendero, se puede observar este pico. Es necesario caminar hacia la izquierda y volver a descender algunos metros, rodeando la peña por su costado sur. Se desescala una pared vertical de unos 3m con muy buenos agarres y pies, y de ahí se vuelve a subir por la roca hacia la zona de la herradura. Una vez que se ha llegado a la parte alta, se vuelve a bajar y desescalar por todo el borde de la peña hasta llegar al primer anclaje. Para llegar al otro extremo es necesario hacer una pequeña travesía relativamente sencilla, y con muy buenos agarres y pies, por toda la herradura hasta el otro lado.

YqCSDvH.name: Krompirjevka
YqCSDvH.description: Line on the sun above small canyon. Next to it is superb winter climbing crag - Bitnje.
YqCSDvH.anchorsInfo:
Tension side A: tree
Static side B: 3x 12mm bolts; 3m spanset, 20cm padding, 40cm padding for weblock, 1,5m padding for webbing

9XSaXIs.name: Scorpio
9XSaXIs.description: Awesome spot in nature next to an old ruin
9XSaXIs.anchorsInfo: Bring a lot of spansets and at leawst one long static rope for backup to the tree on the east side.

VOrt996.name: L’escalfament
VOrt996.description: Big highline between a gap in the middle of the mountains
VOrt996.anchorsInfo:
One side is natural anchors, you use a super big rock and more slings to go to the cliff. We used one 3m sling to circle the rock and then you need like around 6m of slings to reach the cliff and put the weblock.
The other side is 3 bolts of 12mm and you need to bring the hangers. Nuts are already there. Use them and leave them there
VOrt996.accessInfo: Natural anchor side is easy to access. Bolted side is a little more difficult because you need to hike up an uphill but is okey.

J3CDqyg.name: Nieve de coco
J3CDqyg.anchorsInfo: Del lado opuesto a la ola esta algo sketchy el approach.

WDQ6vVn.name: Ludzie z Jeziora

take a lot of slings
far side are three bolts so use some rope

XvnjHVj.name: Just do it

Tension side 4*10mm

8rQ6J4F.name: La ligne des Trépassés
8rQ6J4F.description:
Magnifique ligne et première highline/midline de la pointe du raz qui a un potentiel énorme. La vue est incroyable et la hauteur de la ligne change suivant la marée !

Par Delphin & Florian
8rQ6J4F.anchorsInfo: Ancrages entièrement naturel sur des gros blocs de chaque cotés. Prenez une chiée de padding !
8rQ6J4F.accessInfo:
Depuis Plogoff suivez la route de la Pointe du Raz jusqu'au parking (6 euros). Ensuite, comptez 10 minutes de marche jusqu'au spot qui se trouve tout au bout de la pointe.

Une autre solution est de parquer à la Baie des Trépassés (gratuit). Comptez 30 minutes de marche jusqu'au spot.

Passage de la ligne à pied..

ODUQyEJ.name: Marwil
ODUQyEJ.description: On either side two concrete poles
ODUQyEJ.anchorsInfo: On either side two concrete poles.

anSa5KH.name: Peaky blinders
anSa5KH.description:
Fun easy line with probably easiest approach ever!! Rigged solo, the hardest part of this rig is getting a tag line across, other then that easy as!!
Pretty cool rig as it’s rigged next the coastal road and those driving past have no idea you’re there. Checkout the Surf while you’re there and don park on the road there’s a pull over spot 20m back
anSa5KH.anchorsInfo:
static side is the obvious big tree across the gap.

Tension side is next to the road. Wrap the pohutukawa, car or road barriers I don’t know you’ll figure it out. Hardest part getting the tag line across 

Tension side requires a lot of rope and extending, I think I used 80m of static to connect three pohutukawa trees which are between 12m-20m back from the anchor. 
I used a BFK and it’ll probably help to use an a frame
anSa5KH.accessInfo:
Follow the track leading up the ridge of the island till the end, track leads through heavy flax bush to tension side.
 The static side can be reached from the small track down through the flax bushes to the South or repelling down the choss to the beach. Then up cliff navigating through the poor choss trail till you reach the pohutukawa Don’t fall and die

yzyISRo.name: El salto del diablo
yzyISRo.description: 72m highline in La Muralla, in the State of Querétaro.
yzyISRo.anchorsInfo: 3 SS bolts with rod hangers on each side. Static rope can be threaded directly through the hangers (see pictures). 25 m static rope for each side are needed.

8nQw4Cg.description: Need to abseil down to north anchor. Tag line (hopefully) still there.
8nQw4Cg.anchorsInfo: Bolted, 1 U bolt and two semi-permanent ground stakes with a third semi-permanent stake used as a redirect on the south side, two semi-permanent ground stakes and a sling around a groove in the ledge on the north side
8nQw4Cg.accessInfo: To get to the south side follow the path up the left hand side of the quarry. To get to the north side, follow the path up the right hand side of the quarry, hop over the fence at the top and abseil down to the ledge

GrRiU9L.name: Marwil II

GrRiU9L.anchorsInfo: On either side two concrete poles.

One side: 2 bolts 12mm 
Second side: 2 bolts 12 mm
take your own hangers 12 mm

gb4rtPP.name: Laberinto de bambú
gb4rtPP.description: 40m highline at Peña de la Muela, Hidalgo.
gb4rtPP.anchorsInfo: 4 SS bolts with hangers on the spire. Natural anchor on the only big tree at the bottom of this area in La Muela. Class 4 exposed climb of the top of the spire. Watch out for loose rock and holds in this area!

2SZsLFi.name: The Classic Crestline Gap
2SZsLFi.description: A classic line on the Rim of the World.  It's hard to imagine finding another line with this much perceived exposure just minutes from your car (and margaritas!)
2SZsLFi.accessInfo: Park off the highway east of the crag.  Each side has a short boulder problem to get on top.

dh-ejEY.anchorsInfo: Three 10mm wedge bolts both side. On the wall there is another one bolt for backup and one above for the fix rope that helps you to rig.
dh-ejEY.accessInfo: 10m walk from the parking below. To get to the top both side you need to climb small walls. There are bolts for belaying.

F8wjYmH.description: Highline over the road at Bärenschlucht. hight approx. 20-30m.

1ZMoG1b.name: Arschgeige
1ZMoG1b.description: Easy short beginner friendly line with great views of the coast.

Static side is the big tree to the west, rigged off the lower branches to the back then forward on to the higher branches to get the line level

Tension side is the obvious pohutukawa on the cliff, this looks poor so it’s equallised with a bigger pohutukawa 8m back.
1ZMoG1b.accessInfo: Park at the carpark for moureeses bay then walk alone the beach till you get the the tree line. Look for a track amongst the bushes with ropes leading up the hill. Once at the top of the ridge ditch the main track and trun right following a small trail till you get to the spot

t2qJAK8.name: Brifing

DHRDr-T.anchorsInfo:
One side (left if look at a gap): 3 glue-in ring bolts for a main line + 2 fixed hangers 12mm for a backup. You should bring bolt  hangers and nuts.One small ptrotector is essential on each side.
The rught side: natural anchor, the teeth is about 4-5m circumference.
DHRDr-T.accessInfo: The cable car from passo Pordoi brings you to the top (~16 euro return, cost from summer 2014). It is also possible to hike. From the cable station it is about 500 m to the line. We stood there in a tent on stones, used water from a small stream.

cK_55zR.accessInfo: easy, walking access to reach anchores

Natural anchors: use a pine on a side and 
skirt the peak with slings in another side

hHgA1sW.name: Goodie one Shoes
hHgA1sW.description: Big boulders on either side. Bring lots of padding. Scramble up to the anchors
hHgA1sW.anchorsInfo: Big boulders on either side. Bring lots of padding

RExX7In.name: Goufre du petit pouzail
RExX7In.description: Highline de 30m, ayant une ambiance un peu particulière étant a moitié sous terre.
RExX7In.anchorsInfo:
Coté entrée : Contourner la descente du gouffre par la droite en restant a niveau sur une petite sente, cela mène sur un trou (toboggan ?) caractéristique. Plusieurs points pour faire un rappel en direction de l'entrée du gouffre (et pas dans le "trou" !), 4m plus bas, ce trouve 4 points pour la force et le backup.

Coté fond du gouffre : Remonter sur une des cordes statique (50m ?) qui pend au plafond... 4m avant l'ancrage de la corde dans l’alcôve, il y a un relais de grimpe et deux gougeons supplémentaire a votre droite. Faire son installation sur ceux-ci, backup sur les points qu'il y a autour et sur le sommet de la corde statique de remontée.
RExX7In.accessInfo:
Accès simple : Se garer a coté du contrôle technique (4/5 places), contourner le vieux portail, remonter le chemin qui mène en 10/15 minutes a une première grande grotte. rentrer par le grand porche principale, ressortir par le petit porche de droite et continuer le chemin qui va remonter au dessus d’où vous êtes. Marcher encore 30minutes, vous aller tomber sur le gouffre du petit Pouzail ! 

Accès joueur et spéléo : Une fois dans la première grotte, sorter par le porche de droite et rentrer dans la grotte a votre gauche.... et a vous de jouer pour trouver la liaison avec le gouffre du petit Pouzail.... attention lors de la sortie dans le gouffre, risque de chute de pierre ! Bon courage et prenez vos précaution, cela reste de la spéléo, malgré les apparences de facilité on peux vite tourner en rond pendant plusieurs heures et bien se perdre, inutile de préciser que le gps et téléphone ne sert a rien en cas de problème... ;)

sOr_RcL.name: Chuzenhöhle
sOr_RcL.restriction: none: Be respectful to Mother Nature🌞

59bM_E2.name: One Step Forwards Two Steps Backwards
59bM_E2.description: Meteora is a Climbing, Highlining and generally Outdoors Paradise, this line is one of the many the area has to offer, First successful attempt 9/1/2016

xEcj3Ze.description: 200m line over the top of the quarry
xEcj3Ze.anchorsInfo: Ground stakes, backed up to a tree on the north side. Very rocky ground, especially on the south side, so hard to find good spots for the stakes. Currently some stakes have been left semi-permanently, if they are used be sure to check to make sure they don't move. Use pre-installed ground stakes at your own risk.
xEcj3Ze.accessInfo: Climb up the side of the quarry from the south side anchors of the 80m lines and walk around the top to the north side, or follow the path up the right hand side of the quarry and walk around the top to the south side

3ALXYeC.name: Locked On
3ALXYeC.accessInfo:

in very low flow it is possible to climb down and hop across the rock to access the other side otherwise you need a kayak

dbKNiZc.name: Second base
dbKNiZc.description: Cleaner line than first base. Typically the line I put up for beginners.

XGXtWur.name: The right one (Правая)

XGXtWur.accessInfo: The left side is easy to get by foot. The right one you need to climb from the back of the cliff. It's not quite hard, maybe 5c or less.

X9-9JUj.accessInfo: To get to the left side you need to climb an easy 10m wall. A few nuts would be useful.

IVJkl3b.name: Kislota
IVJkl3b.description: Amandoned museum or something. Good view on Sevastopol bay

iszy5L_.name: Catorce deltas y un Jambito
