DAVE6ZM.name: HMS Hodge
DAVE6ZM.description: Legs 1 and 2 of a spaceline that could be rigged as it's own highline
DAVE6ZM.anchorsInfo: A collection of the larger trees on either side.

UQZykSM.name: Leigh Way
UQZykSM.description:
Two parallel higher 80m lines can be rigged here. 

Not self explanatory so would be best to rig with someone who knows the spot (Bristol Slackline groups are pretty active).

The narrow more open side has a holm oak you can rig to further behind the ridge. Extended anchor that requires a frame/hang frame. 

The shadier side is two large yew trees you can equalise between (also requires a deviation or a-frame to raise the anchor).
UQZykSM.restriction: partial: We have never required permission thus far .. it's been three years of rigging and leaving permarigs without any issues.
UQZykSM.anchorsInfo: Slings galour - or rope and a BFK if thats how you like it
UQZykSM.accessInfo:
Either access via towpath on foot or bikes, then enter into quarry 2/3 and walk up the ridge line.

Park in the woods car park and walk straight through until you get to the quarries.

ayz2xXQ.name: Bobby La Brume
ayz2xXQ.description:
10mm spit already in place. (Master 3 on each side +  1 backup)
3m & 4m lifting sling. Parking : col d'Azet
Access east side : need 30m rope
Access west side : need 15m rope
ayz2xXQ.anchorsInfo:
10mm spit already in place. (Master 3 on each side +  1 backup)
3m & 4m lifting sling
ayz2xXQ.accessInfo:
Parking : col d'Azet
Access east side : need 30m rope
Access west side : need 15m rope

SugtrYL.name: Ghostbuster
SugtrYL.description: We used mms. 4 bolts in each side. Car nearby the spot

khZy4QI.description: Anchor on the new anchors on each side. Edge protection needed.

H9nCVwj.name: La tension du diable
H9nCVwj.anchorsInfo:
2 arbres,
Tension side : arbres loin derrière, nécessité d'un frame (6-8m d'elingue environ)
Far side a environ 10m de haut (facile a grimper)
(Données non vérifiées)
H9nCVwj.accessInfo:
Tension side : parking a 2min a pied
Far side : si pas de paddle ou canoë, nécessité de faire un grand tour en voiture ou velo

zAnOVtC.name: Sosedova
zAnOVtC.description: Longest line here. One side is great and exposed with a blank rock underneath.
zAnOVtC.anchorsInfo: One side tree. The other 2x12mm and 2x10mm bolts with hangers.

L_BZeso.name: Call Him J.C
L_BZeso.accessInfo: Both sides can be accessed by foot..

-ayKexJ.name: Osiemdziesiątka
-ayKexJ.description: This is a private area. If you have questions, ask Urban Highline Festival.

q08cArY.name: The Gathering of Tribes
q08cArY.description:
Highline stablished on 07/03/2021 by Paulo Henrique (PH), Saulo B., Leandro B., Doilson J., Francisco C., Priscila A., Frank A. and Yana H.
Spot location:
Jornalista Phelippe Daou Bridge, better known as Ponte Rio Negro, connecting the cities of Manaus and Iranduba.
The spot has been known and frequented by the local climbing community for years, which seted up the climbing routes on the first two columns (on the Manaus side). The highline connects these two columns. Our gratitude to the climbing community. Doubts you can contact @le.psy92 or @saulo.balieiro
q08cArY.anchorsInfo:
Anchors in 03 points in concrete wall.
We use 10m ropes for main anchorage and anchoring rope for backup. 01 Softrelease. We tension manually with linegrip.
Line data:
High: 25~26m Long: 50m
Great line for highline freestyle.
q08cArY.accessInfo:
Parking: it is possible to park your vehicle near the first anchorage, just accessing a dirt road on the right side of the bridge (on the Manaus side). If you are coming by public transport, you should get off at the bus stop closest to the bridge.
It is necessary to have knowledge climbing and equipment (ascent and abseiling)
Side A: Need to climb a route about 13m high to access the bolts. Anchoring in 03 points.
Side B: Need to climb a route about 26m high to access bolts. Anchoring in 03 points.

CFT0ArW.name: Narebrnica
CFT0ArW.description: Long line with beautiful 360 view. A little low but worth it. Use short segments!
CFT0ArW.restriction: partial: Ask SlackAlien before rigging for air traffic info.
CFT0ArW.anchorsInfo:
Tension side A: Wrapped boulder 5m sling + wrapped bush 2m sling, from A frame to bush 11m, from bush to wrapped boulder 3m, 3,5m high A frame.
Static side B: south one of 2 trees; 1,5m sling
CFT0ArW.accessInfo: Closest to get to the spot is from north side. Follow the path written on google maps.

f2abD6B.name: Saint Antonin - 60m
f2abD6B.description: Highline de 60m qui met bien dans l'ambiance malgré la hauteur.
f2abD6B.anchorsInfo:
Coté droit (face vide) : faire un rappel de 10m pour trouver les points (5).
Coté Gauche (face vide) : 2 points pour faire un rappel de 10m, déviation au milieu a mettre pour être dans l'axe. 5 points de 12 en pleine face.

k8YrxPc.name: China Man Gap
k8YrxPc.description:
A new addition to the Windy Point highlines.
60m long and classic, this highline's perceived exposure is similar to the Old Man Gap and offers a breathtaking view of Mt. Lemmon. Conditions will either be perfect or very windy.
This is an ideal trick/freestyle highline, so get ready to huck yo meat.
Incredibly easy approach from Windy Point Vista (10 min.) so expect occasionally onlookers and climbers!
It has been rigged solo, and really easily rigged with 2 people.
Established by James Xu.
k8YrxPc.anchorsInfo:
Bolted with 3 bolts on each side.
A short A-frame would be helpful for the far-side, not necessary though. Just extend and pad and you'll be fine.
Static rope, quicklinks, and padding necessary!
When tagging, throw the tagline from the side closer to the parking lot. Walk the line to the bottom of the cliff where the far-side anchor is and pull it up with a rope (I usually use the anchor rope).
k8YrxPc.accessInfo:
Park at Windy Point Vista, and walk past the bathroom across the open granite for a few minutes.
Once you get towards the lookout, go down the right side and walk down some sandy steps through some trees. You will see the Old Man Gap right in front of you.
To get to the anchor that's closer to the parking lot (static/soft release side), walk past the Nancy's Thumb Tower on the right to get on top of the North Fin. Go to the very end of the fin and hop across a very small (but very exposed) gap to get on top of a tower where the 3 bolts are. Recommend having a short 20' piece of rope you can tie around a block running to the anchor bolts to back up the hop. Toss tagline from here!
The far-side anchor is on top of the further formation (Mean Mistreater Wall). When walking past Nancy's Thumb, don't stay on the ridge but take small trails down to the right. This will take you to a nice space below the line. Scramble up the right side of the formation to find 3 bolts. This is a great hang out spot.

cix2FeN.description: Roughly 150-155m line on the south side of the 303. Access is easiest from the freeway if you pull off the side of the road on 51st it is not marked so look for the power lines branching off to the south.
cix2FeN.anchorsInfo: 3 3/8 bolts with hangers and quick links on either side.
cix2FeN.accessInfo: Going east on the 303 exit at 51st and there will be a fence. as soon as you pass through the fence immediately turn right and follow the road closest to the fence for about a mile. The trail will fork around the first mountain, stay on the north side and you’ll be able to follow this trail all the way to the foot of both mountains. There are a few spots were will needs a little bit of clearance but not a lot, you will also scrape quite a few trees and bushes so be prepared for scratches.

E2B65Vg.name: Return of the Jedi
E2B65Vg.description: Access is really easy, car leave under the highline, road to the top is also easy. You should find a road (motocross road) to up no dont need to climb there.
E2B65Vg.anchorsInfo: One anchor is a tree, big tree, back-up also tree 5 meters behind the first tree. Second anchor is also tree, back-up again tree 2-3 meters behind main anchor
E2B65Vg.accessInfo: Really easy access. You dont need to climb there

D8RH94H.name: Thorns
D8RH94H.description: Asops river gorge place with unlimited highline options
D8RH94H.anchorsInfo: We have used spansets on each side 2x5m and few 2m 1m

P6E8cR3.name: Mazatzin
P6E8cR3.description: 245m highline at Peña de la Muela, Hidalgo.
P6E8cR3.anchorsInfo: 3 Fixe glue-ins on la Muela. Natural anchor on la Peña del Venado. Class 3 climb to the top of El Venado. Watch out for loose rock and holds in this area!

lCALqkE.name: Sonho que se sonha junto
lCALqkE.accessInfo: A mata embaixo da linha é densa, o que dificulta muito a passagem do cordelete sem drone.

W3jULHG.anchorsInfo: Repel down with 70m rope from the top for other anchor

8pDyHW8.name: Punto de quiebre
8pDyHW8.description: Great 39m highline at Peña de la Muela, Hidalgo.
8pDyHW8.anchorsInfo: 3 Wavebolts each side. The use of maillons or carabiners is needed. Rigging includes climbing a short class 3 climbing of a spire. Watch out for loose rock and holds in this area!

Tliqg6j.name: Trollsnørr
Tliqg6j.description: Contact gravdal.martin@gmail.com for picture and info. Maybe I´ll join. Natural anchors. Have to be creative with the anchors. Some trees and cliff edges. contact Martin Gravdal for pictures of rigging example. Just a few meters from parking ;)
Tliqg6j.anchorsInfo: natural anchors. Have to be creative with the anchors. Some trees and cliff edges. contact Martin Gravdal for pictures of rigging example.
Tliqg6j.accessInfo: just a few meters from parking ;)

UkqcL7A.name: Marins
UkqcL7A.description:
This highline is located between two private properties so we recommend that you keep in touch with the owners of base A whose name is Mateus.
It is recommended to bring water.

As for mounting, this highline is very easy to access both anchors. Anchor "A" (PHOTO) can be reached by car, and on side "B" you have to cross the river and follow into the closed forest until you find the exact opening inside the forest. Track marked.
Extra attention in mounting and dismounting, Has climbing routes in place!
UkqcL7A.anchorsInfo:
only trees to make the anchorages on both sides.
You will need slings, at least a 20 meter long rope and a 1.5m easel.

SZZdMfy.name: Por da Chuva
SZZdMfy.description: Uma linha perfeita para iniciantes, entrada fácil altura media.
SZZdMfy.accessInfo: Acesso pelo Morro do Cristo de Pedreira.

m0sv90a.description: Ligne plein gaz en haut de la falaise du Parmelan, vue imprenable sur le lac. Encore plus magique avec une mer de nuage (automne ou hiver).
m0sv90a.anchorsInfo:
Côté refuge : 1 goujon + un arbuste permettent de faire un rappel pour accéder à l'ancrage. 3 spits de 12.

Chill side : des arbres permettent de faire un rappel pour accéder à l'ancrage. 3 spits de 12. Ancrage commun avec la 120m.
m0sv90a.accessInfo: Monter au refuge du Parmelan depuis le parking de Villaz ou de l’Anglettaz, puis suivre les crêtes vers le col du Pertuis. Le chemin descend dans le lapiaz puis remonte sur la crête, la ligne se trouve 50 m après que le chemin ait retrouvé la crête.

QIrEE7j.description: De chaque coté, 3 points pour la force + 2 points pour le backup. Équipement sur broches (peinte en jaune)
QIrEE7j.anchorsInfo: De chaque coté, 3 points pour la force + 2 points pour le backup. Équipement sur broches (peinte en jaune).

nFxy_3b.description: Macaroni. The most popular line in Crimea. Absolutely must to try. Brilliant.
nFxy_3b.anchorsInfo: Four 10mm and two 12mm wedge bolts on both side. You need hangers.
nFxy_3b.accessInfo: The same approach as to Thyme.

NNhBnqa.name: Nadge highline
NNhBnqa.description:
road close.
crossing the river on the cable (photo)
Base Camp at the foot of the mountain
a steep climb up the hill from the camp 500 meters.
NNhBnqa.accessInfo: 40 meters of ascent by rope on a vertical wall

yb4e9Gp.name: Poolburn Viaduct
yb4e9Gp.anchorsInfo: rigged off some rocks and the bridge

a9TBw5b.name: Linha do Pinóquio
a9TBw5b.description: This line can be rigged just in the dry season, as the left anchors stays under water when the river is high. Best time April to September
a9TBw5b.anchorsInfo:
Bolted anchor on both sides. 4 on the left, 3 on the right. 
There's no hangers, bring them!

_uqoLhD.name: Ave-some
_uqoLhD.description: Nice place with really pleasing exposure.
_uqoLhD.anchorsInfo: 3 x 12mm bolt on each side. Required 6 x 12mm diameter hangers for rigging.

2ND7v9w.name: Deje los pernos
2ND7v9w.description: Frim a hill to a pinacle, nice expo, teide view if you're lucky
2ND7v9w.anchorsInfo: 4 bolts in rocks, not far from the other 112 m.
2ND7v9w.accessInfo: The hill access is obvious, strait to the anchor in the bush. For the pinacle, the acces take the end of a trad multipitch route. It is easy but a bit sketchy if you don’t have equipement.

toCsP62.name: Jake's Peak / Emerald Point Highline
toCsP62.description: Bolt garden both sides. 2000' vertical climb in less than a 1 mile. Brutal approach. Well worth it

wkqWhPZ.name: The Cornwall Line
wkqWhPZ.description: The most well known highline in the area
wkqWhPZ.anchorsInfo:
4 expansion bolts in concrete blocks on either side.
The anchors are 50m apart but the line is 45m long.
wkqWhPZ.accessInfo: Park at the Holywell beach car park and walk along the coastal path.

Dbt2YIt.name: Long Live The King
Dbt2YIt.anchorsInfo: bolts on both sides. On the west side use the glue in to back up the 10+ year old terry chains.

c5gZylU.name: Norteño Amor
c5gZylU.description: Linea muy expuesta. Una experiencia inolvidable si te atreves.
c5gZylU.accessInfo: Es necesario subir la via ferrata.

bDVemtw.name: Lazy Daisy
bDVemtw.description: Nice line, start from a pillar. We made there also a spaceline!
bDVemtw.anchorsInfo:
One anchor is on the wall (you have to abseil), 4x10mm Bolts.
The other anchor is on the pillar, 3x10mm and 1x12mm Bolt.
Bring your hangers.
bDVemtw.accessInfo:
Auer, Italy
Schwarzenbach - Katzenleitern.
You go up the stairs until you come to pylon (15 minutes). From their you go to the left into the wood and follow a small path until you come to a small canyon (5 minutes).

3WfsBvu.name: Miting lajna
3WfsBvu.restriction: partial: Only with permission of the person who live beneath it.

Bm4kE2f.name: el mostro
Bm4kE2f.description: 4 bolts on every anchor. near the edge
Bm4kE2f.anchorsInfo: 4 bolts on every anchor. near the edge

yjxKJ_X.name: Youpiline
yjxKJ_X.description:
petite highline idéal pour débuter...
15m de long pour 80 à 100m d'expo ! Le site est magnifique et malgré cette petite distance à traversé la ligne est majeure.
yjxKJ_X.anchorsInfo:
5 points d'encrages coté sud : l'installation peu se faire facilement sur la dalle (5 plaquettes 12mm sont en place) 
il faudra installer un rappel sur un arbre coté nord pour accéder aux points d'encrages (2 plaquettes 12mm sur la dalle, 2 autres derrière et un 5ème point décalé derrière un arbuste pour le backup général). Prévoir des élingues de 3 et 2m 
l'idéal est d'utiliser un petit poids à lancer pour passer la ligne - prévoir des protections ;-)
yjxKJ_X.accessInfo: l'accès se fait à pied par le bas (site d'escalade) ou par le haut en traversant le champs

mEWUTye.name: Turkey-poult
mEWUTye.description: Newbie's line. Easy to get and easy to rig.
mEWUTye.anchorsInfo: 4 wedge stainless steel bolts both side with 12mm hangers.
mEWUTye.accessInfo: Easy to get by foot from the bottom or by rappeling down from the top.

AQ8EmfX.name: Saving Nuri
AQ8EmfX.description: Risky line to rig because they release the water of the dam and the valley floods while rigging if timed bad.

dnimpRx.name: Waterline Verticale
dnimpRx.description: Depuis le sentier d'accès, la ligne va droit dans la cascade. Attention l'accès à l'ancrage dans la cascade demande un peu de grimpouille facile mais très expo et improtégeable: le caillou part en miettes. 25m plus haut,  il y a une petite vire et l'ancrage est bien à droite sur celle-ci, au seul endroit avec du bon rocher.
dnimpRx.anchorsInfo:
Arbres d'un côté, on a riggé au niveau du sentier pour l'ouverture, on peut faire 3-4m au dessus si on veut, c'est plus à niveau et plus loin des rochers pour le far side.
 4 pts dont 1 seul avec plaquette à demeure en face. Riggé en BFK avec 15m de corde. Penser à prévoir un rappel de 25m en plus.
dnimpRx.accessInfo:
Se garer quelque part à Craponoz, puis longer la rivière pour contourner le château. Une fois qu'on a le bon sentier, c'est plat jusqu'à la cascade. Si on n'a pas le bon sentier, revenir vers le chateau et trouver le bon sentier. 
Attention aux Canyoneur, normalement leur rappel passe à côté de la ligne mais ça peut swinger.

vTD0-m4.name: The longest 6a
vTD0-m4.description: Very pretty line over crystalblue water in a rather secluded bay with climbing crags on both sides of the bay. We rigged a 195m line because we only had a 200m setup but there are many more parallel 200m plus lines possible. Since the approach to the anchors is difficult I advise to rig this line with at least 3 and preferably 4 people.
vTD0-m4.restriction: partial: We asked the owners of the guesthouse ‘Bepin’  if they were oke with us rigging there. They were oke with it and were very welcoming. They also allowed us to sleep in our van on their parking lot since the camping was closed. Please ask them again for permission if you want to repeat the line.
vTD0-m4.anchorsInfo: Natural anchors on bot sides for which you need a lot of slings and rope. There are plenty of boulders and trees that can be used if you are creative. Bring lots of padding because the rock is razor sharp
vTD0-m4.accessInfo: Access to both anchors is difficult. For the westside anchor we climbed a 30m long 6a route called A left of the route ‘smiles’. We hauled the webbing up the climbing route. The alternative is a long strenuous bushwhacking mission, pick your battle. The eastside can be approached by scrambling up on the south side of the cliff and then a relatively long but oke bushwhacking mission.

MmiR0cf.name: Tucson Chainsaw Massacre
MmiR0cf.description:
A beautiful Line over Marshall Gulch
FA: Jep Stockmans 10/21
MmiR0cf.anchorsInfo: Large pine trees on either side of the gulley
MmiR0cf.accessInfo: Walk 0.25 miles down Marshall gulch trailhead to obvious gap with a large tree on your right.

eKUFWn-.name: Naked Instinct
eKUFWn-.description: This line is all natural, and has relatively short no-fall zones. It's an all-natural rig, and it's fairly level. It's in the strong sunlight all day, so the waterfall makes it very refreshing. This area gets very dry in the summer and fall, though, so it is much nicer to visit in the spring when the waterfall is flowing. The Stanislaus river is about 200 meters away and runs all year.
eKUFWn-.anchorsInfo:
Sling a large, flat, round boulder that sits in the waterfall. Use 28 feet of spansets on this side to completely clear the edge. A climber has placed a single bolt at the edge of the cliff, which makes for a perfect stabilizer for the waterfall-side of the anchor (don't use the climbing bolt for protection... it should only be used to keep your anchor slings from sliding around on the rocks.

The side opposite of the waterfall has a large rock outcropping that can be slung. We used four slings total (one for the main line and one for the backup), girth hitched around this rock. You want to us a girth hitch, as basket-style may roll up and off the rock. This side needs greater than 14 feet of slings to get around the rock.
eKUFWn-.accessInfo: Trails lead to all areas. No climbing needed.

sCfLJX0.description: Expedition highline in a heart of Caucasus mountains. Altitude is about 2600m.
sCfLJX0.anchorsInfo: Four 12m glue and one 10mm wedge bolts on left side. One 12mm glue and three 10mm wedge bolts on right side. You need hangers. Also use big stones for backup, bring 30-40m of static rope for this.
sCfLJX0.accessInfo: 1,5-2 hours walk from the lodge on the bottom of valley. Approach is easy you don't need to climb any wall, if you do look for another way.

CjR-IQ2.name: HS 70 (the freestyle line)
CjR-IQ2.description:
A (relatively) newer line using the from the east side of the 80m to the west side of the 40m. For meets with multiple lines this is generally RESERVED for freestyle 😉. CAUTION - no fall zone near the east anchor due to close rocks on the north side.
Tagline can be easily walked around.
CjR-IQ2.restriction: none: Be respectful of climbers, this is a BMC owned quarry so if the climbers complain about slackliners then it will create access issues.
CjR-IQ2.anchorsInfo:
East anchor - Either 3 ground stakes (as for 80) or spanset chain from large tree.

West anchor - 4 glue-ins (Ca. 2013) Shared with the 40m.

The 80m has priority for the stakes rather than the tree as the line goes over a better edge. So when rigging both use the tree for the 70m

When both the 70m and 80m lines are rigged, a small and a large A/hang frame can be supported against each other. See photos.
CjR-IQ2.accessInfo: Park at the main Horseshoe Quarry carpark or lay-bys, approach the quarry then follow the track up the hill to the right. Once level with the top of the quarry follow the path to the left, up a few steps then carefully make you way the large tree.

QcYGCGU.name: Necolandınız
QcYGCGU.anchorsInfo: Both anchors bolted 4 of each. You need long spansets
QcYGCGU.accessInfo: Both side able to hike. But you need to be carefull about rockfall.

mpJo-sl.name: Hangman
mpJo-sl.description: Single tree anchors on both sides. Both sides the tree is set back so static rope and A-frames is best.
mpJo-sl.accessInfo: Short walk on LC Gunn trail from H97

34PRIVY.description: Horizon. Stunning line, with a panoramic view of the sea.
34PRIVY.anchorsInfo: On the left side 3 wedge bolts plus 2 glue bolts a bit higher for the backup. On the right side 2 glue and 1 wedge bolts and a hole stone for wrapping around as a backup. You need 10-20m of static rope. All the bolts are 12mm. Don't forget hangers.
34PRIVY.accessInfo: Ascend from Luchistoe takes 1.5-2 hours. The road goes along the beautiful valley of ghosts. You can also get on a solid 4x4.

lt1TSXc.name: Freestyle ON THE CAVE
lt1TSXc.description:
This is one of the three lines that have been conquered in this location and the first and only for freestyle. This is a beautiful place in Chapada Diamantina Bahia anda its open for visitors. Two of the lines at this spot were conquered by me and my partner while traveling all over Brasil in search for spots to put up the most incredible Highlines. The trail is quiet easy but its also easy to get lost. If you want to get to know this spot or any of the others in the Chapada Diamantina, get in touch with us for more information.
Follow on instagram at @_desinfluente and @juliabluu to check out some of our other adventures conquering Highlines all over Brasil.
lt1TSXc.accessInfo: You will need to rapel to access this line

Wk9AInu.name: Ho aus dem Häuschen
Wk9AInu.description: Wall of fame for those who made it :). Bolts in the wall of Ho's room and a tree
Wk9AInu.anchorsInfo: Bolts in the wall of Ho's room and a tree

xJiuS58.name: Mars
xJiuS58.description: Chill line with easy rigg. Dont worry about electric line, because it's isolated.
xJiuS58.anchorsInfo:
Two trees.

#1 side on "Dupa Słonia" - You need 10-15m slings, tree anchor is few meters from edge, a little bit lower, so take a few pads there.

#2 side - You can see it on photo, line must be 2 meters on tree to safety from edge. It will be safer if you make the backup extraction to another tree, because main tree can move on leashfalls.
xJiuS58.accessInfo: You can go from back path

LLD1IRn.name: Cave Pynard
LLD1IRn.description: Highline. Bolted by Fer Kpok, 1st Send Yvan Pynard
LLD1IRn.anchorsInfo: Natural on one side. Need a 9m sling or 20m rope. Other side have 4 bolts for highline and 2 from a climbing route can be used as backup of the backup. You need to abseil (from another 2 bolts of another climbing route) to a considerable good cliff where the bolts are.
LLD1IRn.accessInfo: On the way to Jane Fonda climbing Crag before starting to walk down the hill.

5M1J9Tf.name: Taft Point
5M1J9Tf.description: Simple rigging with bolts either side (may need your own hangers). Huge exposure and amazing views of El Capitan. Great photo opportunities.
5M1J9Tf.accessInfo: Easy 1 mile walk in from the car park. Follow signs to Taft Point.

px6vj9F.name: Zelenodolskaya
px6vj9F.description: 5-th or 6-th floor, up to 30 meters length and 30 meters height. Just comfortable spot. Sometimes not cool smell from oil and fat combine, but you'll get used to it.

BPBZwG1.name: Życie to Petarda! (Life is a fireworks!)
BPBZwG1.description: Access is really easy, car leave under the highline, road to the top is also easy. You should find a road (motocross road) to up no dont need to climb there.
BPBZwG1.anchorsInfo: One anchor is a tree, big tree, back-up also tree 5 meters behind the first tree. Second anchor is also tree, back-up again tree 2-3 meters behind main anchor
BPBZwG1.accessInfo: Really easy access. You dont need to climb there

fnWSkGN.name: Buches de aire fresco
fnWSkGN.description: Suele hacer viento pero es un lugar tranquilo.
fnWSkGN.anchorsInfo: Complicado montaje. Rapelar por ambos lados para montar los anclajes.

X3yHqWm.name: Devaneios do topo
X3yHqWm.description: menos de 200m de Highline (nao foi medido com laser), ancoragem natural lado A 5 eixos de caminhao lado B, +50m altura, acesso fácil
X3yHqWm.anchorsInfo: 3 bolts lado A, 2 eixos de caminhao lado B com backup natural

5i9unHi.description: Line established at drill and chill festival 2017

biyhBPJ.name: Fajna lajna
biyhBPJ.description: First line on the spot, but the most boring one :D. Still good.
biyhBPJ.anchorsInfo: Tension side A: Tree, 2m sling. Static side B: Tree, 1m sling, 10m rope to rappel and backup.

GJzmZ7Y.name: Džahve
GJzmZ7Y.description: walkable part of the line is about 25 meters
GJzmZ7Y.accessInfo: 10 minutes walk from parking lot between Maninska Gorge & village Záskalie

QNLKTjn.name: Salto del pollo
QNLKTjn.description: First highline in Almería with a beatiful view on the city, cabo the gata and the sea. Due to vertical walls on each side there is almost no no-fall zone.
QNLKTjn.anchorsInfo: Bolted anchors with 3x 12mm bolts (90mm length) in limestone on each side. Bolted in May 2024.
QNLKTjn.accessInfo: Follow the hiking trail that goes up-hill on the right (after the damm) until you end up between the two wall, the access to both anchors is easy and can be done by foot.

Ga4PbqB.name: Jawline

Ga4PbqB.anchorsInfo: 2 Large Douglas fir trees on either side to anchor to, large no fall zone on northeast side

DwBvRZ0.name: 'Bar125'

anchor1 (80m side): straight forward 4 bolts;
anchor2: 5? bolts, one for redirection. use big front boulder for redirection as well. more than 25m slings needed (check out picture)
DwBvRZ0.accessInfo: Easy access by foot, 5 min from parking spot

long line of the devil has 90m cutting the entire civil beach, having an easy access.

Bv0Pygo.description:
Walking during sunset is the best time for taking picture. Bring lots of padding. Anchor: big boulder on the west side need long spanset (3 meter preferably). Boulder on the east side need a raiser.

Bv0Pygo.anchorsInfo:
Anchor: big boulder on the west side need long spanset (3 meter preferably). Boulder on the east side need a raiser.
Bring lots of padding, rock are sharp.

YeqBL8p.name: the gardenline

tfp9OUp.description: Trees, 3m slings needed. Parking lot near by

N7HDilG.description: It's a large field where you can find different configurations for rigging. You can set here a 55m line (180ft). It's a very quiet spot and you can enjoy the nice view on the SuperU market !
N7HDilG.anchorsInfo: One anchor is a big tree (easy to climb, but with a lot of branches which could rub your webbing, so bring protections. Take at least a 2m spanset) and the other one is smaller (a 1m spanset should be enough).
N7HDilG.accessInfo: The spot is close to the “city center”. You can park your bike or car in the street and walk for about 30 seconds.

OZDu8JN.description: A Park with plenty of trees around beside a shopping mall. Easily accessed by the MRT public transport with 2 stations close by.

IMcwS2K.name: Orogenesis  (TWU Longline)

x5lS0ZK.name: Zwierzyniec

iTDHEh3.name: Emsworth 80m

151sfS9.name: Oak Newton Park Longline
151sfS9.description: An easy-to-rig longline with strong tree anchors. There is a broad, low bulge right in the center of the line, which means the line will bottom out sooner than later. There aren't many options for rigging the line higher that are safe for the tree, so height is limited to about 2.3 meters at one of the anchors, and 3.3 at the other. Police have never given us trouble here, and the residents are usually happy to watch. There are a couple of residents that let their dogs shit everywhere in this park, so watch out.
151sfS9.anchorsInfo: Strong oak trees with tough, durable bark. Use 1-2 meter slings with standard tree protection.

9PL26UW.description: If you are blocking public works from mowing the grass or anything, please derig to preserve access! There is also football, baseball and rugby practice here occasionally, they have priority over the field. We used to get kicked out of here all the time, now it is city approved!

Dno_Xmf.name: Cuvette 2

kPPP7kF.name: Bomber

kQbvFM0.description: From palm tree to palm tree

Frequented by slackliner of area.

r99uc8K.anchorsInfo: 3m and 2m sling necessary. A ladder is preferable, as the trees have no branches to climb. Longest line in Tübingen, rigged only once ;D

hUtJ1d3.description:
Line crosses two pathways (one on each end), so keep something handy to mark the tensioned line.
no bicycles in this park. Medium trees. Be tall ;)

hZM2lF1.description: Spansets rigged just above the forks on the trees
hZM2lF1.anchorsInfo: spansets rigged just above the forks on the trees.

pPPZo7o.anchorsInfo: Easy to access trees because of the saggy ground. Still fun to rig a bit higher in the trees ofcourse for added sag.

HII5Fm0.name: Landungssteg
HII5Fm0.description: You can rig 4 lines in this park. 2 small ones, a 40m and an 80m line. The longer lines have a little natural sag (0.7m), which makes them absolutely fantastic to walk. The park is usually very very busy, so you can only slackline in the early hours or on cloudy days. Slacklining is allowed here (approved by the mayor himself), however, noone is really doing it, so people don't know what you are doing. Be aware of bystanders and kids. There is a restaurant right next to the park. Be sure to order a coffee there! The pasta is also great.
HII5Fm0.anchorsInfo: The trees on the far end are really big. You need 4m slings!! The other trees can be slung by 2m slings.

fB1E4lR.name: Victoria Park Longline
fB1E4lR.description: Up to 80m in length, many other lines can be set up. The park can fit any sized line.

17hpKdn.description: make sure to have at least a 10 ft spanset bc u need to anchor a big tree

There is a nice bowl between the two trees that allows setting up the anchors in minimal height. For sag fans, the anchor height is easily adjustable on both trees.

There are no possibilities to rig a beginner line at the spot. 
The spot is surrounded by walking alleys, which are pretty crowded during the sunny days. 

Hammock possible: yesss :)

WZ3Q55_.description: Groupe facebook : Slackline à Lorient Nous contacter pour savoir quand est-ce que nous sommes là !

rm0ll-h.name: Little League
rm0ll-h.description: Awesome longline rig here over some lovely grass spanning between 2 groves of healthy aspen trees. Would highly recommend using a relatively static webbing as tensioning is difficult (but not impossible) with dynamic webbings.
rm0ll-h.anchorsInfo: Each tree can be rigged with 2m spansets + soft or hard shackles. Make sure to bring plenty of tree pro and don't use girth hitches as these are deciduous trees the can be squeezed more easily. I usually rig in a 5:1 pulley system into the rig with static rope and use a removable 9:1 Buckingham pulley system (2 line grips and 3 rollex's) on the webbing.
rm0ll-h.accessInfo: Each aspen tree can be climbed from the ground up without the use of ropes or a harness. May require a piggyback boost depending on how tall you are.

az71Q41.description: Dangerous at the end, hard surface, safely walkable only where grass is

uL0q5NE.accessInfo: Go to Valbonnais or Oris-en-Rattier by car, then follow the path =)

jsNM1jL.description: An anchor is made on tower with rope or link sling .   Other one on the three

czr2fQx.name: Spot de entrene!
czr2fQx.description: Longline in "La Choca", a beautiful park located in the city of Villahermosa
czr2fQx.anchorsInfo: Natural Anchors, you just need ropes or slings to anchor to the big trees, there are many more spots that you can find

AmkCtb-.name: Murcielagoza
AmkCtb-.description: With 135 m is not enough to put cornet to opposite corner, but there are one tree at 120m than you need some tension because the spot have nice high to put longline, because of sag

h6wv96F.description: Dangerous at the end, hard surface, safely walkable only where grass is

cr4GSQ6.description: Easy to park at the shell station and approach the line from there

pIVHbTq.name: Albariño Chongo Mount
pIVHbTq.description: Biload fence on one anchor and iron pool on the other
pIVHbTq.anchorsInfo: Biload fence on one anchor and iron pool on the other

V7CW5KI.name: Commando

V7CW5KI.anchorsInfo: Both are tree anchors. You need to climb one to rig the line but its are pretty easy.
V7CW5KI.accessInfo: Free street parking all around the park!

MSJ255E.name: Ringelhauserpark
MSJ255E.description: about 100m with perfect ground under it. If you like loose lines the anchor points are may be to little.

15T-cpp.description: Gros arbres (platanes), de belles longueurs à poser avec un peu d'imagination, entre 15 et 100m. Pas de cuvette.

GeKf5Tx.description:
Sensible Gegend: Viel Polizei, viel Grünamt. 
IMMER Baumschutz benutzen - IMMER freundlich zu allen Mitarbeitern der Stadt sein. Dann klappt's auch mit den Nachbarn ;)

ZvAzRvc.name: L'inmarchable
ZvAzRvc.description: The line can have a looooot of sag but a part of it is too high. you will have to put the anchors high enough...

fprx8oG.name: Grzesieks home longline in the middle of block :

Sjr_m_W.name: Piscine
Sjr_m_W.description: don't go in winter, the ground is muddy!

HxmrISc.name: XL Lief Eriskon Line
HxmrISc.description: Unfortunately no photos, but looser version of the shorter one. 5ft off the ground at the highest point, crosses right over a manhole cover. Send at your own risk!

hBkv4sP.description: Magnifique spot pour ne pas tendre trop fort : belle cuvette

STk4Y0y.description: only ~2m60 of sag: maybe riggable with very tight low strech line.

pobquBD.description: Sometimes a small group of people plays football besides

to8ru2v.name: Brundage Park 90
to8ru2v.description: Good longline gap if you don't mind keeping it on the tighter side
to8ru2v.anchorsInfo: Beefy trees, 6-8ft spansets or other slings

X4dZR9n.description: If you are blocking public works from mowing the grass or anything, please derig to preserve access! There is also football, baseball and rugby practice here occasionally, they have priority over the field. We used to get kicked out of here all the time, now it is city approved!

ouK-6WC.anchorsInfo: both trees, climb up with a sling

VQl7xTy.name: Calcifers sprinterim
VQl7xTy.description: Decent trees for climbing though bushy higher up, nonetheless a great sandy spot full of sag for plenty of fun tricks, bounces and challengew

PaHykJc.name: Dean's Demise
PaHykJc.description: To set up this line you'll need to contact the campus Slackline organization president due to having to reserve the space. Aside from that, it's a pretty bomber line to walk with the option to set up multiple lines of both shorter/longer lengths and also hammocks.
PaHykJc.anchorsInfo: Regular spansets will do or climbing webbing

BBiGmsC.name: Jedna z regularnie zawieszanych tasiemek w KG

dWmAS__.name: Shek O Beach Longline
dWmAS__.description: Longline right on the beach between the lookouts, only works before lifeguards on duty..
dWmAS__.anchorsInfo: need two 3m spansets and a pulleys system..

utiLEog.name: Ankle Philosophy
utiLEog.description:
There is a big tree just in the corner that you can see on map picture. Use it with protection 

The material you would need it is longlines basics, pulley system, 3 meters slings preferred, shackles..

The weather is hard so use a hat or cap

ZW06lxq.name: Clausthaler Südseestrand
ZW06lxq.description:
Trees, 3m slings needed. Parking lot near by.
2024 - when the weather is good there are quite some people. The lines described is over a path so be aware of that. Cyclists passing over it. The other line is above some bushes so only partially walkable. It's possible to rig a rodeo line / shorterline (see photo) and enjoy swimming :)

j_3g61g.name: Parc des Loges
j_3g61g.description:
This spot is quite good to rig a 75m line (245ft) because it is some kind of a bowl-shaped grass ground. It is easy to access: a bus stops just across. 
Surprisingly, there are not many people on this part of the park, so you could have slackline sessions without a big crowd watching you. Plus, the view of the Carrefour market worth the try !
j_3g61g.anchorsInfo:
The anchors are big trees (take at least 2m spansets).
On one side, it's easy to climb it and set the anchor at any height. 
On the other side, it's less easy : you can use spansets to make some kind of a ladder if you want to rich a good height.
j_3g61g.accessInfo: It's a parc.. You can go there by foot, bike or bus.

uIa5lSJ.description: There is a bank close to the North anchor creating a falling hazard if you try to walk the full line

V6spvdt.description: Shorter of the two lines over the dip

MBv99wh.name: Stade de ergue Armel
MBv99wh.description: La zone peut être occupé par des footeu mais sinon on peut tendre une grande ligne et peut être plusieurs petites en faisant les angles

R-snlbR.name: Ezio Longline
R-snlbR.description: Nice longline in the park, a little bit of a dip but not really enough to make a difference in tension.
R-snlbR.anchorsInfo: Trees for anchors, pine tree at the north end can be rigged. PLEASE USE TREE PRO FOR THIS LINE.

O1En-pK.description: Na środku polany jest wgłębienie terenu dltego nie trzeba wysoko wieszać taśm na stanowiskach. Park Skaryszewski

RNGo43V.name: Elfenau Longline
RNGo43V.description: One of the longest lines in Bern with a lot of natural sag! Wonderful. Can only be rigged when the field is not being used by the farmer. Beware of Cow poop.

DwTSY6d.name: Somewhat long line on the Roßberg
DwTSY6d.description: Nice line. Tensioning side anchor in the forest, preferably 3-4m high. Static side anchor 1.8m high. Perfect natural sag.

lpQDbdA.name: Othello long line
lpQDbdA.description:
North side was a little high at high tension; careful of the movable concrete bricks. I used a 60m and had to extend both anchors a couple feet each. 
Fun line, got attention from the kids :)
lpQDbdA.anchorsInfo: A 12’ (or longer) spanner for south side would be good. I used static rope. Plenty of options north side.

R97ddee.description: The park has some slightly hilly terrain and can be used for longlining quite well. Add each spot if you have rigged here, I have only been here sporadically

A6cEZxV.description: Right by the cannon, can't miss it!

xTGC1FS.description: Boitier électrique accroché à un des deux arbres, pratique pour brancher une sono ou les portables

6Zzke8k.name: Kanavinsky
6Zzke8k.description: 40-65 meters longlines. And cool landscape. Place for exposition.

SU6915P.name: Dune Years Eve
SU6915P.description: Saggy longlines or a stiffer rodeo would do great here!

H3goPE3.description: Nice dip only 2/3 of the way are you high off the ground.

AaS7z9r.name: Russia Dock #1
AaS7z9r.description: Two trees, extra slings to climb them are nice
AaS7z9r.anchorsInfo: Two trees, extra slings to climb them are nice

eFTLiSX.name: de broer van het beest
eFTLiSX.description:
Send a message to me when you want to slackline here:
+31 6 15595439

_4XGOQp.description: be carefull withe the appletrees(best is to have one a frame with out about 1,4m high) and don't make loud musik.
_4XGOQp.anchorsInfo: one massiv tree and one apple tree. it is important to have a loose line, best to have one a frame with you

kKHjAXe.name: Hilly Fields
kKHjAXe.description:
Been there many times and honestly it's a lovely spot on a hill.
I got stopped from a council guy once saying I couldn't but out of many many times.
Plenty of options for shorter lines as well in the same spot. Let's gather there!

kCDhX6o.description: A really good spot and easy to rig. The ground has a natural sag around 5 m and you are never walking higher than 1.5 m with the right tension.
kCDhX6o.anchorsInfo: Trees in each side. Anchor hight is around 1.5 m.

K4NgRFn.description: Line established at drill and chill festival 2017

F_IfLJy.name: Kirchfeldwiese
F_IfLJy.description: Nice sag of 3m. Has a nice dip so it is not high!

78rX1Li.name: Parc des Loges
78rX1Li.description:
This spot is quite flat to rig a 50m line (165ft), so you have to put some tension on the line or to set the anchors high enough. It is easy to access: a bus stops just across. 
Surprisingly, there are not many people on this part of the park, so you could have slackline sessions without a big crowd watching you.
78rX1Li.anchorsInfo: The anchors are big trees (take at least 2m spansets), both easy to be climbed.
78rX1Li.accessInfo: It's a parc.. You can go there by foot, bike or bus.

pzeVGhv.description: If you are blocking public works from mowing the grass or anything, please derig to preserve access! There is also football, baseball and rugby practice here occasionally, they have priority over the field. We used to get kicked out of here all the time, now it is city approved!

nSMoJFs.name: swan line
nSMoJFs.description: beautiful little area. tons of swans in the mystic right by this spot. super chill place to come hang. also, there's a branch to help you get up on to the line if you don't like doing the Highline method,
nSMoJFs.anchorsInfo: tree, super simple. can just do the friction method with a few carabiners
nSMoJFs.accessInfo: there's a branch to help you get up.

lsGUkVb.description:
max long line 90-100m   
possible to built a Rodeo

wH_xRYB.name: Meta-Connection
wH_xRYB.description: Perfect slope so that the line can be set up at less than 50cm on both sides so that even beginners can try the longline without risk. A lot of people watching and passing by. Perfect sunsets, a lake to cool down and the huge dorm complex close by create a wonderful atmosphere...

ISTVp-7.name: Moston
ISTVp-7.description: Across the field, anchoring from the large tree to one of the smaller ones
ISTVp-7.anchorsInfo: Big slings to wrap around the large tree.

S-AjM5u.name: Menemen
S-AjM5u.description: Nice line betwwen two pines over the sand
S-AjM5u.accessInfo:
You can get here by car, 20 meters walking from car :D It's 15 minutes from Belek. 

Not sure it's possible to get here by bus

29WtjuR.name: Charcosa
29WtjuR.description: Perfect to put mora than 80 m, this park Have  showerdesign, you can profit this shape and use The corners and put a little high. But not tu much, enough to don't need much tension. The trees in side the area there aren't so strong, so you coul put you slack on rodeo, using trees more close by themselves.

CFRjWVv.name: Maschpark
CFRjWVv.description: possible only in winter, otherwise very crowded...

ZENn7ep.name: Fette Beute
ZENn7ep.description: Almost 200m with 4m of Sag, easy to rig. Just waiting for someone to walk :)

T_Pis68.name: Bonn journée
T_Pis68.description: super easy to access and rig with polyester webbing because of the natural sag. I rigged it with hangover/linegrip 3:1 + soft release

iJaaS-i.name: Piscine
iJaaS-i.description: don't go in winter, the ground is muddy!

eGs8Pul.name: Muddy Glory

eGs8Pul.anchorsInfo: 2 metre slings fit round the trees

Asgw66M.name: Kinburn 40

O6Hxeod.description: Longline spot, possiblie to tension by car.
O6Hxeod.anchorsInfo: >3m, small hills on both ends of the line

wSgzdTQ.name: Numeric Village
wSgzdTQ.description: For the moment the line is not creat. Is it needed to had a point in the rocks.

8mMnc-B.description: Cars can be parked inside the grass area. Tree Anchors

D4d164P.description: Line on a slope, but a good option for the northerly winds. big tree at the south end needs a big sling to climb

qG2Cyd6.description: Magnifique spot pour ne pas tendre trop fort : belle cuvette

vGSkTCj.description:
Waterline 35m, Great Spot, You'll need long slings or rope for anchors
https://www.facebook.com/groups/slacklinethess/

L0g6jnw.description: Not entirely walkable (safely) due to a no fall zone at the beginning of the line (from the north side.)  Otherwise, there is a nice dip so you don't have to go too high.

te5tf6l.name: Panzerwiese
te5tf6l.description:
Länge der Longline variabel von 40 bis oben offen.
Kurze Lines können auch gespannt werden.

UBcEKnR.name: Lambchop long line

irBj9Kw.description: Nice longline spot on the soccer field, easy to rig because one of the trees is standing on a hill.
irBj9Kw.anchorsInfo: Solid tree anchors, one is on a 3,5m high hill, so rigging and tensioning is easy.

RkNopUk.description: A lot of trees and endless possibilities to rig a line. From 5m up to 70m or even longer, everything is possible. Join us :)

vXG2pdl.name: Zachs dead kids

vXG2pdl.accessInfo: Park in sugarbottom recreation area parking lot. Group of 3 trees in the water. Use closer smaller tree and opposite anchor is skinny tree 7 feet into the brush

BdbI2kL.description: Choice of lines ranging from 60m to 95m depending on which trees you choose. All over a big natural dip.

VeU2r8D.description: Testing new spot, many tries no sends
VeU2r8D.anchorsInfo: trees both sides, but on the middle are elecricity cabels on the air and they are to close, this is not good spot for rigging lines and it probaby should not be rerigged

cuAxYVz.name: 150 footer
cuAxYVz.description: Thick trees, need at least 10 ft anchors
cuAxYVz.anchorsInfo: Thick trees, need at least 10 ft anchors

NCLNc0K.description: A nice park in the middle of the suburbs with lines that can be rigged from about 10m - 80m
NCLNc0K.anchorsInfo: fat trees, 2m-3m slings are ideal for all lines

MxVtSvg.name: Skate Park
MxVtSvg.description: Nice spot to set up a long line with more silence.

qjQN7Zz.description: parallèle à la 50m, ancrage Sud en commun

nl8MaPN.name: Step to your loo, while I do what I do best
nl8MaPN.description:
nice line because the ground is raised on side, so the anchor can be walked off of on one side, but you need a 4m sling for that tree
(from the photo you would want to go off of the far right tree)

klV2KjT.name: Way up
klV2KjT.description: nice and easy to rig. in this rodeo waterline you have a sag of around 2 meter. the thing here is, the static side is a tree on a little hill and he is 3/4m above the level of the rigging tree
klV2KjT.accessInfo:
by car, bike or walk. very easy to get there. 
fast and save.

yuqvgXm.name: Windy One
yuqvgXm.description:
Windy. Usually cut.
Not many people on this field.

TXaEmol.name: Verdant vibes
TXaEmol.description: Line goes over a path but the path is never used and overgrown. Rigged with rubber band and core 2 LS.
TXaEmol.anchorsInfo: 2 metre slings fit round the trees

8xUJ1aU.name: Forsland park big boy
8xUJ1aU.description: Awesome longline that follows the length of the parks valley
8xUJ1aU.anchorsInfo: trees on both sides. BRING TREE PRO

99fsxIN.description: ligne asser longue peu de passage seul défaut, on est près d'une route passante :)

muLAwWy.name: Arletta idzie do domu
muLAwWy.description: Arletta is going home, last try :)

v5rbqw_.description: Line set up every year during the Transalp Waterline Tour.

rKdtpMm.description: Local park. Free parking. Rig within sight of your vehicle!

_kxkUQs.name: Soggy Bottom Boys
_kxkUQs.description: Two tree's at a school park. Very slight upward bulge near one of the trees means less saggy lines work better here.

UhfLVWK.description:
max long line 90-100m   
possible to built a Rodeo

bxbMe-r.description: Nice little bowl allowing for a loose line

NZuGmCS.description: Low tension is possible due to a natural hollow

Q-uBb76.name: 101 Nacht
