LUWJMGE.description: Palm tree to palm tree, easy
LUWJMGE.anchorsInfo: Palm tree to palm tree, easy.

qBnzylA.name: megaline
qBnzylA.description: Great line, really enjoyable with this massive sag, not so many tension in the line

6XtCKOh.name: Pbslacklining.com
6XtCKOh.description: This was my first line in Hereford that no one cares about. You can slack in peace if not with a few spectators.

MHtObut.name: Soggy bottoms
MHtObut.description: Nice line but kindof soggy ground. Fun little longline location. Good view of the lake.

9koPzns.name: Mocambo
9koPzns.description: Two big perfect palms on grass. Public beach
9koPzns.anchorsInfo: two big perfect palms on grass

r6qSHt0.name: The Real Mr. Blue Sky
r6qSHt0.description:
A small bowl with plenty of lines of varying length.
Slacklining is legal on NMU Property, but please use tree pro.

H5YNeSa.name: Hofgarten Ansbach
H5YNeSa.description: The west anchor must be place on tree at approx 4m height to level the 2m above ground east anchor
H5YNeSa.anchorsInfo: The west anchor must be place on tree at approx 4m height to level the 2m above ground east anchor

epByPHt.name: Parque roma
epByPHt.description: Facil de instalar, perfecta al tener un rocódromo al lado

EdH1GoM.name: Beer Passion
EdH1GoM.description:
Actually 67m
Really calm area, nobody to disturb you except some friendly beer addicts trying to motivate you!

WYYrLT1.name: Another beast
WYYrLT1.description: Trees. Open access via Bury Knowles park
WYYrLT1.accessInfo: Open access via Bury Knowles park

TDw0PEB.description: On the map the tree seems near but in reality it is far enough. The ground is a bit bumpy near the tree on the road but if you use this side for the pulley system it's a good line.

y0V_bs5.description: Big trees with a "bowl shaped" spot, so no need to rig too high.

E38CENP.name: Backside of Colwood golfcourse
E38CENP.description:
Some climbing on the eastern tree.  Western trees needs the anchor as high as you can reach.  Some way to get the anchor a couple feet higher will maximize the sag.  

Some times very tall grass and weeds are around. Trees

9P4Fmf3.description: Pas de cuvette donc difficile à tendre et encrages trés hauts

Z4_g1uw.description: Security guards may give you trouble but they have no reason to tell you to take the line down.
Z4_g1uw.anchorsInfo: a two meter spanset can be used on either end. Be sure to use tree protection. To place the spanset on the south side, climb the tree and stand on the fence. Be careful not to fall.

OVzzm8C.name: Johannisthaler card tables
OVzzm8C.description: Pretty out of the way from folks. This park has lots of potential but long lining might be in the way of folks on a weekend day. There are a few lines that could be rigged on the north side of the park.
OVzzm8C.anchorsInfo: East side tree is pretty large. At least 2.5m sling/tree pro to wrap.

hiPPgAP.description:
For details contact Andrei or Matei from Slacksylvania
 slacksylvania@gmail.com or on fb https://www.facebook.com/slacksylvania/

KWWbUL4.name: "We thought you were hovering, then saw the line"

QmnhL0U.name: The Pooey
QmnhL0U.description: Walked with Rubber Band and Core 2 LS, really nice spot with tree anchors
QmnhL0U.anchorsInfo: 2 metre slings are large enough to get round the trees

WOzfemC.name: longest possible line in the park
WOzfemC.description: Crappy thorny tree closest to the corner of the park by the light, all the way past the bathrooms to the split tree. use BOTH sides of the tree equalized for this rig. nice sag on this one
WOzfemC.anchorsInfo: crappy thorny tree closest to the corner of the park by the light, all the way past the bathrooms to the split tree. use BOTH sides of the tree equalized for this rig. nice sag on this one.

NkWF7eH.name: Schützenmatte
NkWF7eH.description: Longline with really little access issues. The tree to the left is kinda hard to climb, bring an extra sling.

Zh4UWXV.name: Les Taillés
Zh4UWXV.description: One of the trees is hard to climb!

KgGntDG.name: creekwalk
KgGntDG.accessInfo: please mark line with caution tape/scarves down the length

2CnTEvd.name: Hawrelak Park longline
2CnTEvd.description: Second biggest line in the park, biggest that is for sure riggable. Needs high tension so bring some big pulleys if you want to rig this
2CnTEvd.anchorsInfo: trees on both sides. BRING TREE PRO

uAbn4XW.name: Calm & quiet 40m spot, easily rigged with a primitive system

-U4Ybiw.description: Magnifique spot pour ne pas tendre trop fort : belle cuvette

MBDhVhW.name: 77 Fontaine
MBDhVhW.description: The parc close at 9pm in summer (but the gardian can be late :-) )

Qb9TcYJ.name: Mudford Recreation Ground
Qb9TcYJ.description: The council only allow longlines in this area of the park, lots of options but try to use bright wind dampeners incase of cyclists

U0zfUhc.name: Karreveld
U0zfUhc.description: Lovely parc with perfect slope to have a nice constant distance from the ground

5ZRtHLG.description: Nice park location. Walking from the shade into the sunlight :)

_xkG7MQ.description:
Longline ex Military Camp Pavlou Mela,SKG
https://www.facebook.com/groups/slacklinethess/

vGgAVpQ.description: Solid tree anchors, at least 3m spansets necessary
vGgAVpQ.anchorsInfo: Solid tree anchors, at least 3m spansets necessary.

eD6WOSl.description: deep water from beginning to end

x7Xac2A.description: It's a large field where you can find different configurations for rigging. You can set here a 105m line (345ft). It's a quiet spot and the Tachen pub is very close to it (if somehow beverages are needed..).
x7Xac2A.anchorsInfo: One anchor is a big tree (easy to climb, but with a lot of branches which could rub your webbing, so bring protections. Take at least a 2m spanset) and the other one is smaller (a 1m spanset is enough).
x7Xac2A.accessInfo: The spot is close to the “city center”. You can park your bike or car in the street and walk for about 30 seconds.

io7IzW5.name: Kareivukai
io7IzW5.description: More or less secluded spot with concave landscape allowing for some nice loose lines. Needs just a bit of pretensioning through weblock to work, no additional tensioning gear if you get anchors high enough on the trees.
io7IzW5.anchorsInfo: Both are pine trees. For loose line you have to you a sling hitched around the tree as a stepping aid to make anchor just high enough.

Nyg9Umi.name: Stur Rec Longline
Nyg9Umi.description: Good sized longline near the midline..

4zfhzt8.name: Longline in Muskingum Park
4zfhzt8.description: Nice long-ish line area with a slight dip in the center. Normally set up about 2-2.25 meters high at one anchor, but less than 2 meters at other. Can be setup higher or lower depending on amount of sag.

R1RfAiX.name: Ride The Elk
R1RfAiX.description: Nice longline spot for highlining practice. This rig features a lot of sag so be ready for a loose walk.
R1RfAiX.anchorsInfo: Trees on either side. 1 to 2m spansets will work for anchors.

aGBh6Ov.name: Franz Park
aGBh6Ov.description: Cool spot to work on your progression. The ground has a fish bowl shape to it so you can set your anchors about 6 - 7 feet high on the trees and you will only be a couple feet off the ground. I usually rig it with mantra webbing and I usually have it kinda loose. Awesome spot overall though!

_eaLZGK.name: Duo is watching
_eaLZGK.description: 60 meter midline over a pond in a park. The line has a beautiful view on the duo building.
_eaLZGK.anchorsInfo: Tree anchors. Most obvious ones. One big fat one on one side and one that is a bit overgrown at the bottom.

QkXa-s-.name: Half Cut Log Line
QkXa-s-.description: While not much different than the Feta Cheese line, it does however provide more space between the line and the central tree. While walking the tag line lookout for the fallen tree that is cut halfway though! While clearing some brush after finding the line, the tree was mostly cut through and then abandoned because of being ti ti.
QkXa-s-.anchorsInfo: All lines here are anchored with bomber trees. Bring whatever gear you need to go tree to tree.
QkXa-s-.accessInfo: Park just after the driveway on the road near by. Then hike down into the ditch a trail should present itself. The lines will be a short 1 to 2 minute hike and the lines will be on the left. However one of the anchors of this line will be on the right side of the trail. Not a high traffic trail so don't worry about blocking it!

23Cr8as.name: CoronaMid
23Cr8as.description: Natural anchors on trees. By feet, and leader would help
23Cr8as.accessInfo: by feet, and leader would help

g4Nki-o.name: The big dream came true
g4Nki-o.description: Nice training Spot in Seih sou forest in Thesaloniki. Easy accesible by car or by foot. Place offers midline from 15m to around 50m long. Nearby there is water spring.
g4Nki-o.anchorsInfo: Rig is super easy from tree to tree I have used 2x2m spansets
g4Nki-o.accessInfo: Look for a walking path M2 from the road. And then place is before view point with bench

8Ln1xU3.name: Midline sfinge
8Ln1xU3.description: To built this line you have to climb a medium difficult boulder over the wather on the south side of the bay .

KGS4BXP.restriction:
partial: Contact the owner for more info.
https://www.facebook.com/search/top?q=k%C3%A4ppeli
KGS4BXP.anchorsInfo: Side A (near store hut): Bolts to the ground. Airframe and long sling needed
KGS4BXP.accessInfo: Easy access. Private property. Contact owner first

lxKqcwK.name: Saimaa Dreaming
lxKqcwK.description: Nice water midline spot,  not far away from Lappeenranta city centrum.  Only bad thing in daytime is traffic noise from the road next to the spot. And remember to watch out those high sailing boats. We were camping in the same spot and It's legal to stay there few days,  If you stay longer I think it's not a problem :)  There's a lots of space to park a car just next to the line. I'm living in Lappeenranta and you can reach me from number +358456346079 if you need some information or help :)  Markus.
lxKqcwK.anchorsInfo: 4 rst glue in bolts both sides.  4/8m sling works really good
lxKqcwK.accessInfo: 8km from Lappeenranta centrum.  Bridge next from line is called Kirjamoin bridge. Road next to the spot is called Taipalsaaren road (408)

LvxU5no.name: JackFruit and Tofu
LvxU5no.description:
Southernmost Midline In the Continental US! 

Access is pretty good but you do pass a “no trespassing” sign when getting close to the line. Park at the mountain biking trailhead and walk along the main mountain biking trail until you see an opening on the left. Walk across the small land bridge into a large canopy opening.
LvxU5no.anchorsInfo: 8 or 10 foot Spansets do just fine
LvxU5no.accessInfo: The Line is from the 2 Largest Cypress Trees with Big Y’s. Send a tag line up around the main split in the 2 trees and attach an static rope with a carabiner on a Bite so you have a fixed line (You can leave the tag line attached so you can easily De-Rig). Jumar up the rope until you can easily climb the branches.

LlvwWPm.name: plan B
LlvwWPm.description: several lines are possible all over the longer bridge to the middle of the short one, if you rig from top it will be harder to set up but you gain about 3m. of height. if you rig longer than 100m you should go up or rig something with super low stretch the shortest is 100m. longest is about 140m.

qcNGoYk.name: Zufall
qcNGoYk.description: Found the line by accident. Long an nice midline to dip a little bit into the lake. The spot is quite obvious.
qcNGoYk.anchorsInfo:
Farside: Spruce tree with a little bit cleaned branches, 1,5m spanset.

Tensioning side: Huge beech tree, 2m spanset.

Just walk along the lakeside for tagging (or swim:)).

BBQ5kMK.name: 70m
BBQ5kMK.description: Easy rig highlines in a forest, there are mulitiple lines to rig here and we have permission to go here from Stadt Zurich
BBQ5kMK.accessInfo: There is a bus stop in witikon called Loorenstrasse about 5min walk from the spot.

LikjE_q.name: Super High Enough
LikjE_q.description: Highlining over or near Hidden Beach. Park on the side of CR 550, follow trail to the lake shore. 5 minute approach.
LikjE_q.anchorsInfo: All anchors are off of trees. Please use tree pro!

4W0AehT.name: Carlos Sampaio
4W0AehT.description: Climb up the trees to get a safe height. Trees

O0pNyAU.name: Ljubljanica midline
O0pNyAU.description: City center midline. Sometimes rigged by slackliners for a lot of attention. Boats are constantly driving underneath and you should be careful not to fall.

VsuMO7W.name: Mam Ser
VsuMO7W.description: Short and low highline for begginers with easy accees and nice exposure.
VsuMO7W.anchorsInfo: Climbing rings + one old climbing bomber ring on one sight, about 5m slings for 3 bolts needed. Second anchor is on the tree, 1,5m sling should fit.

trk1nov.name: El Tendido Secreto
trk1nov.description: Beautiful sights and really enjoyable hike to the line, but it's a really protected area, so it's quite ilegal.
trk1nov.anchorsInfo: Vegan anchors on both sides, difficult access to both.

KlGvlb5.name: The bees knees
KlGvlb5.description: because of branches there's really only 2 possible trees for anchors one being the pecan tree down in the valley area and the other the oak tree up on the hill by the bike path
KlGvlb5.anchorsInfo: Natural anchors on pecan tree and oak tree

zGo5UjH.name: 2long2low
zGo5UjH.description: Pretty much the longest line in the Hollow (for now)..

USPyxBh.description: All lines in this cove must be rigged natural. Both of the anchors are boulder wraps. Static anchor requires a 10' spanset. The tensioning anchor requires 40' of static rope
USPyxBh.anchorsInfo: All lines in this cove must be rigged natural. Both of the anchors are boulder wraps. Static anchor requires a 10' spanset. The tensioning anchor requires 40' of static rope.

PbYYIwM.name: Midline Belle Époque
PbYYIwM.description:
Midline established on 07/18/2020 by the team: Leandro Barroncas, Beatriz, Doilson, Priscila Almeida, Yana, Luan, Shox, Estevão and Maria.
Spot location: Parque Residencial Mestre Chico, Ponte 07 de Setembro, downtown Manaus district.
The spot is located on the promenade of a public park. When we set up the line, there were no impediments by third parties. However, as the place is a public space, there may eventually be inspection by the city hall or the military police. In this case, the dialogue for the authorization of the sport must be emphasized.
Any questions, you can contact via facebook or instagram: Leandro Barroncas.

Midline aberto no dia 18/07/2020 pelo time: Leandro Barroncas, Beatriz, Doilson, Priscila Almeida, Yana, Luan, Shox, Estevão e Maria.
Localização do pico: Parque Residencial Mestre Chico, Ponte 07 de setembro, bairro Centro de Manaus. 
O pico localiza-se no passeio de um parque público. Na ocasião em que abrimos a linha, não houve impedimentos por terceiros. Porém, como o lugar é um espaço público, eventualmente pode haver fiscalização pela prefeitura ou polícia militar. Nesse caso, deve-se frisar o diálogo para a autorização da prática do esporte.
Quaisquer dúvidas, pode contatar via facebook ou instagram: Leandro Barroncas.
PbYYIwM.anchorsInfo:
Anchorage A involving the metallic structure of the bridge. Anchorage B of the natural type involving the branch of a large tree.
The B side (tree) offers different options to arm the tensioning system of the line (ex: pulleys + linegrip).
Below the bridge it is possible to set up longline lines of about 25m.

Ancoragem A envolvendo a estrutura metálica da ponte. Ancoragem B do tipo natural envolvendo o galho de uma arvore de grande porte.
O lado B (arvore) oferece diferentes opções para armar o sistema de tensionamento da linha (ex: polias + linegrip).
Abaixo da ponte é possível armar linhas de longline de cerca de 25m.

-E5pl3J.description:
max long line 90-100m   
possible to built a Rodeo

B1V-CMC.name: Conkouati line
B1V-CMC.description: Little midline in the conkouati national park
B1V-CMC.anchorsInfo: Tree, one tree is weak, I added a pike in the grass + A frame

O2_5t4X.description: Tidal. Large docking bollards. The fences have been used as A frames
O2_5t4X.anchorsInfo: Large docking bollards. The fences have been used as A frames.

xUjCCVa.name: Pantagana
xUjCCVa.description: It's a small bay with 7 Sommer houses. The locals tolerated me. Some houses are for rent. It's easy to make the tagline connection and rigging.
xUjCCVa.accessInfo:
By car use the parking lot 200n in front of the Sajitja bay. It's a small road. 
Or come by boat.

LgP5OST.name: Teerhofbrücke
LgP5OST.description:
Great line for beginners, easy rig. Tie in while standing behind the railing, then slide out. 
Possibly restricted. The buildings are private property and the security guard wasn't quite sure how to handle us during our first rig. he let us be in the end though. 
Dependent on water level, high tide might just get your feet wet, otherwise super fine. 
Very visible, you will have a ton of people watching you, the bridge is quite frequented.
Length can vary as you can rig farther back or closer to the bridge. Everything from 30 - 60m should be possible.
LgP5OST.anchorsInfo:
Bridge side: Railing bolted with 4xM16 (I think)
Buildings side: Also railing, also bolted with 4xM16. 
You can either rig super close to the ground (super good enough) or maybe also at the height of the handles, which we haven't tried yet (Dec 2020).

AH7V-NJ.name: Midline auf dem Sand
AH7V-NJ.description: Small midline across a creek. Be careful, the ground is not very secure. Don't rig the trees right at the edge. The tensioning side tree is on a plateau. Tight backup necessary!!
AH7V-NJ.accessInfo: Park your car at the Sand. Get back to the main street, get across. Then walk around the "hole" and up the hill. There is a small bench, the path takes a bend to the left. Descent into the forest. The line is across the creek. You can rappell down that slide and if you don't mind getting dirty climb up the other side.

ajg_laQ.name: Ere's Line
ajg_laQ.description: Rigged once and unlikely to ever be rigged again. Not high enough to recommend trying safely. For a better line check out the line "Scenikc" just next to this one.
ajg_laQ.restriction: partial: This spot is a retired water reservoir and a premier graffiti destination. The gates are locked but often the locks are broken, be mindful of the unhoused folks & tweakers that wander in.

kunI5kE.name: Grünes Wohnzimmer
kunI5kE.description:
OMG is this place beautiful.
peacefully and complete in the woods you can set up a awesome 55m line. surrounded by birds singing for you, you can enjoy this lovely line
kunI5kE.anchorsInfo: on both sides you have trees. the rigging side you need a 3m long sling and the static side needs a 4 or better 5m sling (big tree).
kunI5kE.accessInfo:
when you come from "Schluchstrasse" you can walk up the stairs on the right side. walk beside the valley on the left side and you will see the spot between the leafs. please watch you step. it`s not so high but no one want to fall an break some bones. the rigging side is on the south side and you have a nice place there where you can put all your stuff and rigg the line. to come there you jump over a small fence (1m high). take some mosquito protection with you :)
there is no shop or water nearby, you have to bring food and water with you.

qyGplbi.name: Fantômas
qyGplbi.description:
Nice midline over a small waterfall.
Possibility to put other lines between 15m and 35m.
qyGplbi.accessInfo: Go to the village of Ponthaux and then take the road "Route de Montagny". Follow the road until you arrive at the forest edge, park there. Take the bike path down to the river (5 min).

mBpiI_u.description: longer version. if loose, you whip in to the water. quite fun.
mBpiI_u.anchorsInfo: one side is rounded concrete piedestal (appx 6 m o f spansets), second is tree (3 m of spansets)

_ivfruS.name: Fortino (forte sofia)
_ivfruS.description:
For one lock you have to enter into fotino . To made this you Climb wall and enter in the structure .
On the other side you climb a three

pJx6U92.description: A shorte rmidline, which is around 10-12 meters high. Easy to rig.

NtKuOzs.name: Spring has sprung
NtKuOzs.description: Short line as you walk in the park.. NOT RECOMMENDED TO RIG, too low. If you mainline failed you are likely to hit the ground.

oJDY461.name: Linha do Mirante
oJDY461.description:
This is one of the many lines conquered by me and my partner while traveling all over Brasil in search for spots to put up the most incredible Highlines.  If you want to get to know this spot or any of the others in Bahia, get in touch with us for more information.
Follow on instagram at @_desinfluente and @juliabluu to check out some of our other adventures conquering Highlines all over Brasil.

uDpm0mO.name: No grites en el bosque
uDpm0mO.description: It's near to the Golfcamp, close to an abandoned house, it's hidden but prefere don't be to loud,
uDpm0mO.anchorsInfo:
One side 2m spanset, if backup 5m rope
Other side from the A frame 6m to a tree, at least 12m rope perfect 18m
uDpm0mO.accessInfo: It's in cañamel, just walk at the side of the river, 10min, easy rig, can leave the line fore a few days,

J3Rcyi7.name: Luna
J3Rcyi7.description: Luna. Midline de 30mts de largo y 12mts de altura en punto más alto. Un anclaje es narutal, va un árbol de 2.5mts de circunfencia; el otro anclaje son 4 bolts, se debe escalar o bien hacer un rapel para acceder a él.
J3Rcyi7.anchorsInfo: We use a tree as an anchor on one side, and four bolts on the other anchor.
J3Rcyi7.accessInfo: You can climb or just do a rappel on one anchor. Climbing is easier, the climbing level of the route is 5.9. On the other anchor you just have to walk.

mCgmtA7.name: 280 bpm
mCgmtA7.description: Beautiful line over the beach, easy rigging through laying out the webbing on the beach and just pulling it up on both sides with taglines. No mobile service at the whole beach.
mCgmtA7.anchorsInfo: Natural rigging, wrapping around a boulder on both sides. Lots of padding needed.

t1AGBBn.description: Line set up every year during the Transalp Waterline Tour.

q6l81pk.name: Seebach Midline
q6l81pk.restriction: partial: contact a member of Slacklions
q6l81pk.accessInfo: easy access from the top of the hill

KcSP0Am.name: Segnali di Fumo
KcSP0Am.description:
Tree anchors both sides
5 3m sling, A frame
KcSP0Am.anchorsInfo:
Tree anchors both sides
5 3m sling, A frame

MzCDNsP.name: Vermišelis
MzCDNsP.description: Impressive midline spot above Šventoji river at Mikieriu horseshoe shaped outcrop.
MzCDNsP.anchorsInfo: Both sides are pine trees. One requires a little bit of climbing.

l5ZmAdC.name: Chleb, kwiaty i Gazeta Wyborcza
l5ZmAdC.description: Nice training midline. Super easy acces with possibility to rig upper beley. 21 or 24m long
l5ZmAdC.anchorsInfo: Concrete posts on both sides. 1,5m Sling is best.
l5ZmAdC.accessInfo: No security found. Acces by stairs.

vkT-T0q.name: Sprinklerville
vkT-T0q.description: Decent midline with nice grass and good views. Park behind the school for easiest access. Trash can and picnic tables but bathrooms are usually closed.
vkT-T0q.anchorsInfo: Aspen trees; each side can be rigged using 2m spansets.

v-Rm3KE.name: In Goja della Peace
v-Rm3KE.anchorsInfo: Contact the association Torino sul filo, to know exactly how to rig the line

BkuKpqW.name: White Lightning
BkuKpqW.description: Park at Elephant rock for close access. It's above a flash flood zone. Sandy riverbed with rocks underneath. It's situated nicely along the Arkansas River which draws in many kayakers, climbers and adventures of all sorts.
BkuKpqW.anchorsInfo: Rock anchors with tree back ups.

uVgS-4m.name: Xavantinho
uVgS-4m.description: Midline ideal para iniciantes localizado no Setor de Escalada Esportiva Pedra Branca no Parque Natural Municipal das Andorinhas.
uVgS-4m.restriction: partial: Parque Natural Municipal - Horário de funcionamento 8h00 AM às 18h00 PM
uVgS-4m.accessInfo: fácil acesso, buscar informações na sede do parque

Xf5DgoL.description: Next to a footpath so you may get the occasional dog walker saying 'is it a tightrope?'

gi7d9af.name: Smooth As Silk
gi7d9af.description:
12mm hangars and bolts required. Bolts placed in solid rock faces a few metres back from exposure, below level of line. On last rig, eyelets were placed and used to rig tensioning system. Line must be rigged tight to avoid decking out in the event of gear failure. Double webbing recommended. 
Easy access to both sides of exposure, long haul up steep hill to the spot, flat ground for camping either side. Beware of loose rock and harsh weather conditions; the spot is an old quarry littered with boulders and loose rock, and is very open to the elements.
The spot is technically on private land, but due to high level of climbing activity in the area, no known problems have been raised by the landowners. Spot is used locally by dirtbike motorcross riders.

gpdARmy.name: Ibergerslack
gpdARmy.description: In the middle of a climbing area chli scheien, reachable in 20min from the Ibergeregg pass where you can park.
gpdARmy.anchorsInfo: bolted on both sides. One side is a tower,2a scramble to get up, here you need to have 2-3m slings to get over the edge and extend. Otherside is a wall, where you just need 1m sling to connect the two bolts.

8YwKg0r.name: Mark of Zoro
8YwKg0r.description: Tree anchors. Rigged but not yet walked to my knowledge.

YONAa8q.name: Galerieline
YONAa8q.description: Easy to connect, about on houre to rick the line.
YONAa8q.accessInfo: The anchorpoint is on top of the gallerie.

8cDXIse.description: Small forest of Gum Trees located next to a dirt jump park. Many options for lines.

5sd7uVr.name: LoopToLoopWonder
5sd7uVr.description: Possibility for the longest casual LoopToLoop rigg I‘ve expirienced.
5sd7uVr.restriction: partial: Those lines are higher then 25m, so they need to be announced at BAZL by SwissHighline. Your contact there for the region: ramun@swiss-highline.ch
5sd7uVr.anchorsInfo:
Riggingside:
4x 12mm stainless steel Raumer Glue-In bolts
1x 10m staticrope for BFK
2x 2.2m Whoopie for backup
Padding
Shakles for your rigg and so on

Farside:
Same
5sd7uVr.accessInfo: Park at the parkings next to the old pass road or at the lake Rodont.

xCTJFwk.anchorsInfo:
Both anchors are thick eucalyptus trees.
It is possible to throw a bag over the branches, or use slings to climb
xCTJFwk.accessInfo:
East bank of the river can be reached by a boat or floating mattress, great for camping.

West bank has a gate and "no entry" sign. We ignore both

qyw3ew_.name: The Biggest Fish
qyw3ew_.description: Before rigg the midline you must ask for permission. There is a phone numer on the shed's doors. WARNING: The owners of area speak only in Polish. The best idea it's ask for someone who live close to this area in Slackline Kraków group on Facebook
qyw3ew_.anchorsInfo: Two very solid trees, 2,5-3m slings to both sides.

xfMDjfo.name: Peñaciña
xfMDjfo.anchorsInfo: Natural anchor on rock. The rocks measure approximately 1.5 m in diameter.
xfMDjfo.accessInfo: by foot, 50 m from the square

GzH5dTS.description: A super beautiful line above a little creek. Fixed on top of a granite boulder block with 3 bolts on one side (make an extension of about 2-3 meters with your slings to really leave the block- the hangers are left on the block) and an electricity pillar on the other side.

otMlfIl.description:
Super easy line. 10 minutes from the Sleepy Bay car park. Amazing rock. Nice place to spend a rest day.
3 10mm bolts each side, no hangers.
The left side (closer o the car park) requires abseiling ~15m rope. Use a massive stone on top as natural anchor for this.
Easy access to the right side.
Crew: Stepan Novikov, Alexandra Boogdanova

3jp7ukq.name: Rainline
3jp7ukq.description: Line spot above rain runoff collection reservoir. Nice and neat environment with cool exposures.
3jp7ukq.anchorsInfo: Both anchors are ok for tie-ins. One tree requires some climbing, but is very easy.

JsdoIIc.name: salam
JsdoIIc.description: bolted by local slackline association, slackline brescia asd
JsdoIIc.anchorsInfo: west side 4 bolts need to repel to reach the ancor, est side tree
JsdoIIc.accessInfo: right over the classics climbing routes, very easy to find

7Er3X_n.name: Christchurch Firefighters
7Er3X_n.description: Midline. Natural anchors both side. Sketchy line, very close to cliff on one side. 1st Send Fer Kpok. Backup must be tight.
7Er3X_n.accessInfo: On the way to Jane Fonda climbing Crag before starting to walk down the hill.

CQQohQ7.name: Limeslade Line
CQQohQ7.description: A nice small mid line. over water at high tide, over rocks at low tide. 4 bolts either side, but they are a bit old.
CQQohQ7.anchorsInfo: 4 bolts on either side, but they are a bit old. there is good trad gear around, which you may want to use as backup.

r0wWqjU.name: Encendiendo la moto
r0wWqjU.description:
The right side anchor is at the top of a climbing zone. We usually rig with the boulder at the top without using the climbing anchor.

At the other side there are multiples boulders too to create your anchor

0VUrPqI.name: Port of Call
0VUrPqI.description: Rocky underneath, unless at hightide. Easy walk around and access.
0VUrPqI.anchorsInfo: big boulders on right hand side take a full 2m spanset each, 1 for mainline one for back up, left side equalised slings (used 2x2m) and back up further back on another sling (use of protection or extension ideal. All natural anchors, and multiple options

QrdPkEi.description: Мидлайн с перепадом высоты 10 - 20 метров. В сторону озера открывается очень красивый вид.
QrdPkEi.anchorsInfo: Станции организованы с помощью толстых деревьев. Пронос стропы по земле.

Y8v3Tml.name: Manu'eel
Y8v3Tml.description:
Beautiful 60m ++ at Victoria's bridge.
First rigging on The 14, may 2017.
Y8v3Tml.anchorsInfo:
We used one of the pillars of the old bridge, the furtherest from the new bridge. On the other side we used the large but not high tree with a A frame. 

You have to make sure you have a lot of slings. More than 15m.

AypW2v_.anchorsInfo: three bolts on one side, natural on the other (tree and rock backup)

Wcjpnse.name: Afternoon Higline
Wcjpnse.description: Midline. Bolted by Joel Martin and Fer Kpok, 1st Send Ben Gingold
Wcjpnse.anchorsInfo: 5 bolts on far side (Hangers and glue in(1)). 4 exclusive bolts for this line and multiple climbing bolts all around for backup(3 hangers 1 glue in).
Wcjpnse.accessInfo: Summit Road. CattleStop climbing crag entrance. Just park and walk 5m to anchors.

OOFrCgu.description: Water backup. Too low for a mainline failure otherwise

SzYSPYi.name: Mala Makedonka
SzYSPYi.description: bolted and first ascent in 2013 by Slovenians
SzYSPYi.anchorsInfo: 4x 10mm bolts with hangers on both sides

4bi_Mzo.name: Blasting ass and getting gassed on a fat tab of acid
4bi_Mzo.description: Beautiful super classic easy rig line that connects one of the most prominent formations in Joshua tree NP the Cyclopes !
4bi_Mzo.anchorsInfo: Tension sides on pewee rock is a Boulder wrap with a fin behind it to back it up, we used rope to equalized the arms using quick links. Static side on cyclopes is 2 climbing bolts
4bi_Mzo.accessInfo: Easy scramble for both anchors and a 5 min walk from intersection rock

Woptjos.name: Trzydziestka
Woptjos.description: This is a private area. If you have questions, ask Urban Highline Festival.

Ww532It.name: Lulimania 165
Ww532It.restriction: partial: Those lines are higher then 25m, so they need to be announced at BAZL by SwissHighline. Your contact there for the region: ramun@swiss-highline.ch
Ww532It.anchorsInfo:
Riggingside:
Gras anchor w/a-frame
Farside:
tree (d=1m): 2x 3m Slings 
(for general backup 6m rope/slings required to reach another stable tree d=80cm)
Ww532It.accessInfo:
The line is right next to the road, be carefull by walking or crossing the road, the cars are very fast in that part.

It‘s actually not allowed to sleep at the parking near the Spot. There is a parking with a toilet down the road ca. 2km direction Ticino form the lines where you can sleep in the cars.
Another possibility is to sleep/park at the parking next to the Hospiz Restaurant, 1km up the road direction Graubünden from the Spot.

In the valley is the Alpe Pertusio, in summer they might be there with cows. If they are there ask them if they are ok if you would rigg the line.

Leave nothing behind when you leave, respect nature and infrastructure.

D-z75ib.name: Hidden Beach Hundo Foota
D-z75ib.description: First rig and onsight by Adrian Wojcik
D-z75ib.accessInfo: Adjacent to north country trail between wetmore landing and little Presque isle.

LFPRtaP.name: Gare d'eau
LFPRtaP.anchorsInfo:
Bridge side: use bridge structure.
End side: tree, really easy to climb.
LFPRtaP.accessInfo: Direct road access to the bridge

fTfzaW4.description: Incorrectly mapped. See 41m "Smooth As Silk" for details

bSvNayc.name: Over the Fence
bSvNayc.description: 18m ish midline, tree-to-tree. Tons of tree-to-tree spots in the area.
bSvNayc.restriction: none: Just private property so get permission before coming. Please come hang out though!!! Tonyarr.905@gmail.com.
bSvNayc.anchorsInfo: Both sides anchored to trees using slacktivity tree slings and spanners. Tension side tree has lots of branches and personal anchor points, some good hammock spots, etc. Both sides require climbing the trees 20m+ to rig. There are permanent taglines and climbing rope anchors up in the trees well above the rigging points.
bSvNayc.accessInfo: Private property but the owners are super cool. They’re my in-laws.

7DJlF73.name: die 2204
7DJlF73.description: Start anchor on the south side, tree number 2204
7DJlF73.anchorsInfo: Start anchor on the south side, tree number 2204.

067fe2H.name: Magüi Line
067fe2H.description: We suggest tell to Lifeguards who are watchting the beach that you are going to try the midline. 2 bolts each side and 1 hold #8 more for MMS next to these bolts
067fe2H.anchorsInfo: 2 bolts each side and 1 hold #8 more for MMS next to these bolts

8VPt5Up.name: La catarata del dragón
8VPt5Up.accessInfo: 20 minutes walking. On the side of the rock there is a bolt in the middle of the wall to reach the anchor. On the other side the anchor is under the hut.

pQsJpGh.name: Capvespres a sa Serra
pQsJpGh.description: Be careful to acces the spot. some roads are forbidden to enter
pQsJpGh.anchorsInfo: there are 4 bolts of 12mm on each side. Bring your own hangers and nuts. A lot of protection needed because there rocks are super sharp

_s2MiaR.name: Sixty Five Cents
_s2MiaR.description: Highline over the water, leashed walking only. Water is not deep enough to take dives.
_s2MiaR.anchorsInfo: Four 1/2 inch bolts with chain link hangers per side.

ZGgLISi.name: Stonehenge
ZGgLISi.description: An abandoned semi-circular theatre construction of ferroconcrete in a city park
ZGgLISi.anchorsInfo: metal rod ears sticking from the concrete and to concrete pillars themselves. You'll need some ropes, slings, carabiners and rope protection to attach the line,
ZGgLISi.accessInfo: rope throwing and jumar is needed or a serie of butterfly loops

kEGBDHc.name: Mark of Zoro
kEGBDHc.description: Line rigged over the pond, using tree anchors. Not yet been sent.

wyarmSI.name: Prouesse effet mer
wyarmSI.description:
Une belle ligne au dessus de l'eau dans une décor magnifique ! Parfait pour observer le coucher de soleil depuis la ligne en expo 🤩
Les ancrages sont full naturel.
En bonus du psycobloc de folie tout le long des rochers (sans oursins).

Ligne ouverte à deux avec Barbara Baugé ❤️
wyarmSI.restriction:
partial: La côte est fragile donc les glissements de terrains peuvent apparaître...
Des panneaux interdisant les poids lourd à ce zone sont présents dans le village avant le spot !
wyarmSI.anchorsInfo:
Tension side :
Un gros rocher à entourer avec une élingue de 4m, ici on avait que 2x 2m !
Backup g sur un autre gros rocher plus loin dans l'axe de la ligne.
20m de corde suffisent pour ça et avoir du rab

Far side :
Beaucoup d'élingues... 6 de 3m. Pour l'ancrage de la ligne une élingue de 3m passée en U au travers d'une grosse lunule. Une autre élingue de 3m passé en tête d'alouette au travers d'une autre lunule, tout ça relié en triangulation avec une manille et une 3ème élingue. Pour atteindre la sortie de ligne une autre manille avec une élingue de 3m qui sort en simple.
Redirection far side :
Tête d'alouette sur un gros rocher avec une élingue de 3m et un delta.
Back up g 30m de corde rallongée avec une élingue de 3m sur un arbre plus haut.
Un tour mort autour du rocher de la redirection pour être serein mentalement 🤯

Passage de ligne :
Avec un drone.
Peut se faire à la main en lançant une pierre d'un ancrage à l'autre, bord de falaise far side glissant par endroit !!
wyarmSI.accessInfo:
Far side : Un chemin à travers la foret puis en descendant vers les rochers, un peu escarpé...
Close side : facile d'accès, à travers les buissons, attention, il y a parfois de gros trou !

YQ2Ui_u.name: Umberto un Po
YQ2Ui_u.description: Line crossing the entire Po river, anchored to trees. It is important to kwon that it's forbidden to rig it without municipal, fluvial and park permission.

twPV9T3.name: Hollywood midline
twPV9T3.description: Called the Hollywood midline for its off the road access. Park right off highway 145 and walk to the base of the two closest big pine trees. The branches have already been cleared at the height to rig at(and we have been asked to not do this to any other trees). Relatively easy to spot the trees due to the cleared out spots 25-30' up.
twPV9T3.anchorsInfo: Big pine trees. Bring tree pro

tlHzpIR.name: Tut's Towers
tlHzpIR.description: 93' Bring your edge pro! I use at least one crash pad on each side. 5.8ish solo climb the tower. Area has lots of glass and trash so please don't make it worse! Classic midline in Flagstaff! Light the fires at night for a good time!!
tlHzpIR.anchorsInfo: Three hangers on tower side and bring 3 3/8 hangers for the dynamic side.
tlHzpIR.accessInfo: Drive right up to it. Turn left off Lake Mary. Can park right off the road, or, if you have some clearance, can drive and park 10 feet behind the anchor

z1Bpyl7.description: The line is tidal and can be 4m off the water in high tides. Big docking bollards on either side
z1Bpyl7.anchorsInfo: Big docking bollards on either side.

qMthERo.name: Märchensee
qMthERo.description: Midline across an old quarry. Not very high, but great scenery. The original name is pink pussy....
qMthERo.anchorsInfo: There used to be a tagline up, but you can also scramble the line around the quarry. For one anchor you have to get 3m high into the tree, it is useful to use a small ratchet line to guy it down to another tree further back. One 2m sling and one 3m sling are sufficient.

CiDg3BW.anchorsInfo: z jednej strony BFK a z drugiej strony fajne brzozy

GOgHx-J.name: Praiera
GOgHx-J.description: Giovanne conquered this line many years ago and comes back almost every year to put on something diferent for the audience of turists at the beach. This is a very crowded beach in Itacaré, every summer its loaded and we´ve done all kinds of presentations on this line including acrobatics on colorful fabric.
GOgHx-J.accessInfo: easy , you can get there by car if you wish

HnRksD2.anchorsInfo: Round clean tree (2 meters max of circumference)

LEiAR9B.name: Pena Branca
LEiAR9B.anchorsInfo: 2 grampos químicos e uma chapeleta de cada lado
LEiAR9B.accessInfo: Line Inside Parque Natural das Andorinhas

fLrfG7u.name: Che Guevara
fLrfG7u.description: Midline, easy to install and walk

hqrPYlS.name: Rodont 1
hqrPYlS.restriction: partial: Those lines are higher then 25m, so they need to be announced at BAZL by SwissHighline. Your contact there for the region: ramun@swiss-highline.ch
hqrPYlS.anchorsInfo: Both sides drilled, either Glue-In bolts or expansion bolts. The plates should be there.
hqrPYlS.accessInfo: Park at the parkings next to the old pass road or at the lake Rodont.

CQdtZm-.name: Urban Delight
CQdtZm-.description: 50m Midline in the woods. nice to play, easy to walk, no exposure
CQdtZm-.accessInfo: park the car up at rothmoosweg, walk 1 minute in the wood

UnAW1i4.name: Sontaran Spire
UnAW1i4.description: Hound Tor spire line, easy rig but really low..
UnAW1i4.anchorsInfo: Use spansets or rope but the rocks are rough so use padding..

lsv30Lc.description: Only rig on Sundays as there is no police ;) easy to set up (tree anchors) ..use a static rope or line backup and keep it fairly tight as it is not very high for this length of line (post on fb in STRASLINE or Slack Alsace for people to rig and walk with)

wIDzr_J.description: Easy acses ,its a good DWS climbing area
wIDzr_J.anchorsInfo: There are just the 6expansion bolt, will need screws, and hangers,

HAZY6x-.name: Neverland
HAZY6x-.description: Very colorful spot with flowery valley beneath. Not very high and one of the anchors is high up in the pine tree, which shakes quite a bit even with catching the line during falls. Not suitable for anything excessively dynamic. Leashfalls seem very close to the ground, but are not exactly - impression is given by very high flower (up to 3m tall) vegetation and bushes - which is the reason for the name.
HAZY6x-.anchorsInfo: Both are pine trees. One is not far from the ground ~3m at the slope above the valley. Another is ~13m high in easy to climb tree on a lower slope

khunGRN.description:
For one lock you have to enter into fotino . To made this you Climb wall and enter in the structure .
On the other side you climb a three

PNaU3-Z.name: Tramway
PNaU3-Z.description: Super fun line under the Sandia peak Tramway
PNaU3-Z.anchorsInfo: Two bolts on each side, boulders for additional.

qSDfpfv.name: Wychel Midline
qSDfpfv.description: In the midle of the line it hafe a bench in the falling line, therefore you need an spotter to support you

RVwnnCf.description: Tidal. Large docking bollards. The fences have been used as A frames
RVwnnCf.anchorsInfo: Large docking bollards. The fences have been used as A frames.

8c7V0Ci.name: Charlie's Lline
8c7V0Ci.description: 20m line, bolted as a beginner-friendly line. Beautiful ocean and sunset views.
8c7V0Ci.anchorsInfo: Glue-in bolts (3 for anchor + 1 independent back-up bolt). Slings or static rope for anchor. Sharp rock, needs good padding.
8c7V0Ci.accessInfo: Easy access, only a few minutes walk from parking area.

OU7KnUZ.name: Your balls
OU7KnUZ.description: the walk zone is not 60m but you will need webbing for the no fall zones
OU7KnUZ.anchorsInfo: M10 bolts 3 on each side. You'll need hangers and nuts to rig your balls.

Ka5kh45.name: Pinching the Crown Jewels
Ka5kh45.description: Walk along the coast to the left of the jetty and continue around across the rocks and up the little goat trail until you get to the cove
Ka5kh45.accessInfo: Walk along the coast to the left of the jetty and continue around across the rocks and up the little goat trail until you get to the cove.

CnQN23G.name: WestWood
CnQN23G.description: Park at Parkinglot "Malberg", follow the way eastwards for 5 Minutes - you'll find the old quarry on the left. Go up a vague path right of the quarry to reach the first anchor-point. The line is on the "upper part" of the quarry
CnQN23G.accessInfo: Park at Parkinglot "Malberg", follow the way eastwards for 5 Minutes - you'll find the old quarry on the left. Go up a vague path right of the quarry to reach the first anchor-point. The line is on the "upper part" of the quarry.

WPBpqxZ.name: Nevasca, midline ciro
WPBpqxZ.description: A linha nevasca é o midline no complexo esportivo ciro nardi

v6r7MfO.anchorsInfo: Both ancors are on one tree each. the nearside is just below the fire place a bit towards the road. The far side is below the road,

f1XT3d6.name: Low and Long
f1XT3d6.description:
never installed, but sure possible
the rock is not the best, we have to test it if we can use regular bolts,
an other option would be natural anchors or a dead man anchor,
please check out the sport first

NMSlYnR.description: Parking at the service station of la Cabrera. Going up the hill is easier on the other side via the white/yellow marked path. When you arrive at the path on the top you turn right Sector 2

qf2I4rG.name: Refous original
qf2I4rG.description: Midline, amateur easy to install and walk

8wnHEF8.name: Deep Quarry Midline
8wnHEF8.description:
Climbing guides say this line is about 15m high but that seems too high, nice walked towards tree's or you can face a quarry wall.
Easily rigged in an hour or so..
8wnHEF8.anchorsInfo:
One side is obvious and the line can be walked/thrown from the tree's..
Quarry wall side is harder, you need to rig from the tree's the other side of the wall down in the hidden quarry area.. you basically use the ridge as an a-frame.

yKmtO_V.name: Downtown Midline
yKmtO_V.description: 40 ft above the ground on some old train tracks surrounded by abandoned buildings. Cool urban midline spot with the view of the arch in the background.

XY6mD0E.name: La commessura
XY6mD0E.description: Natural anchor on one side and three bolts (bring 10mm plates) on the opposite anchor; I did master point with these three bolts and I put the backup behind, on a climbing rest
XY6mD0E.anchorsInfo: Natural anchor on one side and three bolts (bring 10mm plates) on the opposite anchor; I did master point with these three bolts and I put the backup behind, on a climbing rest.

sN5J81X.accessInfo: inside of park, have to pay access fee and hike to location

IpMsI2v.name: camping line
IpMsI2v.anchorsInfo: cobra hitch a big boulder on one side, three bolts on the other side

rCwl9Pv.name: Varques
rCwl9Pv.description: At the big cave, on the flat spots on each side
rCwl9Pv.anchorsInfo: Natural wrap 2 rocks ,6 Bolts12mm there are no hangers,

3seIKiQ.description: Lots of good trees for anchors
3seIKiQ.anchorsInfo: Lots of good trees for anchors

4R-PnsJ.name: BZ Slack
4R-PnsJ.description: contact use on FB for real data

d-F92CC.name: Four-
d-F92CC.description: A lot of mountains all around, beautiful place to put a midline.
d-F92CC.anchorsInfo: trees on both sides, straightforward rigging, just need to walk

5LlUtXF.name: Käppeli 100m
5LlUtXF.anchorsInfo:
Small wooden A-Frame with 3 Bolts in the rock, on the near Side Anchor. 
Garage side
2m sling for the 3-point anchor equalization, then use a 1m sling for the extension to the a-frame. Minimum 3 shackles. Backup with rope or leftover webbing. 
Anchor Tree is on the Käppeli side of the path, place anchors 3-4m up, back up tree is on the other side of the path. Two 1,5m  slings for the anchor tree; ratchet line + 2m sling to back up the tree.
5LlUtXF.accessInfo:
Main Anchor: Walk 30 meters underneath the 100 and 120 lines.
Farside: Follow the street, shortly after the start of the forest, walk up to the tree inside the forest.

NruLjNp.name: Budders
NruLjNp.description:
Named after one of the best dogs in mammoth. One side wraps boulders the other is a tree.
Bring a good size spanset and some rope (12 footer and at least 60’) for the rock wrapping. Best access is by boat
NruLjNp.anchorsInfo:
One side wraps boulders the other is a tree.
Bring a good size spanset and some rope (12 footer and at least 60’) for the rock wrapping

dLtMv3V.name: Ms Perfect
dLtMv3V.restriction: partial: Those lines are higher then 25m, so they need to be announced at BAZL by SwissHighline. Your contact there for the region: ramun@swiss-highline.ch
dLtMv3V.anchorsInfo:
Riggingside:
2x 12mm stainless steel expansion bolts
2x 10mm stainless steel expansion bolts
2x 3m roundsling
4x quicklink (majon rapid)
1x 10m staticrope

Farside:
Big rock to redirection rock ~ 10-15m
3x 10mm stainless steel expansion bolts
1x 12mm stainless steel expansion bolt
1x 3m sling
2x 10-15m of slings or staticrope to reach the redirection point.

Shakles and stuff, plates of the bolts should be there.
dLtMv3V.accessInfo: You can park next to the street during the day.

MIlqYfZ.name: Matou Relax
MIlqYfZ.accessInfo: Contactez Free Dome Line sur Facebook pour plus d'infos =)

Zp7o-8-.name: Rodont 2
Zp7o-8-.restriction: partial: Those lines are higher then 25m, so they need to be announced at BAZL by SwissHighline. Your contact there for the region: ramun@swiss-highline.ch
Zp7o-8-.anchorsInfo: Both sides drilled, either Glue-In bolts or expansion bolts. The plates should be there.
Zp7o-8-.accessInfo: Park at the parkings next to the old pass road or at the lake Rodont.

-sY4R2K.name: Beginners Luck

-sY4R2K.anchorsInfo: Glue in M12 bolts both ends. Bring your own hangers. An A-Frame is useful for one end but can use a log if needed.

GvPImIc.name: En la tranquilidad de la naturaleza
GvPImIc.accessInfo: You will need to climb trees both sides, there are guidelines for ropes.

pQd-BCm.name: Kalamatianos
pQd-BCm.description: Training midline in Kalamata. Nearby there is church with spring. I get there by a van.
pQd-BCm.anchorsInfo: All natural one side 40m rope to 4 trees, the other side spansets around 10m, twoo trees and a rock as a buckup

Ps3HwDi.name: Afterlife
Ps3HwDi.description: New line spot in this area. First time tensioning there was quite an enchanting view of three yearly seasons at once - Autumn, Winter and Spring. And then again there is a graveyard closely and even a bus stop named accordingly, so these mystical circumstances led to current name of the spot.
Ps3HwDi.anchorsInfo: Mounting anchor is a pine tree with a tree-house on an open sunlit hill reachable from standing on the ground. The far side anchor is several meters high on a birch tree.
Ps3HwDi.accessInfo: Far side anchor is not easily accessible. No low branches there so ascension is tricky. Personally we used a couple of slings girth hitched around the tree as a movable stepping/hanging aid.

adRwiZw.name: Žaltys
adRwiZw.description: Named after Adder (grass-snake) seen at the other side water front.
adRwiZw.anchorsInfo: Tensioning side is accessible from the ground and is simple. Far side requires climbing 8 meters up on a pine tree. Also isn't a mistake to have a backup line to a tree behind compensate for bending forces at that height.

6rLV1e2.name: Midline / waterline (à ouvrir,) seulement à un marée haute

7j0rW6S.name: El Parque
7j0rW6S.description: Sobre lago artificial y una isla de patos.
7j0rW6S.anchorsInfo: Pinos de Ambos lados. Necesario realizar contrafuerza a arboles secundarios.

_z7O3AR.description:
For one lock you have to enter into fotino . To made this you Climb wall and enter in the structure .
On the other side you climb a three

 slacksylvania@gmail.com or on fb https://www.facebook.com/slacksylvania/

xItmNV7.name: Sopro do Dragão
xItmNV7.description: 4 pinos ancoragem química na rocha em cada lado. Dispensa conectores nestes. Acesso pela trilha que da acesso a praia do gravatá
xItmNV7.anchorsInfo: 4 pinos ancoragem química na rocha em cada lado. Dispensa conectores nestes.
xItmNV7.accessInfo: Acesso pela trilha que da acesso a praia do gravatá

kJKQSqR.description: Amazing 150 meter line at the top of Castle rock
kJKQSqR.anchorsInfo: Tension side on castle rock is a Boulder wrap with a few pinches for back up and static is a tree on the hill across from castle rock
kJKQSqR.accessInfo: 15 min hike to the very top of castle rock where the climbers top out

1wdXXwC.name: Stour Power
1wdXXwC.description: Midline over the river Stour. Rigged off the ruins of an old bridge. Please contact the farmer before rigging to gain access to the west side of the bridge. see extra notes.
1wdXXwC.anchorsInfo: Sling at least four of the good bridge railings on each side and pad them well. Ideally needs ~70m of static rope on each side.

B2f6UBt.name: Chladný špek
B2f6UBt.description: One unused railway bridge, and another former road bridge now used only by pedestrians and cyclists. Next to the unused railway bridge is an active one, still occasionally used by trains.
B2f6UBt.anchorsInfo: On one side, you need two ~4m slings to rig the handrail; the second anchor requires two ~2m slings to wrap around the bridge pillar.
B2f6UBt.accessInfo: You can park your car right at the beginning of the unused road bridge.

tFLRqmN.name: melezza 1
tFLRqmN.description: Low midline, to use with intermittent connections only and if you know about the risks. very simple rig.
tFLRqmN.anchorsInfo: the twin tree we used to rig has like most other trees at the spot several dead branches. I recommend not to rig this line any more.

wls-bCH.anchorsInfo:
Tree side (static) uses a large notch ~20 feet up as an elevator;
Face side (tension) uses wedge bolts that require hardware (hangers & washers/nuts).

w5aXcau.name: Spiaggia di Testue
w5aXcau.description: There are bolts on one side, in the other need 10mm platelets. Simply walking
w5aXcau.anchorsInfo: There are bolts on one side, in the other need 10mm platelets

up4Aa6W.name: Skimmer
up4Aa6W.description: Fifth established line spot at this lake. Since it's longer and opposite shore anchor is lower it is not that high. With low tension you'd probably skim the water surface on this one. And since some dragonfly species are named 'skimmer' - it was chosen as one for this line as well to be consistent :)
up4Aa6W.anchorsInfo: Both anchors are on trees at reachable from the ground height, so it's the easiest to rig midline at this lake at the moment.

stBoEoX.name: Eucascadinha
stBoEoX.description: Middle line between two heath tree

nKwaZPF.name: Lai da Marmorera
nKwaZPF.description: Sketchy but nice and easy long midline at the dam. You don‘t touch the ground while falling at the most parts of the line.
nKwaZPF.restriction: none: This line is below 25m in hight and doesn‘t need to be annouced at BAZL.
nKwaZPF.anchorsInfo:
Riggingside:
2x tree 
1x 3m roundsling
1x 2.5m roundsling backup
Shakle and stuff 
Padding for the trees

Farside:
3x 12mm Stainless steel expansion bolts
3x quicklink (majon rapid)
1x 3m roundsling 
1x 2m roundsling backup
Shakels and stuff
nKwaZPF.accessInfo:
You can park on the dam during the day.

Camping is forbidden in this area. 
If you plan to stay over night you have to organize you with a campground in Bivio or somwhere else.

C-K_zBG.description: Midline above water, between a bridge and a tree. On the tree, we rigged to an inclined branch that needs backing up to the main trunk.

j8hP_rc.name: Non-GMO Organic Free Range Feta Cheese
j8hP_rc.description: A great 50m line with decent walking distance. Large tree in the middle splitting the line with a no fall zone.
j8hP_rc.anchorsInfo: All lines here are anchored with bomber trees. Bring whatever gear you need to go tree to tree.
j8hP_rc.accessInfo: Park just after the driveway on the road near by. Then hike down into the ditch a trail should present itself. The lines will be a short 1 to 2 minute hike and the lines will be on the left.

BmW1Tve.name: Hounds of Hundatora
BmW1Tve.description: Longer and higher than the spire but a similar rig, padding needed

Pxep9HO.description: very nice and long water- midline
Pxep9HO.restriction: full: same with all other lines at the lake
Pxep9HO.anchorsInfo:
Nearside: same anchor as "Telefonmann 5G"
Farside: Large beech tree on the penninsula, install the anchor at ~4m height.

T7WZPNk.name: C-Trainings line
T7WZPNk.description: easy rig, high enough for the backup to engage, nice chill spot... what do you want more?
T7WZPNk.anchorsInfo:
Big tree on both sides (shoulder high, is enough)
We usually use one tree further back on the south anchor, so we can easily tension the line... but then you definitely have to pad the line well and can't surf!
T7WZPNk.accessInfo:
Parking either here
https://maps.app.goo.gl/GfN3NNt1sB9nNhAP9
Or
https://maps.app.goo.gl/nmEciDT3CctMjeQW8

We usually come by bicycle from the S-Bahn station obertürkheim.

KTkM8yo.name: Spilonca midline
KTkM8yo.description:
Chill spot over river
Many posibilities between 50 and 100m . 
Complicated rigging, on small trees.
Nice parking is close by.
Spot is windy.
KTkM8yo.anchorsInfo: Small trees can be used for anchor, advanced rigging

AEM7_GQ.name: The Emergency Room
AEM7_GQ.description: The first of lines to ever be established in the area. First Rig crew: Alonso Rodriguez, Makynzee Balucas, Leon Aledo. Great line even though it's a little bit off level by maybe 2-4 feet. The line was first rigged on February 18, 2018. This line is to be updated with bolts on the static side. (See rigging for details on this) Please have fun and let me (Alonso) know if you plan on rigging this so that I can join! :)
AEM7_GQ.anchorsInfo:
Anchors are really tough (now). Things will change once the static side is bolted. 

Tension Side: (Boulder closer to the road) 2 bolts + tree trunk for third point
The set up here is straight forward, climb the rock and find the obvious bolts up there. Wrap the obvious tree with something (We used a 3 foot spanset) and equalize the three points. The tree is about 15 feet behind the bolts so make sure you have enough rope to rig this. Bolts are hangars so please bring some quicklinks to rig these. 

Static side: (Boulder across the river) This boulder is a tough one to rig. There is a large boulder leaning against this other huge boulder! You will wrap this boulder right where they lean against each other. We used two ropes to make two separate W3P2 anchors. Overall you probably need close to 100 feet of rope to make one of these anchors. If spansetting, a 12 footer is probably the smallest length you can use. That's the easy part. After that the anchor has to be extended really far. We extended ours about 25 feet with spansets and backed it up with rope. Beware of the sharp edge on this side. Definitely take some sort of abrasion protection! One rusty bolt exists on top of the boulder but don't use it. Use it to keep your anchor in from veering off to the side. This side will have bolts sometime within summer 2018 hopefully. Will update if/when that happens.
AEM7_GQ.accessInfo: Park in the Hospital Rock parking lot and walk across the street to the asphalt trail. Walk down about 50 feet and the first boulder (tension side) will be to your right. Do 1 climbing move to get on top of the boulder and quickly spot the shiny bolts with rap rings. Behind the bolts you will see a tree about 8 inches in diameter, Use this tree as a third point of equalization. To get to the static side, continue down the trail until you are down by the river. Once there, cross the river through the obvious boulder path. The last boulder that looks slick is not too bad. We jumped for it and didn't slip any of the 5 times crossing. Once behind the boulder stand on the rock that you are going to wrap as an anchor. Use your buddy's shoulders for a boost and get up on top! (Take a rope so that you can add a rapel/ascend line for easy access up onto the rock.

A-Wp7F3.name: Terminus de la Voiselle
A-Wp7F3.description: Quand on arrive au centre Nautique (piscine de Bourges) 200m à gauche

a-nwGE9.description: A big line in a beautiful spot.  Hike around to the back side of the butte and rig on natural anchors.  This line has a view of Mt. Hood and the river.
a-nwGE9.anchorsInfo: All natural.  Bring trad gear, static rope, and spansets to equalize slung boulders and gear.

MJ9Oylb.name: Crack
MJ9Oylb.description: Midline through the ravine. Tree stations.
MJ9Oylb.accessInfo: 10 minutes on foot from the parking lot

82S_ebP.name: Euthanasia
82S_ebP.description: Very colorful spot with flowery valley beneath. Not very high and one of the anchors is high up in pine tree, which shakes quite a bit even with catching the line during falls. Hence the name for it - also because of pet care facility nearby. Not suitable for anything excessively dynamic.
82S_ebP.anchorsInfo: Both are pine trees. One is on the ground level at the slope above the valley. Another is  ~13m high in easy to climb tree on a lower slope

u_R3fMj.name: Green Bean
u_R3fMj.anchorsInfo: Three bolts on tension side. Back-tensioned big tree on the static side.

piXd2Fe.description: Get ready for a wet whipper if you leash in here
piXd2Fe.anchorsInfo: Tree on one end, boulder on other

9_6HCyW.name: Jesmond Dream
9_6HCyW.description: Restricted access. Contact a member of Northumbria Slackline Society for info
9_6HCyW.accessInfo: necessary to ascend the far tree. anchor to the east is next to a boulder where we left extra gear and food etc.

yLs3b2P.name: Baby
yLs3b2P.description: 3 points for the mainline, 2 points for the backup in the both sides. You need long slings
yLs3b2P.anchorsInfo: 3 points for the mainline, 2 points for the backup in the both sides. You need long slings.

pHAf-Lk.name: Homestake
pHAf-Lk.restriction: none: Forest Service is aware of the line and has no problem with it as long as it isn't left perma-rigged.
pHAf-Lk.anchorsInfo: 3 bolt glue in anchors both sides

zay5HNF.name: Low tide
zay5HNF.description: The line is not in the exact spot, It's in the Avery drive climbing area on top of JB trout to the opposing wall
zay5HNF.anchorsInfo: 3 bolts per side, Missing hangers on one side

vXh8oRH.name: Dora la Exploradora
vXh8oRH.description: tree anchors, the line can be passed through the bridge. The line is not very high (around 8/9m depending on the height of the anchors) and the water is not deep (and clean) enough to allow waterlining. In other spots over the river the police made derig the line, but it has never happened here because it's a bit more hidden.

uwuzNHo.name: Embracab
uwuzNHo.description: In front of the sea, carefull if there is big wave and inshore wind you will be wet !
uwuzNHo.anchorsInfo: Natural anchor on both sides. 6m sling to suround a rock on one side, 5 m sling to do a biangulation on 2 lunules

6YY0_LS.name: On ne cours pas à côté de la piscine !
6YY0_LS.anchorsInfo: 2 lunulles au nord, un gros bloc au sud (5/6 m de tour)

S0OQllT.name: Copse Corridor
S0OQllT.description: A longer variation of Sugar Snap 60; threads the perfect corridor of trees. Can be rigged at the same time as Sugar Snap 60.
S0OQllT.anchorsInfo: Tensioning side features extensive bolted anchors (1/2" all-stainless, 4" long expansion bolts) with hangers (3/8" shank). Static side uses large trees rooted in the hillside.

W7JX0e0.name: Shorty
W7JX0e0.description: Easy line to rig. Beautiful spot with a small stream under the line.
W7JX0e0.anchorsInfo:
All natural. 
One of the trees are huge (and can be a little difficult to install..), so bring long slings.
W7JX0e0.accessInfo:
Both trees are on the cliff edge.
Don't try to cross the cliff - it's very slippery. Walk around instead.

H1G5yID.name: Southwoods line
H1G5yID.description: This line is small but there is scope for a 20m midline, but if your in Yeovil the Hollow is better.. NOT RECOMMENDED TO RIG, too low. If you mainline failed you are likely to hit the ground.

OOzYPY8.name: Flat Earth 1
OOzYPY8.description: The first rig on this picture is a bit low but can be made 3m higher

ea3RAdt.name: Anna reviens
ea3RAdt.accessInfo: Contactez Free Dome Line sur Facebook pour plus d'infos =)

Zizf-qV.name: Big Fish
Zizf-qV.description: Before rigg the midline you must ask for permission. There is a phone numer on the shed's doors. WARNING: The owners of area speak only in Polish. The best idea it's ask for someone who live close to this area in Slackline Kraków group on Facebook
Zizf-qV.anchorsInfo: Two very solid trees, 2,5m slings to both sides.

ogRpa6K.name: Happy Days
ogRpa6K.description: Underneath the bridge, need plenty of protection. Need to belay down on far anchor
ogRpa6K.anchorsInfo: Underneath the bridge, need plenty of protection
ogRpa6K.accessInfo: Need to belay down on far anchor

MEEczrS.name: La cuerda del moro
MEEczrS.accessInfo: Dame like to "cueva del moro" for climb

4W7eRj0.name: Conto dos Pescadores
4W7eRj0.description: Beautiful midline above the sea and near Pão de Açucar.
4W7eRj0.anchorsInfo:
On the bloc there is one glue in bolt and the other looks like a sleeve bolt with a chain. The backup anchor is the tree.
On the other side there are 2 sleeve bolts for the main and one glue in bolt for the backup
4W7eRj0.accessInfo: The fishermens installed little concrete spots on the rock that show the path and help you walk it. Be extra careful when the rock is wet.

sQGdtdh.name: Capivara's Line
sQGdtdh.description: Midline very good to learn because there is the possibility of using the other pillars of the bridge decreasing or increasing the distance.
sQGdtdh.anchorsInfo: Natural anchorage on both sides, one side uses the bridge and the other side uses the trees,

FU-jWqR.name: Runner
FU-jWqR.description: Two trees one on steep hill side so be careful when rain has occurred within three days prior to rig (use your best judgment) easily done with tag line two 16ft spansets to go around one tree and two 10’s worked fine for static

uzej2y2.name: La Tortuguita
uzej2y2.description: We suggest tell to Lifeguards who are watchting the beach that you are going to try the midline. 2 bolts each side and 1 hold #8 more for MMS next to these bolts
uzej2y2.anchorsInfo: 2 bolts each side and 1 hold #8 more for MMS next to these bolts

2Xqo3TT.description: Water-midline with beautiful surroundings and near climbing spot. IT IS NOT ALLOWED TO SWIM IN THE LAKE. Height of the line changes with water level.
2Xqo3TT.anchorsInfo: Tree to tree.  Some climbing needed, approx. 5m.

1gYN-kU.name: Erotic line
1gYN-kU.description: Beautiful midline over the sea in an amazing cove

y8x3hjT.name: Lomepal spot
y8x3hjT.description: Meilleur spot de slick de Gironde, possibilité de faire la tricks line

arzBVYj.name: san juanazo
arzBVYj.description: línea relativamente sencilla de poner con acceso a los dos anclajes caminando desde el estacionamiento (20.038719, -98.661897)  el tagline se puede pasar caminando
arzBVYj.anchorsInfo: actualmente son dos bolts inoxidables con cadenas de cada lado (2025)
arzBVYj.accessInfo: se recomienda dejar el auto en el estacionamiento principal de la cara de enfrente (20.038719, -98.661897)

cuh9g9f.name: BZ slack
cuh9g9f.description: contact use on FB for real data

45JzrUV.name: Sielecka Sielanka
45JzrUV.description: Popular park in the city center. Nice place for quick session.
45JzrUV.anchorsInfo: Trees, 4 meters above the ground + 3 meters from the ground to the water.

Lynr3jE.description: Beautiful line under the bridge that straddles the river
Lynr3jE.accessInfo: One side is accessible by rappel from previously set bolts on guard rail, other side must be ascended

L2mwf1V.name: wściekłe pięści węża
L2mwf1V.description: Łatwa dostępność miejsca. można podjeżdzać śmiało pod sam wyciąg i tam parkować

hn4htUV.restriction: partial: Highline spot on the “Forest ofTHEN” property. Entrance fee required. For more information, visit @forest_of_then on Instagram.

EN_wwIV.name: Serenity
EN_wwIV.description:
I sea kayaked the gear in & solo rigged this,  definitely would recommend bringing a friend.    Setup static anchor first by landing on the flat rock & climbing the cliff up to anchor area. Static gets rigged off big birch Backup to nearby tree. 

 Then paddle the webbing across the gap (or bring potato cannon & run a tagline to the north side.  Land & crawl through the cave to the northern point,  then scramble up the cliff  to get to a ginormous pine tree, that's your tension anchor.
EN_wwIV.anchorsInfo: 10' Spanset  15'/20' Spanset & backup anchors (BC Sewn loop nylon works & saves weight)

P3pnCff.description: Big tree on river bank to the bridge
P3pnCff.anchorsInfo: Big tree on river bank to the bridge

F61JZJt.name: Bloodflow
F61JZJt.description: Climbing sector Rebro, Blagaj. You have a 20min walk from the parking lot for the Stjepan fortress
F61JZJt.accessInfo: One side easily accessible. Farside you have to throw the rope over the rock approx 20m tall, you can anchor the rope on the other side on 2 climbing bolts. And then jummar up to the anchor. there are 2 anchors one above the other. The longer line goes on the upper anchor

23Dfrqs.name: Dalt Quarry
23Dfrqs.description: Rigged fairly high on decent trees over the quarry. First line rigged here.
23Dfrqs.anchorsInfo: Trees on both sides, which are quite far back from the edge.

BH9jrfW.name: La casa de las iguanas
BH9jrfW.description: Its a midline inside the city in one of the most famous lagoons in Tabasco

4BiVicz.name: Chauzon
4BiVicz.description:
Chauzon is a nice place to setup différent lenght.
But highline in Ardèche is a bit complicated so we try to be respectfull of other nature's users and environment. Therefor we can't put bolts everywhere (it's often forbiden because many places like here are in aera protected)

Also, in front of the spot there is a private beach with tons of tourists in summer.
The best option in high season is to come here only for the evening or the morning.
4BiVicz.anchorsInfo:
For the 20m lines there is bolt as it's the old one (2010 ;) but for the 40m, 60m and 100m it's possible with natural anchors (even if for the 60 somebody put bolts...)
Try to be creative to find the good things to circle ;)
4BiVicz.accessInfo:
As we are not so well welcome in the aera, be carefull with the carepark.
It's forbiden to camp there. Police came few times...
To be sure to spend a good time, the best option is to go to the camping site (you can't be more close to the spot ;)

e7SQW9a.name: Pata Jambito
e7SQW9a.description: Natural anchors, 6m of spansets each side
e7SQW9a.anchorsInfo: Natural anchors, 6m of spansets each side

eBYelbm.name: Baby-on Babylon
eBYelbm.description: Bottom of the hollow, easy to rig but not worth the effort.. NOT RECOMMENDED TO RIG, too low. Likely to hit the ground if mainline failed.

HvSUH51.name: La Perma
HvSUH51.description: Esta cinta es buena parte del año un permarig.
HvSUH51.anchorsInfo: Los anclajes son árboles de Eucalipto de aproximadamente 20 metros de alto que sobrepasan el puente en altura. El anclaje de esta cinta es el mismo árbol que usamos para montar la Terapeuta de 50 metros.
HvSUH51.accessInfo:
Acceder al spot de la cinta es muy sencillo ya que esta en la quebrada del Rio Tahuando que atraviesa la ciudad. Se puede llegar por la Cruz verde para entrar por la calle El Alpargate o se puede llegar por el barrio La Victoria, por la Calle Carlos Barahona.

El terminal terrestre se encuentra a 2km. y casa Monoslack   a 200 metros, del puente-

ifBKEiX.name: "George of the Jungle" line
ifBKEiX.description: Just uphill of the "Hungover Wall" on Keller's peak is this nice little line.  It's not too high, but it's got a great view.  It got it's name from a no-fall zone on the tensioning side.  If you fall there, watch out for that tree!
ifBKEiX.anchorsInfo: Eastern anchors are threaded bolts. Bring climbing hangers and nuts.  The western anchor is natural.  Scorpion wrap part of the tower with a very large (or several) spanset.
ifBKEiX.accessInfo: Park on the dirt pullout and hike uphill, around the right side of the "Hungover" wall.  The western anchor is an easy scramble to the top.  The eastern anchor is the better tensioning anchor.  It's a V1 boulder problem to the top.

Hyusza4.name: BZ Slack
Hyusza4.description: contact use on FB for real data

Zat6XWr.name: Reef or Break
Zat6XWr.description: Beautiful cove with lots of options for different length lines over the pristine water. Sheltered from the wind most days. Great place to snorkel, climb, cliff jump, and slack all in the same day!
Zat6XWr.anchorsInfo: Rock wrapping with large spansets. Sharp rocks, bring pro
Zat6XWr.accessInfo: Park at the lookout for Mokuleia Bay and walk down stairs. Follow beach to the cliffs on the right

XXdo-2U.description: Natural anchors, need Lots of spannsets and crashpods of padding
XXdo-2U.anchorsInfo: Natural anchors, need Lots of spannsets and crashpods of padding

T7RzSIP.name: The angel of the A92
T7RzSIP.description: A nice line through the forest, however some road noise.
T7RzSIP.anchorsInfo: 4 meter spansets needed for the trees on each sides, a 50 meter webbing works, and a  tag line is helpful but not needed. Can be rigged in 30/45 minutes, as it is essential a parkline with a scramble up each side to add height.
T7RzSIP.accessInfo: Park outside the Remus equestrian centre and walk through the abandoned house garden into the Den. From there walk along the path and under the motorway bridge. The valley the line spans is just the other side of the bridge.

72H1_Yf.name: Project
72H1_Yf.description: A lot of mountains all around, beautiful place to put a midline.
72H1_Yf.anchorsInfo: trees on both sides, straightforward rigging, just need to walk
72H1_Yf.accessInfo: Starts from the hiking route, quite long walk though

4eUWYVG.name: Leaping trout
4eUWYVG.description: EPIC MIDLINE!! Goes over the most intense part of the falls. Excellent swimming hole, virtually no approach!

cb5lLlD.description: Training midline spot. Third floor, ~10 meters height. Easy to rig and to get on the slack.

9uWer_C.name: El Tejocote
9uWer_C.description: Midline localizado dentro del Parque ecológico Montetik
9uWer_C.accessInfo: Se pagan 10 pesos por persona para ingresar al parque

C5NnSXe.name: Restauracja na końcu wszechświata
C5NnSXe.description: Beautiful area for walkig alongside the river, canoeing, jogging and even highlining! The best choice is to rigg it on a working day beacuse there is a lot of people on weekends.
C5NnSXe.anchorsInfo:
1. side a big tree and 4 meters sling
2. side smaller tree and 5/6 meters sling - a frame is very recomended.
backups longer
C5NnSXe.accessInfo: 5 minutes from a parking near swimming pool

c_bvM4r.name: Sapiegine
c_bvM4r.description: Nice midline spot with mostly spacious surroundings. Suitable for freestyle rigs.
c_bvM4r.anchorsInfo: Tensioning side is a pine tree on a hill - super practical and easy. Static side is also a pine tree with anchoring at 4 meter height. Also a simple ratchet line to a tree behind there is nice to offset and stabilize the anchor tree.
c_bvM4r.accessInfo: Static side requires a couple slings arround the the tree to climb and reach proper height for anchoring.

_SiJBXz.name: IL FORCONE DEL DIAVOLO
_SiJBXz.description: IN ONE SIDE YOU NEED TO HUG A ROCK AT LIST 8m SLINGS YOU CAN BACK UP IN SEVERAL OLD SPIT, IN THE OTHER SITE 2 SLINGS FOR ANCOR WITH A TREE. IN BOTH SITE THE ANCOR ARE BHEIND AND ABOVE THE CLIMBING WAY
_SiJBXz.anchorsInfo: IN ONE SIDE YOU NEED TO HUG A ROCK AT LIST 8m SLINGS YOU CAN BACK UP IN SEVERAL OLD SPIT, IN THE OTHER SITE 2 SLINGS FOR ANCOR WITH A TREE
_SiJBXz.accessInfo: IN BOTH SITE THE ANCOR ARE BHEIND AND ABOVE THE CLIMBING WAY

S9E9imU.name: Mamá unos manguitos
S9E9imU.description: Midline around 65 meters over the water, if you rig it loose and/or stretchy, you can touch the water in the mid of the line after a leashfall. The height of the anchors its around 8 meters.
S9E9imU.anchorsInfo: Trees, the rig is the same as in the park

wRVsEE2.name: Culo del santo
wRVsEE2.description: So cool place also to do slackline in San Miguel Alto.
wRVsEE2.accessInfo: Like 10min easy Walking from San Miguel Alto

06BDgpA.name: One love
06BDgpA.restriction: none: Un fin de semana sin problemas más de tres días no recomiendo

WShHBK-.name: Más allá de la felicidad
WShHBK-.description: Linea con bungees casi siempre está de perma

JPSVXrM.name: KŁOS
JPSVXrM.description: One tree closer to the road with a 2m sling and another tree with a 2.5m sling
JPSVXrM.anchorsInfo: one tree closer to the road with a 2m sling and another tree with a 2.5m sling

bhoIltL.name: La Escuelita
bhoIltL.description: First midline of Gran Canaria... Nobody try this line for 2 years or more... necessary checking in both sides
bhoIltL.accessInfo: by walking one anchor, anotherone by rappel

LZ4TkVQ.name: Bigger Fish
LZ4TkVQ.description: Before rigg the midline you must ask for permission. There is a phone numer on the shed's doors. WARNING: The owners of area speak only in Polish. The best idea it's ask for someone who live close to this area in Slackline Kraków group on Facebook
LZ4TkVQ.anchorsInfo: Two very solid trees, 2,5-3m slings to both sides.

nHtdaiP.description: A super beautiful line above a little creek. Fixed on top of a granite boulder block with 3 bolts on one side (make an extension of about 2-3 meters with your slings to really leave the block- the hangers are left on the block) and an electricity pillar on the other side. A second 60m line can be rigged from the same boulder bloc to the road. You simply need 1m-slings to fix the line on the road. Unfortunately the first 20m are too low to be walked.

6p09Z36.description: Easy rig highlines in a forest, there are mulitiple lines to rig here and we have permission to go here from Stadt Zurich
6p09Z36.accessInfo: There is a bus stop in witikon called Loorenstrasse about 5min walk from the spot.

XwJ1TPH.name: Sugar Snap
XwJ1TPH.description: The original Pittsburgh midline, a fantastic learning ground for both highlining as well as rigging.

kp355y2.name: Jamapa
kp355y2.description: Dos buenos arboles para anclajes naturales. Se puede llegar caminando por cualquiera de los dos lados
kp355y2.anchorsInfo: Dos buenos arboles para anclajes naturales
kp355y2.accessInfo: Se puede llegar caminando por cualquiera de los dos lados

Iob1y4T.name: Christchurch Slackline and the Crazy Czeck
Iob1y4T.description: Total Midline. Bolted by Fer Kpok. 1st Send Fer Kpok
Iob1y4T.anchorsInfo: 5 bolts on one side (equalize 4 as Rock is bad quality) and 4 bolts on other side (equalize 3, better rock). You need hangers 12mm (4) and the rest are glue in bolts.
Iob1y4T.accessInfo: Albert Terrace Street. Go to Albert Terrace Rock Face (climbing crag). Park the car and walk for 20 minutes and go after the woods.

o0QgO1G.name: Entre o Sol e a Lua

nc1jIX2.name: Homespot nr 1
nc1jIX2.description: Defenetly a Midline, use at least a 3 times segementet Line. Have a Knife ready for emergency.
nc1jIX2.anchorsInfo:
2 x 2 Meter slings with rope Backup, use treepro

tagline can be walked over the bridge
nc1jIX2.accessInfo: Please contact me, usualy there is a permarigg

w5Ru_rL.description: Bolted both ends with hangers installed. Walk down to the beach from up on the cliffs, line is south from access track. Small amount of easy rock climbing to get to both anchors. Can take a rope to rap down to make it easier
w5Ru_rL.anchorsInfo: Bolted both ends with hangers installed
w5Ru_rL.accessInfo: Walk down to the beach from up on the cliffs, line is south from access track. Small amount of easy rock climbing to get to both anchors. Can take a rope to rap down to make it easier.

QY9Xr0R.name: Container full of dreams
QY9Xr0R.description: Lowest area in the quarry, just below 'We don't give a ship'
QY9Xr0R.anchorsInfo:
1) Tree's until I bolt it..
2) Stakes until I bolt it..
QY9Xr0R.accessInfo:
As you enter head left to the abandoned buildings and use the tree's for anchor 1..
Walk around the gravel road until you are opposite the abandoned buildings..

pdnbUbB.name: Fortino midline
pdnbUbB.description: An anchor is made on tower with rope or link sling .   Other one on the three

6NQB9_y.name: Unnamed
6NQB9_y.description: Beautiful midline over the sea in an amazing cove

4FZaCqs.name: “The Draw Bridge”
4FZaCqs.description: Beautiful 80 meter over looking the Wenatchee river
4FZaCqs.accessInfo: Very easy 10 min slightly uphill hike

GO5a-iF.name: kalte Füße
GO5a-iF.description: easy to rig, tree to tree. Easy access to the line from the right side.
GO5a-iF.anchorsInfo: 1.5m spansets are enought for both trees

hkjYO3h.name: Crappy Rock
hkjYO3h.description: Highline. Midline. Bolted by Petr Polak, Numa Besteiro and Fer Kpok. 1st Send Fer Kpok
