hkjYO3h.anchorsInfo: Tree on one side. 5 bolts (glue in) in the other side. You need rope to extend anchors around 4m to the edge of the cliff. On the tree side you can backup with other trees.
hkjYO3h.accessInfo: Albert Terrace Street. Go to Albert Terrace Rock Face (climbing crag). Park the car and walk for 7minutes.

Inq4QYj.name: Pipistrelli
Inq4QYj.description: in a forest, over a pit, you can't walk the hole length (because of the hight)
Inq4QYj.anchorsInfo: one side needs alot of slings (10m) and protection, one side tree anker

2uAD7V5.name: Rebirth
2uAD7V5.description: A nice line over a quarry, fairly easy rig and nearly 0 no fall zone
2uAD7V5.anchorsInfo:
A tagline is nessacery
One anchor comes of a fallen tree which has rerooted and is still growing, this requires 4m spansets
The other anchor requires a sliding x using a 5m spanset
2uAD7V5.accessInfo: Park at the Ley quarry car park and walk into the quarry, visible on UKC

1EVVP29.description: There are a couple dirt jumps nearby, rarely used, but best to be aware of just in case

C1Vodrh.description: In the Boulder Area "Magic Wood", there are several possibilities of rigging midlines. This 77m midline goes from a massive tree to a concrete pillar of a road-gallery. The boulderers of the area enjoy it a lot when we are playing on our lines there.

N3a1xZc.name: Goose Line
N3a1xZc.description: Decent line with a lot of no fall zones. Not the best for walking, but better for beginners.
N3a1xZc.anchorsInfo: Super simple tree to tree line. 6 foot span sets on each tree are perfect!
N3a1xZc.accessInfo: Park just after the driveway on the road near by. Then hike down into the ditch a trail should present itself.

DDycUuf.name: Vallatscha2
DDycUuf.description: Tree on both sides. Easy access by trail

BCZcz7M.name: El anfiteatro
BCZcz7M.description: La tirada cruza el anfiteatro desde un ciprés (Lado A) a 2 o 3 puntos hasta una casuarina (Lado B)

EINBlJK.name: The Bill
EINBlJK.anchorsInfo:
Static side is 2x glue in bolts (bad condition, vandalised), tension side wrap the big block.
Tension side is North, static side South.

Wd87Sd6.anchorsInfo: All natural anchors. Big boulder to hug on one side and rock bridge behind a ficus plant on the other.

lfm1MCK.name: Linha dos F(e/i)lipes
lfm1MCK.description:
Midline de 100m de distància por 9m de altura.
O acesso so e possivel atraves de ascensao em ambas as arvores
lfm1MCK.anchorsInfo: Ancoragem Natural ambos os lados. Somente o lado B que e necessario uma extensao de ancoragem de uns 15m para utilizar outra arvore apoio tipo cavalete.
lfm1MCK.accessInfo: 25m de corda para subir a arovre. Julmar e Freio autoblocante o mais recomendado.

iZnoq_2.name: Barragem
iZnoq_2.description: Conquered by Vitor Luz (insta @vt_luz), and Gustavo Féo (insta @guufeo)
iZnoq_2.anchorsInfo: tree anchor on tension side, made with 2m sling or rope, and "natural" anchor on the bridge made with 4m sling or 20 meter of rope.
iZnoq_2.accessInfo: Super easy approach, parking the car 2 meter of the anchor, acess made through an soccer field

uGkkAjh.name: Tapé dans l'oeil
uGkkAjh.description: Quite long hike compared to the exposure, but it is a beautiful midline ! You may even have some people hiking stopping to talk :)

jiJ4Jcc.name: La Picapierda
jiJ4Jcc.description: Abandoned hospital I south of Madrid. Lines from 10 to 45 meter long. No longer cause of the high.
jiJ4Jcc.anchorsInfo: Cement columns, very easy to equip

XC98_Pu.name: Yıldızların Altında
XC98_Pu.description: Beautiful line that looks towards the bay of Türkbükü, north anchor needs to be rebolted to use the height in a better way. South anchor could be rebolted as well. Line needs at least x2 fractioning points.
XC98_Pu.anchorsInfo:
3 bolts on both sides, I only had zinc plated bolts, didn't use stainless steel on 2023 august so in couple of years it needs to be rebolted with SS bolts if one wants to rig.

North anchor needs extensions and uses a big rock as frame.

3bHfA-8.name: La Cabra Loca
3bHfA-8.description: A beautiful iconic midline in Monteverde area. The first midline/highline ever rigged in the area.
3bHfA-8.anchorsInfo: Natural tree anchor in both sides. The Static side require to ascend a tree which is more inland. The dynamic side is a tree that is close to the bridge at the side of a stone table perfect to hang out.
3bHfA-8.accessInfo: You can access either walking or, with a good 4x4 or AWD, park at the side of the dynamic side tree.

2ERkPjz.name: Cepi Green
2ERkPjz.description: Beautiful Highline/Midline first established and sent (fullman) by Nicolas Bahamonde on 18. June 2017

D2yLALn.description: Longest length possible at the location

S3G96N6.name: sweet deception
S3G96N6.description: roughly 35-40m. uses a large boulder for the static anchor, bring at least 30m rope and 6 draws to climb static side. for better acces info check out monkey town in the icecapades on mtn project.
S3G96N6.anchorsInfo: 3  3/8 bolts with quicklinks on either side(please leave them only finger tight).

fz4KiDG.description: 74m midline in granite. Bolted on both sides. Hangers on place. Easy rig. Only june - october as there is too much snow otherwise.

e-4eaq6.description: A super easy rig 110m midline bolted on stainless A4 bolts. 3 bolts per side. Hangers are left on site. Only in summer from June - October because there is too much snow the rest of the season!

IMn-ZBj.name: Mine
IMn-ZBj.anchorsInfo: Tensioning anchor has a nice  wood framed platform
IMn-ZBj.accessInfo: Left or right trail are good to join you there

VZp8byT.name: Paradise-Island
VZp8byT.description:
The perfect highline-island! But the island is only for rent e.g. for weddings, etc. So definitely restricted access - at least in high-season. But maybe it would even be worth to ask the owner if we could make some highline-shows on this island....

The line can be rigged about 15m above the sea between 2 trees. Super easy rig and such a nice place to chill ;-)

_qm6o1E.name: Midline Königswiesen
_qm6o1E.description:
West: single tree, but must be climbed to achieve equal level
east: form a triangular anchor from two trees
_qm6o1E.anchorsInfo:
west: single tree, but must be climbed to achieve equal level
east: form a triangular anchor from two trees

YRGuIwM.description: This is a good beginner midline with a rather large no-fall-zone on either side. The middle of this line is over the water and good for practicing mounts and whippers.
YRGuIwM.anchorsInfo: The base of two trees are wrapped with spansets for this beginner midline.

62ENhoh.description: Mid line, possibility of a water line if you lower the anchors. 10-13m high dependant on tide. At low tide its a mud pit, high tide up to 1.5m of water.
62ENhoh.anchorsInfo:
2 sizeable trees. Ive cut lots of branches so it should be clear where to put the anchors. 
7m up tree on small island. 5m up tree on big island
62ENhoh.accessInfo: Water access only. Not sure if its private land. I would suggest paddling from cresent beach parking lot, there is a kayak outfitter Cape Lahave kayaks in dublin shore, you can rent boats and sups from, and either paddle from their shop or cresent. Roughly 1.5-2km from either side.

FXnh6oS.name: Ibergeregg 30
FXnh6oS.description: Midline i climbing area , for acces info check the 47m line. Video with this line: https://youtu.be/bAsjaqENvuc
FXnh6oS.anchorsInfo: Bolted both sides. One side is a tower which ia a 3c climb. Other side you can walk.

OEQRlHf.name: Vallatscha1
OEQRlHf.description: Tree on both sides. Easy access by trail

PsX4kKo.name: SpaceDino
PsX4kKo.anchorsInfo: Both ancors are on one tree each. the nearside is just below the fire place a bit towards the road. The far side is below the road, the acces is steep but not very exposed.

VNeg9sV.anchorsInfo: trees booth side, one side sharing tree

fqM4Uuz.description: All lines in this cove must be rigged natural. The static anchor of this line shares the same boulder as the 80m line. A moderately sized spanset will suffice here. The tension side anchor is a large tree further back in the cove. A spanset is opt...
fqM4Uuz.anchorsInfo: All lines in this cove must be rigged natural. The static anchor of this line shares the same boulder as the 80m line. A moderately sized spanset will suffice here. The tension side anchor is a large tree further back in the cove. A spanset is optimal here as well.

eRVayO8.name: Buena Linea
eRVayO8.description: Midline over a Flash Flood area.
eRVayO8.anchorsInfo: One side is a tree the other is wrapped around a rock.

nqpbWrA.name: Suburban midline
nqpbWrA.description: Beautiful little spot tucked away in the suburbs of brisbane. Shielded from the road but not from the footpath.
nqpbWrA.anchorsInfo: One trunk of the big fig tree and a tree on the bank.

aD853mp.name: The Knights Of Hamdon
aD853mp.description: A more direct line across the gap, slightly shorter but easier to rig on the tree side..
aD853mp.anchorsInfo: Behind the quarry wall are tree's, rig from these then go over the top of the wall..

5lAj6sA.description: Cozy spot right next to the bridge. Great for beginners as it's easy to slide out on the line, tie in and to inspect the anchor.
5lAj6sA.anchorsInfo:
All natural. 
One of the trees are huge, so bring long slings.
5lAj6sA.accessInfo: You have to climb one of the trees.

1vvRJQ9.description: Chill easy midline, great for freestyle, two other parallel lines next to it.
1vvRJQ9.restriction: none: Area at risk of destruction by mining company
1vvRJQ9.accessInfo: 1-min walk from parking area. Hand tag scramble through creek and up to rock island on far side.

XTCiR_X.name: Costco
XTCiR_X.description: Tree-to-tree midline, climb trees to anchors. Two super solid, very tall oak trees. Lots of good personal anchor points. Multiple paracord nets up in the tree at the tension side anchor.
XTCiR_X.restriction: none: Private property, pls contact tonyarr.905@gmail.com.
XTCiR_X.anchorsInfo: Tree anchors, both sides about 75ft up. Permanent tag lines up both trees and across  where the line goes. It’s a semi-permarig, I’ll leave it up for a few weeks and take it down if the weather gets especially bad.
XTCiR_X.accessInfo: Private property but the owners are my in-laws and they’re super cool. Just reach out and come sesh with me!

Tfx7o0e.name: Prehistoric Pool Party
Tfx7o0e.description: Easy access near the little foot bridge. Space for 4-5 Midlines over a small gorge. You are very visible right off the path, so be respectful of the area so we don't have access issues. Lines range from 15-20 meters on the short one up to about 60 meters further down. Rigging is easy, but be careful as the area is very eroded and its easy to slip.

IfTC3HE.name: Medellin
IfTC3HE.description: Ubicado en el peñon un highline muy escénico
IfTC3HE.anchorsInfo:
anclajes naturales 
lado A es un arbol grande y el lado B es la base del puente ferroviario
IfTC3HE.accessInfo: Se puede llegar caminando por cualquiera de los dos lados

QYp7V8D.name: Church
QYp7V8D.anchorsInfo: Sling the large block on the west side (typically tension side), and use the two pinicles on the east side. Recomend static rope for both sides.
QYp7V8D.accessInfo: Do not use the church in any way!

-WyH-by.name: Fürwiggetalsperre
-WyH-by.description: Midline/Highline across a dam. Different lengths possible, longest would be around 80m. Not very very high, but awesome. 2m slings are perfect. Careful with the sandstone, it is very abrasive to your equipment. Most certainly not a legal highline, but we ran into no trouble so far.

e8pebuK.name: Crunchy Carrots
e8pebuK.description: Try to use heaps of tree pro, trees are really sappy and makes everything sticky. Anchor off of trees either side of scree gully. Use spansets and back up to trees behind. Walk in as for climbing area. Then continue in direction of fire track and ...
e8pebuK.anchorsInfo: Anchor off of trees either side of scree gully. Use spansets and back up to trees behind.
e8pebuK.accessInfo: Walk in as for climbing area. Then continue in direction of fire track and left behind the crag, continue up and around to the left. Should see a trail and line is off pine trees to the righ before you reach the fence.

Q2X1QoC.name: Lulimania 175
Q2X1QoC.restriction: partial: Those lines are higher then 25m, so they need to be announced at BAZL by SwissHighline. Your contact there for the region: ramun@swiss-highline.ch
Q2X1QoC.anchorsInfo:
Riggingside: 
Gras anchor w/a-frame. 
Farside:
 tree (d=40cm): 2x 2m Slings (for general backup 20m rope/slings required to reach another stable tree, d=30cm)
Q2X1QoC.accessInfo:
The line is right next to the road, be carefull by walking or crossing the road, the cars are very fast in that part. 

It‘s actually not allowed to sleep at the parking near the Spot. There is a parking with a toilet down the road ca. 2km direction Ticino form the lines where you can sleep in the cars. Another possibility is to sleep/park at the parking next to the Hospiz Restaurant, 1km up the road direction Graubünden from the Spot.  

In the valley is the Alpe Pertusio, in summer they might be there with cows. If they are there ask them if they are ok if you would rigg the line.  

Leave nothing behind when you leave, respect nature and infrastructure.

VNkZReK.name: Haole on the Rocks
VNkZReK.description: Single u-bolts on each side. Sharp lava rock, bring padding!

9sTmj4b.name: World of trains
9sTmj4b.description: There two train bridges. You can rig lines from 22 m to 30 m length. Also you can rig three lines at one time.
9sTmj4b.anchorsInfo: There are I-beams. You need some protections. And four loops approx 3m length.
9sTmj4b.accessInfo:
About 7-10 min walk from the metro station Tushinskaya, on the top of the purple line.
Should go close to rail ways towards the river.

wdbf4Ur.name: Hole 19
wdbf4Ur.description: Natural anchors. Rock wrapping. Bring long spansets and padding.

BTyk4iq.name: Shoe Loose Blues
BTyk4iq.description:
One Side open acces (direct street), other side on land of Moritzburg.
Line without Backup (to low), enough space for leash fall.
Be aware of the small river underneath, when slacking with loose clothes.
BTyk4iq.accessInfo: Street side easy, other side a bit harsh (no grounded pathway)

oD5o6hn.name: Bar M
oD5o6hn.anchorsInfo: Tree Anchor One Side, 3 Bolt Glue In's Other Side

ukK5Mfv.name: Walliforline
ukK5Mfv.description:
You need to take protections and 3 slings of 2m (2 for the north) and one of 3m (for the south with the 2m). South : 2 point of 10mm (on the stairs) and a iron barrier 
North : 2 point of 10mm and one point of 12mm (just after a big iron barrier)
ukK5Mfv.anchorsInfo:
South : 2 point of 10mm (on the stairs) and a iron barrier 
North : 2 point of 10mm and one point of 12mm (just after a big iron barrier)

g5v4reE.name: Coburg
g5v4reE.anchorsInfo:
Big docking bollards.
Do not use the fences as Aframes

MEtR-eM.name: Casa Ecologica
MEtR-eM.description: La tirada es entre un ciprés (lado A) y un árbol que se divide en 3 (Lado B)
MEtR-eM.anchorsInfo: El lado B se encuentra unido a 3 puntos con un anclaje permanente tensado con malacate

bE4IDJx.name: Sic transit gloria mundi
bE4IDJx.description: Tree midline, permanently rigged! (unless I am away for a long time or I need the gear for something else)
bE4IDJx.anchorsInfo:
Tree 1: Poplar (populus tremula), anchor is where the trunk divides to several main branches. Leash rings are on this side.
Tree 2: Oak, anchor at the same hight.
bE4IDJx.accessInfo: Line is next to the parking lot, visible from there.

3Wz17IC.description: Two bolts with chain links on each side, half inch stainless bolts. Tension side on a clean patch of rock on the north side of the river, static side on the back side of the concrete bridge support. No falls zones about ten feet each side.

fBXFnil.name: BZ Slack
fBXFnil.description: contact use on FB for real data
fBXFnil.anchorsInfo:
5 smal trees one one side 
on the other one big tree for an A-Frame with three trees behind

KW1XncS.name: Lagun
KW1XncS.description: Popular park in the city, climbing on the trees

C-xlg5h.name: Žan Fu
C-xlg5h.description: Climbing sector Rebro, Blagaj. You have a 20min walk from the parking lot for the Stjepan fortress
C-xlg5h.accessInfo: One side you can throw rope from behind and anchor the rope to 1 bolt and tree and then jumar up from the other side.. Farside you have to throw the rope over the rock approx 20m tall, you can anchor the rope on the other side on 2 climbing bolts. And then jummar up to the anchor. there are 2 anchors one above the other. The longer line goes on the upper anchor and "Žan fu" goes on the lower anchor. You can backup each anchor on the other one

RCH-p9c.name: Darner
RCH-p9c.description: Mid high waterline. Leashfalls don't reach water
RCH-p9c.anchorsInfo: One is high up in a tree, so needs tagline and a rope to get to. The other one is a tree at a person's standing height on slope with easy rigging and tensioning conditions.

X4bzZrN.name: Lepiej późno niż wcale
X4bzZrN.description: First midline in Bielsko-Biała, nice view but a lot of bushes, definitely you need to use a tagline.
X4bzZrN.anchorsInfo: Both tree anchors, on the left side use a conifer (I don't remember what exact tree species it was) where there's a small gap between bushes, that side is a bit sketchy, because you need to rig quite high on the tree and it works a lot, especially once the line gets more loose with leashfalls. But it holds. On the other side the tree is more firm, but the line goes close above one branch, so this is where you should prioritize with your padding.
X4bzZrN.accessInfo: Climb both trees, good branches begin a bit over 2 meters high, below you need to crawl up by hugging the trunk or bring some stool or an extra sling to tie around a branch.

CyNxVat.name: BZ Slack
CyNxVat.description: contact use on FB for real data

bgnriQy.name: Waterfall
bgnriQy.description: 2 anchors to a tree, or better two trees,
bgnriQy.restriction: none: Don't leave the line too long and release the tension. Young people come to jump the waterfall
bgnriQy.anchorsInfo: I prefer to use two trees, one is over the waterfall, the other one is near the pass along the river. You need a protection for the slack
bgnriQy.accessInfo: Go to the picnic's place at the end of the road, walk 2' along the river

CwYe7dL.name: Val Chamuera 2
CwYe7dL.description: Tree on both sides. Easy access by trails

uhb1ecr.name: Tom&Gerry
uhb1ecr.description:
Nice line under the trees, I leave there a lanyard to simplify the rigging hoping it has not been broken by the weather! Be very careful not to drop stones standing at the anchor 'cause it's a famous climbing spot and few people use a safety helmet!!!
Edit: at 06/2024, the tagline was still present.
uhb1ecr.anchorsInfo: there are four fixe for both anchor point (bring a spanner with you), 4/5 spanset in total (maybe 3 on the dinamic anchor). the dinamic anchor is the upstream one.
uhb1ecr.accessInfo:
the pathway is simple but be very careful not to drop down stones/woods.
The static side can be reached from the back, so no need to use the (old) fixed ropes at the front.

_UCwCjk.description: Protect trees or your gear will be confiscated with $400 fine

5FtGtT5.name: Balkan beauty
5FtGtT5.description: bolted and first ascent in 2013 by Slovenians
5FtGtT5.anchorsInfo: 5x 10mm bolts with hangers on each sides. You will need extra spansets to place linelockers at a safe distance from the rock. Anchors are not at the top boulders as they are not solid rock.

pLUuKQe.restriction: partial: Highline spot on the “Forest ofTHEN” property. Entrance fee required. For more information, visit @forest_of_then on Instagram.

JkS7orN.description:
Midline over the water.

See info on the 57m line

Cy7umgm.name: Hmmm... Curious
Cy7umgm.description: Very curious location for a slackline..
Cy7umgm.accessInfo: Easy to get on top with little effort..

znPoKlc.description: Protect trees or your gear will be confiscated with $400 fine

g7nYUVn.name: O Abraço
g7nYUVn.description: The average line, the hug, is approximately 175 m, cutting the bay of Vidigal entrance, having free access and frequented by fishermen. opened on 05/19/2019 by Nova Geração RJ
g7nYUVn.anchorsInfo:
to the two anchors are natural, embracing the back beaks of the two pedroes.
carry 2 large ropes for each anchorage of approximately 15
g7nYUVn.accessInfo: enter the tracks behind the bicycle that is in front of the Vidigal, in the middle of the trail has the fork to go down to the stones and arrive at the anchorage B or go straight to the sea and hit the anchorage A

f57nPsD.description: Midline with a nice ledge to hang out on under the bridge
f57nPsD.anchorsInfo: Glue-in bolts both sides,  3 on the ledge and 3 on the face of the beam
f57nPsD.accessInfo: Walk under the bridge on the south side or ascend cliff after approach from Nichols landing parking lot

84-YjP7.name: Botanical Garden
84-YjP7.description: Nice and open area. It's a park, so it's also quite social.

w5IcQhA.description:
max long line 90-100m   
possible to built a Rodeo

bQH2uW0.name: Schnapszahl
bQH2uW0.description: Slackline next to the Refugio Tejos at an altitude of 5860m. Established by Antonia Rüede-Passul & Lukas Irmler
bQH2uW0.anchorsInfo: Rigged clean, on two fridge size boulders.
bQH2uW0.accessInfo: Walking 5 min from the Refugio Tejos. Easy rigging.

wvSTwT9.name: Breath
wvSTwT9.description: Hidden deep in Greyfield Woods over a picturesque waterfall in North Somerset, Established by Ian Jennings..
wvSTwT9.anchorsInfo: Huge tree on one side, small tree/bushes on the other side..
wvSTwT9.accessInfo: Walk around the top of the waterfall to reach far side anchor..

JAw3c3j.name: Zimzeleni odlschool freedom
JAw3c3j.anchorsInfo: both sides with 2x12mm + 2x10mm (backup) bolts. You will need hangers.

5dRRgG6.name: Hikuwai 40
5dRRgG6.description: Rig between two trees in forest.
5dRRgG6.anchorsInfo:
Two trees, can throw/walk tagline across gap. 
West side anchor takes 6m spansets minimum. 
East side anchor takes 3m spansets minimum. 
Use tree pro :)

wK2zYPr.name: El horchaton
wK2zYPr.description: Línea de facil acceso dentro del parque las grutas del mamut
wK2zYPr.anchorsInfo:
Anclaje mixto

-Lado estático (anclajes mecánicos 4 bolts con chapa) subiendo por las escaleras que llevan a la tirolesa.
-Lado dinámico (2 árboles de pino) cruzando el rio por el puente colgante, tomar el sendero que está atrás de la estación de la tirolesa anaranjada
wK2zYPr.accessInfo: Dentro del parque las grutas del mamut

OjNqaO2.name: Beginners Luck
OjNqaO2.description: Not the most spectacular line, but a really fun length to walk, and very easy to access (less than 1 minute from the parking). Great for a one-day rig.
OjNqaO2.anchorsInfo: All natural. Several spansets and some static cord are recommended. Sling the holes in the rocky upper part of the cliff on one side, and anchor to the large bush and rock on the other. See pictures.
OjNqaO2.accessInfo: Walk to the edge of the cliffs.

znihidb.name: la rame
znihidb.description: good line in a peaceful place and the beach at 100m
znihidb.anchorsInfo: on the High of the tree, need to do back on rope. Easier on north side to put the stretch.

sYrv2xe.name: Varniukų Highline
sYrv2xe.description: As it is a pedestrian bridge to a major park there's a lot of attention from passerby. Named Varniukų (Crow hatchlings) highline as there's a nest a few meters away from the tree used as anchor
sYrv2xe.accessInfo: There is a smaller tree just under a meter away from the anchor tree - very handy to climb up to more sturdy branches.

1Q22UEL.name: INVU
1Q22UEL.description: This line was originally visualized by a member of the community: Don Virgilio Brenes. Was rig for the first time for a show for the community done with Salomé Cholet among Nicolás Ospina and Paco Zubieta. The line is in the middle of the town. Is a good freestyle rig or for beginners practice.
1Q22UEL.anchorsInfo: Natural anchors both sides. Static side is good to go with two 1.5-2 meters slings that will go around a "root" of the majestic fig tree. Dynamic side is good to go with two 3 meters slings that will go around a tree.
1Q22UEL.accessInfo: The line is a the side of the street. The static side is right at the edge of the street and the dynamic side you can access crossing a little bit of grass. Both sides are next to the street.

r-5Az4q.description: Shortest line available at a safe height
r-5Az4q.anchorsInfo: Huge docking bollards on either side

YZGF_tN.name: Outdoor Sports Centre
YZGF_tN.anchorsInfo: Tension onto the obvious tree in the clearing. Static side is a spanset around the tree with in-situ static for ascending.

ZjqYEZY.name: Käppeli Unicorne line
ZjqYEZY.restriction: partial: Please contact the owner for permisson. Rafa https://www.facebook.com/kappeli.ch/?locale=de_DE
ZjqYEZY.anchorsInfo:
Close Side: (near store hug): Bolts in the ground. Airframe and long slings are needed. 2,5m sling or rope for the 3-point anchor equalisation with 5m of slings for the extension to the a-frame. Back up Sliding-X with Rope/Webbing. Minimum 3 shackles.
Far Side (forest behind the house) : trees are used, long slings for backups. Tree Anchor. Bring Treepro, two 1,5m Slings and a rachetline (or something similar) to back up the tree on the large log uphill behind the anchor tree. 
To get there, take the hiking trail for a few metres and then make a 270° turn up the hill.

BVJJXNS.description: The line is tidal and can be 4m off the water in high tides. Big docking bollards on either side
BVJJXNS.anchorsInfo: Big docking bollards on either side

HCnQyGO.name: Seclusion
HCnQyGO.description: A nice midline that can be setup pretty fast and taken down. Great for day trips, or leaving the line up and coming back to it over the course of a few days. About 1/3 is a no fall zone, you'd whip into the ground or a bush.
HCnQyGO.anchorsInfo: 4 bolts with hangers on each side.
HCnQyGO.accessInfo: Scramble. I'd recommend wearing your harness w/ a PA so that you can clip to the walls while rigging, makes standing easier.

s8Ot7pf.description: Small forest of Gum Trees located next to a dirt jump park. Many options for lines.

Hx2XOaW.name: Tendue de Cléchet
Hx2XOaW.description: Midline for beginners and for freestyle training
Hx2XOaW.anchorsInfo: 4 points on each side in D12
Hx2XOaW.accessInfo:
Both sides are accessible by car. 

Side 1 : from the hospital, go down the little hill in direction of Annecy center and turn right before passing the bridge.

Side 2 (gender side) : pass the bridge turn left at the first roundabout and left at the second, you will enter in a living residence of few buildings. Continue straight and go down until a route in rocks appears to the left. Take it and go till the river appears.

TyNwPN9.name: Coral Reefer
TyNwPN9.description: Sunny, no shade. Bring uv protection and water!. Long spansets. I use a couple of 20ft ones. Bring protection, sharp rocks
TyNwPN9.anchorsInfo: Long spansets. I use a couple of 20ft ones. Bring protection, sharp rocks.

XFT5d0e.name: quarantine freedom
XFT5d0e.description: Ask to torino sul filo for more information. Natural tree. From the supermarket just take the pat on the right side 5 min walking to the pat
XFT5d0e.accessInfo: from the supermarket just take the pat on the right side 5 min walking to the pat

SCREFMU.name: Des Frites dans la Croziflette
SCREFMU.description: Scenic mid-waterline that goes the full length of the lake, from the obvious point on the valley side to the small cliff below the path
SCREFMU.anchorsInfo:
Dam side: 3m sling on the top of the highest rock that side. Padding all around
Path side: 3 V-threads (abalakov in rock) drilled in 14mm. They might fill with dirt and need cleaning. About 3m extension needed, and padding
SCREFMU.accessInfo: Helicopters may fly this low!!! Warn PGHM/ local airports even if it's below NOTAM requirements!

wVH1qgk.name: A short line above the lake
wVH1qgk.anchorsInfo:
Dynamic side (higher) is close to the old building, just near parking spot, some tree with the nice view to the static side.

Static side you need to climb a tree with a rope (throw it up, catch a branch and ascend using some device)

ajzQHcJ.name: Don Genaro
ajzQHcJ.description: Beautiful line with a great view on one side and forest on the other. The trees underneath are so tall that you can hit the branches when you fall.
ajzQHcJ.anchorsInfo: Four bolts on the east side and a solid tree on the other.

DHKwfwD.description: 3 MMS far side,  Big natural anchor dynamic side
DHKwfwD.anchorsInfo: 3 MMS far side,  Big natural anchor dynamic side

5-jeQyy.name: Chester mid/highline
5-jeQyy.description: Unfortunately no photos, but looser version of the shorter one. 5ft off the ground at the highest point, crosses right over a manhole cover. Send at your own risk!

vBsYdhv.description: The line is tidal and can be 4m off the water in high tides. Big docking bollards on either side
vBsYdhv.anchorsInfo: Big docking bollards on either side

8A5Xuw1.name: Buchenegger Wasserfall
8A5Xuw1.description: Short Line, but easy to rig.

YcBFN_H.name: pà
YcBFN_H.description: bolted by local slackline association, slackline brescia asd
YcBFN_H.anchorsInfo: west side 3 bolts, est side 4 bolt needs a easel
YcBFN_H.accessInfo: right over the classics climbing routes, very easy to find

29vQ9y7.name: Sitio VM
29vQ9y7.restriction: partial: É uma via em propriedade privada. para acesso é necessário aceite dos moradores; +55 35 999625948

dsJiHOn.name: Plava lama
dsJiHOn.description: Midline above the waterlines. Free solo material.
dsJiHOn.anchorsInfo:
Tension side (south): tree; 4m spanset + 1m for height
Static side: tree; 2m spanset, 10m high on pine tree

NrL2BCT.name: Evergreen jungle midline on the mississippi
NrL2BCT.description: Spansets, plenty of trees to wrap
NrL2BCT.anchorsInfo: Spansets, plenty of trees to wrap

chcSX5v.name: Spacey Gracy
chcSX5v.description: Water-midline between Gracy Cove at Pace Bend Park. As of 2017, this is the best spot for highlining in Austin. Be aware $10-15 admission is required for entry into the park. The south anchor is in the middle of a campsite, so it's potentially a hassle to rig if another party is occupying the site before you.
chcSX5v.anchorsInfo:
Tensioning side (South): Anchor off the big tree next to the picnic table. Be sure to set the anchor high enough (>6ft off ground) so that the line doesn't come in contact with the cliff when someone is on the line.

Static side (North): There are two potential anchor points. 1) is a boulder that is jammed along the cliff. The anchor is to the right of a part of the cliff that clearly looks jumpable. 2) is a tree further to the edge of the cove. Some traversing required (north anchor beta to be revised).
chcSX5v.accessInfo: (Connecting the Line): You can 1) swim to the north cliff of the cove and climb up, 2) swim to a point more inward and walk the line to the anchor (be aware of having to move over trees), or 3) fly a drone across.

20qUtER.name: "Highline"
20qUtER.description: The very first highline spot of Vilnius. Not really used by anyone at the moment. But tribute to the past is a must!

wa6KJLe.name: Perra del infierno
wa6KJLe.description: Easy acses ,its a good DWS climbing area
wa6KJLe.anchorsInfo: There are just the 6expansion bolt, will need screws, and hangers,

QRTVc-f.name: No Brakes
QRTVc-f.description: Contact Micah Carroll in "Slackchat: Michigan" Facebook for beta

vATwS5z.name: La Esperanza de los Calcetines
vATwS5z.description: Parking of the Roman Bridge. Coming from Roda de Isabena and La Puebla de Road it is in the left side going downhill. Then you walk down to the roman bridge.
vATwS5z.anchorsInfo:
Anchor on the parking lot side: Tree with two trunks and backup bolt on a rock on the path....
Anchor on the other side: 4 bolts (10mm) in the rock

lvOr1a7.name: Loco line
lvOr1a7.description: Easy rig highline in a forest, there are mulitiple lines to rig here and we have permission to go here from Stadt Zurich
lvOr1a7.anchorsInfo: Two trees, large size , so 4m slings per tree are needed.
lvOr1a7.accessInfo: There is a bus stop in witikon called Loorenstrasse about 5min walk from the spot.

rddOHU-.name: Scoiattolandia
rddOHU-.description: Super accessible woody midline, just 5min from Cima Sappada Church.
rddOHU-.anchorsInfo: just two big trees plus a hay bale to lift up a bit the line.

ejVBJMG.name: Val Chamuera 3
ejVBJMG.description: Tree on both sides. Easy access by trails

VT_ln85.anchorsInfo: Anchor near the road is a tree, the other anchor was on the metal structure

8Hw58rK.name: Albariño en Seiñas
8Hw58rK.description: Trees, it is needed to climb and setup some rapel
8Hw58rK.anchorsInfo: Trees, it is needed to climb and setup some rapel

4y7d97B.name: Hollow Highline
4y7d97B.description:
Easily linked midline, no climbing required.. Almost all the tree's are bomber..
Has been called 'Hollow-man' but mostly called the 'Hollow Highline'
Half of this line is over ground on the west side, beware the double drop hidden platform  
on this side if you do walk off's.. Good beginners line..
4y7d97B.anchorsInfo:
Trees on both sides, spansets work fine.
The east side tree is on the bank, it has a tree directly behind it for a backup.
The west side tree is large and set back about 10m from the edge.
You can move the west side anchor forward to the edge but its a lot shorter.

kqRie4n.name: Check-a-Line
kqRie4n.description: This spot is tricky to get to. You have to park your car far away and walk about 20 minutes to get to this popular cave visited by deep water soloists. If you're bold, you can set this line up as a waterline. But then it's a 7-8m fall onto water if you slip. So it's better to rig it as a midline using a leash. You can use natural anchors as there are a bunch of rock formations around.

4on-fJr.name: La midline Cita
4on-fJr.description:
Repost des 2 lignes mais avec photos des ancrages, parce que c'est quand même le spot majeur de stras (et de schlag) ;)
D'autres longueurs seraient possibles mais pas assez de hauteur pour les rendre safe. Utiliser un backup cousu dans l'idéal, sinon laisser le moins de mou possible sur le backup.
Prévoir un ficellou ou une corde pour monter la ligne depuis le rempart d'en face, les accacias ont poussé depuis le printemps 2024... (on ne peut plus faire le tour)
4on-fJr.restriction: partial: seulement accessible le dimanche, ne pas installer les autres jours

B6LPvTD.name: Midline del planetario
B6LPvTD.description: Midline rigged in the pillars of this bridge, it's a good one for practice because it's not very exposed
B6LPvTD.anchorsInfo: The anchors are metal pillars, you will need padding for the spansets, and a couple of ropes for being secure entering the line, besides the usual rigging gear. You will see the anchors, as they are slightly bend

-iMHLUZ.name: Reotier
-iMHLUZ.description: 4 points on each side, plus a backup point. Easy midline, on a climbing spot, really easy access, near the road
-iMHLUZ.anchorsInfo: 4 points on each side, plus a backup point

fgJ630R.name: La interestelar
fgJ630R.description:
La interestelar se abrió en Enero del 2025. La primera vez que se montó fue con ayuda e iniciativa de Daniel Garzón y Daniel Bernal, dos buenos amigos rolos que estuvieron de visita una temporada en Ibarra.  

Usamos cintas de poca elongación por que la altura no es tanta. con Sky diamond y una extención de poliester, el sag es lo mínimo posible y se mantiene un estandar alto de seguridad.
fgJ630R.anchorsInfo: Los dos anclajes son árboles. Uno de ellos se encuentra en el parque del Tren, éste es el lado A y el lado B es un eucalipto que hay que escalarlo aproximadamente 10 metros para que el la cinta este a nivel 0.
fgJ630R.accessInfo: Es muy fácil acceder al spot y a los anclajes caminando. los vehículos pueden llegar bastante cerca. por la calle Carlos Barahona, o hasta los comienzos del puente por la Calle el alpargate.

iB_xjGd.description: Small forest of Gum Trees located next to a dirt jump park. Many options for lines.

zfdLLzX.name: La llave oculta
zfdLLzX.description: Nice midline in Los Picachos area in Peña de Bernal.
zfdLLzX.accessInfo: Preferred ascent is from Chichidho side. It's an easy 20 minutos walk to the Picachos

Gwr1D8V.description: Small forest of Gum Trees located next to a dirt jump park. Many options for lines.

47h5Kmq.name: Emar's Freestyle
47h5Kmq.anchorsInfo:
4 bolts on the rock for static side.
Big beech tree and an a-frame for dynamic chill side.
47h5Kmq.accessInfo: Car park or towpath with bikes

N0YyaKH.name: cate nuar cat blanch
N0YyaKH.description: One side is a nice tree the other is more a big busch, so bring tree slings and some statickrope to make an nice equalised ancor
N0YyaKH.anchorsInfo: one side is a nice tree the other is more a big busch, so bring tree slings and some statickrope to make an nice equalised ancor

qi-j2EG.name: Forest freestlye
qi-j2EG.description:
Chill spot in the forest 
Parking close by
Over river
Sheltered from the wind

kksQrRA.name: hardturm water midline
kksQrRA.description: On one side a tree and the other side the rail of a bridge. It is in the middle of the city so easy to get there
kksQrRA.anchorsInfo: on one side a tree and the other side the rail of a bridge.
kksQrRA.accessInfo: it is in the middle of the city so easy to get there

B4kJy55.description: A nice, but off level mid/highline over a beautiful waterfall.
B4kJy55.anchorsInfo: Trees on each side, which can be backed up to neighboring trees, or cement guard-rail posts.  The "Left side anchor" (Facing the waterfall) starts off-level, and to keep the line from rubbing on a large rock may need to be raised to shoulder or head height.

YhsRRfo.name: Zarečki krov Midline
YhsRRfo.description: There are 4 bolts on each side, 7 12mm and one 10mm with no plates on them. Easy set up walking on the top
YhsRRfo.anchorsInfo: There are 4 bolts on each side, 7 12mm and one 10mm with no plates on them
YhsRRfo.accessInfo: easy set up walking on the top

kakhLKG.name: Desire path
kakhLKG.description: Łatwa dostępność miejsca. można podjeżdzać śmiało pod sam wycią i tam parkować

U8TKA0r.name: Chota Ladakh
U8TKA0r.restriction: full: I went to the spot on November 17, 2023, and there was a guard blocking access. After chatting with him, he told me that 4 people had died 2 years ago. As a result, no one was allowed to install a highline.

QzGio4O.name: Tiny Top line
QzGio4O.description:
A tiny line at the top of the Hollow, easily rigged from tree's..

NOT RECOMMENDED TO RIG too low. If mainline fails you are likely to hit the ground.

DWs4Bb-.name: Scenikc
DWs4Bb-.description: Nestled in a banquet of trees with opportunities for lines nearing 200, this spot is a gem. While the surroundings may not be the prettiest due to the homeless population. This is a safe line that your gear is unlikely to be tampered with. Was perma-rigged for a period of time and saw no issues.
DWs4Bb-.anchorsInfo: Easy to find, tree to tree anchors. Bring an extension ladder for the static side (E Reservoir Dr Side) & rig the Tension side (Prospect Ave) as high as you can.

9bZHTTe.name: Fins que plogui
9bZHTTe.description: 10mm bolts, 2 on each side. From Col de Puymorens, following Vall d'En Garcia. Parking close to the spot but difficult road
9bZHTTe.accessInfo: From Col de Puymorens, following Vall d'En Garcia. Parking close to the spot but difficult road.

yG5LyOo.name: Tiene Tutto
yG5LyOo.description: One tree per anchor, 35 cm diameter each one
yG5LyOo.anchorsInfo: One tree per anchor, 35 cm diameter each one

xonT79Z.description:
The line is an about 60m, rigged off of 2 trees on each side of the gully. We used ropes to access each anchors as everything is chossy.
We drank the river Easter worth no problems and a great waterfall just behind to cool off

H5z_efz.name: In da game Line
H5z_efz.description:
Both ways trees 
In one anchors recomend  to use an aframe

Ig26btG.name: Mantra de salamandra
Ig26btG.description:
Next to a waterfall. Tagline usually there. When rainy access and rigging quit difficult because it’s a steep hang.

Edit: Due to a landslide, the original anchor nearby the waterfall is gone. There might be other Trees.
Ig26btG.accessInfo: Parking at the top of the Little Canyon

Jbgx1pb.name: FORSE NON ERANO 40M
Jbgx1pb.description: THERE IS THE SMALL ROPE ALREAY UP, REMEMBER TO LEAVE IT AGAIN.
Jbgx1pb.anchorsInfo: NATURAL ANCHOR BOTH SIDE YOU CAN USE SOLID TREE. IN ONE SIDE YOU NEED 6/8m OF SLINGS AND A (GRILLO STORTO), IN THE OTHER YOU NEED 2 SLINGS OF 2/3m EACH. THE DIFFERENCE OF THE LONGNESS DEPENDS ON HOW YOU WANT TO BUILD YOUR ANCOR. (WE USE 3+3+2  3+2)
Jbgx1pb.accessInfo: WALK/CLIMB UP BOTH SIDE OF THE MOUNTAIN, IN ONE SIDE YOU CAN FIND A DUBBLE TREE.

ovdDFWa.name: La mamahuevo
ovdDFWa.description:
Esta cinta se monto por primera vez en el 2021 con Paul Cevallos que vino de visita unos días.  La distancia puede variar entre 79 y 82 mts, dependiendo de donde se ancle en el puente La altura es de 19 metros exactamente medidos desde el puente hasta la parte de abajo donde pasa el río Tahuando. 

Aquí también, se hizo la primera competencia de speedline simultaneo del País en el Encuentro decembrino de 2024. El primer lugar fue para Jesús Gonzales de Bogotá y el segundo para Cristian Castro, de la Mitad del mundo.
ovdDFWa.anchorsInfo: El Anclaje A es el arból donde también se ancla La perma de 106. El anclaje B, es la estructura del puente. En el anclaje A colocamos la banana y hacemos la tensión y en el anclaje b solo ponemos el Ojal.
ovdDFWa.accessInfo: Fácil acceder al spot y a los dos anclajes

pzRIGb9.name: Zeus
pzRIGb9.description: Une ligne magnifique qui trône au dessus d'abysse...
pzRIGb9.anchorsInfo:
Un gros heure coté sur et un grand chêne cote nord.
Les deux arbres et les deux accès nécessité du matériel de treeclimbing pour installer cette midline

sLKxYja.name: Engagement Line
sLKxYja.description: Contact Micah Carroll in "Slackchat: Michigan" Facebook for beta

FWArnbL.description: Línea de muy fácil acceso en el Ajusco. 40m de largo, alrededor de 17m de alto, un midline de instalación muy sencilla y muy disfrutable por su vista hacia la cumbre del ajusco.
FWArnbL.anchorsInfo: Viendo el midline de frente, el lado izquierdo (1) tiene 3 placas, por lo que se recomienda usar deltas o mosquetones de acero (Hay más de tres placas en la roca, pero las demás se descartan rápido pues están evidentemente mal instaladas). Del lado derecho existe la posibilidad de anclarse directamente a un árbol (2) con eslinga o cuerda estática o usar una enorme laja de roca (3) que se encuentra un poco más hacia afuera, lo que aleja un poco la línea del cerro. En caso de usar la laja, existe de igual forma tres placas, una colocada en la parte exterior y dos en la parte interior.
FWArnbL.accessInfo:
La llegada al sitio es muy simple, basta tomar la carretera hacia el Ajusco y al llegar a la Y griega, tomar a la derecha el circuito del Ajusco. Aproximadamente 8 kilómetros después está una entrada de terracería a la derecha con una caseta (imagen), donde hay que entrar y doblar a la derecha para avanzar unos 200 metros de regreso por la terracería.

Para ascender al lado izquierdo (1) hay que escalar una pared sencilla de unos 7 metros y luego avanzar hacia la izquierda entre la maleza por toda la orilla hasta localizar la roca.

Del lado derecho el ascenso es más simple, se sube caminando por detrás de las peñas, ya sea rodeando por completo por atrás y subiendo, o rodeando un poco y luego escalando una pared muy sencilla de unos 3 o 4 metros. Ya en el sitio está muy accesible tanto el árbol (2) como la laja de roca (3) que también se puede usar como anclaje.

a-xGcfm.name: Diavolina
a-xGcfm.description: spit in both side but in one side you need to bring your own spit (there are already the screw)

CRlacy1.name: La Praline blanche
CRlacy1.description: Superbe ligne devant une cascade et à proximité des pistes de ski de Praz-Sur-Arly, elle dispose d'une vue imprenable sur la Pointe Percée.
CRlacy1.anchorsInfo:
Côté  du Sangle (chill-side) : prendre la base d'un arbre avec une petite plateforme devant.
Coté Ban rouge (far-side): Grimper dans les arbres pour rigger sur le 2eme arbre en partant du bas (CF Photo)
CRlacy1.accessInfo:
Par la piste de Basse-Combe de Praz-Sur-Arly (remonté mécanique).
En ski de randonnée ou à pied depuis le Leutaz.

_p6LnFz.name: Coco Beach
_p6LnFz.anchorsInfo: one side on the rock, on the other side on the first small tree up to the patio

NpLTs1H.name: Homespot exposed
NpLTs1H.description: 4x 12 bolts without plates on each side
NpLTs1H.anchorsInfo: 4x 12 bolts without plates on each side.

LldsPpB.name: Fat Loogeys
LldsPpB.description: In the gully on the far side of the Nautilus, right below torpedo tubes. The line threads through a perfect gap in the trees, with a no fall zone near the dynamic anchor and better exposure the further out you get.
LldsPpB.anchorsInfo: Natural anchors, wrapped boulder or trad gear on  dynamic side and wrapped, balanced boulder on static side

hJf0-au.name: Sunrise Rasta Highline
hJf0-au.description:
A easy line installation and perfect spot for morning. 

Video : https://youtu.be/KYdfAXF1OTU?si=N4X_amF3daROiba4
hJf0-au.anchorsInfo: You can use de two threes for isntallation.
hJf0-au.accessInfo: Falllow the beach and start the little road and you arrived.

yIGV6I-.anchorsInfo: One side natural rock anchor, the other side 3 bolts 10 mm
yIGV6I-.accessInfo: by foot, 10 minutes from the road

iL11T1I.name: Ortega falls
iL11T1I.description: "Ortega falls" typed in the maps will take you directly to the spot. There are bolts on each anchor side.

sk6AZj8.name: Training
sk6AZj8.description: Around 80m long line above beauty watefalls, acces is easy from the parking above. Easy to get on the other side by bridge close to it.
sk6AZj8.anchorsInfo: Trees on both sides 2m and 3m spansets need.

eku9Iyg.name: Warren Drugs
eku9Iyg.description: Nice little line, no no-fall zones in the middle. More complicated rigging than other lines in the area so setup much less often. Nearby this area of state forest on Gopher road drug traffickers would stop to move drugs from Chicago to the Twin cities. The sheriff happens to live on this road! So with the line being located in Warren, MN it only makes sense to call it Warren Drugs.
eku9Iyg.anchorsInfo: Static side anchor is a bomber tree. Tensioning side however requires using static rope to tie off multiple trees and an A-frame for stabilization. Bring more static rope than you think you will need.
eku9Iyg.accessInfo: The is a parking lot at the end of Gopher road to park in. Do not park on side of the road because the must move farm equipment through here. From the parking lot hike down into the valley until you find the rock creek bed. Then follow that until you can take a right turn. To tension side hike up the easy hill. To static side hike around because of the small rock cliff.

ZAAgM4j.name: El techo
ZAAgM4j.accessInfo: by foot, 10 minutes from the road

6fM7fT9.name: Guendalina
6fM7fT9.anchorsInfo: One side 3 bolt anchor, one side with natural

k5GnjRk.name: Adaltree
k5GnjRk.description: Fun midline with lekka sunset vibes. Make sure your backup is toit as it's not super high up.
k5GnjRk.anchorsInfo: 3m spansets around the trees. We have cut some branches on the North East tree so rig about that height.
k5GnjRk.accessInfo: Public park but keep the psssst screams to a minimum as it's in a residential area

35vT7M0.name: Midline/waterline (à ouvrir) seulement à marée haute

AZpNXlo.name: Fenton
AZpNXlo.description: Amongst the trees over the shallow river.
AZpNXlo.anchorsInfo: Trees - Road side/ tension (large) sling at the very bottom; far side/static (smaller) sling as high as you can reach, use other tree slightly further back for redundancy.

Mod1iZ3.description: line spots from middle lines to 100m longline

TGQTWsV.name: Gaute da suta
TGQTWsV.description: tree anchors, there is a small cord to pass the line and a bridge to bring equipment to the other side. Not very high but enough to be safe and I don't know the real length but it seems around 40. There are several small branches touching the line.
TGQTWsV.accessInfo: It is at the beginning of the via ferrata Infernone, after having left the car in Sordevolo you have to follow the signs for "ferrata dell' Infernone" and you'll see the cord over the river really close to the beginning of the climbing path.

_DZjSrJ.name: Adeline
_DZjSrJ.description: 4 bolts on one side, pole on the other
_DZjSrJ.anchorsInfo: 4 bolts on one side, pole on the other

je3VaP-.name: Pirmokas
je3VaP-.description: A nice midline spot along Šventoji river bend above Mikieriu outcrop.
je3VaP-.anchorsInfo: A pine and an oak trees.

8r39h8A.name: Freedom Park
8r39h8A.description: great stand of trees for many different lines

1wFcB0w.anchorsInfo: trees both sides, slings of 2 or 3 m

zYoGtQL.name: another bridge
zYoGtQL.anchorsInfo:
Trees on both sides. 
Start on the northern side, very steep, rappelling down. Cross with SUP or similar, the river can be fast there

8ozPc4y.name: Horsetheif 180
8ozPc4y.description: Easy approach, beautiful views, natural anchors.  Cool line!
8ozPc4y.anchorsInfo: The northern anchor is built out from trad gear, so bring your cams!  There are several clean cracks to choose from.  The southern anchor is made by slinging rock at the south end of the tower, and extending out to the norther part.  Bring lots of spansets or at least 100 feet of static.

wnm26NB.name: Chinchuline
wnm26NB.description: 4 bolts one side, 3 on the other
wnm26NB.anchorsInfo: 4 bolts one side, 3 on the other

MRfG4gZ.description: Easy rig highlines in a forest, there are mulitiple lines to rig here and we have permission to go here from Stadt Zurich
MRfG4gZ.accessInfo: There is a bus stop in witikon called Loorenstrasse about 5min walk from the spot.

g-MgIEP.name: Скалы на Халактырском плже
g-MgIEP.description: С одной стороны деревья, с другой химические анкера(шпильки 12мм, две на основную линию, две на бэкап). Подъезд на машине, подъем на сопку 5 минут
g-MgIEP.anchorsInfo: С одной стороны деревья, с другой химические анкера(шпильки 12мм, две на основную линию, две на бэкап)
g-MgIEP.accessInfo: Подъезд на машине, подъем на сопку 5 минут.

Ie2d35s.name: Giants Bite
Ie2d35s.description:
WARNING - BOLTS OVERDUE FOR REPLACEMENT. RIG WITH CAUTION.

10mm hangars and bolts required. Abseil down south face to access bolts in wall (one of which is a reach round the nose of face). Rig ab rope round rock plate on top. Recommended rig with a rope backup, but must be static due to short falling distance. Must be rigged tight to avoid deck out in event of gear failure. 
Easy access to both sides of exposure, long haul up steep hill to the spot, flat ground for camping either side. Beware of loose rock and harsh weather conditions; the spot is an old quarry littered with boulders and loose rock, and is very open to the elements.
The spot is technically on private land, but due to high level of climbing activity in the area, no known problems have been raised by the landowners. Spot is used locally by dirtbike motorcross riders.

b0uv835.name: Sueco viejo
b0uv835.description: Parking of the Roman Bridge. Coming from Roda de Isabena and La Puebla de Road it is in the left side going downhill. Then you walk down to the roman bridge.
b0uv835.anchorsInfo:
Anchor on the parking lot side: 5 bolts on a rock were you have to go down one meter from the path.
Anchor on the other side: 5 bolts (12mm) on the rock , quite obvious

sieTcoK.restriction: partial: Its on the farm land to cross the tag line. Pay attention to the warnings

hBFEAVP.description: great midline over water with simple tree anchors. You will need a kayak or a boat to access from boat ramp 7 or gain permission from property owners backed up to the cove.
hBFEAVP.anchorsInfo: obvious trees, although the cedar could use backing up if you want to err on the side of caution

BuMVD4N.name: Bárbol
BuMVD4N.description: Una segunda línea en el Ajusco. 42m de largo, alrededor de 17m de alto, un midline de instalación más compleja pues uno de los anclajes se realiza en un árbol que hay que escalar para poder montarlo.
BuMVD4N.anchorsInfo: Viendo el midline de frente, el lado izquierdo (1) es un enorme árbol en el que se recomienda usar unos 10 a 15 m de cuerda estática en dos  o tres vueltas para asegurar el anclaje, así como otro tanto de cuerda para el backup. Del lado derecho el anclaje es a una enorme laja de roca (2) con tres placas, una colocada en la parte exterior y dos en la parte interior.
BuMVD4N.accessInfo: La llegada al sitio es muy simple, basta tomar la carretera hacia el Ajusco y al llegar a la Y griega, tomar a la derecha el circuito del Ajusco. Aproximadamente 8 kilómetros después está una entrada de terracería a la derecha con una caseta (imagen), donde hay que entrar y doblar a la derecha para avanzar unos 200 metros de regreso por la terracería. Para ascender al lado izquierdo (1) hay que escalar el árbol de alguna forma segura. Actualmente se está considerando colocar alguna especie de peldaños fijos o una cuerda fija para poder ascender con mayor facilidad. Del lado derecho el ascenso es más simple, se sube caminando por detrás de las peñas, ya sea rodeando por completo por atrás y subiendo, o rodeando un poco y luego escalando una pared muy sencilla de unos 3 o 4 metros. Ya en el sitio está muy accesible la laja de roca (2) que usa como anclaje.

LoiGhxd.name: Skinny Butt
LoiGhxd.description: Short midline between two trees on top of Skinners Butte.
LoiGhxd.anchorsInfo: You have to climb up 2 trees to rig this line. You will need a throw line, a rope and ascenders. Climb up 30ft into the fir tree to set the static side. Climb up 15 feet into the cedar tree on the hill.
LoiGhxd.accessInfo: Easy access from the parking lot. Have not had any complaints from law enforcement. This line has only been rigged 2x

DgYmuyi.name: Golden Leaves
DgYmuyi.description: Tree on river bank and bridge
DgYmuyi.anchorsInfo: Tree on river bank and bridge

Cw49W6b.name: offlevel line
Cw49W6b.anchorsInfo: natural both sides. farside 12-14m slings, nearside 9m with a coprahitch, lots of padding

OIaV9uM.name: El Yesito del Lobo
OIaV9uM.description: 3 bolts in one side and spanset arrount the palm on the another side
OIaV9uM.anchorsInfo: 3 bolts in one side and spanset arrount the palm on the another side

4i4d9L8.description: Midline over water, if you fall in the middle you might get wet!

zVwMNRI.name: About time..
zVwMNRI.description:
A good length midline with an easy tree rig, the diagonal position gives a good feeling of exposure
as you spend more time over the hollow..

IAY5NIT.name: Kairos
IAY5NIT.description: The water needs to be really calm to be able to set this line up.

oJpQ23x.name: Devil's bridge
oJpQ23x.description: Safe distance to walk is 25m at max with high tention, would not recomend to use 1 hangover method, line would just be to low to even bother
oJpQ23x.anchorsInfo: Static tree is lower so i use wooden ladder if no one steals it ( but possible to rig without it) tention side is 15m away from the edge so there are no problems there

9TSbGq3.name: Russian River midline
9TSbGq3.description:
60ish meter midline you can leash fall without hitting the water. Great line for freestyle sessions that you can end but untying and jumping in. 
Recommend to use the the south anchor as the dynamic side even though you might not want to set up base camp on that side of the river.
We did a scorpion wrap around the tree and diverted around another to get the angle right. Dealers choice when you rig, there’s a few options.
Static side has to be this one tree tucked behind a little beach. It’s off a trail, pretty obvious. Use a bunch of spam sets or aid ladder to climb the tree. 
Watch out for people boating, rafting and swimming they have the right on way.
9TSbGq3.anchorsInfo:
Two trees
12ft span set for dynamic, 6ft for static.
Bring extra to climb about 25 ft in the static tree, or an aid ladder. Takes some significant rigging knowledge to do rig off this tree, don’t make it your tensioning side like we did lol.
9TSbGq3.accessInfo:
Park at sunset beach parking lot and walk in on the trail south facing the parking lot behind the gate, don’t take the trail by the Porto potties
$6 to park on a weekend

t3cDOD7.name: Dunker
t3cDOD7.restriction: none: No official parking / direct trail
t3cDOD7.anchorsInfo:
Beach side (static) uses trees to get ~17 feet off the ground;
Boulder side (tension) 2 wedge bolts that require hardware (hangers & washers/nuts)

wsd-HD9.description: This part of Warmer Damm offers a few options 10-50m
wsd-HD9.anchorsInfo: The trees are pretty old and have a large diameter.

0JeJyNh.name: Ryanair no vuela hoy
0JeJyNh.description: Urban midline in an abandoned hospital

B35ybNh.name: Finally family

g_oQMy5.anchorsInfo:
Tree to Tree Natural rig. 

Homeside (North): 

2 large junipers at (xxx.xxxXxxx.xxx). Both trees can be equalized, but I originally just used one. Run your anchor to the edge and use the small junipers as a directional only. 

Farside (South)
2 medium junipers at (xxx.xxxXxxx.xxx). Best to equalize both of these or use one as an "oh shit" backup. I'd consider them both bomber, but this is the dessert so YMMV. Run your anchor towards the edge and use the block as a directional by wrapping it.

nMtSLS_.name: (EDRIS GANJI) #2 Slack Park Line – Steel Pole Anchored System (Afghanistan)
nMtSLS_.description:
This slackline was installed inside a slack park using an engineered anchoring system based on steel electrical utility poles.
The line was rigged following engineering safety principles, with a focus on redundancy, load distribution, and material compatibility.
Due to limited access to slackline-specific equipment, certified industrial safety gear was used.
All connections were secured with backup knots and secondary systems to minimize single-point failure.
nMtSLS_.anchorsInfo:
The anchors consist of fixed steel electrical utility poles mounted on reinforced concrete foundations.
These poles are designed to withstand high static and dynamic loads and provide a stable anchoring structure.

Load was applied directly to the steel structure using industrial webbing slings.
The anchoring system was configured to ensure proper load alignment and avoid torsional stress on the poles.
All anchor points were redundantly backed up, and each primary connection was secured with additional backup knots.

The system was visually inspected and mechanically checked before use.
nMtSLS_.accessInfo:
The slackline is located inside a controlled slack park environment.
Access is limited to trained individuals with proper safety knowledge and permission.
Any future setup or modification should follow the same engineering and safety principles used in the original installation.

This highline was opened for an event held at the Caracol State Park with the purpose of encouraging the practice of slackline in the region and showing where the sport can go.
THE INSTALLATION OF THIS HIGHLINE WILL NOT BE PERMITTED WITHOUT AUTHORIZATION OF THE MUNICIPAL CITY HALL.
m9DuEFs.anchorsInfo:
4 fixed bolt inox with 10mm diameter each side.
need 8 bolt hanger for all
m9DuEFs.accessInfo:
Access to anchorage 1 is made by a rappel of ~ 20 meters. Mooring of abseiling is done on trees.
Beware there are many loose rocks and wasps


Access to base 2 is more complicated, needs to cross the river,
climb on trees ~ 12 meters make use of a fixed rope and use another rope of at least 25 meters to the second abseil.
This abseiling occurs quite friction, take protections for rope.

FYkL0-a.name: (EDRIS GANJI) Slackline Park Line in Afghanistan#1
FYkL0-a.description:
This is the first slackline installed in a slack park in Afghanistan.
The line was rigged and walked by(EDRIS GANJI) the first Afghan highliner .
All safety considerations were fully respected. Due to limited access to slackline-specific equipment, certified industrial safety gear was used.
All connections were backed up with additional knots and redundant systems to ensure a higher level of safety
FYkL0-a.anchorsInfo:
The anchors were attached to sturdy, reliable trees within the park. 

All anchor points were evenly aligned and additionally supported. 

After each main connection, support knots were applied. 

The anchor system was carefully checked and tested before the rope was used.
FYkL0-a.accessInfo:
The slackline is located inside a slack park in Afghanistan.
Access is possible only with permission and proper safety knowledge.
Visitors should respect park rules and safety guidelines.

When the trifecta is complete and all three lines are rigged its a perfect location to hang on a sunny day after class.
FhF5umv.restriction: none: Once again probably only rig in this location if your a student as its a small campus

by car, bike or walk. very easy to get there.

hHI_mmt.name: Perfect Dip
hHI_mmt.description:
This spot is perfect for highline practice. The dip means almost no tension needed for a good walk. There are options to make slightly shorter by using neighbouring trees.

This park gets very busy, but even on a late Sunday afternoon there was not that many foot trafic at this specific spot. It is a little bit away from the path as well which gives a bit of sense of privacy.
hHI_mmt.anchorsInfo: Two trees. On the tree at the bridge, I rigged anchor at the first bough of the tree. On the hillside anchor, roughly hip height. Could maybe do lower, but with this I hardly had to tension to not touch ground.

xZfLxtC.name: Concrete Beach
xZfLxtC.description: rodeo line with bolted anchors in concrete
xZfLxtC.anchorsInfo: The anchor closest to the train tracks can be accessed by hiking up to train tracks and dropping down to the ledge where the concrete trestle meets the bridge; you should see the hanger bolted into the ground. The far anchor can be accessed by hiking up the overgrown ridge to the top of the opposing concrete wall and trekking along the edge of the ledge until you see the hanger bolted into the ground; very close to one of the stone block outcroppings on the top of the concrete ridge.

xN6twYQ.name: Antaqua

xN6twYQ.accessInfo: Requires sling to climb up on one side. The other is doable with chimney climb between two trees

GSAdqlv.name: Rybiskiu Rodeo

C9Kx9ut.name: Sesimbra spot
C9Kx9ut.description: Very nice location, at 30min by walk from bus 207 leaving from lisboa to sesimbra. Quite sketchy with a 15m but works. If you have 20m+ it's probably more convenient and further from the rocks
C9Kx9ut.anchorsInfo: Rocks, plan on takings nice slackprotectors (towels, etc)
C9Kx9ut.accessInfo: Have shoes because rocks can be sharp

bZB6vLw.name: Klindts Cove
bZB6vLw.description: A nice, longer waterline above the Columbia River.  Klind's Cove is a popular swimming spot and there are several businesses next by; that means you will probably have an audience.  The Columbia can be EXTREMELY windy some days, so wind dampeners are recommended.  Water level can also be affected by The Dalles Dam.
bZB6vLw.anchorsInfo: For the park side of the line, use the large circle-bolt, large boat cleat, or wrapped rocks.  Consider bringing an A-frame because these anchoring options are a little far from the water.  On the far side, wrap the top of one of the basalt cliffs.

xvuKI6r.name: Wiel L-Ghasri waterline
xvuKI6r.description:
Summer swinging spot. Plenty of natural rock anchors all over the place
nothing special needed but slings or protection recommended. Easy access, stone carved stairs
xvuKI6r.anchorsInfo:
Plenty of natural rock anchors all over the place
nothing special needed but slings or protection recommended

x8Y3Sad.name: Three River Sliver
x8Y3Sad.description: A large concrete post sits about 50ft off the shoreline and provides your bomber anchor out in the water. Kayak out there to set the anchor
x8Y3Sad.anchorsInfo: A large concrete post sits about 50ft off the shoreline and provides your bomber anchor out in the water. Kayak out there to set the anchor.

Rdeai0-.description: Sur arbre et sur grosse roche. L'installation demande un grand spanset
Rdeai0-.anchorsInfo: sur arbre et sur grosse roche. L'installation demande un grand spanset.

1bMMwNa.name: Linha de água
1bMMwNa.description: Stunning water line infront of a waterfall
1bMMwNa.anchorsInfo: Two bolts on each anchor (bolted on 13 June 2025 with 12mm Stainless Steel expansion bolts)
1bMMwNa.accessInfo: Scramble down to the waterfall from the road on the South or park on the North and walk a steep path down.

8oo7oMP.name: Arch Enemy
8oo7oMP.description: Beautiful waterline rigged inside a sea arch at Durdle Door in Dorset
8oo7oMP.anchorsInfo: Use Hexes/Cams in cracks on both sides of the arch..
8oo7oMP.accessInfo: Although it is assumed there is a right to access this line under CRoW legistation, the rangers and estate think otherwise. In order to avoid incidents with the rangers, make sure to only come here on quiet days. No bank holidays, no sunny weekends. Try to keep it to weekdays and colder conditions in your wetsuits! Behaving nicely will keep access for years to come for future slackliners! Thanks :)

pS5h1Hj.description:
Tree 2-3m sling
6m sling connect under the bridge. fotos follow
pS5h1Hj.anchorsInfo:
Tree 2-3m sling
6m sling connect under the bridge. fotos follow

7enHCkV.name: Waterline sentier du littoral
7enHCkV.description: The rocks have rusty boat anchors that obviously hold up well for the required tension of this 32m. You need a rope that is not too elastic because it is only 50-60cm high above the water.
7enHCkV.accessInfo: Follow "Sentier du Littoral" from St Florent (Marines du Soleil) or from the little beach up on the north

GMStnHp.description: Trees, 4' island side 6' on the arbutus
GMStnHp.anchorsInfo: Trees, 4' island side 6' on the arbutus

Df-6lXi.name: Oakledge Water Line
Df-6lXi.description: AMazing water line very close to downtown Burlington.  All you need is some way to get your line out there (Kayak, floaty tube, etc..).  It's maybe a 5 min swim so not very far off land.  Enjoy!
Df-6lXi.anchorsInfo: Two trees about 2 ft diameter each.

3FqSt0x.anchorsInfo:
2 broches scellées.
Bien vérifier l'état des broches: le rocher n'est pas de grande qualité et le fier en crue peut abimer les broches.
L'ancrage rive droite est partagée avec une autre ligne, prévoir des petites sangles.
L'ancrage rive gauche est un peu en retrait car le rocher au bord de l'eau n'a pas l'air très solide (son creux). Prévoir une sangle de 1m+ pour éviter que les frottements de la slack sur le rocher. Une bonne idée d'entourer la sangle avec des protections d'arbre pour les frottements.
3FqSt0x.accessInfo: Voir info sur la 31m a côté.

bj9lf4G.name: Hai la Pistola
bj9lf4G.anchorsInfo: On each side 2 x 10mm Inox bolds, bring the hangers
bj9lf4G.accessInfo: Access to the spot with the Car

xxf8aF1.name: Poseidon
xxf8aF1.description: It is a waterline with anchors 20-25 meters over the sea... we couldn't cross it all cause we were under water in the middle of the line... let's try with less streaching webbing and some a-frames on the anchors
xxf8aF1.anchorsInfo: 3 holds for mms on each anchor

eGXMddS.name: Montaña amarilla
eGXMddS.description: Pequeña waterline al lado de un psycoblock

_Pdmh4v.description: NOT YET RIGGED Stunning bay, with potential for waterlines, thin slings or climbing protection.  Sharp limestone, so be careful about edge protection.  Probably mid to high tide.

n88pM_0.description: Really nice spot for waterlines, from 25 to 140m lenght

B) keeps changing. at the moment I would use a tree further away and rig the line to 80m (2024)

SRAlIud.description: 2 glue-in rings at each end. Drop off the footpath just before the council gate, and scramble along the rocks to reach the anchors. Follow Behana Gorge Rd to the end until you reach the council water supply carpark. Walk along the footpath for 3.2...
SRAlIud.accessInfo: Drop off the footpath just before the council gate, and scramble along the rocks to reach the anchors

LNIpsKD.name: Calcifers
LNIpsKD.description:
beautiful line over clear deep water (30m). natural anchors (looped holes in solid but sharp rock), 5m sag max. We used an extended 6m sling 100m windu tensioned with pulleys to not touch the water. parking above and a small bar with seats and music. 
Rigged Lewis and Laura with Dimitri and Kris from Hybrid Slacklines- Cheers for the spot guys! See you next time!

gKqm2Ac.description: Shallow waters near both anchors, but the middle is plenty deep.
gKqm2Ac.anchorsInfo: bomber tree on one side, probably 15' up the tree. Bolts or feature wrap on the far side.

1jPbEhS.name: Castagna Line
1jPbEhS.description: it is an old abandoned port

mYoa9ta.name: Vizinha da Valeria

mYoa9ta.anchorsInfo:
ancoragem A: 2m Sling, manilha, lime lock, softrelease.

mYoa9ta.accessInfo:
Árvore Ancoragem A 15m
Árvore ancoragem B 10m

rz-5e8S.name: Save the last bay of Albania
rz-5e8S.description:
Its in the bay called "The last bay" in Ksamil, a city in the very south of beautiful Albania (with tons and tons of possibilities for water and highlines everywhere!!!!!)

All natural rig, no bolts needed 😍

On both sides massive boulders, but only 1,5m above sea level.
Left side boulder has a perfect hole where i girth hitched a 2m sling through it and made it my tension side.

On the right i took a 2,5m sling and girth hitch wrapped it around some edges at the top of the boulder for the static side.

Its very sharp rocks so make sure youre padding well where the slings are moving. 

I rigged a Unicorn webbing with a lot of tension so the sag in the middle wasnt goin too deep under water 😋

Really beautiful spot. Currently its off season so very little people around, mostly travellers/hippies from around the world. 

Potential for more shorter lines close by.
rz-5e8S.anchorsInfo:
All natural rig with roundslings.

Sharp rock so pad well!
rz-5e8S.accessInfo: Super easy, watch out for sea urchins close to the anchor rocks. There is a small olive yard where you can park directly at the beach and chill in the shade or at the bar and restaurant if its open. From the parking its 50m to the beach and the rocks are pretty close at the beginning.

wepGpjZ.description: Short waterline easy to rig and walk, fun with tubular webbing
wepGpjZ.anchorsInfo: Boat anchor on one side with cement A-frame, tree on the other side

aySCY0k.anchorsInfo: Northern side: tree, rig high enough to not block the path along the river. On the bridge: I chose the blind end pillar.

TnBY5FL.name: water du camping

Ligne majeure installée avec le maire de la commune...
Respecter la règlement et ne sauter pas d'en haut de l'arche - merci ;)

élingues de 4m pour le pilier sud
élingue de 3m pour le pilier nord

KqB0FVR.description:
Beautiful Line in the natural park of Stelvio 
Recomend to not ring during the summer cause of really touristic place

rHmjpge.restriction: full: Restrictions in the area changed, it is now fully forbidden and will cost you a hefty fine!

QRoSvF4.name: Enogerra waterline :D
QRoSvF4.description: Nicest water in brisbane (except for the lilies)

3PI_I1l.description: We use the base of a single tree for the tensioning side anchor and utilize a very skinny tunnel in the rock face to pass a spanset through for a boulder wrap on the static anchor. The static anchor is difficult to find unless pointed out by someo...
3PI_I1l.anchorsInfo: We use the base of a single tree for the tensioning side anchor and utilize a very skinny tunnel in the rock face to pass a spanset through for a boulder wrap on the static anchor. The static anchor is difficult to find unless pointed out by someone who knows it's location.

fm0ByKb.name: Blue Horizon
fm0ByKb.description: Beautiful line over a small bay with rocks to cliffjump from.
fm0ByKb.anchorsInfo:
Two Bolts each side. 
Tension Side: 2 delta quicklink, padding, shakel and 2m spanset

Far side: 2 delta quicklinks, shakel and 1m spanset

A small dry bag (Not needed but IT saves you some climbing around)
fm0ByKb.accessInfo:
From bigger bay (parking) around the corner, down to the smaller bay (small path on the right at the First Corner).
There is a small beach, from There you can Walk over the rocks to the tension Side. Take a dry-bag and swim to the far Side 😃

JQof_3N.description:
Nice natural anchor 60m waterline. With a low-stretch line you can keep it loose all the time with sag up to about 4m.

It's very windy location, so don't forget a set of wind dampeners!

The line is very high so if you are not sure you can easily walk it, I'd reccommend leaving a 5m "ledder" made of slings somewhere in 1/4 of the line. Otherwise you will have to slide on your butt to the safe starting spot several times... ;)

For more information about the area and the ascent check the 16m Rusty Bolt line description.
JQof_3N.anchorsInfo: Ohhh and look for the anchor spots on about 4-5m of height.

4jJM76Q.name: Diable en aval
4jJM76Q.description: Un rocher à entourer avec élingue de 3m et 4 points posés en face. Apporter plaquettes.
4jJM76Q.restriction: partial: Avant 11h ou après 18h pendant la période de vacances scolaires d'été
4jJM76Q.accessInfo: Approche facile. Impossible à poser en pleine saison après 11h car trop de monde

EC3Xrkp.description: Stair Hole, small waterline in a beautiful setting and can be rigged longer..

Best and easiest waterline spot in basel.
cafe nearby, close to town, barbeque station

con: there is no shadow and in summer you get burned on the line
Jq62JSs.anchorsInfo: the rigging side you need a 2m long sling and the static side needs a 4 or better 5m sling (big bollard).
Jq62JSs.accessInfo:
by car, bike or walk. very easy to get there. sometime there a ships, so then it`s not possible but this happens not very often.
you can rig this line complete without geting in the water. fast and save.

fXAZlV8.name: Parking plos ligne éphémère
fXAZlV8.description: Quand le lac déborde, on en profite.
fXAZlV8.anchorsInfo: Arbre du parking + A frame. Arbre en face

AoeFbpm.name: Sculpture Falls Waterline 1
AoeFbpm.description: 1 of 3 waterlines to set up by Sculpture Falls. Spot availability depends on water levels of the creek. At times the creek is dry or too low so check before rigging.
AoeFbpm.anchorsInfo: East Anchor (Tensioning): Look for a rock at the top of the bank with 5 bolts drilled into it. It's a couple hundred feet downstream from the falls and the trail forks off to it. West Anchor (Static): There's a bolt on the rock ledge that is closest to the falls. Set padding underneath the anchors (both sides) to prevent abrasion from the rock.
AoeFbpm.accessInfo: Park on the frontage road south of the intersection of Mopac and Highway 360, just before Mopac crosses over the gully. Hike north on the trail about 1.5mi and it will be on the left. It's the second set of waterfalls, the first one you'll pass on the trail is Twin Falls earlier on.

tc6biOi.name: Dead River Waterline
tc6biOi.description: Anchors off two big trees on the right side of the beach. Please use tree pro!

CghpOka.name: De los cuervos
CghpOka.description:
ENG: The first waterline in Uruguay. Its a private camping, so you need to talk with the owner. Beautifull place to rest and chill with a amazing river to swin and waterline! :D

ESP: El primer waterline de Uruguay. Esta ubicado en un camping particular, para armar esa cinta tenes que hablar con el dueno del lugar. Un lugar hermoso con el rio santa lucia al lado. Lindo para nadar y praticar slackline. :D
CghpOka.anchorsInfo: anchor on trees on each side, from camping side you will establish the base anchor from where you'll take off, the other side is relatively dense bush with trees, you'll have to clean the way for the connection, there's need to bush walk a bit reach a denser tree about 2-3 meters from the edge, advise wearing shoes and pants for protecion.

U9bgoJA.name: Show your muscles
U9bgoJA.description: A waterline on Lac d'argent near some climbing spots. very nice line. The lake is deep enough around this spot, no worries to dive.
U9bgoJA.anchorsInfo: Two big trees. One near a lunch table. The other one is just near some big rocks.
U9bgoJA.accessInfo: To get to the lake, you can do a hike for about 1 hour passing around the lake. Or you can use a another route that takes about 15 min, but it has a lot or up and downs.

-awmVPl.description:
1 tree Sag at 2m at least
other maybe 1m max

pipe in the water!!
but able to see it

469eBg3.description:
_Natural anchors. (I will upload photos of them when I will go back there.)
_Deep enough to take good fall into the water. Be careful when it is low tide though.
469eBg3.anchorsInfo: Natural anchors. slings around 2 meter 2.5 meter are enough.

April - September 7.00 - 21.00 
October - March   10.00 - 18.00

wSi6B5o.name: La grande bleue
wSi6B5o.description: Plusieurs possibilités sur ancrages naturels faciles à trouver : 35 mètres, voire 40 à 45 en cherchant les pointes de l'anse. Rodéolines car les ancrages utilisables sont hauts. Lignes de folie qui rendent sa gloire au sud et à la méditérannée française !!! Belle expo, belle eau (pour pas dire splendide !!!), calme et tranquilité, plongée magnifique aux alentours directs, … bref !!! Si le sud vous parait inaccueillant par l'incivisme de nombreux sudistes, venez ici vous ne serez pas déçus !!!
wSi6B5o.anchorsInfo: Naturel sur rochers, ya même des backups lunules. Prendre au moins 2 élingues par côté ou bien des bouts de sangles pour entourer certains rochers, + protections mais le rocher est pas coupant.

Lox0kqQ.name: Smith's Cove Waterline Fun!
Lox0kqQ.anchorsInfo: The rock itself. Bring lots of line protection as they are sharp!

3pC0QSg.name: Longline Water Humaita , By : Edney Junior
